02-11.text: The computer worm Anna Kournikova, which would affect millions of users worldwide, was released by a 20-year-old Dutch student.

02-11.title: 2008 East Timorese assassination attempts
02-11.subtitle: Rebel East Timorese soldiers invaded the homes of President José Ramos-Horta and Prime Minister Xanana Gusmão, seriously wounding the former.
02-11.text: Rebel East Timorese soldiers invaded the homes of President José Ramos-Horta and Prime Minister Xanana Gusmão, seriously wounding the former.

02-11.title: European Space Agency
02-11.subtitle: The European Space Agency's Intermediate eXperimental Vehicle is launched on a Vega rocket.
02-11.text: The European Space Agency (ESA), pronounced 'ee-sah', is a 23-member international organisation devoted to space exploration. It has its headquarters in Paris and a staff of around 2,547 people globally as of 2023. ESA was founded in 1975 in the context of European integration. Its 2026 annual budget was around US$10 billion.
02-11.location: European organisation dedicated to space exploration

02-11.title: Saratov Airlines Flight 703
02-11.subtitle: Saratov Airlines Flight 703 crashed shortly after take-off in Russia due to icing conditions, killing all 71 people on board.
02-11.text: Saratov Airlines Flight 703 crashed shortly after take-off in Russia due to icing conditions, killing all 71 people on board.

02-12.title: Britannicus Was Born
02-12.subtitle: An imperial heir doomed by a poisoned cup
02-12.text: Britannicus was born to Emperor Claudius and Empress Messalina, a prince whose very name commemorated his father's conquest of Britain. Yet his birthright would prove a death sentence. When Claudius was poisoned and replaced by Nero, the young Britannicus became a rival too dangerous to tolerate. At a palace dinner, Nero had him poisoned. The boy who should have been emperor died at fourteen, a casualty of Rome's most ruthless dynasty.

02-12.title: Roger Traynor Was Born
02-12.subtitle: A jurist who reshaped California law
02-12.text: Roger John Traynor was born in Utah, destined to become one of the most influential jurists in American legal history. As Chief Justice of California, he authored groundbreaking opinions that transformed tort law, tax law, and criminal procedure. His intellectual rigor and progressive vision helped make the California Supreme Court one of the most respected and innovative courts in the nation during the mid-twentieth century.

02-12.title: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
02-12.subtitle: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn, winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1970, is exiled from the Soviet Union.
02-12.text: Aleksandr Isayevich Solzhenitsyn was a Soviet and Russian author and dissident who helped to raise global awareness of political repression in the Soviet Union, especially the Gulag prison system. He was awarded the 1970 Nobel Prize in Literature "for the ethical force with which he has pursued the indispensable traditions of Russian literature". His nonfiction work The Gulag Archipelago "amounted to a head-on challenge to the Soviet state" and sold tens of millions of copies.

02-12.title: Cold War
02-12.subtitle: Cold War: The 1988 Black Sea bumping incident: The U.
02-12.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
02-12.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

02-12.title: Murder of James Bulger
02-12.subtitle: Two-year-old James Bulger was led away from New Strand Shopping Centre in Bootle, England, and murdered by two 10-year-o...
02-12.text: On 12 February 1993 in Merseyside, England, two 10-year-old boys, Robert Thompson and Jon Venables, abducted, tortured, and murdered a two-year-old boy, James Patrick Bulger. Thompson and Venables led Bulger away from the New Strand Shopping Centre in Bootle, where Bulger was visiting shops with his mother. His mutilated body was found on a railway line 2+1⁄2 miles away in Walton, Liverpool, two days later.

02-12.title: Kim Ji-min Was Born
02-12.subtitle: A rising star of Korean drama arrives
02-12.text: Kim Ji-min was born in South Korea at the dawn of the new millennium, part of a generation that would grow up alongside the global explosion of Korean popular culture. As an actress, she would contribute to the vibrant landscape of Korean drama, a medium that has captivated audiences from Seoul to Sao Paulo. Her career reflects the unprecedented international reach of Korean entertainment in the twenty-first century.

02-12.title: NEAR Shoemaker
02-12.subtitle: NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft touches down in the "saddle" region of 433 Eros, becoming the first spacecraft to land on an asteroid.
02-12.text: NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft touches down in the "saddle" region of 433 Eros, becoming the first spacecraft to land on an asteroid.

02-12.title: Trial of Slobodan Milošević
02-12.subtitle: The trial of Slobodan Milošević, the former President of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, begins at the United Nation...
02-12.text: The trial of Slobodan Milošević, the former President of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, begins at the United Nations International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia in The Hague, Netherlands. He dies four years later before its conclusion.

02-12.title: Buffalo Niagara International Airport
02-12.subtitle: Just before landing at Buffalo Niagara International Airport, Colgan Air Flight 3407 crashes into a house in Clarence Ce...
02-12.text: Just before landing at Buffalo Niagara International Airport, Colgan Air Flight 3407 crashes into a house in Clarence Center, New York, killing the house's occupant and all 49 people on board the aircraft.

02-12.title: Pope Francis
02-12.subtitle: Pope Francis and Patriarch Kirill sign an Ecumenical Declaration in the first such meeting between leaders of the Cathol...
02-12.text: Pope Francis and Patriarch Kirill sign an Ecumenical Declaration in the first such meeting between leaders of the Catholic and Russian Orthodox Churches since their split in 1054.

02-13.title: Otto and the Pope's Pact
02-13.subtitle: Emperor and pontiff divide power in Rome
02-13.text: Emperor Otto I and Pope John XII co-signed the Diploma Ottonianum, a landmark agreement that recognized the pope as ruler of Rome while establishing the emperor's authority over papal elections. This delicate balance of power between church and state defined medieval European politics for centuries. The document formalized a relationship that would generate both cooperation and conflict between popes and emperors for the rest of the Middle Ages.

02-13.title: Barbara von Annenkoff Born
02-13.subtitle: A Russian-born star of German cinema
02-13.text: Barbara von Annenkoff was born in Russia, a future actress who would make her mark on the stages and screens of Germany. Her career spanned both film and theater during a tumultuous era of European history. Emigrating from Russia, she brought a depth of experience and emotion to her performances that reflected the upheavals of the twentieth century, contributing to the rich tapestry of German-language cinema and theater.

02-13.title: World War II
02-13.subtitle: World War II: The siege of Budapest concludes with the unconditional surrender of German and Hungarian forces to the Red Army.
02-13.text: World War II: The siege of Budapest concludes with the unconditional surrender of German and Hungarian forces to the Red Army.

02-13.title: Nashville sit-ins
02-13.subtitle: African-American college students staged the first of the Nashville sit-ins (sign at protest pictured) at three lunch co...
02-13.text: African-American college students staged the first of the Nashville sit-ins (sign at protest pictured) at three lunch counters in Nashville, Tennessee, as part of a nonviolent direct-action campaign to end racial segregation in the U.S.

02-13.title: Coso artifact
02-13.subtitle: An allegedly 500,000-year-old rock is discovered near Olancha, California, US, that appears to anachronistically encase a spark plug.
02-13.text: An allegedly 500,000-year-old rock is discovered near Olancha, California, US, that appears to anachronistically encase a spark plug.

02-13.title: Gulf War
02-13.subtitle: Gulf War: The United States Air Force dropped two laser-guided "smart bombs" on an air-raid shelter in Baghdad, Iraq, wh...
02-13.text: The Gulf War was an armed conflict between Iraq and a 42-country coalition led by the United States. The coalition's efforts were in two phases: Operation Desert Shield, which marked the military buildup from August 1990 to January 1991; and Operation Desert Storm, from the bombing campaign against Iraq on 17 January until the American-led liberation of Kuwait on 28 February.

02-13.title: Vitinha Was Born
02-13.subtitle: Portugal's midfield jewel enters the world
02-13.text: Vitinha was born in Portugal, a nation that produces footballing talent like few others on earth. The gifted midfielder would develop through Porto's youth system before catching the eye of Europe's top clubs. His technical brilliance, vision, and composure on the ball made him a prized asset, continuing Portugal's extraordinary tradition of producing world-class midfielders who combine artistry with tactical intelligence.

02-13.title: Harvard–Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics
02-13.subtitle: The Harvard–Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics announces the discovery of the universe's largest known diamond, white dwarf star BPM 37093.
02-13.text: The Harvard–Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics announces the discovery of the universe's largest known diamond, white dwarf star BPM 37093. Astronomers named this star "Lucy" after The Beatles' song "Lucy in the Sky with Diamonds".

02-13.title: Confederated Tribes of the Umatilla Indian
02-13.subtitle: For the first time in more than 100 years the Umatilla, an American Indian tribe, are able to hunt and harvest a bison j...
02-13.text: For the first time in more than 100 years the Umatilla, an American Indian tribe, are able to hunt and harvest a bison just outside Yellowstone National Park, restoring a centuries-old tradition guaranteed by a treaty signed in 1855.

02-13.title: European Space Agency
02-13.subtitle: The European Space Agency (ESA) conducted the first launch of the European Vega rocket from Europe's spaceport in Kourou, French Guiana.
02-13.text: The European Space Agency (ESA), pronounced 'ee-sah', is a 23-member international organisation devoted to space exploration. It has its headquarters in Paris and a staff of around 2,547 people globally as of 2023. ESA was founded in 1975 in the context of European integration. Its 2026 annual budget was around US$10 billion.
02-13.location: European organisation dedicated to space exploration

02-13.title: Kim Jong-nam
02-13.subtitle: Kim Jong-nam, the half-brother of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, was assassinated using VX nerve agent in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.
02-13.text: Kim Jong-nam, the half-brother of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, was assassinated using VX nerve agent in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

02-14.title: Abbasid Revolution Strikes
02-14.subtitle: A dynasty falls as rebels seize Khorasan
02-14.text: The Hashimi rebels under Abu Muslim Khorasani captured Merv, the capital of the Umayyad province of Khorasan, in a decisive blow of the Abbasid Revolution. The fall of this great Central Asian city marked the beginning of the end for the Umayyad Caliphate. The revolution would reshape the Islamic world, transferring power from Damascus to Baghdad and inaugurating a golden age of Islamic science, culture, and learning.

02-14.title: Battle of Tugela Heights
02-14.subtitle: Britain fights to relieve besieged Ladysmith
02-14.text: The British Army launched the Battle of the Tugela Heights in a determined effort to lift the siege of Ladysmith during the Second Boer War. Across the hilly terrain of Natal, British soldiers advanced against well-entrenched Boer positions in some of the war's fiercest fighting. The battle would rage for days, testing the endurance and courage of both sides in a struggle that defined the brutal character of the conflict.

02-14.title: Discovery of the chemical elements
02-14.subtitle: Discovery of the chemical elements: Element 103, Lawrencium, is first synthesized at the University of California.
02-14.text: Discovery of the chemical elements: Element 103, Lawrencium, is first synthesized at the University of California.

02-14.title: Ruhollah Khomeini
02-14.subtitle: Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini of Iran issued a fatwa calling for the death of Salman Rushdie, the author of The Satanic Ve...
02-14.text: Ruhollah Mostafavi Musavi Khomeini was an Iranian political revolutionary and Shia cleric who served as the first supreme leader of Iran from 1979 until his death in 1989. He was the main leader of the Iranian Revolution, which overthrew Mohammad Reza Pahlavi and transformed Iran into an Islamic republic.
02-14.location: Supreme Leader of Iran from 1979 to 1989

02-14.title: Voyager 1
02-14.subtitle: The Voyager 1 spacecraft takes the photograph of planet Earth that later becomes famous as Pale Blue Dot.
02-14.text: The Voyager 1 spacecraft takes the photograph of planet Earth that later becomes famous as Pale Blue Dot.

02-14.title: Sri Temasek
02-14.subtitle: Sri Temasek (pictured), the official residence of the prime minister of Singapore, was declared a national monument.
02-14.text: Sri Temasek (pictured), the official residence of the prime minister of Singapore, was declared a national monument.

02-14.title: NEAR Orbits an Asteroid
02-14.subtitle: Humanity's first embrace of a space rock
02-14.text: The NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft slipped into orbit around asteroid 433 Eros, becoming the first human-made object to orbit an asteroid. The mission, which had traveled hundreds of millions of miles through space, began a year-long study of the potato-shaped rock, revealing its composition, structure, and history. The achievement opened a new chapter in planetary science and demonstrated that even the smallest worlds in our solar system hold secrets worth discovering.

02-14.title: Iraq disarmament crisis
02-14.subtitle: Iraq disarmament crisis: UNMOVIC Executive Chairman Hans Blix reports to the United Nations Security Council that disarm...
02-14.text: Iraq disarmament crisis: UNMOVIC Executive Chairman Hans Blix reports to the United Nations Security Council that disarmament inspectors have found no weapons of mass destruction in Ba'athist Iraq.

02-14.title: YouTube
02-14.subtitle: YouTube is launched by a group of college students, eventually becoming the largest video sharing website in the world a...
02-14.text: YouTube is launched by a group of college students, eventually becoming the largest video sharing website in the world and a main source for viral videos.

02-14.title: 2008 Northern Illinois University shooting
02-14.subtitle: A gunman opened fire into a crowded lecture hall at Northern Illinois University in DeKalb, killing five people and injuring twenty-one others.
02-14.text: A gunman opened fire into a crowded lecture hall at Northern Illinois University in DeKalb, killing five people and injuring twenty-one others.

02-14.title: Arab Spring
02-14.subtitle: Arab Spring: On the Day of Rage, Bahraini youths began an uprising against the government in 55 marches across 25 locations.
02-14.text: The Arab Spring was a series of pro-democracy anti-government protests, uprisings, and armed rebellions that spread across much of the Arab world in the early 2010s. It began in Tunisia in response to the death of Mohamed Bouazizi by self-immolation. From Tunisia, the protests initially spread to five other countries: Libya, Egypt, Yemen, Syria and Bahrain.
02-14.location: 2010s protests and revolutions in the Arab world

02-15.title: Codex Theodosianus Published
02-15.subtitle: Roman law gets its definitive codification
02-15.text: The Codex Theodosianus was published, compiling the laws of the Roman Empire into a single, authoritative collection. This monumental legal achievement organized centuries of imperial edicts and senatorial decrees, creating a foundation for governance that would influence both Byzantine and Western European law for a millennium. The codex preserved the legal wisdom of Rome and ensured that even as the empire fell, its laws endured.

02-15.title: Siege of Kimberley Ends
02-15.subtitle: British cavalry breaks through Boer lines
02-15.text: After one hundred and twenty-four days of siege, British cavalry under General John French smashed through Boer lines to relieve the diamond-mining city of Kimberley. The besieged inhabitants, who had endured months of bombardment and dwindling supplies, greeted their liberators with joy and relief. The relief of Kimberley was one of the first major British successes of the Second Boer War, boosting morale across the empire.

02-15.title: World War II
02-15.subtitle: World War II: Fall of Singapore.
02-15.text: World War II: Fall of Singapore. Following an assault by Japanese forces, the British General Arthur Percival surrenders. About 80,000 Indian, United Kingdom and Australian soldiers become prisoners of war, the largest surrender of British-led military personnel in history.

02-15.title: Sound recording and reproduction
02-15.subtitle: Sound recordings are granted U.
02-15.text: Sound recordings are granted U.S. federal copyright protection for the first time.

02-15.title: Constitution of Cuba
02-15.subtitle: The first constitution since the Cuban Revolution,  providing for a system of government and law based on those of the S...
02-15.text: The first constitution since the Cuban Revolution,  providing for a system of government and law based on those of the Soviet Union and Eastern Bloc countries, was adopted by a national referendum.

02-15.title: Kevin Mitnick
02-15.subtitle: Kevin Mitnick, the most wanted computer hacker at the time in the U.
02-15.text: Kevin Mitnick, the most wanted computer hacker at the time in the U.S., was arrested and charged with computer fraud and wire fraud.

02-15.title: Xichang Satellite Launch Center
02-15.subtitle: At the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in China, a Long March 3B rocket carrying Intelsat 708 veers off course and crash...
02-15.text: At the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in China, a Long March 3B rocket carrying Intelsat 708 veers off course and crashes into a rural village after liftoff, killing somewhere between six and 100 people.

02-15.title: Abdullah Öcalan
02-15.subtitle: Abdullah Öcalan, one of the founding members of the militant organization the Kurdistan Workers' Party, was arrested by ...
02-15.text: Abdullah Öcalan, one of the founding members of the militant organization the Kurdistan Workers' Party, was arrested by Turkish security forces in Nairobi, Kenya.

02-15.title: Jakub Kiwior Was Born
02-15.subtitle: Poland's defensive wall begins to form
02-15.text: Jakub Kiwior was born in Poland, a nation with a growing presence in European football. The young defender would develop his skills in Poland and Italy before earning a move to Arsenal in the English Premier League. His reading of the game, aerial prowess, and ability to play from the back made him a valuable addition to one of Europe's most ambitious clubs and a key figure in the Polish national team's defense.

02-15.title: Human genome
02-15.subtitle: The first draft of the complete human genome is published in Nature.
02-15.text: The first draft of the complete human genome is published in Nature.

02-15.title: Ariane 4
02-15.subtitle: The last Ariane 4 rocket is launched from the Guiana Space Centre, carrying Intelsat 907.
02-15.text: The last Ariane 4 rocket is launched from the Guiana Space Centre, carrying Intelsat 907.

02-15.title: 2014–2016 Oromo protests
02-15.subtitle: Following nationwide protests, Hailemariam Desalegn announced his resignation as prime minister of Ethiopia, remaining i...
02-15.text: Following nationwide protests, Hailemariam Desalegn announced his resignation as prime minister of Ethiopia, remaining in post as a caretaker until he was succeeded by Abiy Ahmed.

02-16.title: Nichiren Was Born
02-16.subtitle: The founder of a Buddhist revolution
02-16.text: On the coast of medieval Japan, Nichiren was born into a fisherman's family. He would grow to become one of the most influential and controversial figures in Japanese Buddhism, founding a school that emphasized the Lotus Sutra as the ultimate Buddhist teaching. His fiery personality, bold criticisms of other Buddhist schools, and prophetic warnings to the Japanese government made him a revolutionary figure whose followers number in the millions today.

02-16.title: Farthest South Achieved
02-16.subtitle: Explorers push deeper into Antarctica's grip
02-16.text: The Southern Cross Expedition, led by Norwegian explorer Carsten Borchgrevink, achieved a new Farthest South of 78 degrees 50 minutes south latitude, penetrating deeper into Antarctica than any human expedition before. Battling brutal cold, treacherous ice, and the vast loneliness of the polar wilderness, the explorers pushed the boundaries of human endurance and geographical knowledge, bringing the South Pole itself tantalizingly closer to reach.

02-16.title: World War II
02-16.subtitle: World War II: In Athens, the Greek People's Liberation Army is established
02-16.text: World War II: In Athens, the Greek People's Liberation Army is established

02-16.title: Fidel Castro
02-16.subtitle: Fidel Castro was sworn in as Prime Minister of Cuba, beginning his decades-long rule over the country.
02-16.text: Fidel Castro was sworn in as Prime Minister of Cuba, beginning his decades-long rule over the country.

02-16.title: USS Triton
02-16.subtitle: The U.
02-16.text: The U.S. Navy submarine USS Triton begins Operation Sandblast, setting sail from New London, Connecticut, to begin the first submerged circumnavigation of the globe.

02-16.title: Haleyville, Alabama
02-16.subtitle: In Haleyville, Alabama, the first 9-1-1 emergency telephone system goes into service.
02-16.text: In Haleyville, Alabama, the first 9-1-1 emergency telephone system goes into service.

02-16.title: Bulletin board system
02-16.subtitle: The first computer bulletin board system is created (CBBS in Chicago).
02-16.text: The first computer bulletin board system is created (CBBS in Chicago).

02-16.title: Ash Wednesday bushfires
02-16.subtitle: The Ash Wednesday bushfires burned over half a million acres (over 2,000 km2) each in both South Australia and Victoria,...
02-16.text: The Ash Wednesday bushfires burned over half a million acres (over 2,000 km2) each in both South Australia and Victoria, killing 75 people and injuring 2,676 others.

02-16.title: 1996 Maryland train collision
02-16.subtitle: Eleven people died in a train collision in Silver Spring, Maryland, leading to the creation of comprehensive U.
02-16.text: Eleven people died in a train collision in Silver Spring, Maryland, leading to the creation of comprehensive U.S. federal rules for the design of passenger cars.

02-16.title: Taoyuan International Airport
02-16.subtitle: While on approach to Chiang Kai-shek International Airport, China Airlines Flight 676 crashed due to an over-reliance of...
02-16.text: While on approach to Chiang Kai-shek International Airport, China Airlines Flight 676 crashed due to an over-reliance of the autopilot, which resulted in the deaths of 202 people.

02-16.title: Emery Worldwide Crash
02-16.subtitle: A cargo plane falls near Sacramento
02-16.text: Emery Worldwide Airlines Flight 17, a DC-8 cargo aircraft, crashed near Sacramento Mather Airport in Rancho Cordova, California, killing all three crew members on board. The accident, caused by an improperly secured cargo load that shifted during takeoff, highlighted the unique dangers of freight aviation. The investigation led to improved cargo loading procedures and renewed attention to the safety challenges facing the air freight industry.

02-16.title: Kherrata
02-16.subtitle: Five thousand people gathered in the town of Kherrata, Bejaia Province to mark the second anniversary of the Hirak protest movement.
02-16.text: Five thousand people gathered in the town of Kherrata, Bejaia Province to mark the second anniversary of the Hirak protest movement. Demonstrations had been suspended because of the COVID-19 pandemic in Algeria.

02-17.title: Wu Zetian Was Born
02-17.subtitle: China's only female emperor draws first breath
02-17.text: Wu Zetian was born in the Tang dynasty, the woman who would become the only female emperor in Chinese history. Beginning as a concubine, she climbed the treacherous ladder of palace politics to seize supreme power, ruling China for over two decades. Her reign was marked by political cunning, military expansion, and cultural patronage. Wu Zetian shattered every convention of gender and power in one of history's most extraordinary ascents.

02-17.title: Ruth Clifford Was Born
02-17.subtitle: A silent film star with a century of stories
02-17.text: Ruth Clifford was born in Pawtucket, Rhode Island, a future actress whose career would span the entire arc of Hollywood history. Beginning in the silent film era, she transitioned seamlessly to talkies and continued working in film and television for decades. Her remarkable longevity in the entertainment industry, living to ninety-eight, made her one of the last living links to cinema's earliest days.

02-17.title: Turkish Airlines
02-17.subtitle: A Turkish Airlines Vickers Viscount crashes near Gatwick Airport, killing 14; Turkish prime minister Adnan Menderes survives the crash.
02-17.text: A Turkish Airlines Vickers Viscount crashes near Gatwick Airport, killing 14; Turkish prime minister Adnan Menderes survives the crash.

02-17.title: Project Ranger
02-17.subtitle: Project Ranger: The Ranger 8 probe launches on its mission to photograph the Mare Tranquillitatis region of the Moon in ...
02-17.text: Project Ranger: The Ranger 8 probe launches on its mission to photograph the Mare Tranquillitatis region of the Moon in preparation for the crewed Apollo missions. Mare Tranquillitatis or the "Sea of Tranquility" would become the site chosen for the Apollo 11 lunar landing.

02-17.title: Bell UH-1 Iroquois
02-17.subtitle: A U.
02-17.text: A U.S. Army soldier stole a Bell UH-1 helicopter (pictured) and landed it on the South Lawn of the White House in Washington, D.C.

02-17.title: The Troubles
02-17.subtitle: The Troubles: The Provisional Irish Republican Army carried out a bombing of a restaurant near Belfast, Northern Ireland...
02-17.text: The Troubles were an ethno-nationalist conflict in Northern Ireland that lasted for about 30 years from the late 1960s to 1998. Also known internationally as the Northern Ireland conflict, it began in the late 1960s and is usually deemed to have ended with the Good Friday Agreement of 1998. Although the Troubles mostly took place in Northern Ireland, at times violence spilled over into parts of the Republic of Ireland, England, and mainland Europe.

02-17.title: First Nagorno-Karabakh War
02-17.subtitle: First Nagorno-Karabakh War: Armenian troops massacre more than 20 Azerbaijani civilians during the Capture of Garadaghly.
02-17.text: The First Nagorno-Karabakh War was an ethnic and territorial conflict that took place from February 1988 to May 1994 in the enclave of Nagorno-Karabakh in southwestern Azerbaijan between the majority ethnic Armenians of Nagorno-Karabakh backed by Armenia, and the Republic of Azerbaijan with support from Turkey. As the war progressed, Armenia and Azerbaijan, both former Soviet republics, entangled themselves in protracted, undeclared mountain warfare in the mountainous heights of Karabakh as Azerbaijan attempted to curb the Karabakh Movement.

02-17.title: NASA
02-17.subtitle: NASA's Discovery Program begins as the NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft lifts off on the first mission ever to orbit and land on an asteroid, 433 Eros.
02-17.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

02-17.title: Philippines Mudslide Disaster
02-17.subtitle: A mountain buries a village in mud
02-17.text: A massive mudslide engulfed the village of Guinsaugon in Southern Leyte, Philippines, burying an elementary school and hundreds of homes under tons of earth and rock. The official death toll reached 1,126, making it one of the deadliest natural disasters in Philippine history. Days of heavy rain had destabilized the mountainside, and in an instant, an entire community was erased, leaving a scar on the landscape and in the nation's memory.

02-17.title: Arab Spring
02-17.subtitle: Arab Spring: Bahraini security forces killed four protesters in a pre-dawn raid at the Pearl Roundabout in Manama, while...
02-17.text: The Arab Spring was a series of pro-democracy anti-government protests, uprisings, and armed rebellions that spread across much of the Arab world in the early 2010s. It began in Tunisia in response to the death of Mohamed Bouazizi by self-immolation. From Tunisia, the protests initially spread to five other countries: Libya, Egypt, Yemen, Syria and Bahrain.
02-17.location: 2010s protests and revolutions in the Arab world

02-18.title: Kali Yuga Begins
02-18.subtitle: Hindu scriptures mark the age of darkness
02-18.text: According to Hindu scriptures, the Kali Yuga began, the last and darkest of four cosmic ages through which the world cycles. This era is characterized by moral decline, spiritual ignorance, and the gradual dissolution of dharma. Whether understood as literal history or cosmic metaphor, the concept of Kali Yuga has profoundly shaped Hindu philosophy, offering both a warning about human nature and a promise of eventual renewal.

02-18.title: Bloody Sunday at Paardeberg
02-18.subtitle: Britain's worst day in the Boer War
02-18.text: On a day that became known as Bloody Sunday, British imperial forces suffered their worst single-day casualties of the Second Boer War during the first assault of the Battle of Paardeberg. Advancing across open terrain against entrenched Boer positions, the British soldiers were cut down in devastating numbers. The carnage stunned the British public and revealed the terrible cost of outdated tactics against modern defensive firepower.

02-18.title: Church of Scientology
02-18.subtitle: The first Church of Scientology is established in Los Angeles.
02-18.text: The first Church of Scientology is established in Los Angeles.

02-18.title: Space Shuttle Enterprise
02-18.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Enterprise test vehicle is carried on its maiden "flight" on top of a Boeing 747.
02-18.text: The Space Shuttle Enterprise test vehicle is carried on its maiden "flight" on top of a Boeing 747.

02-18.title: Richard Petty
02-18.subtitle: Richard Petty wins a then-record sixth Daytona 500 after leaders Donnie Allison and Cale Yarborough crash on the final l...
02-18.text: Richard Petty wins a then-record sixth Daytona 500 after leaders Donnie Allison and Cale Yarborough crash on the final lap of the first NASCAR race televised live flag-to-flag.

02-18.title: Wah Mee massacre
02-18.subtitle: Thirteen people die and one is seriously injured in the Wah Mee massacre in Seattle.
02-18.text: Thirteen people die and one is seriously injured in the Wah Mee massacre in Seattle. It is said to be the largest robbery-motivated mass-murder in U.S. history.

02-18.title: Zakaria Aboukhlal Was Born
02-18.subtitle: A Moroccan football talent takes shape
02-18.text: Zakaria Aboukhlal was born at the dawn of the new millennium, a future Moroccan international footballer whose journey would take him from youth academies to professional leagues across Europe. His skill, pace, and versatility on the pitch reflect the growing strength of Moroccan football, which has increasingly produced players capable of competing at the highest levels of the European game.

02-18.title: Federal Bureau of Investigation
02-18.subtitle: American FBI agent Robert Hanssen was arrested for having spied for the KGB and GRU over a 22-year period.
02-18.text: American FBI agent Robert Hanssen was arrested for having spied for the KGB and GRU over a 22-year period.

02-18.title: WikiLeaks
02-18.subtitle: WikiLeaks published the first of hundreds of thousands of classified documents disclosed by Chelsea Manning.
02-18.text: WikiLeaks published the first of hundreds of thousands of classified documents disclosed by Chelsea Manning.

02-18.title: Revolution of Dignity
02-18.subtitle: A series of violent events (pictured) involving protesters, riot police, and unknown shooters began in Kyiv that culmina...
02-18.text: A series of violent events (pictured) involving protesters, riot police, and unknown shooters began in Kyiv that culminated in the ousting of Ukrainian president Viktor Yanukovych five days later.

02-18.title: Perseverance
02-18.subtitle: Perseverance, a Mars rover designed to explore Jezero crater on Mars, as part of NASA's Mars 2020 mission, lands successfully.
02-18.text: Perseverance, a Mars rover designed to explore Jezero crater on Mars, as part of NASA's Mars 2020 mission, lands successfully.

02-19.title: Battle of Lugdunum
02-19.subtitle: Rome's bloodiest battle between its own armies
02-19.text: Near the city of Lugdunum, modern-day Lyon, Emperor Septimius Severus clashed with the usurper Clodius Albinus in the bloodiest battle ever fought between Roman armies. Tens of thousands of soldiers fell on both sides before Severus emerged victorious. The defeat of Albinus secured Severus's grip on the empire but left a trail of devastation across Gaul, demonstrating the terrifying cost of Rome's internal power struggles.

02-19.title: Kay Boyle Was Born
02-19.subtitle: An expatriate voice of literary Paris
02-19.text: Kay Boyle was born in Saint Paul, Minnesota, a future novelist, short story writer, and educator who would become a leading figure in the American expatriate literary scene in Paris. Her fiction, noted for its lyrical prose and social consciousness, explored themes of love, politics, and displacement. Boyle's life was as adventurous as her writing, spanning decades of literary and political engagement on both sides of the Atlantic.

02-19.title: T-7
02-19.subtitle: China successfully launches the T-7, its first sounding rocket.
02-19.text: China successfully launches the T-7, its first sounding rocket.

02-19.title: Betty Friedan
02-19.subtitle: Betty Friedan's The Feminine Mystique, a non-fiction book credited with sparking the beginning of second-wave feminism i...
02-19.text: Betty Friedan's The Feminine Mystique, a non-fiction book credited with sparking the beginning of second-wave feminism in the United States, was first published.

02-19.title: Phạm Ngọc Thảo
02-19.subtitle: Colonel Phạm Ngọc Thảo of the Army of the Republic of Vietnam, and a communist spy of the North Vietnamese Viet Minh, al...
02-19.text: Phạm Ngọc Thảo, also known as Albert Thảo, was a Vietnamese military officer and spy. He was a sleeper agent of the Việt Minh who infiltrated the Army of the Republic of Vietnam (ARVN) and also became a major provincial leader in South Vietnam.

02-19.title: Lee Kang-in Was Born
02-19.subtitle: South Korea's prodigious midfield talent arrives
02-19.text: Lee Kang-in was born in South Korea, a footballing prodigy who would announce himself on the world stage at a remarkably young age. Joining Valencia's academy as a teenager, he became the youngest player to win the Golden Ball at a FIFA U-17 World Cup. His technical brilliance and creative vision made him one of the most sought-after young players in world football, carrying South Korean hopes into European top-flight competition.

02-19.title: NASA
02-19.subtitle: NASA's Mars Odyssey space probe begins to map the surface of Mars using its thermal emission imaging system.
02-19.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

02-19.title: Methane
02-19.subtitle: A methane explosion in a coal mine in Nueva Rosita, Mexico, trapped and killed 65 miners.
02-19.text: A methane explosion in a coal mine in Nueva Rosita, Mexico, trapped and killed 65 miners.

02-19.title: Belitung shipwreck
02-19.subtitle: The debut exhibition of the Belitung shipwreck, containing the largest collection of Tang dynasty artifacts found in one...
02-19.text: The debut exhibition of the Belitung shipwreck, containing the largest collection of Tang dynasty artifacts found in one location, begins in Singapore.

02-19.title: Mexican drug war
02-19.subtitle: Mexican drug war: Forty-four inmates died in a prison riot in Apodaca, Mexico, between members of Los Zetas and the Gulf Cartel.
02-19.text: Mexican drug war: Forty-four inmates died in a prison riot in Apodaca, Mexico, between members of Los Zetas and the Gulf Cartel.

02-19.title: Killing of Mya Thwe Thwe Khine
02-19.subtitle: Mya Thwe Thwe Khine, a 19-year-old protester, becomes the first known casualty of anti-coup protests that formed in resp...
02-19.text: Mya Thwe Thwe Khine, a 19-year-old protester, becomes the first known casualty of anti-coup protests that formed in response to the 2021 Myanmar coup d'état.

02-20.title: Battle of Parabiago
02-20.subtitle: Mercenaries clash in the fields of Lombardy
02-20.text: On the frost-hardened plains outside Milan, the Milanese army clashed with the San Giorgio mercenaries of Lodrisio Visconti in the Battle of Parabiago. The fierce medieval engagement pitted rival factions of the powerful Visconti family against each other in a struggle for control of one of Italy's wealthiest cities. The battle's outcome shaped the political landscape of northern Italy for generations to come.

02-20.title: Hawaii Legislature Convenes
02-20.subtitle: A new territory finds its democratic voice
02-20.text: The legislature of Hawaii Territory convened for the first time, marking a new chapter in the islands' political history. Just two years after the United States had annexed the Hawaiian Kingdom, the elected representatives gathered to begin the work of self-governance under American rule. This moment launched Hawaii on the path that would eventually lead to statehood in 1959, transforming a Pacific island chain into America's fiftieth state.

02-20.title: John Diefenbaker
02-20.subtitle: Canadian prime minister John Diefenbaker cancelled the Avro CF-105 Arrow (pictured) interceptor-aircraft program amid much political debate.
02-20.text: John George Diefenbaker was the 13th prime minister of Canada, serving from 1957 to 1963. He was the only Progressive Conservative party leader between 1935 and 1979 to lead the party to an election victory, doing so three times, although only once with a majority of the seats in the House of Commons.
02-20.location: Prime Minister of Canada from 1957 to 1963

02-20.title: Mercury program
02-20.subtitle: Mercury program: While aboard Friendship 7, John Glenn becomes the first American to orbit the Earth, making three orbits in four hours, 55 minutes.
02-20.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

02-20.title: Ranger 8
02-20.subtitle: Ranger 8 crashes into the Moon after a successful mission of photographing possible landing sites for  the Apollo program astronauts.
02-20.text: Ranger 8 crashes into the Moon after a successful mission of photographing possible landing sites for  the Apollo program astronauts.

02-20.title: Soviet Union
02-20.subtitle: The Soviet Union launches its Mir spacecraft.
02-20.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

02-20.title: Larry King Live
02-20.subtitle: Appearing on the talk show Larry King Live, U.
02-20.text: Appearing on the talk show Larry King Live, U.S. industrialist Ross Perot announced that he would begin a presidential campaign if "ordinary people" wanted him to run for office.

02-20.title: Figure skating
02-20.subtitle: At the age of 15, American figure skater Tara Lipinski became the then-youngest winner of an Olympic gold medal in the h...
02-20.text: Figure skating is a sport in which individuals, pairs, or groups perform on figure skates on ice. It was the first winter sport to be included in the Olympic Games, with its introduction occurring at the 1908 Olympics in London. The Olympic disciplines are men's singles, women's singles, pair skating, and ice dance; the four individual disciplines are also combined into a team event, which was first included in the Winter Olympics in 2014.
02-20.location: Ice sport performed on figure skates

02-20.title: Josh Sargent Was Born
02-20.subtitle: America's soccer future heads to England
02-20.text: Josh Sargent was born in the United States, a future striker who would represent the growing ambitions of American soccer on the world stage. After developing in the U.S. youth system, Sargent moved to Werder Bremen in Germany before joining Norwich City in the English Championship. His willingness to compete in demanding European leagues embodies the increasingly global outlook of American soccer talent.

02-20.title: 2005 Spanish European Constitution referendum
02-20.subtitle: Spain becomes the first country to vote in a referendum on ratification of the proposed Constitution of the European Uni...
02-20.text: Spain becomes the first country to vote in a referendum on ratification of the proposed Constitution of the European Union, passing it by a substantial margin, but on a low turnout.

02-20.title: Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam
02-20.subtitle: The Tamil Tigers attempted to crash two aircraft laden with C-4 in suicide attacks on Colombo, Sri Lanka, but the planes...
02-20.text: The Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam was a Tamil militant organization, that was based in northern and eastern Sri Lanka. The LTTE fought to create an independent Tamil state called Tamil Eelam in the northeast of the island in response to violent persecution and discriminatory policies against Sri Lankan Tamils by the Sinhalese-dominated Sri Lankan government.
02-20.location: Tamil militant organisation in Sri Lanka (1976–2009)

02-21.title: Abe no Seimei Was Born
02-21.subtitle: Japan's legendary astrologer-sorcerer arrives
02-21.text: Abe no Seimei was born in Heian-era Japan, destined to become the most famous onmyoji, or yin-yang master, in Japanese history. Part astrologer, part court advisor, and entirely legendary, Seimei served six emperors and became the subject of countless folktales depicting his supernatural powers. His legacy endures in shrines, manga, and films, making him one of Japanese culture's most enduring and beloved mystical figures.

02-21.title: Jeanne Aubert Was Born
02-21.subtitle: A voice that enchanted Parisian audiences
02-21.text: Jeanne Aubert was born in France, a future singer and actress who would grace the stages and cabarets of Paris. Her vocal performances captivated audiences during the interwar years, a golden age of French entertainment. Aubert's career spanned decades, from the roaring twenties through the postwar era, making her a fixture of the Parisian cultural scene that drew artists and audiences from around the world.

02-21.title: Bavaria
02-21.subtitle: Bavarian socialist Kurt Eisner, who had organized the German Revolution that overthrew the Wittelsbach monarchy and esta...
02-21.text: Bavarian socialist Kurt Eisner, who had organized the German Revolution that overthrew the Wittelsbach monarchy and established Bavaria as a republic, was assassinated.

02-21.title: Constituent Assembly of Georgia
02-21.subtitle: Constituent Assembly of the Democratic Republic of Georgia adopts the country's first constitution.
02-21.text: Constituent Assembly of the Democratic Republic of Georgia adopts the country's first constitution.

02-21.title: Nanjing decade
02-21.subtitle: Nanjing decade: In the first battle of the Warlord Rebellion in northeastern Shandong against the Nationalist government...
02-21.text: Nanjing decade: In the first battle of the Warlord Rebellion in northeastern Shandong against the Nationalist government of China, a 24,000-strong rebel force led by Zhang Zongchang was defeated at Zhifu by 7,000 NRA troops.

02-21.title: Bengali language movement
02-21.subtitle: A number of student protesters demanding the establishment of Bengali as an official language were killed by police in Dhaka, East Pakistan.
02-21.text: A number of student protesters demanding the establishment of Bengali as an official language were killed by police in Dhaka, East Pakistan.

02-21.title: Peace symbols
02-21.subtitle: The CND symbol, aka peace symbol, commissioned by the Direct Action Committee in protest against the Atomic Weapons Rese...
02-21.text: The CND symbol, aka peace symbol, commissioned by the Direct Action Committee in protest against the Atomic Weapons Research Establishment, is designed and completed by Gerald Holtom.

02-21.title: Soviet Union
02-21.subtitle: The Soviet uncrewed spaceship Luna 20 lands on the Moon.
02-21.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

02-21.title: Israeli occupation of the Sinai Peninsula
02-21.subtitle: After accidentally straying into the airspace of the Israeli-occupied Sinai Peninsula, Libyan Arab Airlines Flight 114 w...
02-21.text: After accidentally straying into the airspace of the Israeli-occupied Sinai Peninsula, Libyan Arab Airlines Flight 114 was shot down by two Israeli fighter aircraft, killing 108 of the 113 people on board.

02-21.title: Steve Fossett
02-21.subtitle: Steve Fossett lands in Leader, Saskatchewan, Canada becoming the first person to make a solo flight across the Pacific Ocean in a balloon.
02-21.text: Steve Fossett lands in Leader, Saskatchewan, Canada becoming the first person to make a solo flight across the Pacific Ocean in a balloon.

02-21.title: Leeseo Was Born
02-21.subtitle: The youngest star of IVE enters the world
02-21.text: Leeseo was born in South Korea, the youngest member of the K-pop girl group IVE. Debuting as a teenager, she brought youthful charm and impressive stage presence to one of K-pop's most successful fourth-generation groups. Her rapid rise to prominence reflects the extraordinary training systems and global ambitions of the Korean entertainment industry, which continues to produce stars who captivate audiences worldwide.

02-21.title: Prelude to the 2022 Russian invasion
02-21.subtitle: In the prelude to the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine Russian President Vladimir Putin declares the Luhansk People's Re...
02-21.text: In the prelude to the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine Russian President Vladimir Putin declares the Luhansk People's Republic and Donetsk People's Republic as independent from Ukraine, and moves troops into the region. The action is condemned by the United Nations.

02-22.title: Arnulf Crowned Emperor
02-22.subtitle: A stroke fells a newly crowned ruler
02-22.text: Pope Formosus placed the imperial crown upon the head of Arnulf of Carinthia in Rome, making him Emperor of the Holy Roman Empire. But fortune is fickle: soon after his coronation, Arnulf suffered a devastating stroke that forced his retreat from Italy. The ceremony that should have cemented his power instead marked the beginning of his decline, leaving the empire without strong leadership at a critical moment in European history.

02-22.title: Luis Bunuel Was Born
02-22.subtitle: Cinema's great surrealist provocateur arrives
02-22.text: Luis Bunuel was born in Calanda, Spain, destined to become one of cinema's most daring and influential directors. From the razor-sliced eyeball of Un Chien Andalou to the bourgeois nightmares of The Discreet Charm of the Bourgeoisie, Bunuel spent six decades subverting conventions and scandalizing audiences. His collaborations with Salvador Dali and his unflinching examination of religion, desire, and social hypocrisy made him a titan of world cinema.

02-22.title: Ngo Dinh Diem
02-22.subtitle: Ngô Đình Diệm of South Vietnam survives a communist shooting assassination attempt in Buôn Ma Thuột.
02-22.text: Ngô Đình Diệm of South Vietnam survives a communist shooting assassination attempt in Buôn Ma Thuột.

02-22.title: Lee Petty
02-22.subtitle: Lee Petty won the first edition of the Daytona 500, a NASCAR auto race at the Daytona International Speedway (pictured) in Florida.
02-22.text: Lee Petty won the first edition of the Daytona 500, a NASCAR auto race at the Daytona International Speedway (pictured) in Florida.

02-22.title: Official Irish Republican Army
02-22.subtitle: The Official Irish Republican Army detonates a car bomb at Aldershot barracks, killing seven and injuring nineteen others.
02-22.text: The Official Irish Republican Army detonates a car bomb at Aldershot barracks, killing seven and injuring nineteen others.

02-22.title: Cold War
02-22.subtitle: Cold War: Following President Richard Nixon's visit to the People's Republic of China, the two countries agree to establish liaison offices.
02-22.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
02-22.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

02-22.title: Samuel Byck
02-22.subtitle: Samuel Byck attempted to hijack an aircraft at Baltimore/Washington International Airport with the intention of crashing...
02-22.text: Samuel Byck attempted to hijack an aircraft at Baltimore/Washington International Airport with the intention of crashing it into the White House to assassinate U.S. president Richard Nixon, but he was stopped by police.

02-22.title: 1980 Winter Olympics
02-22.subtitle: At the Winter Olympics in Lake Placid, New York, the United States ice hockey team defeated the Soviet team in an unlike...
02-22.text: At the Winter Olympics in Lake Placid, New York, the United States ice hockey team defeated the Soviet team in an unlikely victory that became known as the Miracle on Ice.

02-22.title: Jonas Savimbi Killed
02-22.subtitle: Angola's rebel leader falls in an ambush
02-22.text: Jonas Savimbi, the charismatic and controversial leader of UNITA, was killed in a military ambush in eastern Angola. For nearly three decades, Savimbi had led an armed rebellion against the Angolan government, fueled by Cold War rivalries and diamond wealth. His death effectively ended one of Africa's longest and most destructive civil wars, which had claimed over half a million lives and devastated a nation rich in oil and diamonds.

02-22.title: Securitas depot robbery
02-22.subtitle: The Securitas depot robbery was the UK's largest heist.
02-22.text: The Securitas depot robbery was the UK's largest heist. Almost £53m (about $92.5 million or €78 million) was stolen from a Securitas depot in Tonbridge, Kent.

02-22.title: 2011 Christchurch earthquake
02-22.subtitle: An earthquake registering 6.
02-22.text: An earthquake registering 6.3 ML struck Christchurch, New Zealand, killing 185 people and causing around NZ$40 billion in damage.

02-23.title: Diocletian Destroys Church
02-23.subtitle: Rome's last great persecution of Christians begins
02-23.text: Emperor Diocletian ordered the destruction of the Christian church in Nicomedia, igniting eight years of the most severe persecution Christians had ever endured under Roman rule. Churches were razed, scriptures burned, and clergy imprisoned across the empire. The Diocletianic Persecution was Rome's final, desperate attempt to crush a faith that had grown too large to contain. Within two decades, Christianity would become the empire's official religion.

02-23.title: Battle at Hart's Hill
02-23.subtitle: Britain fails to take a crucial ridge
02-23.text: During the Battle of the Tugela Heights, British forces made their first attempt to capture Hart's Hill, a strategic position held by Boer defenders. The assault failed, repulsed by withering rifle fire from entrenched positions. The setback at Hart's Hill underscored the difficulty of dislodging Boer forces from their defensive positions and the high cost in blood that each yard of South African soil demanded from the British Empire.

02-23.title: Joe Rosenthal
02-23.subtitle: American photographer Joe Rosenthal took the Pulitzer Prize–winning photograph Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima during the B...
02-23.text: American photographer Joe Rosenthal took the Pulitzer Prize–winning photograph Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima during the Battle of Iwo Jima, an image that was later reproduced on the Marine Corps War Memorial.

02-23.title: Polio
02-23.subtitle: The first mass inoculation of children against polio with the Salk vaccine begins in Pittsburgh.
02-23.text: The first mass inoculation of children against polio with the Salk vaccine begins in Pittsburgh.

02-23.title: Syria
02-23.subtitle: In Syria, Ba'ath Party member Salah Jadid leads an intra-party military coup that replaces the previous government of Ge...
02-23.text: In Syria, Ba'ath Party member Salah Jadid leads an intra-party military coup that replaces the previous government of General Amin al-Hafiz, also a Baathist.

02-23.title: Vietnam War
02-23.subtitle: Vietnam War: South Vietnamese General Đỗ Cao Trí was killed in a helicopter crash en route to taking control of the faltering Operation Lam Son 719.
02-23.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
02-23.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

02-23.title: Symbionese Liberation Army
02-23.subtitle: The Symbionese Liberation Army demands $4 million more to release kidnap victim Patty Hearst.
02-23.text: The Symbionese Liberation Army demands $4 million more to release kidnap victim Patty Hearst.

02-23.title: SN 1987A
02-23.subtitle: SN 1987A, the first supernova that modern astronomers were able to study in great detail, was observed from Earth occurr...
02-23.text: SN 1987A, the first supernova that modern astronomers were able to study in great detail, was observed from Earth occurring in the Large Magellanic Cloud.

02-23.title: Femke Bol Was Born
02-23.subtitle: The Netherlands' fastest woman takes first steps
02-23.text: Femke Bol was born in the Netherlands, a future track and field sensation who would dominate the 400-meter hurdles and sprint events. Her explosive speed and elegant hurdling technique made her one of the most exciting athletes in world athletics, breaking records and collecting medals at major championships. Bol's rise represents a new golden age for Dutch women's athletics on the global stage.

02-23.title: Ariane 4
02-23.subtitle: An Ariane 4 rocket is launched from the Guiana Space Centre carrying Intelsat 904.
02-23.text: An Ariane 4 rocket is launched from the Guiana Space Centre carrying Intelsat 904.

02-23.title: Northrop B-2 Spirit
02-23.subtitle: A B-2 Spirit stealth bomber crashed on the runway shortly after takeoff from Andersen Air Force Base in Guam in the most expensive crash in U.
02-23.text: A B-2 Spirit stealth bomber crashed on the runway shortly after takeoff from Andersen Air Force Base in Guam in the most expensive crash in U.S. Air Force history.

02-23.title: Syrian civil war
02-23.subtitle: Syrian civil war: Allied troops led by the Turkish Armed Forces captured the city of al-Bab from the Islamic State.
02-23.text: The Syrian civil war was an armed conflict that began with the Syrian revolution in March 2011, when popular discontent with the Ba'athist regime ruled by Bashar al-Assad triggered large-scale protests and pro-democracy rallies across Syria, as part of the wider Arab Spring. The Assad regime responded to the protests with lethal force, which led to a series of defections, the emergence of armed opposition groups, and the civilian uprising descending into a civil war.

02-24.title: Diocletianic Persecution Begins
02-24.subtitle: The Roman Empire's war on Christianity erupts
02-24.text: The Diocletianic Persecution officially began, marking the last and most severe episode of the persecution of Christians in the Roman Empire. Emperor Diocletian's edicts stripped Christians of their legal rights, demanded the destruction of churches and sacred texts, and subjected believers to imprisonment, torture, and death. The persecution's ultimate failure demonstrated that Christianity had grown beyond the empire's ability to suppress it, paving the way for Constantine's embrace of the faith.

02-24.title: Irmgard Bartenieff Was Born
02-24.subtitle: A pioneer who healed through movement
02-24.text: Irmgard Bartenieff was born in Berlin, Germany, a future dancer and physical therapist who would become a leading pioneer of dance therapy. Emigrating to America, she developed methods that used movement as a healing tool, transforming the understanding of how the body and mind connect. Her work laid the foundations for a therapeutic discipline that continues to help people around the world recover from physical and emotional trauma.

02-24.title: 1966 Ghanaian coup d'état
02-24.subtitle: Ghanaian coup d'état by National Liberation Council overthrows Kwame Nkrumah's Government
02-24.text: Ghanaian coup d'état by National Liberation Council overthrows Kwame Nkrumah's Government

02-24.title: Cultural Revolution
02-24.subtitle: Cultural Revolution: Zhang Chunqiao announces the dissolution of the Shanghai People's Commune, replacing its local gove...
02-24.text: The Cultural Revolution, formally known as the Great Proletarian Cultural Revolution, was a sociopolitical movement in the People's Republic of China (PRC). It was launched by CCP chairman Mao Zedong in 1966 and lasted until his death in 1976. Its stated goal was to preserve Chinese communism by purging remnants of capitalist and traditional elements from Chinese society.
02-24.location: Period of sociopolitical turmoil in China (1966–1976)

02-24.title: Vietnam War
02-24.subtitle: Vietnam War: Led by Ngô Quang Trưởng, South Vietnamese forces recaptured the citadel of Huế.
02-24.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
02-24.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

02-24.title: Yuba County Five
02-24.subtitle: Five men disappeared after attending a college basketball game in Chico, California; the bodies of four of them were discovered months later.
02-24.text: Five men disappeared after attending a college basketball game in Chico, California; the bodies of four of them were discovered months later.

02-24.title: Uganda–Tanzania War
02-24.subtitle: Uganda–Tanzania War: Ugandan government forces fled Masaka as the Tanzania People's Defence Force bombarded and captured the town.
02-24.text: Uganda–Tanzania War: Ugandan government forces fled Masaka as the Tanzania People's Defence Force bombarded and captured the town.

02-24.title: United Airlines Flight 811
02-24.subtitle: United Airlines Flight 811 experienced uncontrolled decompression after leaving Honolulu International Airport, Hawaii, ...
02-24.text: United Airlines Flight 811 experienced uncontrolled decompression after leaving Honolulu International Airport, Hawaii, blowing seats out of the aircraft and killing nine passengers.

02-24.title: Antony Matheus dos Santos Born
02-24.subtitle: Brazil's football pipeline continues to flow
02-24.text: Antony Matheus dos Santos was born in Brazil, a nation that produces more professional footballers than any other country on earth. The young talent would develop on Brazil's sun-drenched pitches, joining the endless conveyor belt of skilled players who leave South America to compete in Europe's top leagues. His story is one of millions of Brazilian boys who dream of football glory and a precious few who achieve it.

02-24.title: President of the Philippines
02-24.subtitle: Philippine President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo declares Proclamation 1017 placing the country in a state of emergency in a...
02-24.text: President of the Philippines is the title of the head of state, head of government and chief executive of the Philippines. The president leads the executive branch of the Philippine government and is the commander-in-chief of the Armed Forces of the Philippines.
02-24.location: Head of state and government of the Philippines

02-24.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
02-24.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on its final mission, STS-133.
02-24.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on its final mission, STS-133.

02-24.title: Uherský Brod
02-24.subtitle: At a restaurant in Uherský Brod, Czech Republic, a gunman opened fire in a restaurant, killing eight people before committing suicide.
02-24.text: At a restaurant in Uherský Brod, Czech Republic, a gunman opened fire in a restaurant, killing eight people before committing suicide.

02-24.title: Russo-Ukrainian War
02-24.subtitle: Russo-Ukrainian War: Days after recognising Donetsk and Luhansk as independent states, Russian president Vladimir Putin ...
02-24.text: Russo-Ukrainian War: Days after recognising Donetsk and Luhansk as independent states, Russian president Vladimir Putin orders the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine.

02-25.title: Hadrian Adopts Antoninus
02-25.subtitle: An emperor secures Rome's golden succession
02-25.text: Ailing Emperor Hadrian adopted Antoninus Pius as his son and successor, following the untimely death of his first chosen heir. This act of adoption would prove one of the most consequential decisions in Roman history, inaugurating a line of philosopher-emperors known as the Antonine dynasty. Under Antoninus and his successors, the Roman Empire would experience its longest period of stability and prosperity, the age the historian Edward Gibbon called humanity's happiest.

02-25.title: Vince Gair Was Born
02-25.subtitle: A future premier of Queensland arrives
02-25.text: Vince Gair was born in Australia, a future politician who would serve as the twenty-seventh Premier of Queensland. His political career spanned decades of Australian public life, navigating the complex currents of Labor and Democratic Labor politics. Gair's tenure as premier was marked by both progressive reforms and the fractious splits that characterized Australian politics in the mid-twentieth century.

02-25.title: Pan American Games
02-25.subtitle: The first Pan American Games are officially opened in Buenos Aires by Argentine President Juan Perón.
02-25.text: The first Pan American Games are officially opened in Buenos Aires by Argentine President Juan Perón.

02-25.title: Suriname
02-25.subtitle: The government of Suriname is overthrown by a military coup led by Dési Bouterse.
02-25.text: The government of Suriname is overthrown by a military coup led by Dési Bouterse.

02-25.title: People Power Revolution
02-25.subtitle: People Power Revolution: President of the Philippines Ferdinand Marcos flees the nation after 20 years of rule; Corazon ...
02-25.text: People Power Revolution: President of the Philippines Ferdinand Marcos flees the nation after 20 years of rule; Corazon Aquino becomes the Philippines' first female president.

02-25.title: Baruch Goldstein
02-25.subtitle: Israeli physician Baruch Goldstein opened fire on Palestinian Muslims praying at the mosque in Hebron's Cave of the Patr...
02-25.text: Baruch Kopel Goldstein was an American and Israeli physician and religious extremist who, in 1994, murdered 29 Palestinian people in Hebron in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, an incident of Jewish terrorism. Goldstein was a supporter of Kach, a religious Zionist party that the United States, the European Union and other countries designate as a terrorist organization. Kach was banned less than a month after Goldstein's attacks on account of statements made in support of his actions.

02-25.title: Bo Nix Was Born
02-25.subtitle: A quarterback born to play the game
02-25.text: Bo Nix was born into a football family in the United States, the son of a former college quarterback. He would follow in his father's footsteps and then surpass them, becoming one of the most prolific passers in college football history. His journey through Auburn and Oregon showcased both resilience and growth, leading to an NFL career that fulfilled the promise shown on countless autumn Saturdays.

02-25.title: Turkish Airlines Flight 1951
02-25.subtitle: Turkish Airlines Flight 1951 crashes during landing at the Amsterdam Schiphol Airport, Netherlands, primarily due to a f...
02-25.text: Turkish Airlines Flight 1951 crashes during landing at the Amsterdam Schiphol Airport, Netherlands, primarily due to a faulty radio altimeter, resulting in the death of nine passengers and crew including all three pilots.

02-26.title: Nabonassar Era Begins
02-26.subtitle: Babylon marks a new chapter in timekeeping
02-26.text: According to the astronomer Ptolemy, the reign of Babylonian king Nabonassar began on this day, marking the start of a new era in chronology. Nabonassar's reign became a reference point for ancient astronomers and historians, anchoring the vast sweep of Mesopotamian history to a fixed date. The era of Nabonassar connected Babylonian, Greek, and later Islamic astronomical traditions in a chain of knowledge that spanned millennia.

02-26.title: Halina Konopacka Was Born
02-26.subtitle: An Olympic champion with a poet's soul
02-26.text: Halina Konopacka was born in Poland, a future Olympic champion and accomplished poet. She won the gold medal in discus throw at the 1928 Amsterdam Olympics, becoming the first Polish woman to win an Olympic gold. But Konopacka was more than an athlete; she was also a published poet whose verses revealed an artistic sensibility that complemented her physical prowess, making her a Renaissance figure in Polish culture.

02-26.title: HMHS Britannic
02-26.subtitle: RMS Britannic, the third and largest Olympic-class ocean liner of the White Star Line after RMS Olympic and RMS Titanic,...
02-26.text: RMS Britannic, the third and largest Olympic-class ocean liner of the White Star Line after RMS Olympic and RMS Titanic, was launched at the Harland & Wolff shipyard in Belfast.

02-26.title: Apollo program
02-26.subtitle: Apollo program: Launch of AS-201, the first flight of the Saturn IB rocket.
02-26.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

02-26.title: Secretary-General of the United Nations
02-26.subtitle: U.
02-26.text: U.N. Secretary-General U Thant signs United Nations proclamation of the vernal equinox as Earth Day.

02-26.title: First Nagorno-Karabakh War
02-26.subtitle: First Nagorno-Karabakh War: Khojaly Massacre: Armenian armed forces open fire on Azeri civilians at a military post outs...
02-26.text: The First Nagorno-Karabakh War was an ethnic and territorial conflict that took place from February 1988 to May 1994 in the enclave of Nagorno-Karabakh in southwestern Azerbaijan between the majority ethnic Armenians of Nagorno-Karabakh backed by Armenia, and the Republic of Azerbaijan with support from Turkey. As the war progressed, Armenia and Azerbaijan, both former Soviet republics, entangled themselves in protracted, undeclared mountain warfare in the mountainous heights of Karabakh as Azerbaijan attempted to curb the Karabakh Movement.

02-26.title: Barings Bank
02-26.subtitle: The UK's oldest investment banking institute, Barings Bank, collapses after a rogue securities broker Nick Leeson loses $1.
02-26.text: The UK's oldest investment banking institute, Barings Bank, collapses after a rogue securities broker Nick Leeson loses $1.4 billion by speculating on the Singapore International Monetary Exchange using futures contracts.

02-26.title: Yeat Was Born
02-26.subtitle: A new voice in American hip-hop emerges
02-26.text: The rapper known as Yeat was born in the United States, part of a generation of hip-hop artists who would reshape the genre through streaming platforms and social media. His distinctive vocal style, hypnotic production choices, and internet-savvy approach to music distribution made him one of the most buzzed-about new rappers of the early 2020s, embodying the DIY spirit of the SoundCloud and TikTok era.

02-26.title: Korean War
02-26.subtitle: In the first significant cultural visit from the United States to North Korea since the Korean War, the New York Philhar...
02-26.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

02-26.title: Trayvon Martin
02-26.subtitle: Seventeen-year-old African-American student Trayvon Martin is shot to death by neighborhood watch coordinator George Zim...
02-26.text: Seventeen-year-old African-American student Trayvon Martin is shot to death by neighborhood watch coordinator George Zimmerman in an altercation in Sanford, Florida.

02-26.title: Ming Pao
02-26.subtitle: Former editor-in-chief of Hong Kong newspaper Ming Pao Kevin Lau was stabbed, prompting concerns and protests about media freedom.
02-26.text: Former editor-in-chief of Hong Kong newspaper Ming Pao Kevin Lau was stabbed, prompting concerns and protests about media freedom.

02-27.title: Constantine the Great Born
02-27.subtitle: The emperor who made Christianity Rome's faith
02-27.text: Constantine was born in Naissus, in the province of Moesia, the son of a Roman officer who would one day become emperor himself. Constantine's own rise to power was forged in civil war and culminated in his embrace of Christianity, a decision that transformed the Roman Empire and the entire Western world. From the Edict of Milan to the founding of Constantinople, his reign reshaped civilization in ways that echo to this day.

02-27.title: FC Bayern Munich Founded
02-27.subtitle: Germany's greatest football dynasty is born
02-27.text: In a Munich gymnasium, a group of eleven football enthusiasts founded FC Bayern Munich, a club that would become the most successful in German football history. From these humble beginnings, Bayern grew into a European powerhouse, winning dozens of league titles and multiple Champions League trophies. The club's founding moment, witnessed by a handful of idealistic sportsmen, planted the seed of a dynasty that would dominate German football for over a century.

02-27.title: Twenty-second Amendment to the United States
02-27.subtitle: The Twenty-second Amendment to the United States Constitution, limiting Presidents to two terms, is ratified.
02-27.text: The Twenty-second Amendment to the United States Constitution limits the number of times a person can be elected to the office of President of the United States to two, and sets additional eligibility conditions for presidents who succeed to the unexpired terms of their predecessors. Congress approved the Twenty-second Amendment on March 21, 1947, and submitted it to the state legislatures for ratification.
02-27.location: 1951 amendment limiting presidents to two terms

02-27.title: Vietnam War
02-27.subtitle: Vietnam War: Two dissident Republic of Vietnam Air Force pilots bomb the Independence Palace in Saigon in a failed attem...
02-27.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
02-27.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

02-27.title: Dominican Republic
02-27.subtitle: The Dominican Republic receives its first democratically elected president, Juan Bosch, since the end of the dictatorship led by Rafael Trujillo.
02-27.text: The Dominican Republic receives its first democratically elected president, Juan Bosch, since the end of the dictatorship led by Rafael Trujillo.

02-27.title: Western Sahara
02-27.subtitle: The former Spanish territory of Western Sahara, under the auspices of the Polisario Front declares independence as the S...
02-27.text: The former Spanish territory of Western Sahara, under the auspices of the Polisario Front declares independence as the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic.

02-27.title: D'Oyly Carte Opera Company
02-27.subtitle: The D'Oyly Carte Opera Company, known for its performances of Gilbert and Sullivan's Savoy operas, gave its final performance.
02-27.text: The D'Oyly Carte Opera Company, known for its performances of Gilbert and Sullivan's Savoy operas, gave its final performance.

02-27.title: Pokémon
02-27.subtitle: The multimedia franchise Pokémon was launched with the release of the video games Pocket Monsters Red and Green.
02-27.text: The multimedia franchise Pokémon was launched with the release of the video games Pocket Monsters Red and Green.

02-27.title: Loganair Flight 670A Crash
02-27.subtitle: A plane goes down in Scottish waters
02-27.text: Loganair Flight 670A crashed while attempting to make a water landing in the Firth of Forth in Scotland. The aircraft, a Shorts 360 turboprop, went down in the frigid waters of the estuary in an incident that shocked the Scottish aviation community. The crash prompted investigations into the safety procedures of regional airlines and the challenges of flying in Scotland's demanding weather conditions.

02-27.title: 2002 Gujarat violence
02-27.subtitle: Violent riots, perceived to have been instigated by a train fire that killed 59 Hindu pilgrims, broke out in the Indian ...
02-27.text: Violent riots, perceived to have been instigated by a train fire that killed 59 Hindu pilgrims, broke out in the Indian state of Gujarat, killing at least 1,000 people, mostly Muslims, over three days.

02-27.title: 2010 Chile earthquake
02-27.subtitle: An earthquake measuring 8.
02-27.text: An earthquake measuring 8.8 on the moment magnitude scale strikes central parts of Chile leaving over 500 victims, and thousands injured. The quake triggers a tsunami which strikes Hawaii shortly after.

02-27.title: Boris Nemtsov
02-27.subtitle: Russian statesman and politician Boris Nemtsov, an outspoken critic of Vladimir Putin, was assassinated in central Moscow.
02-27.text: Russian statesman and politician Boris Nemtsov, an outspoken critic of Vladimir Putin, was assassinated in central Moscow.

02-28.title: Han Dynasty Founded
02-28.subtitle: A rebel becomes the emperor of all China
02-28.text: Liu Bang, a former peasant who had risen to lead a rebel army, declared himself Emperor Gaozu of Han after overthrowing the brutal Qin dynasty. The Han dynasty he founded would endure for over four centuries, becoming one of China's golden ages and giving the Chinese people their enduring ethnic identity. From a countryside revolt to the throne of heaven, Liu Bang's story is one of history's most remarkable ascents from nothing to everything.

02-28.title: Linus Pauling Was Born
02-28.subtitle: The only person to win two solo Nobel Prizes
02-28.text: Linus Carl Pauling was born in Portland, Oregon, destined to become one of the twentieth century's greatest scientists and most passionate activists. His groundbreaking work on chemical bonding earned him the Nobel Prize in Chemistry, and his tireless campaigning against nuclear weapons testing won him the Nobel Peace Prize, making him the only person to receive two unshared Nobel Prizes. Pauling's dual legacy of scientific brilliance and moral courage remains unmatched.

02-28.title: Discoverer 1
02-28.subtitle: Discoverer 1, an American spy satellite that is the first object intended to achieve a polar orbit, is launched but fails to achieve orbit.
02-28.text: Discoverer 1, an American spy satellite that is the first object intended to achieve a polar orbit, is launched but fails to achieve orbit.

02-28.title: Northrop T-38 Talon
02-28.subtitle: A NASA T-38 Talon crashes into the McDonnell Aircraft factory while attempting a poor-visibility landing at Lambert Field, St.
02-28.text: A NASA T-38 Talon crashes into the McDonnell Aircraft factory while attempting a poor-visibility landing at Lambert Field, St. Louis, killing astronauts Elliot See and Charles Bassett.

02-28.title: Law enforcement in Japan
02-28.subtitle: Japanese police stormed a mountain lodge near Karuizawa, Nagano Prefecture, to end a ten-day siege by members of the par...
