02-28.text: Japanese police stormed a mountain lodge near Karuizawa, Nagano Prefecture, to end a ten-day siege by members of the paramilitary group United Red Army.

02-28.title: February 1974 United Kingdom general election
02-28.subtitle: The British election ended in a hung parliament after the Liberal Party, under Jeremy Thorpe, achieved their highest ever number of votes.
02-28.text: The British election ended in a hung parliament after the Liberal Party, under Jeremy Thorpe, achieved their highest ever number of votes.

02-28.title: The Troubles
02-28.subtitle: The Troubles: The Provisional Irish Republican Army launched a mortar attack on a Royal Ulster Constabulary station in N...
02-28.text: The Troubles were an ethno-nationalist conflict in Northern Ireland that lasted for about 30 years from the late 1960s to 1998. Also known internationally as the Northern Ireland conflict, it began in the late 1960s and is usually deemed to have ended with the Good Friday Agreement of 1998. Although the Troubles mostly took place in Northern Ireland, at times violence spilled over into parts of the Republic of Ireland, England, and mainland Europe.

02-28.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
02-28.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-36.
02-28.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

02-28.title: GRB 970228
02-28.subtitle: GRB 970228, a highly luminous 80-second flash of gamma rays, was detected on Earth, providing early evidence that gamma-...
02-28.text: GRB 970228, a highly luminous 80-second flash of gamma rays, was detected on Earth, providing early evidence that gamma-ray bursts occur well beyond the Milky Way.

02-28.title: Moise Kean Was Born
02-28.subtitle: Italy's youngest Serie A goalscorer takes shape
02-28.text: Moise Kean was born in Italy to Ivorian parents, a future footballer whose precocious talent would make him one of the youngest goalscorers in Serie A history. His journey from Juventus's youth academy to the senior national team embodied both the opportunities and challenges facing young players of African descent in Italian football. Kean's explosive pace and finishing ability marked him as a generational talent from his earliest appearances.

02-28.title: 2001 Nisqually earthquake
02-28.subtitle: The 2001 Nisqually earthquake, having a moment magnitude of 6.
02-28.text: The 2001 Nisqually earthquake, having a moment magnitude of 6.8, with epicenter in southern Puget Sound, damages the Seattle metropolitan area.

02-28.title: 2002 Gujarat violence
02-28.subtitle: During a period of religious violence in Gujarat, India, mobs of Hindus attacked Muslims in Naroda Patiya and in Chamanpura, resulting in 166 deaths.
02-28.text: During a period of religious violence in Gujarat, India, mobs of Hindus attacked Muslims in Naroda Patiya and in Chamanpura, resulting in 166 deaths.

02-28.title: Prime minister
02-28.subtitle: Prime Minister Modi of India inaugurates the 2nd Space Port of India - Kulasekarapattinam Spaceport.
02-28.text: Prime Minister Modi of India inaugurates the 2nd Space Port of India - Kulasekarapattinam Spaceport.

02-29.title: Odo Crowned King of France
02-29.subtitle: A defender of Paris earns a crown
02-29.text: Odo, the Count of Paris who had heroically defended the city against a devastating Viking siege, was crowned King of West Francia at Compiegne by Archbishop Walter of Sens. His coronation recognized not only his military valor but also the growing power of local lords over a weakening Carolingian monarchy. Odo's reign, born of necessity and merit, foreshadowed the feudal fragmentation that would define medieval France.

02-29.title: Jimmy Dorsey Was Born
02-29.subtitle: A saxophone legend born on the rarest day
02-29.text: Jimmy Dorsey was born on the rarest date in the calendar, February 29, a leap day baby destined for big band immortality. With his virtuosic saxophone playing and his own orchestra, Dorsey became one of the defining musicians of the swing era. Alongside his brother Tommy, he helped create the soundtrack of wartime America, filling dance halls with music that kept a nation's spirits alive during its darkest hours.

02-29.title: Berkeley, California
02-29.subtitle: In a ceremony held in Berkeley, California, physicist Ernest Lawrence receives the 1939 Nobel Prize in Physics from Swed...
02-29.text: In a ceremony held in Berkeley, California, physicist Ernest Lawrence receives the 1939 Nobel Prize in Physics from Sweden's consul general in San Francisco.

02-29.title: 1960 Agadir earthquake
02-29.subtitle: The deadliest earthquake in Moroccan history (damaged building pictured) struck the city of Agadir, killing at least 12,000 people.
02-29.text: The deadliest earthquake in Moroccan history (damaged building pictured) struck the city of Agadir, killing at least 12,000 people.

02-29.title: Costello's
02-29.subtitle: Costello's, a New York City bar popular with journalists, writers, novelists, and cartoonists, closed.
02-29.text: Costello's, a New York City bar popular with journalists, writers, novelists, and cartoonists, closed.

02-29.title: Faucett Perú Flight 251
02-29.subtitle: In the deadliest aviation accident in Peruvian history, Faucett Flight 251 crashed on approach to Rodríguez Ballón Inter...
02-29.text: In the deadliest aviation accident in Peruvian history, Faucett Flight 251 crashed on approach to Rodríguez Ballón International Airport, killing all 123 people aboard.

02-29.title: Battle Near Ulus Kert
02-29.subtitle: Eighty-four Russian paratroopers fall in Chechnya
02-29.text: In the mountains of Chechnya, Chechen fighters attacked a Russian guard post near Ulus Kert, eventually killing eighty-four paratroopers from Russia's elite airborne forces. The devastating losses shocked Russia and exposed the brutal reality of the Second Chechen War. The battle became one of the conflict's bloodiest engagements for Russian forces and a painful reminder of the human cost of the war in the North Caucasus.

02-29.title: Jean-Bertrand Aristide
02-29.subtitle: Jean-Bertrand Aristide is removed as president of Haiti following a coup.
02-29.text: Jean-Bertrand Aristide is a Haitian former Salesian priest and politician who served as president of Haiti in 1991, from 1993 to 1994, from 1994 to 1996, and from 2001 to 2004. He was in exile after the 1991 military coup until 1994 and again after his overthrow in 2004 until 2011. Aristide was a member of the Lavalas Political Organization before he founded the party Fanmi Lavalas in 1996.

02-29.title: United States
02-29.subtitle: The United States and the Taliban sign the Doha Agreement for bringing peace to Afghanistan.
02-29.text: The United States and the Taliban sign the Doha Agreement for bringing peace to Afghanistan.

02-29.title: Flour Massacre
02-29.subtitle: The Flour Massacre took place in the Gaza Strip: Israeli forces opened fire on Palestinians waiting for aid amidst the G...
02-29.text: The Flour Massacre took place in the Gaza Strip: Israeli forces opened fire on Palestinians waiting for aid amidst the Gaza war, killing over 100 and wounding 750.

03-01.title: Rome's First Triumph
03-01.subtitle: A republic celebrates its founding victory
03-01.text: Publius Valerius Publicola celebrated the first triumph of the Roman Republic, parading through the streets of Rome after his victory over the deposed King Lucius Tarquinius Superbus. The triumph, a ceremonial procession reserved for Rome's greatest military victories, established a tradition that would define Roman martial culture for five centuries. This first celebration marked not just a military victory but the birth of a republic's identity.

03-01.title: Paul Hartman Was Born
03-01.subtitle: A triple threat of stage and screen
03-01.text: Paul Hartman was born in the United States, a future performer whose talents spanned acting, singing, and dancing. His versatility made him a familiar face on both Broadway stages and Hollywood screens during the golden age of American entertainment. Hartman's career embodied the multi-talented ideal of the mid-century performer, equally at home in a musical number as in a dramatic role.

03-01.title: Australia national cricket team
03-01.subtitle: The Australian cricket team, led by Warwick Armstrong, became the first team to complete a whitewash in the Ashes, an ac...
03-01.text: The Australia men's national cricket team represents Australia in international cricket. Along with England, it is the joint oldest team in Test cricket history, playing and winning the first ever Test match in 1877; the team also plays One-Day International and Twenty20 International cricket, participating in both the first ODI, against England in the 1970–71 season and the first T20I, against New Zealand in the 2004–05 season, winning both games.

03-01.title: World War II
03-01.subtitle: World War II: American and Australian troops won the Battle of Sio against Japanese forces as part of the New Guinea campaign.
03-01.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

03-01.title: Cold War
03-01.subtitle: Cold War: Klaus Fuchs is convicted of spying for the Soviet Union by disclosing top secret atomic bomb data.
03-01.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-01.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-01.title: Nuclear weapons testing
03-01.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: The Castle Bravo, a 15-megaton hydrogen bomb, is detonated on Bikini Atoll in the Pacific Ocean...
03-01.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
03-01.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

03-01.title: Venera 3
03-01.subtitle: Venera 3 Soviet space probe crashes on Venus becoming the first spacecraft to land on another planet's surface.
03-01.text: Venera 3 Soviet space probe crashes on Venus becoming the first spacecraft to land on another planet's surface.

03-01.title: Pink Floyd
03-01.subtitle: Pink Floyd's critically acclaimed album The Dark Side of the Moon was released.
03-01.text: Pink Floyd's critically acclaimed album The Dark Side of the Moon was released.

03-01.title: Serbs of Bosnia and Herzegovina
03-01.subtitle: A Bosnian-Serb wedding procession was attacked in Sarajevo, resulting in what is widely considered the first casualty of the Bosnian War.
03-01.text: A Bosnian-Serb wedding procession was attacked in Sarajevo, resulting in what is widely considered the first casualty of the Bosnian War.

03-01.title: Ja'Marr Chase Was Born
03-01.subtitle: Football's next great receiver enters the world
03-01.text: Ja'Marr Chase was born in the United States, a future wide receiver whose combination of speed, route-running, and natural playmaking ability would make him one of the most electrifying players in the NFL. After dominating college football at LSU, Chase was selected fifth overall in the 2021 NFL draft by the Cincinnati Bengals, immediately becoming one of the league's most dangerous offensive weapons.

03-01.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
03-01.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-109 to service the Hubble Space Telescope.
03-01.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

03-02.title: Benedict of Nursia Born
03-02.subtitle: The father of Western monasticism arrives
03-02.text: In the town of Nursia in the Umbrian hills of Italy, Benedict was born, the man who would become the father of Western monasticism. His Rule of Saint Benedict, with its balanced prescription of prayer, work, and communal living, became the blueprint for monastic life across Europe. Benedictine monasteries preserved classical learning through the Dark Ages and shaped the cultural, spiritual, and intellectual life of medieval Christendom.

03-02.title: Kurt Weill Was Born
03-02.subtitle: The composer who married music to politics
03-02.text: Kurt Weill was born in Dessau, Germany, a future composer who would revolutionize musical theater on two continents. His collaboration with Bertolt Brecht produced The Threepenny Opera and its immortal 'Mack the Knife.' Fleeing Nazi Germany, Weill reinvented himself on Broadway, creating works that blended European sophistication with American vitality. His music, sharp-edged and hauntingly melodic, bridged high art and popular culture like no other.

03-02.title: Golden State Warriors
03-02.subtitle: Playing for the Philadelphia Warriors, American basketball player Wilt Chamberlain (pictured) scored 100 points in a gam...
03-02.text: Playing for the Philadelphia Warriors, American basketball player Wilt Chamberlain (pictured) scored 100 points in a game against the New York Knicks, which remains an NBA record.

03-02.title: Vietnam War
03-02.subtitle: Vietnam War: The American and South Vietnamese air forces began Operation Rolling Thunder, a sustained bombing campaign ...
03-02.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
03-02.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

03-02.title: Concorde
03-02.subtitle: The maiden flight of Concorde, the Anglo-French supersonic jet, was conducted in Toulouse, France using a prototype airc...
03-02.text:
Concorde is a retired Anglo-French supersonic airliner jointly developed and manufactured by Sud Aviation and the British Aircraft Corporation (BAC).

Studies began in 1954 and a UK–France treaty followed in 1962, as the programme cost was estimated at £70 million .
Construction of six prototypes began in February 1965, with the first flight from Toulouse on 2 March 1969.

The market forecast was 350 aircraft, with manufacturers receiving up to 100 options from major airlines.

03-02.title: Pioneer 10
03-02.subtitle: The Pioneer 10 space probe is launched from Cape Canaveral, Florida with a mission to explore the outer planets.
03-02.text: The Pioneer 10 space probe is launched from Cape Canaveral, Florida with a mission to explore the outer planets.

03-02.title: Czechoslovakia
03-02.subtitle: Czech Vladimír Remek becomes the first non-Soviet or non-American to go into space, when he is launched aboard Soyuz 28.
03-02.text: Czech Vladimír Remek becomes the first non-Soviet or non-American to go into space, when he is launched aboard Soyuz 28.

03-02.title: Armenia
03-02.subtitle: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Moldova, San Marino, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan and Uzbekistan, all of which ...
03-02.text: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Moldova, San Marino, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan and Uzbekistan, all of which (except San Marino) were former Soviet republics, join the United Nations.

03-02.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
03-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches from the Kennedy Space Center on STS-67, carrying the ASTRO-2 spacelab observatory.
03-02.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

03-02.title: Galileo
03-02.subtitle: Data sent from the Galileo spacecraft indicates that Jupiter's moon Europa has a liquid ocean under a thick crust of ice.
03-02.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
03-02.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

03-02.title: Illan Meslier Was Born
03-02.subtitle: France's towering goalkeeper talent emerges
03-02.text: Illan Meslier was born in France, a nation with a proud tradition of producing world-class goalkeepers. The tall, commanding young keeper would move to England to join Leeds United, where his shot-stopping ability and composure under pressure made him one of the Premier League's most promising goalkeepers. Meslier's emergence continued France's remarkable run of producing elite talent between the posts.

03-02.title: Chemical element
03-02.subtitle: A naming ceremony for the chemical elements moscovium, tennessine, and oganesson took place at the Russian Academy of Sciences in Moscow.
03-02.text: A chemical element is a species of atom defined by its number of protons. The number of protons is called the atomic number of that element. For example, oxygen has an atomic number of 8: each oxygen atom has 8 protons in its nucleus. Atoms of the same element can have different numbers of neutrons in their nuclei, known as isotopes of the element.
03-02.location: Chemical substance not composed of simpler ones

03-02.title: Battle of Kherson
03-02.subtitle: Russian forces captured the city of Kherson, the only regional capital to be taken during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
03-02.text: Russian forces captured the city of Kherson, the only regional capital to be taken during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

03-03.title: Glycerius Named Emperor
03-03.subtitle: A puppet placed on a crumbling throne
03-03.text: Gundobad, the powerful Burgundian general and nephew of the late kingmaker Ricimer, placed Glycerius on the throne of the Western Roman Empire. The new emperor was little more than a figurehead in a realm that was rapidly disintegrating. Glycerius's brief and largely powerless reign illustrated the terminal decline of Western Rome, where emperors were made and unmade by barbarian generals who held the real power.

03-03.title: Edna Best Was Born
03-03.subtitle: A star of British stage and Hollywood screen
03-03.text: Edna Best was born in Hove, England, a future actress who would achieve fame on both the London stage and the Hollywood screen. Her performances in plays and films during the 1920s through the 1940s earned her critical acclaim on both sides of the Atlantic. Best's talent and elegance made her a beloved figure in the transatlantic entertainment world during its golden age of theater and cinema.

03-03.title: Woman Suffrage Procession
03-03.subtitle: Thousands of women marched in Washington, D.
03-03.text: Thousands of women marched in Washington, D.C., "in a spirit of protest" against the exclusion of women from American society.

03-03.title: Nohra concentration camp
03-03.subtitle: The first Nazi concentration camp was opened in Nohra to reduce overcrowding in prisons from the arrest of German Communists.
03-03.text: The first Nazi concentration camp was opened in Nohra to reduce overcrowding in prisons from the arrest of German Communists.

03-03.title: Jethro Tull
03-03.subtitle: The British rock band Jethro Tull released Thick as a Brick, a parody concept album allegedly adapted from an eight-year-old boy's epic poem.
03-03.text: The British rock band Jethro Tull released Thick as a Brick, a parody concept album allegedly adapted from an eight-year-old boy's epic poem.

03-03.title: Metallica
03-03.subtitle: The American heavy metal band Metallica (pictured) released their third studio album, Master of Puppets, considered one ...
03-03.text: Metallica is an American heavy metal band. It was formed in Los Angeles in 1981 by vocalist/guitarist James Hetfield and drummer Lars Ulrich, and has been based in San Francisco for most of its career. The band's fast tempos, instrumentals and aggressive musicianship made them one of the founding "big four" bands of thrash metal, alongside Megadeth, Anthrax and Slayer.

03-03.title: Jevon Holland Was Born
03-03.subtitle: A Canadian-American defensive standout arrives
03-03.text: Jevon Holland was born with roots in both Canada and the United States, a future football safety whose instincts and athleticism would make him a standout in the NFL. After a stellar college career at the University of Oregon, Holland was drafted by the Miami Dolphins, where his ball-hawking skills and versatility in the secondary quickly established him as one of the league's most promising young defenders.

03-03.title: Szczekociny rail crash
03-03.subtitle: Two passenger trains collided near Szczekociny, Poland, resulting in 16 deaths and 58 injuries.
03-03.text: Two passenger trains collided near Szczekociny, Poland, resulting in 16 deaths and 58 injuries.

03-04.title: Nero Named Youth Leader
03-04.subtitle: A future tyrant receives his first honor
03-04.text: The Roman Senate bestowed upon the young Nero the title princeps iuventutis, head of the youth, a traditional honor for imperial heirs. The boy, adopted by Emperor Claudius, showed no signs yet of the madness and cruelty that would later define his reign. This early accolade set Nero on the path to the throne, where he would become one of history's most infamous rulers, remembered for persecution, excess, and Rome in flames.

03-04.title: Herbert Biberman Was Born
03-04.subtitle: A filmmaker who defied the blacklist
03-04.text: Herbert Biberman was born in Philadelphia, a future director and screenwriter who would become one of Hollywood's most controversial figures. As one of the Hollywood Ten, Biberman refused to cooperate with the House Un-American Activities Committee and was imprisoned and blacklisted. His defiant film Salt of the Earth, made outside the studio system with blacklisted cast and crew, became a landmark of independent American cinema.

03-04.title: Voyager 1
03-04.subtitle: The space probe Voyager 1 discovered the rings of Jupiter, the third ring system to be discovered in the Solar System, a...
03-04.text: The space probe Voyager 1 discovered the rings of Jupiter, the third ring system to be discovered in the Solar System, after those of Saturn and Uranus.

03-04.title: Robert Mugabe
03-04.subtitle: Nationalist leader Robert Mugabe wins a sweeping election victory to become Zimbabwe's first black prime minister.
03-04.text: Robert Gabriel Mugabe was a Zimbabwean revolutionary and politician who led Zimbabwe from 1980 until he was deposed in a coup in 2017. He served as the first Prime Minister of Zimbabwe from internationally recognised independence in 1980 to 1987, then as the second president of Zimbabwe from 1987 to 2017.

03-04.title: Lennox Sebe
03-04.subtitle: Lennox Sebe, President for life of the South African Bantustan of Ciskei, is ousted from power in a bloodless military c...
03-04.text: Lennox Sebe, President for life of the South African Bantustan of Ciskei, is ousted from power in a bloodless military coup led by Brigadier Oupa Gqozo.

03-04.title: Space Shuttle program
03-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: The Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-62.
03-04.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

03-04.title: PlayStation 2 Launches
03-04.subtitle: The best-selling console ever hits shelves
03-04.text: Sony released the PlayStation 2 in Japan, launching what would become the best-selling video game console in history. With its built-in DVD player, backward compatibility, and an enormous library of games, the PS2 transformed gaming from a niche hobby into a mainstream entertainment powerhouse. Over 155 million units would eventually be sold worldwide, making it a cultural phenomenon that defined an entire generation of gamers.

03-04.title: Death of Charlotte Shaw
03-04.subtitle: Fourteen-year-old English schoolgirl Charlotte Shaw drowned on Dartmoor, becoming the first person to die in connection ...
03-04.text: Fourteen-year-old English schoolgirl Charlotte Shaw drowned on Dartmoor, becoming the first person to die in connection with the annual Ten Tors challenge.

03-04.title: International Criminal Court
03-04.subtitle: The International Criminal Court (ICC) issues an arrest warrant for Sudanese President Omar Hassan al-Bashir for war cri...
03-04.text: The International Criminal Court (ICC) issues an arrest warrant for Sudanese President Omar Hassan al-Bashir for war crimes and crimes against humanity in Darfur. Al-Bashir is the first sitting head of state to be indicted by the ICC since its establishment in 2002.

03-04.title: The Buddha
03-04.subtitle: Construction began on a 69-metre (226 ft) statue of the Buddha at Wat Paknam Bhasicharoen in Bangkok.
03-04.text: Construction began on a 69-metre (226 ft) statue of the Buddha at Wat Paknam Bhasicharoen in Bangkok.

03-05.title: Julian Marches Against Persia
03-05.subtitle: Rome's last pagan emperor leads his army east
03-05.text: Emperor Julian, the last pagan ruler of Rome, marshaled his legions and set out from Antioch to attack the Sasanian Empire. The campaign was Julian's grand ambition: to match the conquests of Alexander the Great and restore Rome's eastern glory. His army marched through the Syrian desert with supreme confidence, but the campaign would end in Julian's death and a humiliating Roman retreat, marking the end of Rome's offensive ambitions in the East.

03-05.title: Prince Friedrich Gunther Born
03-05.subtitle: A German prince in a vanishing world
03-05.text: Friedrich Gunther, Prince of Schwarzburg, was born into one of Germany's ancient noble families at a time when the old European order was beginning to crack. The prince would live through the fall of the German Empire, two world wars, and the division of Germany, witnessing the complete transformation of the world into which he had been born. His life spanned the twilight of European aristocracy and the dawn of the modern age.

03-05.title: World War II
03-05.subtitle: World War II: The Gloster Meteor, the Allies' only operational jet aircraft, made its maiden flight.
03-05.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

03-05.title: Alberto Korda
03-05.subtitle: Cuban photographer Alberto Korda took his iconic photograph Guerrillero Heroico of Marxist revolutionary Che Guevara.
03-05.text: Cuban photographer Alberto Korda took his iconic photograph Guerrillero Heroico of Marxist revolutionary Che Guevara.

03-05.title: BOAC Flight 911
03-05.subtitle: BOAC Flight 911 disintegrated and crashed near Mount Fuji shortly after departure from Tokyo International Airport, kill...
03-05.text: BOAC Flight 911 disintegrated and crashed near Mount Fuji shortly after departure from Tokyo International Airport, killing all 113 passengers and 11 crew members on board.

03-05.title: ZX81
03-05.subtitle: The ZX81, a pioneering British home computer, was launched by Sinclair Research, and went on to sell more than 1.
03-05.text: The ZX81, a pioneering British home computer, was launched by Sinclair Research, and went on to sell more than 1.5 million units around the world.

03-05.title: Doug Edert Was Born
03-05.subtitle: March Madness finds a new folk hero
03-05.text: Doug Edert was born in the United States, a future college basketball player who would become an unlikely March Madness folk hero. His mustache, swagger, and clutch shooting for Saint Peter's University during their stunning 2022 NCAA Tournament run captured the imagination of the nation. Edert embodied the Cinderella spirit that makes the tournament America's most beloved sporting event.

03-06.title: Augustus Becomes Pontifex Maximus
03-06.subtitle: Emperor and high priest become one
03-06.text: Emperor Augustus was named Pontifex Maximus, the highest religious office in Rome, merging sacred and secular authority in a single person. By incorporating the position into the imperial title, Augustus ensured that future emperors would control not just the armies and treasury of Rome, but its spiritual life as well. This fusion of religious and political power would endure for centuries, shaping the relationship between church and state throughout Western history.

03-06.title: Gina Cigna Was Born
03-06.subtitle: A soprano who conquered opera's greatest stages
03-06.text: Gina Cigna was born in Paris to Italian parents, a future soprano whose powerful voice and dramatic intensity would make her one of the leading opera singers of the interwar era. Her performances in Verdi, Puccini, and Wagner roles at La Scala and other great opera houses earned her international acclaim. Cigna's remarkable longevity, living past one hundred, made her one of the last living links to opera's pre-war golden age.

03-06.title: MS Herald of Free Enterprise
03-06.subtitle: The ferry Herald of Free Enterprise capsized while leaving the harbour of Zeebrugge, Belgium, killing 193 people on board.
03-06.text: The ferry Herald of Free Enterprise capsized while leaving the harbour of Zeebrugge, Belgium, killing 193 people on board.

03-06.title: The Troubles
03-06.subtitle: The Troubles: In Operation Flavius, the Special Air Service killed three volunteers of the Provisional Irish Republican ...
03-06.text: The Troubles were an ethno-nationalist conflict in Northern Ireland that lasted for about 30 years from the late 1960s to 1998. Also known internationally as the Northern Ireland conflict, it began in the late 1960s and is usually deemed to have ended with the Good Friday Agreement of 1998. Although the Troubles mostly took place in Northern Ireland, at times violence spilled over into parts of the Republic of Ireland, England, and mainland Europe.

03-06.title: Singapore's Largest Mural Opens
03-06.subtitle: Art transforms a community building
03-06.text: The Marine Parade Community Building in Singapore opened, featuring a mural cladding that became the largest installation art piece in the city-state. The building, designed to serve the community while making a bold artistic statement, demonstrated Singapore's commitment to integrating art into public spaces. The massive mural transformed a functional building into a landmark, reflecting the island nation's ambition to be a hub of culture and design.

03-07.title: Marcus Aurelius Becomes Emperor
03-07.subtitle: The philosopher-king ascends to Rome's throne
03-07.text: Upon the death of Antoninus Pius, Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus became joint emperors of Rome, an unprecedented sharing of supreme power. Marcus Aurelius, the Stoic philosopher whose Meditations would become one of the most influential books ever written, now bore the weight of an empire stretching across three continents. His reign would be tested by plague, war, and rebellion, but his philosophical composure never wavered.

03-07.title: Ottoman Empire
03-07.subtitle: A peace treaty is signed between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice, ending the Ottoman–Venetian War and leaving Cyprus in Ottoman hands.
03-07.text: A peace treaty is signed between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice, ending the Ottoman–Venetian War and leaving Cyprus in Ottoman hands.

03-07.title: First Ship Sends Wireless
03-07.subtitle: A German liner radios land from the open sea
03-07.text: The German ocean liner SS Kaiser Wilhelm der Grosse became the first ship to send a wireless telegraph message to an onshore receiver, bridging the gap between sea and land with invisible waves. This breakthrough in maritime communication heralded a revolution in ocean travel and safety. The era of ships disappearing silently over the horizon was ending; from this moment forward, the sea would never be quite so lonely or so dangerous.

03-07.title: Remilitarisation of the Rhineland
03-07.subtitle: Nazi German forces re-occupied the demilitarized Rhineland, violating both the Treaty of Versailles and the Locarno Trea...
03-07.text: The remilitarisation of the Rhineland began on 7 March 1936, when military forces of Nazi Germany entered the Rhineland, which directly contravened the Treaty of Versailles and the Locarno Treaties. Neither France nor Britain was prepared for a military response, so they did not act. After 1939, commentators often said that a strong military move in 1936 might have ruined the expansionist plans of Adolf Hitler, the dictator of Germany.

03-07.title: Korean War
03-07.subtitle: Korean War: Operation Ripper: United Nations troops led by General Matthew Ridgway begin an assault against Chinese forces.
03-07.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

03-07.title: Bloody Sunday
03-07.subtitle: Bloody Sunday: A group of 600 civil rights marchers are brutally attacked by state and local police in Selma, Alabama.
03-07.text: Bloody Sunday: A group of 600 civil rights marchers are brutally attacked by state and local police in Selma, Alabama.

03-07.title: Vietnam War
03-07.subtitle: Vietnam War: The United States and South Vietnam began Operation Truong Cong Dinh to sweep the area surrounding the Meko...
03-07.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
03-07.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

03-07.title: Rasmus Sandin Was Born
03-07.subtitle: Sweden's next hockey defender takes shape
03-07.text: Rasmus Sandin was born in Sweden, a nation that produces elite ice hockey talent at a rate that dwarfs its population. The smooth-skating defenseman would develop through the Swedish hockey system before being drafted by the Toronto Maple Leafs. His poise with the puck, smart positioning, and offensive instincts made him a valued contributor in the NHL, continuing Sweden's tradition of exporting world-class defensemen.

03-07.title: Kepler space telescope
03-07.subtitle: The Kepler space telescope (depicted), designed to discover Earth-like planets orbiting other stars, was launched.
03-07.text: The Kepler space telescope (depicted), designed to discover Earth-like planets orbiting other stars, was launched.

03-07.title: Sweden
03-07.subtitle: Sweden officially joins NATO, becoming its 32nd member.
03-07.text: Sweden officially joins NATO, becoming its 32nd member.

03-08.title: Shahnameh Completed
03-08.subtitle: Persia's epic poem reaches its final verse
03-08.text: The poet Ferdowsi completed the Shahnameh, the Book of Kings, an epic poem of over fifty thousand couplets that chronicles the mythological and historical past of the Persian world from creation to the Islamic conquest. Thirty years in the writing, the Shahnameh preserved the Persian language and identity at a time when both were under threat from Arabization. It remains the longest poem ever written by a single author and the crown jewel of Persian literature.

03-08.title: Louise Beavers Was Born
03-08.subtitle: A pioneering Black actress in Hollywood
03-08.text: Louise Beavers was born in Cincinnati, Ohio, a future actress and singer who would become one of Hollywood's most recognized Black performers during the segregated studio era. Despite being largely confined to stereotypical roles, Beavers brought dignity and depth to every performance, particularly in Imitation of Life. Her career, spanning over thirty years, paved the way for future generations of Black actresses in American cinema.

03-08.title: President of France
03-08.subtitle: President of France Vincent Auriol and ex-Vietnamese emperor Bảo Đại sign the Élysée Accords, giving Vietnam greater ind...
03-08.text: President of France Vincent Auriol and ex-Vietnamese emperor Bảo Đại sign the Élysée Accords, giving Vietnam greater independence from France and creating the State of Vietnam to oppose Viet Minh-led Democratic Republic of Vietnam.

03-08.title: Ba'ath Party
03-08.subtitle: The Ba'ath Party came to power in a coup d'état by a clique of quasi-leftist Syrian Army officers calling themselves the...
03-08.text: The Ba'ath Party came to power in a coup d'état by a clique of quasi-leftist Syrian Army officers calling themselves the National Council for the Revolutionary Command.

03-08.title: BBC Radio 4
03-08.subtitle: BBC Radio 4 began broadcasting Douglas Adams's The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, a science fiction radio series that...
03-08.text: BBC Radio 4 began broadcasting Douglas Adams's The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, a science fiction radio series that was later adapted into novels, a television series, and other formats.

03-08.title: Voyager 1
03-08.subtitle: Images taken by Voyager 1 prove the existence of volcanoes on Io, a moon of Jupiter.
03-08.text: Images taken by Voyager 1 prove the existence of volcanoes on Io, a moon of Jupiter.

03-08.title: Cold War
03-08.subtitle: Cold War: While addressing a convention of Evangelicals, U.
03-08.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-08.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-08.title: Discovery Carries Expedition 2
03-08.subtitle: A new crew heads to humanity's orbital outpost
03-08.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launched on mission STS-102, carrying the Expedition 2 crew to the International Space Station. The mission marked the continuation of permanent human habitation in space, a milestone in international cooperation and scientific exploration. The new crew would spend months conducting experiments in microgravity, maintaining the station, and advancing humanity's understanding of living and working beyond Earth.
03-08.location: Kennedy Space Center, Florida, United States

03-08.title: Malaysia Airlines Flight 370
03-08.subtitle: Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappeared en route from Kuala Lumpur to Beijing with 239 people on board, with aircraft d...
03-08.text: Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 was an international passenger flight operated by Malaysia Airlines that disappeared from radar on 8 March 2014, while flying from Kuala Lumpur International Airport in Malaysia to its planned destination, Beijing Capital International Airport in China. The cause of its disappearance has not been determined. It is widely regarded as the greatest mystery in aviation history, and remains the single deadliest case of aircraft disappearance.

03-08.title: Aurat March
03-08.subtitle: The first Aurat March (social/political demonstration) is held on International Women's Day in Karachi, Pakistan, since ...
03-08.text: The first Aurat March (social/political demonstration) is held on International Women's Day in Karachi, Pakistan, since then held annually across Pakistan, and the feminist slogan "Mera Jism Meri Marzi" (My body, my choice), in demand for women's right to bodily autonomy and against gender-based violence, came into vogue in Pakistan.

03-09.title: Emperor Wu Takes the Throne
03-09.subtitle: China's greatest Han ruler begins his reign
03-09.text: Liu Che ascended the throne of the Han dynasty at the age of fifteen, becoming Emperor Wu, one of the most transformative rulers in Chinese history. Over a reign spanning fifty-four years, he expanded the empire to its greatest extent, established the Silk Road trade routes, and made Confucianism the state philosophy. His rule marked the zenith of the Han dynasty and helped shape Chinese civilization for two millennia to come.

03-09.title: Will Geer Was Born
03-09.subtitle: From the blacklist to Walton's Mountain
03-09.text: William Aughe Geer was born in Indiana, a future actor best known to millions as Grandpa Zebulon Walton on The Waltons. But Geer's career was far more complex than that beloved role suggested. A committed activist, he was blacklisted during the McCarthy era for his political beliefs. His later success on television represented both a personal triumph and a reminder of the human cost of ideological persecution in America.

03-09.title: World War II
03-09.subtitle: World War II: As part of the Battle of Narva, the Soviet Air Forces heavily bombed Tallinn, Estonia, killing up to 800 people, mostly civilians.
03-09.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

03-09.title: McCarthyism
03-09.subtitle: McCarthyism: CBS television broadcasts the See It Now episode, "A Report on Senator Joseph McCarthy", produced by Fred Friendly.
03-09.text: McCarthyism: CBS television broadcasts the See It Now episode, "A Report on Senator Joseph McCarthy", produced by Fred Friendly.

03-09.title: Tbilisi
03-09.subtitle: In Tbilisi, Georgia, soldiers suppressed mass demonstrations against Soviet leader Nikita Khrushchev's policy of de-Stalinization.
03-09.text: In Tbilisi, Georgia, soldiers suppressed mass demonstrations against Soviet leader Nikita Khrushchev's policy of de-Stalinization.

03-09.title: Korabl-Sputnik 4
03-09.subtitle: Sputnik 9 successfully launches, carrying a dog and a human dummy, and demonstrating that the Soviet Union was ready to begin human spaceflight.
03-09.text: Sputnik 9 successfully launches, carrying a dog and a human dummy, and demonstrating that the Soviet Union was ready to begin human spaceflight.

03-09.title: Svetlana Alliluyeva
03-09.subtitle: Svetlana Alliluyeva, the daughter of former Soviet leader Joseph Stalin, defected to the United States.
03-09.text: Svetlana Alliluyeva, the daughter of former Soviet leader Joseph Stalin, defected to the United States.

03-09.title: Washington, D.C.
03-09.subtitle: Twelve gunmen seized three buildings in Washington, D.
03-09.text: Twelve gunmen seized three buildings in Washington, D.C., and took 149 hostages in a 39-hour standoff that ended in two deaths.

03-09.title: Suharto
03-09.subtitle: President Soeharto inaugurated Jagorawi Toll Road, the first toll highway in Indonesia, connecting Jakarta, Bogor and Ciawi, West Java.
03-09.text: Suharto was an Indonesian military officer and politician who served as the second and longest-serving president of Indonesia from 1967 to 1998. Widely regarded as a military dictator by international observers, Suharto led Indonesia as an authoritarian regime from 1967 until his resignation in 1998 following nationwide unrest.

03-09.title: Nupedia Goes Online
03-09.subtitle: The ancestor of Wikipedia takes its first steps
03-09.text: Nupedia, a free multi-language online encyclopedia, was launched with the ambitious goal of creating expert-reviewed, high-quality articles accessible to everyone. Though Nupedia's slow, peer-reviewed process would prove impractical, it planted the seed for an idea that would change the world. Within a year, its successor Wikipedia would be born, becoming the largest encyclopedia in human history and transforming how humanity shares knowledge.

03-09.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
03-09.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery makes its final landing after 39 flights.
03-09.text: Space Shuttle Discovery makes its final landing after 39 flights.

03-10.title: Battle of the Aegates
03-10.subtitle: Rome wins the seas and ends the First Punic War
03-10.text: Off the western coast of Sicily, the Roman Republic's navy crushed the Carthaginian fleet at the Battle of the Aegates, decisively ending the First Punic War. The victory transformed Rome from a land power into a naval force capable of dominating the Mediterranean. Carthage, exhausted and defeated, sued for peace, ceding Sicily to Rome. The battle marked the beginning of Rome's rise as a Mediterranean superpower and Carthage's slow decline.

03-10.title: Pandelis Pouliopoulos Born
03-10.subtitle: A Greek revolutionary with a pen and a cause
03-10.text: Pandelis Pouliopoulos was born in Greece, a future lawyer and politician who would become one of the founders of Greek communism. His sharp legal mind and passionate oratory made him a formidable advocate for workers' rights and social justice. Pouliopoulos's life, cut short by execution during the Axis occupation in 1943, became a symbol of resistance and intellectual courage in the face of tyranny.

03-10.title: Anglo-Siamese Treaty of 1909
03-10.subtitle: By signing the Anglo-Siamese Treaty of 1909, Thailand relinquishes its sovereignty over the Malay states of Kedah, Kelan...
03-10.text: By signing the Anglo-Siamese Treaty of 1909, Thailand relinquishes its sovereignty over the Malay states of Kedah, Kelantan, Perlis and Terengganu, which become British protectorates.

03-10.title: 1959 Tibetan uprising
03-10.subtitle: An anti-Chinese uprising began as thousands of Tibetans surrounded the Potala Palace in Lhasa to prevent the Dalai Lama ...
03-10.text: An anti-Chinese uprising began as thousands of Tibetans surrounded the Potala Palace in Lhasa to prevent the Dalai Lama from leaving or being removed by the Chinese army.

03-10.title: Nguyễn Cao Kỳ
03-10.subtitle: Military prime minister of South Vietnam Nguyễn Cao Kỳ sacked rival General Nguyễn Chánh Thi, precipitating large-scale ...
03-10.text: Military prime minister of South Vietnam Nguyễn Cao Kỳ sacked rival General Nguyễn Chánh Thi, precipitating large-scale civil and military dissension in parts of the nation.

03-10.title: Vietnam War
03-10.subtitle: Vietnam War/Laotian Civil War: North Vietnamese and Pathet Lao forces overwhelmed the American, Laotian, Thai, and Hmong defenders of Lima Site 85.
03-10.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
03-10.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

03-10.title: Dot-Com Bubble Peaks
03-10.subtitle: The NASDAQ reaches its euphoric summit
03-10.text: The NASDAQ Composite stock market index reached its all-time peak of 5,048.62, the apex of the dot-com bubble. Internet companies with no profits and fantastical valuations had sent stock prices soaring to unsustainable heights. Within weeks, the bubble would burst, wiping out trillions of dollars in market value and ending the decade's most speculative frenzy. The crash reshaped the technology industry and taught a generation of investors about the dangers of irrational exuberance.

03-10.title: NASA
03-10.subtitle: NASA's Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter (artist's impression pictured) reached and entered orbit around Mars.
03-10.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

03-10.title: The New York Times
03-10.subtitle: The New York Times revealed that Eliot Spitzer (pictured), Governor of New York, had patronized a prostitution ring.
03-10.text: The New York Times revealed that Eliot Spitzer (pictured), Governor of New York, had patronized a prostitution ring.

03-10.title: Impeachment of Park Geun-hye
03-10.subtitle: The impeachment of President Park Geun-hye of South Korea in response to a major political scandal is unanimously upheld...
03-10.text: The impeachment of President Park Geun-hye of South Korea in response to a major political scandal is unanimously upheld by the country's Constitutional Court, ending her presidency.

03-10.title: 2022 Hungarian presidential election
03-10.subtitle: 2022 Hungarian presidential election: The National Assembly of Hungary elects former minister for Family Affairs, Katali...
03-10.text: 2022 Hungarian presidential election: The National Assembly of Hungary elects former minister for Family Affairs, Katalin Novák, as president of Hungary in a 137–51 vote, becoming the first female president in the country's history.

03-10.title: Silicon Valley Bank
03-10.subtitle: Silicon Valley Bank collapses due to a run on its deposits, in the second largest bank failure in US history.
03-10.text: Silicon Valley Bank collapses due to a run on its deposits, in the second largest bank failure in US history. Its operations are taken over by the FDIC.

03-10.title: 2024 Portuguese legislative election
03-10.subtitle: 2024 Portuguese legislative election: Elections are held in Portugal for all 230 seats in the Assembly of the Republic.
03-10.text: 2024 Portuguese legislative election: Elections are held in Portugal for all 230 seats in the Assembly of the Republic. The Partido Socialista loses its absolute majority to the Partido Social Democrata, winning 77 and 79 seats respectively.

03-11.title: Triumph of Orthodoxy
03-11.subtitle: Icons return to the churches of Byzantium
03-11.text: Empress Theodora II restored the veneration of icons in the Orthodox churches of the Byzantine Empire, ending decades of iconoclasm. The Triumph of Orthodoxy, as it came to be known, was celebrated as a victory for tradition and devotion over theological abstraction. To this day, the first Sunday of Lent in the Orthodox calendar commemorates this restoration, a testament to the enduring power of sacred images in Eastern Christian worship.

03-11.title: Ronald Syme Was Born
03-11.subtitle: The historian who reimagined Roman politics
03-11.text: Ronald Syme was born in New Zealand, a future historian whose work would revolutionize the study of Roman history. His masterpiece, The Roman Revolution, published in 1939, reinterpreted the fall of the Republic through the lens of factional politics and patronage networks. Syme's prosopographical method, tracing the careers and connections of individual Romans, transformed how historians understand power in the ancient world.

03-11.title: World War II
03-11.subtitle: World War II: The Empire of Vietnam, a short-lived Japanese puppet state, is established.
03-11.text: World War II: The Empire of Vietnam, a short-lived Japanese puppet state, is established.

03-11.title: Coastal road massacre
03-11.subtitle: After hijacking a bus north of Tel Aviv, Israel, members of the Palestine Liberation Organization faction Fatah engaged ...
03-11.text: After hijacking a bus north of Tel Aviv, Israel, members of the Palestine Liberation Organization faction Fatah engaged in a shootout with police, resulting in the deaths of 38 civilians and most of the perpetrators.

03-11.title: International Criminal Court Opens
03-11.subtitle: Global justice gets its own courthouse
03-11.text: The International Criminal Court held its inaugural session in The Hague, Netherlands, establishing the world's first permanent tribunal for prosecuting genocide, war crimes, and crimes against humanity. The court represented a historic step toward international accountability, born from the lessons of Rwanda, Yugoslavia, and a century of unpunished atrocities. Its creation signaled that the most serious crimes against human dignity would no longer go unanswered.

03-11.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
03-11.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches on STS-123, carrying the first component of the Japanese Kibō module to the International Space Station.
03-11.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

03-11.title: Sebastián Piñera
03-11.subtitle: During the inauguration of Chilean president Sebastián Piñera, earthquakes registering 6.
03-11.text: During the inauguration of Chilean president Sebastián Piñera, earthquakes registering 6.9 and 7.0 .mw-parser-output .tooltip-dotted{border-bottom:1px dotted;cursor:help}Mw struck the O'Higgins Region near the city of Pichilemu, causing widespread damage.

03-11.title: 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami
03-11.subtitle: An earthquake measuring 9.
03-11.text: An earthquake measuring 9.0 in magnitude strikes 130 km (81 mi) east of Sendai, Japan, triggering a tsunami killing thousands of people. This event also triggered the second largest nuclear accident in history, and one of only two events to be classified as a Level 7 on the International Nuclear Event Scale.

03-11.title: United States Army
03-11.subtitle: U.
03-11.text: The United States Army is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces. It is designated as the army of the United States in the United States Constitution. As a part of the United States Department of Defense, it is one of the six armed forces of the United States and one of the eight uniformed services of the United States. The Army is the most senior branch in order of precedence amongst the armed services.
03-11.location: Land service branch of the U.S. military

03-11.title: World Health Organization
03-11.subtitle: The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the COVID-19 virus epidemic a pandemic.
03-11.text: The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the COVID-19 virus epidemic a pandemic.

03-11.title: Tatmadaw
03-11.subtitle: The Burmese military kills at least 30 villagers, including 3 Buddhist monks, during the Pinlaung massacre in Shan State, Myanmar.
03-11.text: The Burmese military kills at least 30 villagers, including 3 Buddhist monks, during the Pinlaung massacre in Shan State, Myanmar.

03-12.title: Siege of Rome Ends
03-12.subtitle: The Ostrogoths retreat and Byzantium prevails
03-12.text: King Vitiges and his Ostrogothic army abandoned their year-long siege of Rome and retreated to Ravenna, leaving the Eternal City in the hands of the victorious Byzantine general Belisarius. The siege had tested Rome's ancient walls and the endurance of its defenders, but the city held. The retreat marked a turning point in the Gothic War and demonstrated that the Byzantine Empire could project power deep into the Italian peninsula.

03-12.title: Rinus van den Berge Born
03-12.subtitle: A Dutch athlete of the early century
03-12.text: Rinus van den Berge was born in the Netherlands, a future athlete whose career would unfold during one of the most turbulent periods in European history. His story, while less famous than many of his contemporaries, represents the countless sportsmen and women of the early twentieth century whose achievements were intertwined with the dramatic political and social upheavals of their times.

03-12.title: Mahatma Gandhi
03-12.subtitle: Mahatma Gandhi began the Salt March (pictured), a 24-day nonviolent walk to defy the British salt tax in colonial India.
03-12.text: Mahatma Gandhi began the Salt March (pictured), a 24-day nonviolent walk to defy the British salt tax in colonial India.

03-12.title: Battle of Java
03-12.subtitle: The Battle of Java ends with the surrender of the American-British-Dutch-Australian Command to the Empire of Japan in Ba...
03-12.text: The Battle of Java was a battle of the Pacific theatre of World War II. It occurred on the island of Java from 28 February – 12 March 1942. It involved forces from the Empire of Japan, which invaded on 28 February 1942, and Allied personnel. Allied commanders signed a formal surrender at Japanese headquarters at Bandung on 12 March.
03-12.location: Battle in the Pacific theatre of World War II

03-12.title: Suharto
03-12.subtitle: General Suharto (pictured) seized power from President Sukarno when the Provisional People's Consultative Assembly sworn...
03-12.text: Suharto was an Indonesian military officer and politician who served as the second and longest-serving president of Indonesia from 1967 to 1998. Widely regarded as a military dictator by international observers, Suharto led Indonesia as an authoritarian regime from 1967 until his resignation in 1998 following nationwide unrest.

03-12.title: Turkish Armed Forces
03-12.subtitle: The Turkish Armed Forces executed a "coup by memorandum", forcing the resignation of Prime Minister Süleyman Demirel (pictured).
03-12.text: The Turkish Armed Forces executed a "coup by memorandum", forcing the resignation of Prime Minister Süleyman Demirel (pictured).

03-12.title: North Korea
03-12.subtitle: North Korea announces that it will withdraw from the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons and refuses to a...
03-12.text: North Korea, officially the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), is a country in East Asia. It constitutes the northern half of the Korean Peninsula and borders China and Russia to the north at the Yalu (Amnok) and Tumen rivers, and South Korea to the south at the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ). The country's western border is formed by the Yellow Sea, while its eastern border is defined by the Sea of Japan.

03-12.title: Warsaw Pact
03-12.subtitle: Former Warsaw Pact members the Czech Republic, Hungary and Poland join NATO.
03-12.text: Former Warsaw Pact members the Czech Republic, Hungary and Poland join NATO.

03-12.title: Max Duggan Was Born
03-12.subtitle: A Heisman finalist born for the big stage
03-12.text: Max Duggan was born in the United States, a future college football quarterback who would capture the nation's attention with his remarkable 2022 season at TCU. Leading the Horned Frogs to the College Football Playoff National Championship game, Duggan finished as a Heisman Trophy finalist, embodying the grit and determination that makes college football America's most emotionally compelling sport.

03-12.title: President of South Korea
03-12.subtitle: President of South Korea, Roh Moo-hyun, is impeached by its National Assembly, the first such impeachment in the nation's history.
03-12.text: President of South Korea, Roh Moo-hyun, is impeached by its National Assembly, the first such impeachment in the nation's history.

03-12.title: Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant
03-12.subtitle: A reactor at the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant explodes and releases radioactivity into the atmosphere a day aft...
03-12.text: A reactor at the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant explodes and releases radioactivity into the atmosphere a day after the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami.

03-12.title: 2014 East Harlem gas explosion
03-12.subtitle: A gas leak caused an explosion in East Harlem, New York City, destroying two apartment buildings and causing eight deaths.
03-12.text: A gas leak caused an explosion in East Harlem, New York City, destroying two apartment buildings and causing eight deaths.

03-13.title: Elagabalus Murdered
03-13.subtitle: Rome's most scandalous emperor meets his end
03-13.text: Emperor Elagabalus, whose brief and tumultuous reign had scandalized even Rome's jaded aristocracy, was murdered alongside his mother Julia Soaemias by the Praetorian Guard. His body was dragged through the streets and dumped in the Tiber. The Guard replaced him with his fourteen-year-old cousin Severus Alexander, hoping for a more conventional ruler. Elagabalus's reign remains one of the most bizarre and controversial episodes in Roman imperial history.

03-13.title: British Occupy Bloemfontein
03-13.subtitle: An Orange Free State capital falls to the empire
03-13.text: British forces marched into Bloemfontein, the capital of the Orange Free State, during the Second Boer War. The occupation of this strategically important city marked a significant British advance into Boer territory and signaled the shifting momentum of the war. For the Boers, the loss of their capital was a psychological blow, though their guerrilla resistance would continue for another two years across the South African veldt.

03-13.title: Battle of Dien Bien Phu
03-13.subtitle: The Battle of Điện Biên Phủ begins with an artillery barrage by Viet Minh forces under Võ Nguyên Giáp; Viet Minh victory...
03-13.text: The Battle of Điện Biên Phủ begins with an artillery barrage by Viet Minh forces under Võ Nguyên Giáp; Viet Minh victory led to the end of the First Indochina War and French withdrawal from Vietnam.

03-13.title: Cuba
03-13.subtitle: Cuban student revolutionaries storm the presidential palace in Havana in a failed attempt on the life of President Fulgencio Batista.
03-13.text: Cuba, officially the Republic of Cuba, is an island country in the Caribbean. It comprises the eponymous main island as well as 4,195 islands, islets, and cays. Situated at the convergence of the Caribbean Sea, Gulf of Mexico, and Atlantic Ocean, Cuba is located east of the Yucatán Peninsula, south of both Florida and the Bahamas, west of Hispaniola, and north of Jamaica and the Cayman Islands. Havana is the largest city and capital.

03-13.title: New JEWEL Movement
03-13.subtitle: The New Jewel Movement, headed by Maurice Bishop, ousts the Prime Minister of Grenada, Eric Gairy, in a coup d'état.
03-13.text: The New Jewel Movement, headed by Maurice Bishop, ousts the Prime Minister of Grenada, Eric Gairy, in a coup d'état.

03-13.title: Football hooliganism
03-13.subtitle: One of England's worst incidents of football hooliganism occurred when supporters of Luton Town and Millwall rioted befo...
03-13.text: One of England's worst incidents of football hooliganism occurred when supporters of Luton Town and Millwall rioted before a match at Kenilworth Road stadium.

03-13.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
03-13.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-29 carrying the TDRS-4 satellite.
03-13.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-29 carrying the TDRS-4 satellite.

03-13.title: Dunblane massacre
03-13.subtitle: A mass shooting at a primary school occurred in Dunblane, Scotland, killing 16 children and a teacher and prompting tigh...
03-13.text: The Dunblane massacre took place at Dunblane Primary School in Dunblane, near Stirling, Scotland, on 13 March 1996, when 43-year-old Thomas Hamilton killed 16 pupils and one teacher and injured 15 others before killing himself. It remains the deadliest mass shooting in British history.

03-13.title: Beomgyu Was Born
03-13.subtitle: A K-pop star is born in South Korea
03-13.text: Beomgyu was born in Daegu, South Korea, a future singer-songwriter and member of the globally acclaimed boy band TXT, formed by Big Hit Entertainment. His guitar skills, expressive voice, and charismatic stage presence helped propel TXT to international fame. As K-pop continued its global conquest, Beomgyu emerged as one of the genre's most talented and versatile young performers.

03-13.title: Rachelle Ann Go
03-13.subtitle: The Filipino singer Rachelle Ann Go won the reality talent show Search for a Star, performing a cover of Mariah Carey's ...
03-13.text: The Filipino singer Rachelle Ann Go won the reality talent show Search for a Star, performing a cover of Mariah Carey's "Through the Rain" in the grand final.

03-13.title: 2013 conclave
03-13.subtitle: Cardinal Jorge Mario Bergoglio was elected as Pope Francis, making him the first Jesuit pope, the first from the America...
03-13.text: Cardinal Jorge Mario Bergoglio was elected as Pope Francis, making him the first Jesuit pope, the first from the Americas, and the first from the Southern Hemisphere.

03-13.title: Donald Trump
03-13.subtitle: President Donald Trump declares the COVID-19 pandemic to be a national emergency in the United States.
03-13.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
03-13.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

03-14.title: Battle of Mogyorod
03-14.subtitle: A Hungarian throne is won on the battlefield
03-14.text: At the Battle of Mogyorod, Dukes Geza and Ladislaus defeated their cousin King Solomon of Hungary, forcing him to flee the country. The battle was a decisive moment in Hungarian royal politics, settling a bitter dynastic dispute that had divided the kingdom. Ladislaus would eventually become one of Hungary's most beloved kings, canonized as a saint and remembered as a model of chivalric virtue and pious rule.

03-14.title: Gold Standard Act Signed
03-14.subtitle: America anchors its currency to gold
03-14.text: The Gold Standard Act was ratified, formally placing United States currency on the gold standard. Every dollar was now backed by a fixed quantity of gold, bringing stability and predictability to American finance at the dawn of the twentieth century. The act resolved decades of fierce debate between gold advocates and silver populists, establishing a monetary framework that would endure until the turbulent economics of the Great Depression forced its reconsideration.

03-14.title: Korean War
03-14.subtitle: Korean War: United Nations troops recapture Seoul for the second time.
03-14.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

03-14.title: United States Air Force
03-14.subtitle: A USAF B-52 bomber carrying nuclear weapons crashes near Yuba City, California.
03-14.text: A USAF B-52 bomber carrying nuclear weapons crashes near Yuba City, California.

03-14.title: Giangiacomo Feltrinelli
03-14.subtitle: Italian publisher Giangiacomo Feltrinelli, known for his translation of Boris Pasternak's novel Doctor Zhivago after it ...
03-14.text: Italian publisher Giangiacomo Feltrinelli, known for his translation of Boris Pasternak's novel Doctor Zhivago after it had been smuggled out of the Soviet Union, died in an explosion.

03-14.title: Vietnam War
03-14.subtitle: Vietnam War: After having been captured by North Vietnamese forces five and a half years earlier, U.
03-14.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
03-14.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

03-14.title: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007
03-14.subtitle: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007 crashes during final approach near Warsaw, Poland, killing 87 people, including a 14-man American boxing team.
03-14.text: LOT Polish Airlines Flight 007 crashes during final approach near Warsaw, Poland, killing 87 people, including a 14-man American boxing team.

03-14.title: Gerry Adams
03-14.subtitle: Gerry Adams, leader of Sinn Féin, was seriously wounded in an assassination attempt by Ulster Freedom Fighters in central Belfast, Northern Ireland.
03-14.text: Gerry Adams, leader of Sinn Féin, was seriously wounded in an assassination attempt by Ulster Freedom Fighters in central Belfast, Northern Ireland.

03-14.title: Johnson South Reef skirmish
03-14.subtitle: China defeated Vietnam in a naval altercation while attempting to establish oceanographic observation posts on the Spratly Islands.
03-14.text: China defeated Vietnam in a naval altercation while attempting to establish oceanographic observation posts on the Spratly Islands.

03-14.title: Norman Thagard
03-14.subtitle: Norman Thagard becomes the first American astronaut to ride to space on board a Russian launch vehicle.
03-14.text: Norman Thagard becomes the first American astronaut to ride to space on board a Russian launch vehicle.

03-14.title: Jihoon Was Born
03-14.subtitle: A South Korean voice finds its melody
03-14.text: Jihoon was born in South Korea, part of a generation of talented young performers who would fuel the global K-pop phenomenon. His journey from aspiring singer to professional artist reflects the rigorous training, dedication, and competitive spirit required to succeed in South Korea's demanding entertainment industry, a world that produces some of the most polished performers on the planet.

03-14.title: 2006 Chadian coup attempt
03-14.subtitle: The 2006 Chadian coup d'état attempt ends in failure.
03-14.text: The 2006 Chadian coup d'état attempt ends in failure.

03-14.title: Tatmadaw
03-14.subtitle: The Burmese military and police forces killed at least 65 civilians during the Hlaingthaya massacre in Yangon, including...
03-14.text: The Burmese military and police forces killed at least 65 civilians during the Hlaingthaya massacre in Yangon, including those protesting a recent coup d'état.

03-15.title: Consul Celebrates Victory
03-15.subtitle: Rome marks forty years of peace with Veii
03-15.text: Roman consul Aulus Manlius Vulso celebrated an ovation, a lesser form of triumph, for concluding the war against the Etruscan city of Veii and securing a forty-year truce. The peace with Veii gave the young Roman Republic breathing room to consolidate its power in central Italy. This early diplomatic success demonstrated that Rome could achieve its aims through negotiation as well as warfare, a balance that would serve the republic for centuries.

03-15.title: Assassination of Julius Caesar
03-15.subtitle: The assassination of Julius Caesar, the dictator of the Roman Republic, by a group of senators takes place on the Ides of March.
03-15.text: The assassination of Julius Caesar, the dictator of the Roman Republic, by a group of senators takes place on the Ides of March.

03-15.title: Treaty of Lublowa
03-15.subtitle: Treaty of Lublowa: After the Peace of Thorn, Grand Master Heinrich von Plauen asks Sigismund of Hungary for economocial aid.
03-15.text: Treaty of Lublowa: After the Peace of Thorn, Grand Master Heinrich von Plauen asks Sigismund of Hungary for economocial aid. Sigismund agrees to mediate reduction to the third installment, demarcation of the Samogitian border, and other matters - with a grand tournament. Hunts and lavish feasts were also organized. Sigismund invited, among others, polish king Wladyslaw Jagiello, Heinrich von Plauen and bosnian king Tvrtko II. There were people from 17 countries and languages - 40.

03-15.title: Gilberto Freyre Was Born
03-15.subtitle: Brazil's great interpreter of race and culture
03-15.text: Gilberto Freyre was born in Recife, Brazil, destined to become one of the twentieth century's most influential social thinkers. His masterwork, The Masters and the Slaves, offered a groundbreaking analysis of Brazilian society's formation through the intermingling of Portuguese, African, and indigenous cultures. Though later criticized for romanticizing slavery, Freyre's work fundamentally changed how Brazilians understood their own national identity.

03-15.title: Russian Revolution
03-15.subtitle: Russian Revolution: Tsar Nicholas II was forced to abdicate in the February Revolution, ending three centuries of Romanov rule.
03-15.text: Russian Revolution: Tsar Nicholas II was forced to abdicate in the February Revolution, ending three centuries of Romanov rule.

03-15.title: Ukrainian War of Independence
03-15.subtitle: Ukrainian War of Independence: The Kontrrazvedka is established as the counterintelligence division of the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.
03-15.text: Ukrainian War of Independence: The Kontrrazvedka is established as the counterintelligence division of the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.

03-15.title: Nationalization of the Iranian oil industry
03-15.subtitle: The Iranian oil industry was nationalized in a movement led by Mohammad Mosaddegh.
03-15.text: The Iranian oil industry was nationalized in a movement led by Mohammad Mosaddegh.

03-15.title: My Fair Lady
03-15.subtitle: The musical My Fair Lady, based on George Bernard Shaw's play Pygmalion, debuted at the Mark Hellinger Theatre in New York City.
03-15.text: The musical My Fair Lady, based on George Bernard Shaw's play Pygmalion, debuted at the Mark Hellinger Theatre in New York City.

03-15.title: Atlanta University Center
03-15.subtitle: Students from the Atlanta University Center, inspired by similar actions in Greensboro, North Carolina, began occupying ...
03-15.text: Students from the Atlanta University Center, inspired by similar actions in Greensboro, North Carolina, began occupying lunch counters in Atlanta, Georgia.

03-15.title: Sterling Airways Flight 901
03-15.subtitle: Fifteen people are killed when Sterling Airways Flight 901, a Sud Aviation Caravelle, catches fire following a landing g...
03-15.text: Fifteen people are killed when Sterling Airways Flight 901, a Sud Aviation Caravelle, catches fire following a landing gear collapse at Mehrabad International Airport in Tehran, Iran.

03-15.title: Cold War
03-15.subtitle: Cold War: The Treaty on the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany comes into effect, granting full sovereignty to the Federal Republic of Germany.
03-15.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
03-15.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

03-15.title: Kristian Kostov Was Born
03-15.subtitle: A Eurovision voice from two Slavic nations
03-15.text: Kristian Kostov was born in Moscow to a Bulgarian father, a future singer-songwriter who would represent Bulgaria at the Eurovision Song Contest. His powerful vocals and emotional performances at a young age demonstrated exceptional talent, earning him a devoted fanbase across Europe. Kostov's bicultural heritage, straddling Russia and Bulgaria, gave his artistry a unique depth that resonated with audiences far beyond either country.

03-16.title: Nebuchadnezzar Takes Jerusalem
03-16.subtitle: Babylon's king captures the holy city
03-16.text: King Nebuchadnezzar II of Babylon captured Jerusalem and installed Zedekiah as a puppet King of Judah. The conquest was a pivotal moment in biblical history, beginning the period of Babylonian dominion over the Jewish people that would culminate in the destruction of Solomon's Temple and the Babylonian Exile. The fall of Jerusalem echoed through the centuries, shaping Jewish identity, prophecy, and the yearning for return to Zion.

03-16.title: Cyril Hume Was Born
03-16.subtitle: A novelist who became Hollywood's secret weapon
03-16.text: Cyril Hume was born in New York, a future novelist and screenwriter who would leave his mark on Hollywood during its golden age. His scripts, which included science fiction and adventure films, demonstrated a versatility and imagination that kept audiences entertained for decades. Hume's work behind the camera helped shape some of mid-century Hollywood's most memorable productions, even as his name remained less known than the stars who spoke his words.

03-16.title: African Americans
03-16.subtitle: African American civil rights activists in Savannah, Georgia, conducted a sit-in, commencing the Savannah Protest Movement.
03-16.text: African American civil rights activists in Savannah, Georgia, conducted a sit-in, commencing the Savannah Protest Movement.

03-16.title: Gemini 8
03-16.subtitle: Launch of Gemini 8 with astronauts Neil Armstrong and David Scott.
03-16.text: Launch of Gemini 8 with astronauts Neil Armstrong and David Scott. It would perform the first docking of two spacecraft in orbit.

03-16.title: Vietnam War
03-16.subtitle: Vietnam War: My Lai massacre occurs; between 347 and 500 Vietnamese villagers are killed by American troops.
03-16.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies.
