The prototypical dwarf planet is Pluto, which for decades was regarded as a planet before the "dwarf" concept was adopted in 2006.

03-31.title: NASA
03-31.subtitle: NASA astronaut Scott Kelly and Roscosmos cosmonaut Mikhail Kornienko return to Earth after a yearlong mission at the International Space Station.
03-31.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

03-31.title: Gyumri
03-31.subtitle: Nikol Pashinyan began his protest walk, starting in the city of Gyumri, opening the 2018 Armenian revolution.
03-31.text: Nikol Pashinyan began his protest walk, starting in the city of Gyumri, opening the 2018 Armenian revolution.

03-31.title: Tornado
03-31.subtitle: A tornado causes the collapse of a venue hosting a sold-out concert in Belvidere, Illinois, United States, resulting in one death and 48 injuries.
03-31.text: A tornado causes the collapse of a venue hosting a sold-out concert in Belvidere, Illinois, United States, resulting in one death and 48 injuries.

04-01.title: Justinian Named Heir
04-01.subtitle: A nephew steps into the light of empire
04-01.text: In the grand palace of Constantinople, Emperor Justin I made a fateful decision that would reshape civilization. He elevated his brilliant nephew Justinian as co-ruler and successor to the Byzantine throne. The young man who had risen from peasant origins in the Balkans would go on to reconquer lost Roman territories, codify Roman law, and commission the Hagia Sophia -- leaving an imprint on history that endures to this day.

04-01.title: Crete's New Monarch
04-01.subtitle: A Greek prince claims a restless island
04-01.text: On the sun-drenched shores of Crete, Prince George of Greece and Denmark assumed absolute authority over the Cretan State. The second son of King George I, he became high commissioner during a turbulent era as Crete struggled between Ottoman rule and its dream of union with Greece. His reign marked a pivotal chapter in the long, winding road toward Cretan self-determination and eventual unification with the Greek homeland.

04-01.title: Television Infrared Observation Satellite
04-01.subtitle: The TIROS-1 satellite transmits the first television picture from space.
04-01.text: The TIROS-1 satellite transmits the first television picture from space.

04-01.title: On Her Majesty's Secret Service
04-01.subtitle: On Her Majesty's Secret Service, the tenth novel of Ian Fleming's James Bond series, was released in the United Kingdom.
04-01.text: On Her Majesty's Secret Service, the tenth novel of Ian Fleming's James Bond series, was released in the United Kingdom.

04-01.title: Hawker Siddeley Harrier
04-01.subtitle: The Hawker Siddeley Harrier, the first operational fighter aircraft with V/STOL capabilities, entered service with the Royal Air Force.
04-01.text: The Hawker Siddeley Harrier is a British jet-powered attack aircraft designed and produced by the British aerospace company Hawker Siddeley. It was the first operational ground attack and reconnaissance aircraft with vertical/short takeoff and landing (V/STOL) capabilities and the only truly successful V/STOL design of its era.

04-01.title: Richard Nixon
04-01.subtitle: U.
04-01.text: Richard Milhous Nixon was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974. A member of the Republican Party, he represented California in both houses of the United States Congress before serving as the 36th vice president under President Dwight D. Eisenhower from 1953 to 1961. His presidency saw the reduction of U.S.
04-01.location: President of the United States from 1969 to 1974

04-01.title: Bangladesh Liberation War
04-01.subtitle: Bangladesh Liberation War: The Pakistan Army massacre more than a thousand people in Keraniganj Upazila, Bangladesh.
04-01.text: The Bangladesh Liberation War, also known as the Bangladesh War of Independence, was an armed conflict sparked by the rise of the Bengali nationalist and self-determination movement in East Pakistan, which resulted in the independence of Bangladesh with the help of India. The war began when the Pakistani military junta based in West Pakistan—under the orders of Yahya Khan—launched Operation Searchlight against East Pakistanis on the night of 25 March 1971, initiating the Bangladesh genocide.

04-01.title: Steve Jobs
04-01.subtitle: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak found Apple Computer, Inc.
04-01.text: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak found Apple Computer, Inc.

04-01.title: Strangeways Prison riot
04-01.subtitle: The longest prison riot in British penal history began at Strangeways Prison in Manchester, lasting 25 days.
04-01.text: The longest prison riot in British penal history began at Strangeways Prison in Manchester, lasting 25 days.

04-01.title: The Hainan Collision
04-01.subtitle: Two superpowers meet at thirty thousand feet
04-01.text: Over the South China Sea near Hainan Island, an American EP-3 reconnaissance aircraft and a Chinese J-8 fighter jet collided in mid-air, igniting one of the most tense diplomatic standoffs of the early twenty-first century. The Chinese pilot was killed and the American crew was forced to make an emergency landing on Hainan, where they were detained for eleven days. The incident tested the fragile relationship between Washington and Beijing at a critical juncture.

04-02.title: Emperor Xian Was Born
04-02.subtitle: The last sovereign of a dying dynasty
04-02.text: In the waning years of the Han dynasty, a child was born who would become Emperor Xian -- the final ruler of a four-century-old imperial line. Placed on the throne as a puppet amid warring factions, he watched helplessly as warlords carved up the empire. His forced abdication in 220 AD would formally end the Han dynasty and usher in the tumultuous Three Kingdoms era, one of the most storied periods in Chinese history.

04-02.title: Roberto Arlt Was Born
04-02.subtitle: Buenos Aires found its fiercest storyteller
04-02.text: In the immigrant neighborhoods of Buenos Aires, Roberto Arlt entered a world he would later dissect with savage honesty. The son of poor European immigrants, he became one of Argentina's most original writers, crafting novels and plays that exposed the raw underbelly of urban life. His masterwork, 'The Seven Madmen,' depicted a city teeming with dreamers and schemers. Though he died at just forty-two, his literary legacy towers over Latin American letters.

04-02.title: As the World Turns
04-02.subtitle: As the World Turns and The Edge of Night premiere on CBS.
04-02.text: As the World Turns and The Edge of Night premiere on CBS. The two soaps become the first daytime dramas to debut in the 30-minute format.

04-02.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
04-02.subtitle: 2001: A Space Odyssey, the epic science fiction film produced and directed by Stanley Kubrick, premiered at the Uptown Theater in Washington, D.
04-02.text: 2001: A Space Odyssey, the epic science fiction film produced and directed by Stanley Kubrick, premiered at the Uptown Theater in Washington, D.C.

04-02.title: Spore
04-02.subtitle: Spores of anthrax were accidentally released from a military research facility near the city of Sverdlovsk, causing at least 68 deaths.
04-02.text: Spores of anthrax were accidentally released from a military research facility near the city of Sverdlovsk, causing at least 68 deaths.

04-02.title: Bijeljina massacre
04-02.subtitle: Forty-two civilians are massacred in the town of Bijeljina in Bosnia and Herzegovina.
04-02.text: Forty-two civilians are massacred in the town of Bijeljina in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

04-02.title: Rodrigo Riquelme Was Born
04-02.subtitle: A future star emerges in Madrid
04-02.text: In the football-mad heart of Spain, Rodrigo Riquelme Reche was born into a world where the beautiful game is a way of life. Rising through the youth academy of Atletico Madrid, he developed into a dynamic winger with electric pace and creative vision. His journey from the streets of Madrid to the top flight of Spanish football represents the enduring power of the sport to transform young lives into something extraordinary.

04-02.title: Second Intifada
04-02.subtitle: Second Intifada: Palestinian militants sought refuge from advancing Israeli forces in the Church of the Nativity in Beth...
04-02.text: The Second Intifada, also known as the Al-Aqsa Intifada, was a major uprising by Palestinians against Israel and its occupation from 2000. Initially sparked by civilian protests in Jerusalem and areas within Israel proper, the uprising escalated into a prolonged period of heightened violence in Israel, the West Bank and the Gaza Strip. This violence, which included shootings, suicide bombings, and military operations, continued until the Sharm el-Sheikh Summit of 2005, which ended hostilities.

04-02.title: India national cricket team
04-02.subtitle: India wins the Cricket World Cup for the second time in history under the captaincy of MS Dhoni.
04-02.text: India wins the Cricket World Cup for the second time in history under the captaincy of MS Dhoni.

04-02.title: Hatton Garden safe deposit burglary
04-02.subtitle: Four men steal items worth up to £200 million from an underground safe deposit facility in London's Hatton Garden area i...
04-02.text: Four men steal items worth up to £200 million from an underground safe deposit facility in London's Hatton Garden area in what has been called the "largest burglary in English legal history."

04-03.title: A Maya King Crowned
04-03.subtitle: The jungle throne of Calakmul awaits
04-03.text: Deep in the rainforests of the Yucatan Peninsula, Yuknoom Yich'aak K'ahk' ascended to the throne of Calakmul, one of the most powerful Maya city-states. His coronation came during a golden age of Maya civilization, when great cities competed for dominance through warfare, diplomacy, and monumental architecture. The new king would lead his people through an era of intense rivalry with neighboring Tikal, shaping the political landscape of ancient Mesoamerica.

04-03.title: Camille Chamoun Was Born
04-03.subtitle: A leader forged in Lebanon's crucible
04-03.text: In the mountains of Lebanon, Camille Nimr Chamoun was born into a land of ancient cedars and complex sectarian politics. He would rise to become president during one of Lebanon's most turbulent decades, navigating Cold War pressures and regional upheaval. A dominant Christian political figure, he weathered the Suez Crisis and shaped Lebanese politics for generations, remaining influential through the devastating civil war that would later consume his country.

04-03.title: Martin Cooper
04-03.subtitle: Martin Cooper of Motorola makes the first handheld mobile phone call to Joel S.
04-03.text: Martin Cooper of Motorola makes the first handheld mobile phone call to Joel S. Engel of Bell Labs.

04-03.title: 1974 Super Outbreak
04-03.subtitle: The 1974 Super Outbreak occurs, the second largest tornado outbreak in recorded history (after the 2011 Super Outbreak).
04-03.text: The 1974 Super Outbreak occurs, the second largest tornado outbreak in recorded history (after the 2011 Super Outbreak). The death toll is 315, with nearly 5,500 injured.

04-03.title: Bobby Fischer
04-03.subtitle: Bobby Fischer refuses to play in a chess match against Anatoly Karpov, giving Karpov the title of World Champion by default.
04-03.text: Bobby Fischer refuses to play in a chess match against Anatoly Karpov, giving Karpov the title of World Champion by default.

04-03.title: Osborne 1
04-03.subtitle: The Osborne 1, the first successful portable computer, is unveiled at the West Coast Computer Faire in San Francisco.
04-03.text: The Osborne 1, the first successful portable computer, is unveiled at the West Coast Computer Faire in San Francisco.

04-03.title: Boeing T-43
04-03.subtitle: A U.
04-03.text: A U.S. Air Force CT-43 crashed into a mountainside while attempting an instrument approach to Dubrovnik Airport in Croatia, killing all 35 people on board, including Secretary of Commerce Ron Brown.

04-03.title: Microsoft Found Guilty
04-03.subtitle: The tech giant meets its legal reckoning
04-03.text: In a Washington courtroom, a federal judge delivered a landmark ruling that sent shockwaves through Silicon Valley. Microsoft was found to have violated antitrust law by bundling Internet Explorer with its Windows operating system, effectively crushing competitor Netscape. The decision marked a turning point in how governments would regulate technology monopolies, setting precedents that continue to shape the digital landscape decades later.

04-03.title: Apple Inc.
04-03.subtitle: Apple Inc.
04-03.text: Apple Inc. is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Cupertino, California, in Silicon Valley, best known for its consumer electronics, software and online services. Founded in 1976 as Apple Computer Company by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak and Ronald Wayne, the company was incorporated by Jobs and Wozniak as Apple Computer, Inc. the following year. It was renamed to its current name in 2007 as the company had expanded its focus from computers to consumer electronics.

04-03.title: Buenos Aires Province
04-03.subtitle: The northeastern section of Buenos Aires Province, Argentina, experienced several flash floods that killed at least 100 people.
04-03.text: The northeastern section of Buenos Aires Province, Argentina, experienced several flash floods that killed at least 100 people.

04-03.title: Panama Papers
04-03.subtitle: The first news stories on the Panama Papers were published, revealing that shell corporations represented by the Panaman...
04-03.text: The first news stories on the Panama Papers were published, revealing that shell corporations represented by the Panamanian law firm Mossack Fonseca had been used for illegal purposes.

04-04.title: Rome Triumphs Over Sabines
04-04.subtitle: A consul celebrates on sacred ground
04-04.text: In the early days of the Roman Republic, consul Agrippa Menenius Lanatus led his legions to victory against the Sabines, the fierce hill people who had long contested Roman expansion. Returning to Rome in triumph, he paraded through the streets in a ceremony that affirmed the Republic's growing military might. This victory helped consolidate Roman control over central Italy and demonstrated the young republic's capacity to project power beyond its walls.

04-04.title: Stanley Weinbaum Was Born
04-04.subtitle: Science fiction would never be the same
04-04.text: In Louisville, Kentucky, Stanley Grauman Weinbaum entered a world that had not yet imagined the futures he would create. His 1934 story 'A Martian Odyssey' revolutionized science fiction by introducing Tweel -- the first truly alien alien in literature, a creature that thinks but not like a human. Though lung cancer claimed him at just thirty-three, his brief career blazed a trail that writers like Asimov and Clarke would follow for decades.

04-04.title: Civil rights movement
04-04.subtitle: American civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr.
04-04.text: The civil rights movement was a social movement in the United States from 1954 to 1968 which aimed to abolish legalized racial segregation, discrimination, and disenfranchisement in the country, which most commonly affected African Americans. The movement had origins in the Reconstruction era in the late 19th century, and modern roots in the 1940s.

04-04.title: World Trade Center
04-04.subtitle: A year after the completion of the second of the complex's twin towers, the World Trade Center in New York City was officially dedicated.
04-04.text: A year after the completion of the second of the complex's twin towers, the World Trade Center in New York City was officially dedicated.

04-04.title: Microsoft
04-04.subtitle: Microsoft is founded as a partnership between Bill Gates and Paul Allen in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
04-04.text: Microsoft is founded as a partnership between Bill Gates and Paul Allen in Albuquerque, New Mexico.

04-04.title: Space Shuttle program
04-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Challenger makes its maiden voyage into space on STS-6.
04-04.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

04-04.title: Evan Mecham
04-04.subtitle: Evan Mecham, Governor of Arizona, was removed from office after being convicted in his impeachment trial.
04-04.text: Evan Mecham, Governor of Arizona, was removed from office after being convicted in his impeachment trial.

04-04.title: John Heinz
04-04.subtitle: Senator John Heinz of Pennsylvania and six others are killed when a helicopter collides with their airplane over an elem...
04-04.text: Senator John Heinz of Pennsylvania and six others are killed when a helicopter collides with their airplane over an elementary school in Merion, Pennsylvania.

04-04.title: Space Shuttle program
04-04.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-83.
04-04.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

04-04.title: Anzor Alem Was Born
04-04.subtitle: A Congolese voice rises through art
04-04.text: In the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Rashidi Alema Anzor was born into a nation rich with creative energy. He would grow to become a celebrated actor and singer, earning recognition for roles in films like 'Baby Boy of the House' and 'Ima.' His brief appearance alongside Idris Elba in the thriller 'Beast' brought him international attention, making him a symbol of the vibrant and expanding Congolese entertainment industry.

04-04.title: MPLA
04-04.subtitle: The MPLA government of Angola and UNITA rebels sign a peace treaty ending the Angolan Civil War.
04-04.text: The MPLA government of Angola and UNITA rebels sign a peace treaty ending the Angolan Civil War.

04-05.title: Lothair Crowned in Italy
04-05.subtitle: The Pope anoints a Carolingian king
04-05.text: In the sacred halls of Rome, Pope Paschal I placed the crown of Italy upon the head of young Lothair I, son of Emperor Louis the Pious. This coronation cemented the Carolingian grip on the Italian peninsula and set in motion decades of dynastic struggle that would eventually fracture Charlemagne's great empire. Lothair would go on to rule as emperor, but his kingdom would be divided among his sons, reshaping the map of medieval Europe.

04-05.title: Spencer Tracy Was Born
04-05.subtitle: Hollywood's most natural actor arrives
04-05.text: In Milwaukee, Wisconsin, Spencer Bonaventure Tracy was born -- the man who would become Hollywood's gold standard for naturalistic acting. Over a career spanning seventy-five films, he won two consecutive Best Actor Oscars and earned nine nominations in total. His understated style and emotional depth made him the actor other actors admired most. From 'Captains Courageous' to 'Guess Who's Coming to Dinner,' Tracy proved that the greatest performances are the ones that look effortless.

04-05.title: Ripple Rock
04-05.subtitle: Ripple Rock, an underwater threat to navigation in the Seymour Narrows in Canada is destroyed in one of the largest non-...
04-05.text: Ripple Rock, an underwater threat to navigation in the Seymour Narrows in Canada is destroyed in one of the largest non-nuclear controlled explosions of the time.

04-05.title: Buddhist Uprising
04-05.subtitle: During the Buddhist Uprising, South Vietnamese military prime minister Nguyễn Cao Kỳ personally attempted to lead the ca...
04-05.text: During the Buddhist Uprising, South Vietnamese military prime minister Nguyễn Cao Kỳ personally attempted to lead the capture of the restive city of Đà Nẵng before backing down.

04-05.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
04-05.subtitle: The Space shuttle Atlantis launches on STS-37 to deploy the Compton Gamma Ray Observatory.
04-05.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

04-05.title: Suada and Olga bridge
04-05.subtitle: Peace protesters Suada Dilberovic and Olga Sučić are killed on the Vrbanja Bridge in Sarajevo, becoming the first casualties of the Bosnian War.
04-05.text: Peace protesters Suada Dilberovic and Olga Sučić are killed on the Vrbanja Bridge in Sarajevo, becoming the first casualties of the Bosnian War.

04-05.title: Akashi Kaikyo Bridge
04-05.subtitle: The Akashi Kaikyo Bridge (pictured), then the world's longest suspension bridge, linking Awaji Island and Kobe in Japan, opened to traffic.
04-05.text: The Akashi Kaikyo Bridge (pictured), then the world's longest suspension bridge, linking Awaji Island and Kobe in Japan, opened to traffic.

04-05.title: Tragedy in Istanbul
04-05.subtitle: Football rivalry turns deadly on the streets
04-05.text: On the eve of a UEFA Cup semifinal in Istanbul, the festive atmosphere between Leeds United and Galatasaray supporters erupted into horrific violence. Two English fans were stabbed to death in the streets, transforming a sporting occasion into an international tragedy. The killings sent shockwaves through European football, led to Galatasaray fans being banned from the return leg in England, and raised urgent questions about fan safety that continue to resonate.

04-05.title: North Korea
04-05.subtitle: North Korea launches its controversial Kwangmyŏngsŏng-2 satellite.
04-05.text: North Korea, officially the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), is a country in East Asia. It constitutes the northern half of the Korean Peninsula and borders China and Russia to the north at the Yalu (Amnok) and Tumen rivers, and South Korea to the south at the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ). The country's western border is formed by the Yellow Sea, while its eastern border is defined by the Sea of Japan.

04-05.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
04-05.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-131 to resupply the International Space Station.
04-05.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-131 to resupply the International Space Station.

04-06.title: Caesar Wins at Thapsus
04-06.subtitle: The Republic's last defenders fall in Africa
04-06.text: On the sunbaked plains of North Africa, Julius Caesar's veteran legions crushed the combined forces of Metellus Scipio and Cato the Younger at the Battle of Thapsus. The victory was decisive and brutal -- Caesar's troops, ignoring his calls for mercy, slaughtered thousands of enemy soldiers. Cato, the Republic's most famous champion, chose suicide rather than submission. With this battle, Caesar effectively ended all organized resistance to his rule over Rome.

04-06.title: Leo Robin Was Born
04-06.subtitle: The man behind the melodies we hum
04-06.text: In Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, Leo Robin was born into a world that he would fill with unforgettable songs. As a lyricist and composer, he crafted some of the twentieth century's most enduring popular music, including the Oscar-winning 'Thanks for the Memory' and the iconic 'Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend.' His words were sung by Bob Hope, Marilyn Monroe, and countless others, weaving themselves into the fabric of American culture.

04-06.title: Intelsat I
04-06.subtitle: Launch of Early Bird, the first commercial communications satellite to be placed in geosynchronous orbit.
04-06.text: Launch of Early Bird, the first commercial communications satellite to be placed in geosynchronous orbit.

04-06.title: California Highway Patrol
04-06.subtitle: Four California Highway Patrol officers were killed in a shootout following a traffic stop in Newhall, north of Los Angeles.
04-06.text: Four California Highway Patrol officers were killed in a shootout following a traffic stop in Newhall, north of Los Angeles.

04-06.title: California Jam
04-06.subtitle: The first California Jam festival takes place at the Ontario Motor Speedway in Ontario, California.
04-06.text: The first California Jam festival takes place at the Ontario Motor Speedway in Ontario, California. Co-headlined by Deep Purple and Emerson, Lake & Palmer. The festival set what were then records for the loudest amplification system ever installed, the highest paid attendance, and highest gross in history.

04-06.title: Gaafar Nimeiry
04-06.subtitle: Sudanese President Gaafar Nimeiry is ousted from power in a coup d'état led by Field Marshal Abdel Rahman Swar al-Dahab.
04-06.text: Sudanese President Gaafar Nimeiry is ousted from power in a coup d'état led by Field Marshal Abdel Rahman Swar al-Dahab.

04-06.title: Juvénal Habyarimana
04-06.subtitle: The aircraft carrying Rwandan president Juvénal Habyarimana and Burundian president Cyprien Ntaryamira was shot down in ...
04-06.text: Juvénal Habyarimana was a Rwandan politician and military officer who was the second president of Rwanda, from 1973 until his assassination in 1994. He was nicknamed Kinani, a Kinyarwanda word meaning "invincible".

04-06.title: Shaheen Afridi Was Born
04-06.subtitle: Pakistan's pace sensation enters the world
04-06.text: In the tribal regions of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Shaheen Shah Afridi was born into a cricket-obsessed nation hungry for its next great fast bowler. By eighteen, he was terrorizing international batting lineups with his searing left-arm pace. Named ICC Cricketer of the Year in 2021, he became the first Pakistani to win the Sir Garfield Sobers Trophy, leading Lahore Qalandars to three consecutive PSL titles and cementing his place among cricket's elite.

04-06.title: Rolandas Paksas
04-06.subtitle: Rolandas Paksas becomes the first president of Lithuania to be peacefully removed from office by impeachment.
04-06.text: Rolandas Paksas becomes the first president of Lithuania to be peacefully removed from office by impeachment.

04-06.title: Kurds
04-06.subtitle: Kurdish leader Jalal Talabani (pictured) was elected by the National Assembly as the first non-Arab president of Iraq.
04-06.text: Kurdish leader Jalal Talabani (pictured) was elected by the National Assembly as the first non-Arab president of Iraq.

04-06.title: 2009 L'Aquila earthquake
04-06.subtitle: An earthquake measuring 6.
04-06.text: An earthquake measuring 6.3 Mw struck near L'Aquila in the central Italian region of Abruzzo, killing 308 people and leaving more than 66,000 homeless.

04-06.title: Naxalite–Maoist insurgency
04-06.subtitle: Naxalite–Maoist insurgency: Insurgents from the Communist Party of India (Maoist) ambushed a Central Reserve Police Forc...
04-06.text: Naxalite–Maoist insurgency: Insurgents from the Communist Party of India (Maoist) ambushed a Central Reserve Police Force convoy in Dantewada district, India, killing 76 officers.

04-07.title: Attila Destroys Metz
04-07.subtitle: The Scourge of God descends on Gaul
04-07.text: Under a darkened sky, Attila the Hun and his vast horde descended upon the Roman city of Metz in Gaul, leaving nothing but ashes and corpses in their wake. The city's inhabitants were slaughtered nearly to the last, its buildings put to the torch. This devastating assault was part of Attila's terrifying campaign across Western Europe, a rampage that earned him the epithet 'the Scourge of God' and sent tremors of fear throughout the crumbling Roman world.

04-07.title: Adolf Dymsza Was Born
04-07.subtitle: Poland's king of laughter takes his first breath
04-07.text: In Warsaw, Adolf Dymsza was born -- the man who would become the most beloved comic actor of interwar Poland. Performing in cabarets like the legendary Qui Pro Quo alongside Kazimierz Krukowski as the duo Lopek and Florek, he mastered the art of making audiences laugh until they wept. His film comedies defined an era of Polish cinema. Director Andrzej Wajda later called Dymsza a symbol of pre-war Polish cinema itself.

04-07.title: AT&T Corporation
04-07.subtitle: AT&T engineer Herbert Ives transmits the first long-distance public television broadcast (from Washington, D.
04-07.text: AT&T Corporation, an abbreviation of its former name, the American Telephone and Telegraph Company, was an American telecommunications company that provided voice, video, data, and Internet telecommunications and professional services to businesses, consumers, and government agencies.

04-07.title: Bruce W. Klunder
04-07.subtitle: American Presbyterian minister Bruce W.
04-07.text: American Presbyterian minister Bruce W. Klunder was killed by a bulldozer while protesting the construction of a segregated school in Cleveland, Ohio.

04-07.title: STS-6
04-07.subtitle: During STS-6, astronauts Story Musgrave and Don Peterson perform the first Space Shuttle spacewalk.
04-07.text: STS-6 was the sixth NASA Space Shuttle mission and the maiden flight of the Space Shuttle Challenger. Launched from Kennedy Space Center on April 4, 1983, the mission deployed the first Tracking and Data Relay Satellite, TDRS-1, into orbit, before landing at Edwards Air Force Base on April 9, 1983. STS-6 was the first Space Shuttle mission during which a Extravehicular activity was conducted, and hence was the first in which the Extravehicular Mobility Unit (EMU) was used.
04-07.location: 1983 American crewed spaceflight and maiden flight of Space Shuttle Challenger

04-07.title: Rwandan Civil War
04-07.subtitle: Rwandan Civil War: The Rwandan genocide began a few hours after the assassination of President Juvénal Habyarimana, with...
04-07.text: Rwandan Civil War: The Rwandan genocide began a few hours after the assassination of President Juvénal Habyarimana, with hundreds of thousands killed in the following 100 days.

04-07.title: First Chechen War
04-07.subtitle: First Chechen War: Russian paramilitary troops begin a massacre of civilians in Samashki, Chechnya.
04-07.text: The First Chechen War, also referred to as the First Russo-Chechen War, was a conflict between the separatist Chechen Republic of Ichkeria and the Russian Federation from 1994 to 1996. The conflict ended in a peace treaty that saw Russian forces withdraw from the territory only for them to invade again three years later sparking the Second Chechen War of 1999–2009.
04-07.location: 1994–1996 war between Russia and Chechen separatists

04-07.title: Mars Odyssey Launches
04-07.subtitle: A spacecraft begins its record-breaking vigil
04-07.text: From the launchpad at Cape Canaveral, NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey rocket soared into the Florida sky, beginning a journey that would make history. The spacecraft entered Mars orbit later that year and began mapping the Red Planet's surface, detecting water ice beneath its dusty exterior. Decades later, it remains the longest-surviving continually active spacecraft orbiting another planet -- a testament to human engineering and our relentless curiosity about our cosmic neighbors.

04-07.title: Israel Defense Forces
04-07.subtitle: The Israel Defense Forces use their Iron Dome missile system to successfully intercept a BM-21 Grad launched from Gaza, ...
04-07.text: The Israel Defense Forces use their Iron Dome missile system to successfully intercept a BM-21 Grad launched from Gaza, marking the first short-range missile intercept ever.

04-07.title: 2017 Stockholm truck attack
04-07.subtitle: A hijacker deliberately drove a truck into crowds along Drottninggatan in Stockholm, Sweden, killing five people.
04-07.text: A hijacker deliberately drove a truck into crowds along Drottninggatan in Stockholm, Sweden, killing five people.

04-08.title: Caracalla Assassinated
04-08.subtitle: An emperor falls on a roadside in Mesopotamia
04-08.text: Near the ancient city of Harran in Mesopotamia, Roman Emperor Caracalla stopped to relieve himself during a march -- and was struck down by a single sword blow from a disgruntled soldier. His Praetorian Guard prefect Macrinus, widely suspected of orchestrating the murder, seized the purple for himself. Caracalla's brutal reign, marked by the massacre of thousands in Alexandria and extravagant public baths, ended as violently as it had been lived.

04-08.title: Marie Byles Was Born
04-08.subtitle: A trailblazer for women in law and wilderness
04-08.text: In the English countryside, Marie Beuzeville Byles was born -- destined to become the first practicing female solicitor in New South Wales, Australia. But her ambitions ranged far beyond the courtroom. She became a pioneering mountaineer, an ardent conservationist, and one of Australia's earliest Buddhists. Her life was a tapestry of firsts: scaling unclimbed peaks, protecting bushland from development, and opening doors that had been closed to women for centuries.

04-08.title: World War I
04-08.subtitle: World War I: German naval officer Wilhelm Werner murdered 19 captured crew members of the British cargo ship Torrington ...
04-08.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

04-08.title: World War II
04-08.subtitle: World War II: The United States Army Air Forces flew its first mission from India over the Hump (the eastern end of the ...
04-08.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

04-08.title: Computer science
04-08.subtitle: A team of computer scientists and others met to discuss the creation of a common business-oriented programming language, which became COBOL.
04-08.text: A team of computer scientists and others met to discuss the creation of a common business-oriented programming language, which became COBOL.

04-08.title: BOAC Flight 712
04-08.subtitle: BOAC Flight 712 experienced an engine fire shortly after take-off from London Heathrow, leading to the deaths of five pe...
04-08.text: BOAC Flight 712 experienced an engine fire shortly after take-off from London Heathrow, leading to the deaths of five people on board, including flight attendant Jane Harrison, who was posthumously awarded a George Cross for heroism.

04-08.title: New Democracy
04-08.subtitle: The conservative New Democracy party of Constantine Mitsotakis is elected in the Greek parliamentary election.
04-08.text: The conservative New Democracy party of Constantine Mitsotakis is elected in the Greek parliamentary election.

04-08.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
04-08.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on mission STS-56.
04-08.text: The Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on mission STS-56.

04-08.title: Atlantis Reaches the ISS
04-08.subtitle: A shuttle carries the station's backbone to orbit
04-08.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis thundered off the launchpad at Kennedy Space Center on mission STS-110, carrying the S0 truss -- the central backbone of the International Space Station -- into orbit. During the mission, astronaut Jerry Ross made history by becoming the first person to fly on seven spaceflights. The installation of the truss was a critical milestone in the station's construction, providing the framework for the solar arrays that power humanity's orbital outpost.
04-08.location: Kennedy Space Center, Florida, United States

04-08.title: Barack Obama
04-08.subtitle: U.
04-08.text: Barack Hussein Obama II is an American politician who served as the 44th president of the United States from 2009 to 2017. A member of the Democratic Party, he was the first African American president. Obama previously served as a U.S. senator representing Illinois from 2005 to 2008 and as an Illinois state senator from 1997 to 2004.
04-08.location: President of the United States from 2009 to 2017

04-08.title: Solar eclipse of April 8, 2024
04-08.subtitle: Solar eclipse of April 8, 2024: A total solar eclipse takes place at the Moon's ascending node, visible across North America.
04-08.text: Solar eclipse of April 8, 2024: A total solar eclipse takes place at the Moon's ascending node, visible across North America.

04-09.title: Severus Seizes Power
04-09.subtitle: A general's legions proclaim a new emperor
04-09.text: At the frontier fortress of Carnuntum in modern-day Austria, the legions of Pannonia raised their general Septimius Severus on their shields and proclaimed him emperor of Rome. It was the Year of the Five Emperors, a chaotic period that saw five men claim the imperial title in rapid succession. Severus would march on Rome, defeat his rivals one by one, and found a dynasty that would rule the empire for four decades.

04-09.title: Allen Jenkins Was Born
04-09.subtitle: A face America would know and love
04-09.text: In New York City, Allen Curtis Jenkins entered a world of vaudeville stages and flickering silent films. He would grow into one of Hollywood's most recognizable character actors, playing tough-talking sidekicks and lovable mugs across decades of film, stage, and television. Modern audiences may know him best as the voice of Officer Charlie Dibble in the animated series 'Top Cat,' a role that brought his distinctive New York charm to a new generation.

04-09.title: Hugo Ballivián
04-09.subtitle: Hugo Ballivián's government is overthrown by the Bolivian National Revolution, starting a period of agrarian reform, uni...
04-09.text: Hugo Ballivián's government is overthrown by the Bolivian National Revolution, starting a period of agrarian reform, universal suffrage and the nationalization of tin mines

04-09.title: Project Mercury
04-09.subtitle: Project Mercury: NASA announces the selection of the United States' first seven astronauts, whom the news media quickly dub the "Mercury Seven".
04-09.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

04-09.title: Hendrik Verwoerd
04-09.subtitle: Dr.
04-09.text: Dr. Hendrik Verwoerd, Prime Minister of South Africa and architect of apartheid, narrowly survives an assassination attempt by a white farmer, David Pratt in Johannesburg.

04-09.title: EMD F40PH
04-09.subtitle: The first EMD F40PH, Amtrak's most widespread diesel locomotive for many years, entered into service.
04-09.text: The first EMD F40PH, Amtrak's most widespread diesel locomotive for many years, entered into service.

04-09.title: Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr
04-09.subtitle: Iraqi philosopher Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr (pictured) and his sister Amina were executed by the regime of Saddam Hussein.
04-09.text: Iraqi philosopher Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr (pictured) and his sister Amina were executed by the regime of Saddam Hussein.

04-09.title: Tbilisi massacre
04-09.subtitle: Tbilisi massacre: An anti-Soviet peaceful demonstration and hunger strike in Tbilisi, demanding restoration of Georgian ...
04-09.text: Tbilisi massacre: An anti-Soviet peaceful demonstration and hunger strike in Tbilisi, demanding restoration of Georgian independence, is dispersed by the Soviet Army, resulting in 20 deaths and hundreds of injuries.

04-09.title: Space Shuttle program
04-09.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on STS-59.
04-09.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

04-09.title: Tiago Djalo Was Born
04-09.subtitle: A defender shaped by two footballing cultures
04-09.text: Born in Portugal to a family with roots in Guinea-Bissau, Tiago Emanuel Embalo Djalo grew up straddling two rich footballing cultures. He developed into a commanding centre-back, rising through the ranks of European football with spells at major clubs. His physical presence and composure on the ball earned him caps for the Portuguese national team, embodying the multicultural talent pipeline that has made Portugal a powerhouse of modern football.

04-09.title: Invasion of Iraq
04-09.subtitle: Invasion of Iraq: Coalition forces captured Baghdad and the statue of Saddam Hussein in Firdos Square was toppled.
04-09.text: Invasion of Iraq: Coalition forces captured Baghdad and the statue of Saddam Hussein in Firdos Square was toppled.

04-09.title: Charles III
04-09.subtitle: Charles, Prince of Wales, married Camilla Parker Bowles in a civil ceremony at the Windsor Guildhall.
04-09.text: Charles, Prince of Wales, married Camilla Parker Bowles in a civil ceremony at the Windsor Guildhall.

04-09.title: Myanmar civil war
04-09.subtitle: Myanmar civil war: Burmese military and police forces killed at least 82 civilians in the Bago massacre, including peopl...
04-09.text: The Myanmar civil war began following the military coup on 1 February 2021. The seizure of power triggered mass anti-coup demonstrations and a violent crackdown by the Tatmadaw, which significantly escalated the country's longstanding insurgencies.
04-09.location: Ongoing armed conflict in Southeast Asia

04-10.title: Theodosius II Was Born
04-10.subtitle: An infant emperor who shaped Roman law
04-10.text: In Constantinople, the infant Theodosius II was born into the purple, destined to become emperor before he could walk. Proclaimed Augustus as a baby, he would reign for nearly half a century -- the longest tenure of any Eastern Roman emperor. His lasting legacy was the Theodosian Code, a comprehensive compilation of Roman law, and the mighty walls of Constantinople that would protect the city for a thousand years against every invader until 1453.

04-10.title: Arnold Beckman Was Born
04-10.subtitle: The inventor who revolutionized chemistry
04-10.text: In the small town of Cullom, Illinois, Arnold Orville Beckman was born -- a quiet genius who would transform the practice of science. As a professor at Caltech, he invented the pH meter in 1934, a device that revolutionized chemistry and biology. He went on to develop the DU spectrophotometer, hailed as the most important instrument in bioscience history. His funding of Shockley Semiconductor Laboratory helped give rise to Silicon Valley itself.

04-10.title: United States Navy
04-10.subtitle: The U.
04-10.text: The U.S. Navy nuclear submarine USS Thresher (SSN-593) sank with all hands lost during deep-diving tests in the North Atlantic Ocean.

04-10.title: Paul McCartney
04-10.subtitle: Paul McCartney announces that he is leaving The Beatles for personal and professional reasons.
04-10.text: Paul McCartney announces that he is leaving The Beatles for personal and professional reasons.

04-10.title: Yinqueshan Han Tombs Bamboo Slips Museum
04-10.subtitle: Tombs containing bamboo slips, among them Sun Tzu's Art of War and Sun Bin's lost military treatise, are discovered by c...
04-10.text: Tombs containing bamboo slips, among them Sun Tzu's Art of War and Sun Bin's lost military treatise, are discovered by construction workers in Shandong.

04-10.title: Invicta International Airlines Flight 435
04-10.subtitle: In the deadliest aviation accident in Swiss history, Invicta International Airlines Flight 435 crashed into a hillside n...
04-10.text: In the deadliest aviation accident in Swiss history, Invicta International Airlines Flight 435 crashed into a hillside near Hochwald, killing 108 people of 145 on board.

04-10.title: South Atlantic tropical cyclone
04-10.subtitle: A rare tropical storm develops in the South Atlantic Ocean near Angola; the first to be documented by satellites.
04-10.text: A rare tropical storm develops in the South Atlantic Ocean near Angola; the first to be documented by satellites.

04-10.title: First Nagorno-Karabakh War
04-10.subtitle: First Nagorno-Karabakh War: Dozens of Armenian civilians were massacred in the village of Maraga by Azerbaijani forces.
04-10.text: The First Nagorno-Karabakh War was an ethnic and territorial conflict that took place from February 1988 to May 1994 in the enclave of Nagorno-Karabakh in southwestern Azerbaijan between the majority ethnic Armenians of Nagorno-Karabakh backed by Armenia, and the Republic of Azerbaijan with support from Turkey. As the war progressed, Armenia and Azerbaijan, both former Soviet republics, entangled themselves in protracted, undeclared mountain warfare in the mountainous heights of Karabakh as Azerbaijan attempted to curb the Karabakh Movement.

04-10.title: Fidias Panayiotou Was Born
04-10.subtitle: From YouTube fame to European Parliament
04-10.text: On the Mediterranean island of Cyprus, Fidias Panayiotou was born into the digital age. He would become one of the internet's most unlikely political stories, leveraging his massive YouTube following into an independent candidacy for the European Parliament -- and winning. His election in 2024 stunned the political establishment and sparked global debate about influencer culture, direct democracy, and the future of political participation in the social media era.

04-10.title: President of Fiji
04-10.subtitle: President of Fiji Ratu Josefa Iloilo announces the abrogation of the constitution and assumes all governance in the coun...
04-10.text: President of Fiji Ratu Josefa Iloilo announces the abrogation of the constitution and assumes all governance in the country, creating a constitutional crisis.

04-11.title: Septimius Severus Was Born
04-11.subtitle: Africa's first emperor draws his first breath
04-11.text: In the North African city of Leptis Magna, Septimius Severus was born into a prominent Punic-Roman family. He would become the first emperor of African origin to rule Rome, seizing power during the chaotic Year of the Five Emperors and founding a dynasty that lasted four decades. A ruthless military commander and ambitious builder, he expanded the empire's borders, reformed its army, and left his mark on Rome with the triumphal arch that still stands in the Forum.

04-11.title: Sandor Marai Was Born
04-11.subtitle: Hungary's exiled literary conscience
04-11.text: In the city of Kassa in the Kingdom of Hungary, Sandor Marai was born -- a writer whose elegant prose would capture the twilight of Central European civilization. His novels, particularly 'Embers,' explored the decay of the old Austro-Hungarian world with devastating precision. Forced into exile by the communist regime, he spent decades in obscurity before being rediscovered as one of the twentieth century's greatest European novelists.

04-11.title: Harry S. Truman
04-11.subtitle: U.
04-11.text: U.S. president Harry S. Truman relieved General of the Army Douglas MacArthur of his commands for making public statements about the Korean War that contradicted the administration's policies.

04-11.title: Air India
04-11.subtitle: The Air India Kashmir Princess is bombed and crashes in a failed assassination attempt on Zhou Enlai by the Kuomintang.
04-11.text: Air India is the flag carrier of India with its main hub at Indira Gandhi International Airport in Delhi, and secondary hubs at Kempegowda International Airport in Bengaluru and Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport in Mumbai, alongside several focus cities across India. Headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana, the airline is owned by Air India Limited, which is owned by the Tata Group (74.9%) and Singapore Airlines (25.1%).

04-11.title: Pope John XXIII
04-11.subtitle: Pope John XXIII issues Pacem in terris, the first encyclical addressed to all Christians instead of only Catholics, and ...
04-11.text: Pope John XXIII issues Pacem in terris, the first encyclical addressed to all Christians instead of only Catholics, and which described the conditions for world peace in human terms.

04-11.title: On the Art of the Cinema
04-11.subtitle: On the Art of the Cinema, a treatise on film propaganda in support of the ruling Workers' Party of Korea written by the ...
04-11.text: On the Art of the Cinema, a treatise on film propaganda in support of the ruling Workers' Party of Korea written by the future North Korean leader Kim Jong Il, was published.

04-11.title: Uganda–Tanzania War
04-11.subtitle: Uganda–Tanzania War: The allied forces of Tanzania and the Uganda National Liberation Front captured the capital Kampala...
04-11.text: Uganda–Tanzania War: The allied forces of Tanzania and the Uganda National Liberation Front captured the capital Kampala, deposing Ugandan president Idi Amin.

04-11.title: Milly Alcock Was Born
04-11.subtitle: A future queen of dragons takes her first bow
04-11.text: In Sydney, Australia, Amelia May Alcock was born with no idea she would one day command dragons on screen. After honing her craft in Australian television, she burst onto the global stage as young Rhaenyra Targaryen in HBO's 'House of the Dragon,' captivating millions with her portrayal of a princess fighting for her birthright. Her performance earned critical acclaim and transformed her overnight from a little-known Australian actress into an international star.

04-11.title: Australia men's national soccer team
04-11.subtitle: The Australia national men's soccer team sets a world record for the largest victory in an international association foo...
04-11.text: The Australia national men's soccer team sets a world record for the largest victory in an international association football match, winning the game 31–0 against American Samoa at the 2002 FIFA World Cup qualifiers for OFC. Australia's Archie Thompson also breaks the record for most goals scored by a player in an international match by scoring 13 goals.

04-11.title: 2011 Minsk Metro bombing
04-11.subtitle: A bomb exploded at the central Oktyabrskaya station of the Minsk Metro in Belarus, killing 15 people and injuring more than 200.
04-11.text: A bomb exploded at the central Oktyabrskaya station of the Minsk Metro in Belarus, killing 15 people and injuring more than 200.

04-11.title: Borussia Dortmund
04-11.subtitle: The tour bus of the German football team Borussia Dortmund was attacked with roadside bombs in Dortmund, Germany.
04-11.text: The tour bus of the German football team Borussia Dortmund was attacked with roadside bombs in Dortmund, Germany. Three bombs exploded as the bus ferried the team to the Westfalenstadion for the first leg of their quarter-final against Monaco.

04-12.title: Shapur Becomes Co-Emperor
04-12.subtitle: Father and son rule Persia together
04-12.text: In the heartland of ancient Persia, Shapur I rose to become co-emperor of the Sasanian Empire alongside his father Ardashir I. Together they conquered the fortress city of Hatra and expanded their dominion across the Middle East. Shapur would go on to become one of Persia's greatest warrior-kings, defeating three Roman emperors and capturing Emperor Valerian himself -- an unprecedented humiliation for Rome that Shapur immortalized in rock reliefs that still survive.

04-12.title: Puerto Rico's Foraker Act
04-12.subtitle: An island receives a measure of self-rule
04-12.text: President William McKinley signed the Foraker Act into law, granting Puerto Rico its first taste of civilian self-governance under American rule. The legislation established a civil government on the island, replacing military authority just two years after the Spanish-American War transferred control from Madrid to Washington. While limited in scope, the act marked the beginning of Puerto Rico's complex political relationship with the United States, a relationship still debated today.

04-12.title: Shanghai massacre of 1927
04-12.subtitle: Shanghai massacre of 1927: Chiang Kai-shek orders the Chinese Communist Party members executed in Shanghai, ending the First United Front.
04-12.text: Shanghai massacre of 1927: Chiang Kai-shek orders the Chinese Communist Party members executed in Shanghai, ending the First United Front.[citation needed]

04-12.title: Space Race
04-12.subtitle: Space Race: The Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human to travel into outer space and perform the first c...
04-12.text: The Space Race was a 20th-century competition between the Cold War rivals, the United States and the Soviet Union, to achieve superior spaceflight capability. It had its origins in the ballistic missile-based nuclear arms race between the two nations following World War II and the onset of the Cold War.

04-12.title: Samuel Doe
04-12.subtitle: Samuel Doe took control of Liberia in a coup d'état, overthrowing President William Tolbert and ending over 130 years of...
04-12.text: Samuel Doe took control of Liberia in a coup d'état, overthrowing President William Tolbert and ending over 130 years of national democratic presidential succession.

04-12.title: Space Shuttle
04-12.subtitle: The first launch of a Space Shuttle (Columbia) takes place: The STS-1 mission.
04-12.text: The first launch of a Space Shuttle (Columbia) takes place: The STS-1 mission.

04-12.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
04-12.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-51D to deploy two communications satellites.
04-12.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-51D to deploy two communications satellites.

04-12.title: Bosnian War
04-12.subtitle: Bosnian War: NATO forces began Operation Deny Flight (aircraft pictured) to enforce a no-fly zone over Bosnia and Herzeg...
04-12.text: Bosnian War: NATO forces began Operation Deny Flight (aircraft pictured) to enforce a no-fly zone over Bosnia and Herzegovina ordered by the United Nations Security Council.

04-12.title: NATO bombing of Yugoslavia
04-12.subtitle: During the NATO bombing of Yugoslavia, an American McDonnell Douglas F-15E Strike Eagle shoots a passenger train, killing between 20 and 60 people.
04-12.text: During the NATO bombing of Yugoslavia, an American McDonnell Douglas F-15E Strike Eagle shoots a passenger train, killing between 20 and 60 people.

04-12.title: Jerusalem Market Bombing
04-12.subtitle: Terror strikes at the heart of a holy city
04-12.text: At the bustling entrance to Jerusalem's Mahane Yehuda Market, a suicide bomber detonated her explosives among morning shoppers, killing seven people and wounding over a hundred. The al-Aqsa Martyrs' Brigades claimed responsibility for the attack, which targeted the site for maximum casualties during peak hours. The bombing was one of many devastating attacks during the Second Intifada that scarred daily life in Israel and deepened the cycle of violence.

04-12.title: 2012 Guinea-Bissau coup d'état
04-12.subtitle: The Guinea-Bissau military seized control in a coup amid a presidential election, later handing power to a transitional ...
04-12.text: The Guinea-Bissau military seized control in a coup amid a presidential election, later handing power to a transitional administration under Manuel Serifo Nhamadjo.

04-13.title: Henry V Crowned Emperor
04-13.subtitle: The last Salian takes the imperial throne
04-13.text: In the ancient basilicas of Rome, Henry V received the crown of the Holy Roman Empire from Pope Paschal II, becoming the last ruler of the Salian dynasty. His coronation came after years of bitter conflict with the papacy over the right to appoint bishops -- the famous Investiture Controversy. Henry's reign would see the resolution of this epochal struggle with the Concordat of Worms, a compromise that reshaped the relationship between church and state in medieval Europe.

04-13.title: Jacques Lacan Was Born
04-13.subtitle: Psychoanalysis would never think the same
04-13.text: In Paris, Jacques Marie Emile Lacan was born -- the man who would become the most controversial psychoanalyst since Freud. For nearly three decades, his packed seminars at the Ecole Normale Superieure electrified intellectual Paris, as he reimagined the unconscious as structured like a language. His dense, provocative writings transformed not just psychoanalysis but philosophy, literary theory, and film criticism, making him one of the twentieth century's most influential -- and debated -- thinkers.

04-13.title: World War II
04-13.subtitle: World War II: The discovery of mass graves of Polish prisoners of war killed by Soviet forces in the Katyń Forest Massac...
04-13.text: World War II: The discovery of mass graves of Polish prisoners of war killed by Soviet forces in the Katyń Forest Massacre is announced, causing a diplomatic rift between the Polish government-in-exile in London and the Soviet Union, which denies responsibility.

04-13.title: Ian Fleming
04-13.subtitle: Ian Fleming's novel Casino Royale was published as the first work to feature the British spy character James Bond.
04-13.text: Ian Fleming's novel Casino Royale was published as the first work to feature the British spy character James Bond.

04-13.title: Vietnamese National Army
04-13.subtitle: The Vietnamese National Army captured Ba Cụt, a military commander of the religious sect Hòa Hảo, which ran a de facto s...
04-13.text: The Vietnamese National Army captured Ba Cụt, a military commander of the religious sect Hòa Hảo, which ran a de facto state in South Vietnam in opposition to Prime Minister Ngô Đình Diệm.

04-13.title: Cold War
04-13.subtitle: In the midst of the Cold War, American pianist Van Cliburn (pictured) won the inaugural International Tchaikovsky Competition in Moscow.
04-13.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
04-13.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

04-13.title: Transit
04-13.subtitle: The United States launches Transit 1-B, the world's first satellite navigation system.
04-13.text: The United States launches Transit 1-B, the world's first satellite navigation system.

04-13.title: 36th Academy Awards
04-13.subtitle: At the Academy Awards, Sidney Poitier becomes the first African-American man to win the Best Actor award for the 1963 film Lilies of the Field.
04-13.text: At the Academy Awards, Sidney Poitier becomes the first African-American man to win the Best Actor award for the 1963 film Lilies of the Field.

04-13.title: Oxygen
04-13.subtitle: At 10:08 PM EST an oxygen tank aboard the Apollo 13 Service Module explodes, putting the crew in great danger and causin...
04-13.text: At 10:08 PM EST an oxygen tank aboard the Apollo 13 Service Module explodes, putting the crew in great danger and causing major damage to the Apollo command and service module (codenamed "Odyssey") while en route to the Moon.

04-13.title: 1975 Beirut bus massacre
04-13.subtitle: An attack by the Phalangist resistance kills 26 militia members of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine, ma...
04-13.text: The 1975 Beirut bus massacre, also known as the Ain el-Rammaneh incident and Black Sunday, was the collective name given to a short series of armed clashes involving Phalangist and Palestinian elements in the streets of central Beirut, which is commonly presented as the spark that set off the Lebanese Civil War in the mid-1970s.
04-13.location: 1975 armed clashes between Phalangist and Palestinian organizations in Beirut, Lebanon

04-13.title: Rasmus Dahlin Was Born
04-13.subtitle: Sweden's next great defenseman arrives
04-13.text: In Lidkoping, Sweden, Rasmus Erik Dahlin was born with hockey in his blood. By eighteen, he was universally considered the most talented prospect in the 2018 NHL draft and was selected first overall by the Buffalo Sabres. His combination of skating ability, offensive instinct, and defensive awareness made him a generational talent. Named captain of the Sabres, he carries the hopes of a franchise and a nation on his broad shoulders.

04-13.title: Kamień Pomorski homeless hostel fire
04-13.subtitle: Twenty-three people died in a fire at a homeless hostel in Kamień Pomorski; it was Poland's deadliest fire since 1980.
04-13.text: Twenty-three people died in a fire at a homeless hostel in Kamień Pomorski; it was Poland's deadliest fire since 1980.

04-13.title: War in Afghanistan
04-13.subtitle: War in Afghanistan: In an airstrike in Nangarhar Province, the U.
04-13.text: War in Afghanistan: In an airstrike in Nangarhar Province, the U.S. military dropped the most powerful conventional bomb used in combat.

04-14.title: Battle Near the Via Aemilia
04-14.subtitle: The Republic strikes back against Antony
04-14.text: Along the ancient Via Aemilia in northern Italy, the forces of the Roman Senate clashed with Mark Antony's legions in the War of Mutina. Though Antony's troops initially gained ground, the Senate's forces rallied and drove them back in a bloody engagement. The battle was part of the first civil war after Caesar's assassination, a desperate struggle between those who sought to restore the Republic and those who would build an empire on its ruins.

04-14.title: Paris World's Fair Opens
04-14.subtitle: The City of Light dazzles a new century
04-14.text: Along the banks of the Seine, the Exposition Universelle of 1900 threw open its gates to celebrate the achievements of the departing century and herald the dawn of a new one. Over fifty million visitors marveled at moving sidewalks, talking films, and the newly completed Gare d'Orsay. The fair showcased Art Nouveau at its peak and introduced the world to escalators, diesel engines, and the Palace of Electricity -- a glittering vision of the future that Paris embodied like no other city.

04-14.title: Soviet Union
04-14.subtitle: The Soviet satellite Sputnik 2 falls from orbit after a mission duration of 162 days.
04-14.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

04-14.title: Apollo 13
04-14.subtitle: After an oxygen tank aboard Apollo 13 exploded, disabling the spacecraft's electrical and life-support systems, astronau...
04-14.text: After an oxygen tank aboard Apollo 13 exploded, disabling the spacecraft's electrical and life-support systems, astronaut Jack Swigert reported: "Houston, we've had a problem here" (audio featured).

04-14.title: STS-1
04-14.subtitle: STS-1: The first operational Space Shuttle, Columbia completes its first test flight.
04-14.text: STS-1 was the first orbital spaceflight of NASA's Space Shuttle program. The first orbiter, Columbia, launched on April 12, 1981, and returned on April 14, 1981, 54.5 hours later, having orbited the Earth 37 times. Columbia carried a crew of two—commander John W. Young and pilot Robert L. Crippen. It was the first American crewed space flight since the Apollo–Soyuz Test Project (ASTP) in 1975.
04-14.location: First Space Shuttle mission, first orbital flight of the Space Shuttle Columbia

04-14.title: United Nations
04-14.subtitle: In a United Nations ceremony in Geneva, Switzerland, the Soviet Union signs an agreement pledging to withdraw its troops from Afghanistan.
04-14.text: In a United Nations ceremony in Geneva, Switzerland, the Soviet Union signs an agreement pledging to withdraw its troops from Afghanistan.

04-14.title: Georgia
04-14.subtitle: The Republic of Georgia introduces the post of President following its declaration of independence from the Soviet Union.
04-14.text: The Republic of Georgia introduces the post of President following its declaration of independence from the Soviet Union.

04-14.title: Iraqi no-fly zones conflict
04-14.subtitle: Iraqi no-fly zones conflict: In a friendly-fire incident during Operation Provide Comfort, two U.
04-14.text: Iraqi no-fly zones conflict: In a friendly-fire incident during Operation Provide Comfort, two U.S. Air Force aircraft mistakenly shot down two U.S. Army helicopters over northern Iraq, killing 26 people.

04-14.title: 1999 Sydney hailstorm
04-14.subtitle: A storm dropped around 500,000 tonnes of hailstones on Sydney (examples pictured) and the east coast of New South Wales, causing about A$2.
04-14.text: A storm dropped around 500,000 tonnes of hailstones on Sydney (examples pictured) and the east coast of New South Wales, causing about A$2.3 billion in damages, the costliest natural disaster in Australian insurance history.

04-14.title: Patrick Surtain II Was Born
04-14.subtitle: Football royalty begets a new dynasty
04-14.text: In Fort Lauderdale, Florida, Patrick Frank Surtain II was born into football royalty -- his father a former Pro Bowl cornerback. The younger Surtain would surpass even his father's achievements, winning a national championship at Alabama before being drafted ninth overall by the Denver Broncos. Four consecutive Pro Bowl selections and the 2024 Defensive Player of the Year award cemented his status as the premier cornerback of his generation.

04-14.title: 2010 eruptions of Eyjafjallajökull
04-14.subtitle: Plumes of ash from a major eruption of the Eyjafjallajökull volcano in Iceland led to widespread disruption of air trave...
04-14.text: Plumes of ash from a major eruption of the Eyjafjallajökull volcano in Iceland led to widespread disruption of air travel throughout Europe for several days.

04-14.title: Boko Haram
04-14.subtitle: Boko Haram militants kidnapped 276 schoolgirls from a government secondary school in the town of Chibok, Nigeria.
04-14.text: Boko Haram militants kidnapped 276 schoolgirls from a government secondary school in the town of Chibok, Nigeria.

04-14.title: Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer
04-14.subtitle: The Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer (JUICE) is launched by the European Space Agency.
04-14.text: The Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer is an interplanetary spacecraft developed by the European Space Agency (ESA) and on its way to orbit and study three icy moons of Jupiter: Ganymede, Callisto, and Europa. These planetary-mass moons are planned to be studied because they are thought to have significant bodies of liquid water beneath their frozen surfaces, which would make them potentially habitable for extraterrestrial life.
04-14.location: European mission to study Jupiter and its moons since 2023

04-15.title: Maecenas Was Born
04-15.subtitle: History's greatest patron of the arts
04-15.text: In the Etruscan heartland of Italy, Gaius Cilnius Maecenas was born into a family of ancient aristocratic lineage. He would become the trusted advisor to Emperor Augustus and, more enduringly, the greatest patron of poets the ancient world ever knew. His generous support of Virgil and Horace produced some of the finest literature in human history. So profound was his impact that his very name became a byword for artistic patronage in languages across the world.

04-15.title: Siege of Catubig Begins
04-15.subtitle: Filipino guerrillas strike in a forgotten war
04-15.text: In the remote town of Catubig in the Philippines, Filipino guerrillas launched a devastating surprise attack on American infantry, beginning a four-day siege that epitomized the fierce resistance to U.S. colonial rule. The Philippine-American War, often overshadowed by the Spanish-American conflict that preceded it, was a brutal struggle for independence that claimed hundreds of thousands of lives and left deep scars on both nations.

04-15.title: John B. Kendrick
04-15.subtitle: U.
04-15.text: U.S. Senator John B. Kendrick of Wyoming introduces a resolution calling for an investigation of a secret land deal, which leads to the discovery of the Teapot Dome scandal.

04-15.title: Boeing B-52 Stratofortress
04-15.subtitle: The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress, a long-range, subsonic, jet-powered, strategic bomber operated by the U.
04-15.text: The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress is an American subsonic jet-powered strategic bomber. The B-52 was designed and built by Boeing, which has continued to provide support and upgrades. It has been operated by the United States Air Force (USAF) since 1955 and was flown by NASA from 1959 to 2007. The bomber can carry up to 70,000 pounds (32,000 kg) of weapons and has a typical combat range of around 8,800 miles (14,200 km) without aerial refueling.

04-15.title: Walter O'Malley
04-15.subtitle: On Walter O'Malley's initiative, the Los Angeles Dodgers and San Francisco Giants played the first Major League Baseball game on the U.
04-15.text: On Walter O'Malley's initiative, the Los Angeles Dodgers and San Francisco Giants played the first Major League Baseball game on the U.S. West Coast.

04-15.title: Shaw University
04-15.subtitle: At Shaw University in Raleigh, North Carolina, Ella Baker leads a conference that results in the creation of the Student...
04-15.text: At Shaw University in Raleigh, North Carolina, Ella Baker leads a conference that results in the creation of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, one of the principal organizations of the civil rights movement in the 1960s.

04-15.title: The EC-121 shootdown incident
04-15.subtitle: The EC-121 shootdown incident: North Korea shoots down a United States Navy aircraft over the Sea of Japan, killing all 31 on board.
04-15.text: The EC-121 shootdown incident: North Korea shoots down a United States Navy aircraft over the Sea of Japan, killing all 31 on board.

04-15.title: Cambodian Civil War
04-15.subtitle: During the Cambodian Civil War, massacre of the Vietnamese minority results in 800 bodies flowing down the Mekong river into South Vietnam.
04-15.text: During the Cambodian Civil War, massacre of the Vietnamese minority results in 800 bodies flowing down the Mekong river into South Vietnam.

04-15.title: Hillsborough disaster
04-15.subtitle: Hillsborough disaster: A human crush occurs at Hillsborough Stadium, home of Sheffield Wednesday, in the FA Cup Semi-fin...
04-15.text: Hillsborough disaster: A human crush occurs at Hillsborough Stadium, home of Sheffield Wednesday, in the FA Cup Semi-final, resulting in the deaths of 97 Liverpool fans.

04-15.title: Air China 129 Crashes
04-15.subtitle: A fog-shrouded hillside claims 129 lives
04-15.text: On approach to Gimhae International Airport in Busan, South Korea, Air China Flight 129 slammed into Mount Dotdae in poor visibility, killing 129 of the 166 people aboard. The Boeing 767 had been flying a routine route from Beijing when the crew lost situational awareness during a circling approach. The disaster prompted sweeping reviews of cockpit communication and approach procedures at airports surrounded by mountainous terrain.

04-15.title: Dzhokhar Tsarnaev
04-15.subtitle: Dzhokhar and Tamerlan Tsarnaev set off two pressure cooker bombs during the running of the Boston Marathon, killing thre...
04-15.text: Dzhokhar and Tamerlan Tsarnaev set off two pressure cooker bombs during the running of the Boston Marathon, killing three people and injuring 264 others.

04-15.title: Notre-Dame fire
04-15.subtitle: A fire severely damaged Notre-Dame de Paris, destroying the cathedral's timber spire and much of the roof.
04-15.text: A fire severely damaged Notre-Dame de Paris, destroying the cathedral's timber spire and much of the roof.

04-16.title: The Battle of Megiddo
04-16.subtitle: History's first reliably recorded battle
04-16.text: On the plains before the fortress city of Megiddo in ancient Canaan, Pharaoh Thutmose III led his Egyptian army in what would become the first battle recorded in relatively reliable detail. The engagement against a coalition of Canaanite rebels set the standard for military documentation, with scribes carefully noting troop movements, terrain, and tactics. The site -- later known as Armageddon -- would echo through millennia as a symbol of decisive conflict.

04-16.title: Paul Waner Was Born
04-16.subtitle: Big Poison steps up to the plate of life
04-16.text: In Harrah, Oklahoma, Paul Glee Waner was born -- the man who would become 'Big Poison' to adoring Pittsburgh Pirates fans. A masterful right fielder with a supernatural ability to make contact, he won the 1927 NL MVP Award and collected a team-record 237 hits that season. His 62 doubles in 1932 set a National League record. Alongside his brother Lloyd, 'Big Poison' helped define the golden age of Pirates baseball.

04-16.title: Polish–Soviet War
04-16.subtitle: Polish–Soviet War: The Polish army launched the Vilna offensive to capture Vilnius (now in Lithuania) from the Red Army.
04-16.text: Polish–Soviet War: The Polish army launched the Vilna offensive to capture Vilnius (now in Lithuania) from the Red Army.

04-16.title: Martin Luther King Jr.
04-16.subtitle: U.
04-16.text: U.S. civil rights campaigner Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. writes his open letter from Birmingham Jail, sometimes known as "The Negro Is Your Brother", while incarcerated in Birmingham, Alabama, for protesting against segregation.

04-16.title: Apollo program
04-16.subtitle: Apollo program: The launch of Apollo 16 from Cape Canaveral, Florida.
04-16.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

04-16.title: India-Bangladesh Border Clash
04-16.subtitle: Two neighbors spar over invisible lines
04-16.text: Along the poorly marked border between India and Bangladesh, troops from the Bangladesh Rifles and the Indian Border Security Force exchanged fire in the first of what would become six days of armed clashes. The skirmishes, triggered by disputes over the tangled, often unmarked international boundary, ended in stalemate but highlighted the volatile tensions simmering between the two South Asian nations over territory, migration, and sovereignty.

04-16.title: Treaty of Accession 2003
04-16.subtitle: The Treaty of Accession is signed in Athens admitting ten new member states to the European Union.
04-16.text: The Treaty of Accession is signed in Athens admitting ten new member states to the European Union.

04-16.title: Virginia Tech shooting
04-16.subtitle: In one of the deadliest shooting incidents in United States history, a gunman killed 32 people and wounded over 20 more ...
04-16.text: In one of the deadliest shooting incidents in United States history, a gunman killed 32 people and wounded over 20 more before committing suicide at the Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University in Blacksburg, Virginia (memorial pictured).

04-16.title: 2012 Pulitzer Prize
04-16.subtitle: The Pulitzer Prize winners were announced, it was the first time since 1977 that no book won the Fiction Prize.
04-16.text: The Pulitzer Prize winners were announced, it was the first time since 1977 that no book won the Fiction Prize.

04-16.title: MV Sewol
04-16.subtitle: The ferry MV Sewol (pictured) capsized and sank off Donggeochado, South Korea, killing 306 people, mainly students from Danwon High School.
04-16.text: The ferry MV Sewol (pictured) capsized and sank off Donggeochado, South Korea, killing 306 people, mainly students from Danwon High School.

04-16.title: The New York Times
04-16.subtitle: The New York Times and the New Yorker win the Pulitzer Prize for Public Service for breaking news of the Harvey Weinstein sexual abuse scandal.
04-16.text: The New York Times and the New Yorker win the Pulitzer Prize for Public Service for breaking news of the Harvey Weinstein sexual abuse scandal.

04-17.title: Sweyn Forkbeard Was Born
04-17.subtitle: A Viking king who conquered three nations
04-17.text: In the halls of Danish royalty, Sweyn Forkbeard entered the world -- a prince who would become one of the most formidable Viking rulers in history. He seized the Danish throne from his own father, conquered Norway, and in 1013 drove King Ethelred the Unready from England to become its undisputed ruler. Though his reign over England lasted only five weeks before his death, his son Cnut would build upon his conquests to create a vast North Sea empire.

04-17.title: Nicolas Nabokov Was Born
04-17.subtitle: A composer caught between worlds and wars
04-17.text: In the Russian Empire, Nicolas Nabokov was born into the same illustrious family that would produce his more famous cousin, the novelist Vladimir. But Nicolas carved his own remarkable path as a composer, cultural organizer, and Cold War intellectual. He became a U.S. citizen in 1939, and his orchestral works blended Russian romanticism with modernist experimentation. As secretary-general of the Congress for Cultural Freedom, he became one of the most influential cultural figures of the Cold War era.

04-17.title: Apollo program
04-17.subtitle: Apollo program: The damaged Apollo 13 spacecraft returns to Earth safely.
04-17.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

04-17.title: Ustaše
04-17.subtitle: Ustaše-affiliated Croatian separatists attacked the Yugoslav embassy in Stockholm, fatally shooting the ambassador, Vladimir Rolović.
