05-20.text: First publications of the discovery of the HIV virus that causes AIDS in the journal Science by a team of French scientists including Françoise Barré-Sinoussi, Jean-Claude Chermann, and Luc Montagnier.

05-20.title: Democracy
05-20.subtitle: The Chinese authorities declare martial law in the face of pro-democracy demonstrations, setting the scene for the Tiananmen Square massacre.
05-20.text: The Chinese authorities declare martial law in the face of pro-democracy demonstrations, setting the scene for the Tiananmen Square massacre.

05-20.title: Civil rights
05-20.subtitle: Civil rights: The Supreme Court of the United States rules in Romer v.
05-20.text: Civil rights: The Supreme Court of the United States rules in Romer v. Evans against a law that would have prevented any city, town or county in the state of Colorado from taking any legislative, executive, or judicial action to protect the rights of gays and lesbians.

05-20.title: East Timor Gains Freedom
05-20.subtitle: A nation is born after decades of struggle
05-20.text: After twenty-three years of Indonesian occupation and three years of UN transitional administration, East Timor's independence was formally recognized by its former colonizer, Portugal. The tiny Southeast Asian nation, which had endured brutal repression and a devastating independence referendum in 1999, finally took its place among the world's sovereign states. The moment represented the culmination of one of the late twentieth century's most harrowing decolonization struggles.

05-20.title: 2013 Moore tornado
05-20.subtitle: A tornado struck Moore, Oklahoma, United States, killing 24 people and causing an estimated $2 billion of damage.
05-20.text: A tornado struck Moore, Oklahoma, United States, killing 24 people and causing an estimated $2 billion of damage.

05-20.title: Singapore
05-20.subtitle: The government of Singapore authorised the controversial execution of convicted murderer Kho Jabing for the murder of a ...
05-20.text: The government of Singapore authorised the controversial execution of convicted murderer Kho Jabing for the murder of a Chinese construction worker despite the international pleas for clemency, notably from Amnesty International and the United Nations.

05-21.title: The Tetrarchy Begins
05-21.subtitle: Four rulers for one vast empire
05-21.text: In a radical experiment in imperial governance, Emperors Diocletian and Maximian appointed Galerius as Caesar in the East, inaugurating the Tetrarchy -- a system of government by four rulers. The innovation was born of necessity: the Roman Empire had grown too vast and too threatened for one man to govern. By dividing power among two senior emperors and two junior Caesars, Diocletian hoped to end the cycle of civil wars that had nearly destroyed Rome. For a time, it worked brilliantly.

05-21.title: Horace Heidt Was Born
05-21.subtitle: A bandleader who became America's entertainer
05-21.text: In Alameda, California, Horace Heidt was born -- a pianist and bandleader who would become one of the most popular entertainers of the big band era. His orchestra, Horace Heidt and his Musical Knights, toured the vaudeville circuit and became a fixture of American radio and television during the 1930s and 1940s. His talent discovery shows launched careers and brought music into millions of American homes during a golden age of broadcast entertainment.

05-21.title: American civil rights movement
05-21.subtitle: American civil rights movement: Alabama Governor John Malcolm Patterson declares martial law in an attempt to restore or...
05-21.text: American civil rights movement: Alabama Governor John Malcolm Patterson declares martial law in an attempt to restore order after race riots break out.

05-21.title: White Night riots
05-21.subtitle: White Night riots in San Francisco following the manslaughter conviction of Dan White for the assassinations of George Moscone and Harvey Milk.
05-21.text: White Night riots in San Francisco following the manslaughter conviction of Dan White for the assassinations of George Moscone and Harvey Milk.

05-21.title: Rajiv Gandhi
05-21.subtitle: Former Indian prime minister Rajiv Gandhi was assassinated by a suicide bomber in Sriperumbudur, Tamil Nadu.
05-21.text: Former Indian prime minister Rajiv Gandhi was assassinated by a suicide bomber in Sriperumbudur, Tamil Nadu.

05-21.title: Suharto
05-21.subtitle: Indonesian president Suharto resigned after a collapse of support for his presidency amid economic and political crises, ending 32 years in power.
05-21.text: Suharto was an Indonesian military officer and politician who served as the second and longest-serving president of Indonesia from 1967 to 1998. Widely regarded as a military dictator by international observers, Suharto led Indonesia as an authoritarian regime from 1967 until his resignation in 1998 following nationwide unrest.

05-21.title: Wilkes-Barre Plane Crash
05-21.subtitle: A casino charter flight ends in tragedy
05-21.text: In the mountains near Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania, a chartered Jetstream aircraft carrying seventeen professional gamblers returning from Atlantic City's Caesar's Palace crashed into mountainous terrain, killing all nineteen people aboard. The small turboprop had descended into rugged terrain in poor conditions, leaving no survivors. The disaster highlighted the risks of small-charter aviation and the dangers of mountainous approaches in marginal weather.

05-21.title: 2003 Boumerdès earthquake
05-21.subtitle: The 6.
05-21.text: The 6.8 Mw  Boumerdès earthquake shakes northern Algeria with a maximum Mercalli intensity of X (Extreme). More than 2,200 people were killed and a moderate tsunami sank boats at the Balearic Islands.

05-21.title: Roller coaster
05-21.subtitle: The world's tallest roller coaster, Kingda Ka, opened at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, U.
05-21.text: The world's tallest roller coaster, Kingda Ka, opened at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, U.S.

05-21.title: Republic of Montenegro
05-21.subtitle: The Republic of Montenegro holds a referendum proposing independence from the State Union of Serbia and Montenegro; 55% ...
05-21.text: The Republic of Montenegro was a constituent federated state of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia and then Serbia and Montenegro between 1992 and 2006. The declaration of independence of Montenegro in 2006 ended the ex-Yugoslav state. After the collapse of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (SFRY), the remaining republics of Montenegro and Serbia agreed to the formation of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (FRY) which officially abandoned communism and endorsed liberal institutions.
05-21.location: Federal unit of Yugoslavia/Serbia & Montenegro between 1992 and 2006

05-21.title: JAXA
05-21.subtitle: JAXA, the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency, launches the solar-sail spacecraft IKAROS aboard an H-IIA rocket.
05-21.text: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA)  is the Japanese national air and space agency. Through the merger of three previously independent organizations, JAXA was formed on 1 October 2003. JAXA is responsible for research, technology development and launch of satellites into orbit, and is involved in many more advanced missions such as asteroid exploration and possible human exploration of the Moon. Its motto is One JAXA and its corporate slogan is Explore to Realize.
05-21.location: Japan's national air and space agency

05-21.title: 2014 Taipei Metro attack
05-21.subtitle: A Taiwanese man carried out a stabbing spree on a Taipei Metro train, killing four people and injuring 24 others.
05-21.text: A Taiwanese man carried out a stabbing spree on a Taipei Metro train, killing four people and injuring 24 others.

05-21.title: Greenfield tornado
05-21.subtitle: The Greenfield tornado, estimated to have produced winds in excess of 309 miles per hour (497 km/h), moves across Iowa.
05-21.text: The Greenfield tornado, estimated to have produced winds in excess of 309 miles per hour (497 km/h), moves across Iowa.

05-22.title: Dong Zhuo Assassinated
05-22.subtitle: A tyrant falls by his own son's hand
05-22.text: In the imperial capital of Chang'an, the feared warlord Dong Zhuo was assassinated by his own adopted son, Lu Bu. Dong Zhuo had terrorized the Eastern Han dynasty, seizing control of the emperor and ruling through fear and brutality. His death, orchestrated by ministers who could no longer endure his tyranny, sent the empire spiraling into the chaos of the Three Kingdoms period -- an era of war, heroism, and treachery that would become the most romanticized epoch in Chinese history.

05-22.title: Juan Arvizu Was Born
05-22.subtitle: Mexico's silken voice enters the world
05-22.text: In Queretaro, Mexico, Juan Nepomuceno Arvizu was born -- the tenor who would earn the nickname 'The Voice of Silk' for his extraordinarily beautiful vocal tone. His interpretations of boleros and tangos by composers like Agustin Lara and Maria Grever made him one of the most beloved singers in Latin American history. Through radio, film, and the concert stage, Arvizu's voice carried the romantic soul of Mexico to audiences throughout the Spanish-speaking world.

05-22.title: 1960 Valdivia earthquake
05-22.subtitle: The Great Chilean earthquake, measuring 9.
05-22.text: The Great Chilean earthquake, measuring 9.5 on the moment magnitude scale, hits southern Chile, becoming the most powerful earthquake ever recorded.

05-22.title: Apollo 10
05-22.subtitle: Apollo 10's Lunar Module flies within 8.
05-22.text: Apollo 10's Lunar Module flies within 8.4 nautical miles (16 km) of the Moon's surface.

05-22.title: Sri Lankan Constitution of 1972
05-22.subtitle: Ceylon adopts a new constitution, becoming a republic and changing its name to Sri Lanka.
05-22.text: The Sri Lankan Constitution of 1972 was a constitution of Sri Lanka, replaced by the 1978 constitution currently in force. It was Sri Lanka's first republican constitution, and its second since independence in 1948. The constitution changed the country's name from Ceylon to Sri Lanka, and established it as an independent republic. The country was officially designated as the "Republic of Sri Lanka," leading to the constitution being known as the 1972 Republican Constitution.
05-22.location: Fundamental law of Sri Lanka from 1972 to 1978

05-22.title: Pac-Man
05-22.subtitle: Location testing for Pac-Man, a Japanese arcade game that became an icon of 1980s popular culture, began in Shibuya, Tokyo.
05-22.text: Location testing for Pac-Man, a Japanese arcade game that became an icon of 1980s popular culture, began in Shibuya, Tokyo.

05-22.title: 1987 Rugby World Cup
05-22.subtitle: First ever Rugby World Cup kicks off with New Zealand playing Italy at Eden Park in Auckland, New Zealand.
05-22.text: First ever Rugby World Cup kicks off with New Zealand playing Italy at Eden Park in Auckland, New Zealand.

05-22.title: Public Prosecutor v Taw Cheng Kong
05-22.subtitle: In Public Prosecutor v Taw Cheng Kong, the Court of Appeal of Singapore overruled a High Court decision in the only time...
05-22.text: In Public Prosecutor v Taw Cheng Kong, the Court of Appeal of Singapore overruled a High Court decision in the only time a statute in Singapore had been ruled unconstitutional.

05-22.title: Fighting Erupts in Jaffna
05-22.subtitle: Sri Lanka's civil war claims more lives
05-22.text: In the embattled northern city of Jaffna, Sri Lanka's security forces clashed with Tamil Tiger rebels in two days of intense fighting that left over one hundred fifty separatist fighters dead. The battle for control of the Jaffna Peninsula was one of the bloodiest chapters in the country's decades-long civil war, a conflict that pitted the Sinhalese-dominated government against Tamil separatists seeking an independent homeland in the north and east of the island.

05-22.title: Federal Bureau of Prisons
05-22.subtitle: Police announced that the skeletal remains of Federal Bureau of Prisons intern Chandra Levy, who had been missing for a ...
05-22.text: Police announced that the skeletal remains of Federal Bureau of Prisons intern Chandra Levy, who had been missing for a year, had been found in Rock Creek Park in Washington, D.C.

05-22.title: SpaceX COTS Demo Flight 2
05-22.subtitle: SpaceX COTS Demo Flight 2 launches a Dragon capsule on a Falcon 9 rocket in the first commercial flight to the International Space Station.
05-22.text: SpaceX COTS Demo Flight 2 launches a Dragon capsule on a Falcon 9 rocket in the first commercial flight to the International Space Station.

05-22.title: Prayut Chan-o-cha
05-22.subtitle: Prayut Chan-o-cha (pictured), the commander-in-chief of the Royal Thai Army, launched a coup d'état against the caretake...
05-22.text: Prayut Chan-o-cha (pictured), the commander-in-chief of the Royal Thai Army, launched a coup d'état against the caretaker government following six months of political crisis.

05-23.title: Maya Prince Born
05-23.subtitle: Palenque's future great king enters the world
05-23.text: In the ancient Maya city of Palenque, deep in the jungles of what is now Chiapas, Mexico, K'inich Kan Bahlam II was born. He would grow to become one of the greatest rulers of this magnificent city-state, ascending to the throne in 684 AD following the death of his legendary father, K'inich Janaab Pakal. Under his rule, Palenque reached new heights of artistic and architectural achievement, leaving behind temples and sculptures that still awe visitors today.

05-23.title: Carney Wins Medal of Honor
05-23.subtitle: A hero from 1863 finally gets his due
05-23.text: Thirty-seven years after the guns fell silent, Sergeant William Harvey Carney was awarded the Medal of Honor for his extraordinary heroism during the Assault on Battery Wagner in 1863. A member of the all-Black 54th Massachusetts Infantry, Carney had seized the American flag when the color bearer fell and carried it through withering Confederate fire, sustaining multiple wounds but never letting the banner touch the ground. His was one of the first Medals of Honor awarded to an African American soldier.

05-23.title: Abdul Hamid II
05-23.subtitle: Abdul Hamid II, Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, publicly announces the creation of the Ullah millet for the Aromanians of ...
05-23.text: Abdul Hamid II, Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, publicly announces the creation of the Ullah millet for the Aromanians of the empire, which had been established one day earlier. For this reason, the Aromanian National Day is usually celebrated on May 23, although some do so on May 22 instead.

05-23.title: USS Sailfish
05-23.subtitle: The U.
05-23.text: The U.S. Navy submarine Squalus sank off Portsmouth, New Hampshire, during tests, causing 26 men to drown.

05-23.title: Tsunami
05-23.subtitle: A tsunami caused by an earthquake in Chile the previous day kills 61 people in Hilo, Hawaii.
05-23.text: A tsunami caused by an earthquake in Chile the previous day kills 61 people in Hilo, Hawaii.

05-23.title: Aviogenex Flight 130
05-23.subtitle: Seventy-eight people are killed when Aviogenex Flight 130 crashes on approach to Rijeka Airport in present-day Rijeka, C...
05-23.text: Seventy-eight people are killed when Aviogenex Flight 130 crashes on approach to Rijeka Airport in present-day Rijeka, Croatia (then the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia).

05-23.title: Airbus A300
05-23.subtitle: The Airbus A300, the first twin-engined wide-body airliner, went into service with Air France.
05-23.text: The Airbus A300, the first twin-engined wide-body airliner, went into service with Air France.

05-23.title: Owen Hart
05-23.subtitle: Professional wrestler Owen Hart died immediately before a World Wrestling Federation match after dropping 70 feet (21 m)...
05-23.text: Professional wrestler Owen Hart died immediately before a World Wrestling Federation match after dropping 70 feet (21 m) onto the ring during a botched entrance.

05-23.title: Felipe Drugovich Was Born
05-23.subtitle: A Brazilian racer fueled by dual heritage
05-23.text: In Maringa, Brazil, Felipe Drugovich Roncato was born with racing fuel in his veins and an Italian-Brazilian heritage that connected him to two of the world's great motorsport cultures. He would climb through the junior racing categories to become the 2022 Formula 2 champion, demonstrating the speed and racecraft needed to compete at the highest levels. His career in Formula E continues the Brazilian tradition of producing world-class drivers.

05-23.title: International Court of Justice
05-23.subtitle: The International Court of Justice (ICJ) awards Middle Rocks to Malaysia and Pedra Branca (Pulau Batu Puteh) to Singapor...
05-23.text: The International Court of Justice (ICJ) awards Middle Rocks to Malaysia and Pedra Branca (Pulau Batu Puteh) to Singapore, ending a 29-year territorial dispute between the two countries.

05-23.title: Interstate 5 in Washington
05-23.subtitle: A freeway bridge carrying Interstate 5 over the Skagit River collapses in Mount Vernon, Washington.
05-23.text: A freeway bridge carrying Interstate 5 over the Skagit River collapses in Mount Vernon, Washington.

05-24.title: Germanicus Was Born
05-24.subtitle: Rome's golden prince and beloved general
05-24.text: In Rome, Germanicus Julius Caesar was born into the heart of the imperial family -- nephew and adopted son of Emperor Tiberius, great-nephew of Augustus himself. He would become Rome's most beloved military hero, leading campaigns against the Germanic tribes of Arminius that avenged the catastrophic defeat at Teutoburg Forest. His early death at thirty-three, widely believed to be poison, plunged Rome into mourning and gave rise to a legend that endured for centuries.

05-24.title: Orange Free State Annexed
05-24.subtitle: Britain swallows a Boer republic
05-24.text: During the Second Boer War, the United Kingdom formally annexed the Orange Free State, absorbing the independent Boer republic into the British Empire. The annexation, carried out while fighting still raged across South Africa, was part of Britain's campaign to consolidate control over the gold and diamond-rich interior of southern Africa. The move ended decades of Boer self-governance and sowed the seeds of Afrikaner nationalism that would shape South African politics for a century.
05-24.location: Bloemfontein, Orange Free State (modern South Africa)

05-24.title: Soviet Union
05-24.subtitle: Acting on the orders of Soviet leader Joseph Stalin, NKVD agent Iosif Grigulevich orchestrates an unsuccessful assassina...
05-24.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

05-24.title: 1960 Valdivia earthquake
05-24.subtitle: Following the 1960 Valdivia earthquake, the largest ever recorded earthquake, Cordón Caulle begins to erupt.
05-24.text: Following the 1960 Valdivia earthquake, the largest ever recorded earthquake, Cordón Caulle begins to erupt.

05-24.title: American civil rights movement
05-24.subtitle: American civil rights movement: Freedom Riders are arrested in Jackson, Mississippi, for "disturbing the peace" after disembarking from their bus.
05-24.text: American civil rights movement: Freedom Riders are arrested in Jackson, Mississippi, for "disturbing the peace" after disembarking from their bus.

05-24.title: Project Mercury
05-24.subtitle: Project Mercury: American astronaut Scott Carpenter orbits the Earth three times in the Aurora 7 space capsule.
05-24.text: Project Mercury was the first human spaceflight program of the United States, running from 1958 through 1963. An early highlight of the Space Race, its goal was to put a man into Earth orbit and return him safely, ideally before the Soviet Union. Taken over from the US Air Force by the newly created civilian space agency NASA, it conducted 20 uncrewed developmental flights, and six successful flights by astronauts.

05-24.title: Kola Peninsula
05-24.subtitle: On the Kola Peninsula in Russia, drilling began on the Kola Superdeep Borehole, which eventually reached a depth of 12,2...
05-24.text: On the Kola Peninsula in Russia, drilling began on the Kola Superdeep Borehole, which eventually reached a depth of 12,262 metres (40,230 ft), making it the deepest borehole ever drilled and the lowest artificial point on Earth.

05-24.title: Liberation of Khorramshahr
05-24.subtitle: Liberation of Khorramshahr: Iranians recapture of the port city of Khorramshahr from the Iraqis during the Iran–Iraq War.
05-24.text: Liberation of Khorramshahr: Iranians recapture of the port city of Khorramshahr from the Iraqis during the Iran–Iraq War.

05-24.title: Section 28
05-24.subtitle: Section 28 of the United Kingdom's Local Government Act of 1988, a controversial amendment stating that a local authorit...
05-24.text: Section 28 of the United Kingdom's Local Government Act of 1988, a controversial amendment stating that a local authority cannot intentionally promote homosexuality, was enacted.

05-24.title: Israel Defense Forces
05-24.subtitle: The Israel Defense Forces began Operation Solomon, a covert operation to bring thousands of Ethiopian Jews to Israel (evacuees pictured).
05-24.text: The Israel Defense Forces began Operation Solomon, a covert operation to bring thousands of Ethiopian Jews to Israel (evacuees pictured).

05-24.title: Israel Leaves Lebanon
05-24.subtitle: Twenty-two years of occupation end overnight
05-24.text: Under cover of darkness, the last Israeli troops withdrew from southern Lebanon, ending a military occupation that had lasted twenty-two years. The pullout, prompted by mounting casualties and domestic opposition, was hailed as a victory by Hezbollah and celebrated across Lebanon. But the withdrawal left behind a political vacuum and an emboldened militant movement that would reshape the region's geopolitics, culminating in another devastating war just six years later.

05-24.title: An Inconvenient Truth
05-24.subtitle: An Inconvenient Truth, a documentary film that has been credited for raising international public awareness of climate c...
05-24.text: An Inconvenient Truth, a documentary film that has been credited for raising international public awareness of climate change and re-energizing the environmental movement, was released.

05-24.title: Islamic extremism
05-24.subtitle: A gunman involved in Islamic extremism opened fire at the Jewish Museum of Belgium in Brussels, killing four people.
05-24.text: A gunman involved in Islamic extremism opened fire at the Jewish Museum of Belgium in Brussels, killing four people.

05-25.title: Rome's King Triumphs
05-25.subtitle: Servius Tullius celebrates over the Etruscans
05-25.text: Through the streets of ancient Rome, King Servius Tullius rode in triumph, celebrating his military victory over the Etruscans -- the very people from whom his dynasty had sprung. The sixth king of Rome, Servius was credited with establishing the first Roman census and organizing the city into classes based on wealth rather than birth. His triumph over the Etruscans demonstrated Rome's growing independence from its cultural forebears and its emergence as a power in its own right.

05-25.title: George Lennon Was Born
05-25.subtitle: An IRA commander forged in revolution
05-25.text: In Ireland, George Lennon was born into a land consumed by the struggle for independence from British rule. He would become a leader of the Irish Republican Army during both the War of Independence and the bitter Civil War that followed, commanding forces in some of the conflict's most intense engagements. His life spanned nearly the entire arc of modern Irish history, from revolution through partition to the peace process.

05-25.title: Nuclear weapons testing
05-25.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: At the Nevada Test Site, the United States conducts its first and only nuclear artillery test.
05-25.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
05-25.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

05-25.title: First ascent of Mount Kangchenjunga
05-25.subtitle: First ascent of Mount Kangchenjunga: On the British Kangchenjunga expedition led by Charles Evans, Joe Brown and George ...
05-25.text: First ascent of Mount Kangchenjunga: On the British Kangchenjunga expedition led by Charles Evans, Joe Brown and George Band reach the summit of the third-highest mountain in the world (8,586 meters); Norman Hardie and Tony Streather join them the following day.

05-25.title: Apollo program
05-25.subtitle: Apollo program: U.
05-25.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

05-25.title: Kapsan faction incident
05-25.subtitle: Having purged a group of rivals, Supreme Leader of North Korea Kim Il Sung delivered the "May 25 teaching", entrenching ...
05-25.text: The Kapsan faction incident was an unsuccessful attempt to undermine the power of Kim Il Sung, the leader of North Korea, around the year 1967. The "Kapsan faction" was a group of veterans of the anti-Japanese struggle of the 1930s and 1940s that was initially close to Kim Il Sung.
05-25.location: 1967 military solstice attempt in North Korea

05-25.title: Lebanon's Liberation Day
05-25.subtitle: A nation celebrates the end of occupation
05-25.text: Eighteen years after the Israeli invasion of 1982, the last Israeli soldiers withdrew from Lebanese territory, marking what would become known as Resistance and Liberation Day. The withdrawal, accomplished with the exception of the disputed Shebaa Farms zone, triggered celebrations across Lebanon and was claimed as a historic victory by Hezbollah. The date was enshrined as a national holiday, commemorating the end of one of the longest military occupations in the modern Middle East.

05-25.title: NASA
05-25.subtitle: NASA's Phoenix lander touches down in the Green Valley region of Mars to search for environments suitable for water and microbial life.
05-25.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

05-25.title: SpaceX Dragon 1
05-25.subtitle: The SpaceX Dragon 1 becomes the first commercial spacecraft to successfully rendezvous and berth with the International Space Station.
05-25.text: The SpaceX Dragon 1 becomes the first commercial spacecraft to successfully rendezvous and berth with the International Space Station.

05-25.title: Naxalite–Maoist insurgency
05-25.subtitle: Naxalite insurgents of the Communist Party of India (Maoist) attacked a convoy of Indian National Congress leaders in th...
05-25.text: Naxalite insurgents of the Communist Party of India (Maoist) attacked a convoy of Indian National Congress leaders in the state of Chhattisgarh, causing at least 27 deaths.

05-25.title: George Floyd
05-25.subtitle: George Floyd, a black American man, was murdered (memorial pictured) during an arrest by a white police officer in Minne...
05-25.text: George Perry Floyd Jr. was an African American man who was murdered by a white police officer in Minneapolis, Minnesota, during an arrest made after a store clerk suspected Floyd had used a counterfeit twenty-dollar bill, on May 25, 2020. One of four police officers who arrived on the scene, Derek Chauvin, knelt on Floyd's neck and back for over nine minutes, fatally asphyxiating him.

05-26.title: Germanicus Triumphs in Rome
05-26.subtitle: A beloved general parades through the capital
05-26.text: Through the ancient streets of Rome, the general Germanicus rode in triumph, celebrating his victories over the Cherusci, Chatti, and other Germanic tribes west of the Elbe. The spectacle was one of the grandest the capital had witnessed, with captured weapons, enslaved prisoners, and painted panoramas of battle paraded before cheering crowds. For the Roman people, Germanicus embodied the glory of conquest -- a golden prince whose premature death would transform him into a legend.

05-26.title: Battle of Palonegro
05-26.subtitle: Colombia's civil war reaches its turning point
05-26.text: On the blood-soaked fields of Palonegro in Colombia, the Conservative Party won a decisive victory over the Liberals in the costliest battle of the Thousand Days' War. Fought over two weeks of brutal combat in mountainous terrain, Palonegro turned the tide of Colombia's civil war and set the stage for Conservative dominance. The war itself would claim over a hundred thousand lives and leave scars on the nation's political landscape that persisted for generations.

05-26.title: Românul de la Pind
05-26.subtitle: Românul de la Pind, the longest-running newspaper by and about Aromanians until World War II, is founded.
05-26.text: Românul de la Pind, the longest-running newspaper by and about Aromanians until World War II, is founded.

05-26.title: Second World War
05-26.subtitle: Second World War: The Allies began a mass evacuation of British, French and Belgian troops cut off by the German army during the Battle of Dunkirk.
05-26.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

05-26.title: The Beatles
05-26.subtitle: The Beatles'  Sgt.
05-26.text: The Beatles were an English rock band formed in Liverpool in 1960. The core lineup of the band comprised John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr. They are widely regarded as the most influential band in popular music and were integral to the development of 1960s counterculture and the recognition of popular music as an art form.

05-26.title: Apollo program
05-26.subtitle: Apollo program: Apollo 10 returns to Earth after a successful eight-day test of all the components needed for the forthc...
05-26.text: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Apollo was conceived during Project Mercury and executed after Project Gemini. It was conceived in 1960 as a three-person spacecraft during the presidency of Dwight D. Eisenhower. Apollo was later dedicated to President John F.

05-26.title: Cold War
05-26.subtitle: Cold War: The United States and the Soviet Union sign the Anti-Ballistic Missile Treaty.
05-26.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
05-26.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

05-26.title: 1983 Sea of Japan earthquake
05-26.subtitle: The 7.
05-26.text: The 7.8 Mw  Sea of Japan earthquake shakes northern Honshu with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe). A destructive tsunami is generated that leaves about 100 people dead.

05-26.title: Tropical Storm Cecil
05-26.subtitle: Tropical Storm Cecil dissipated over Laos after devastating Quảng Nam province, Vietnam, and causing the deaths of 751 people.
05-26.text: Tropical Storm Cecil dissipated over Laos after devastating Quảng Nam province, Vietnam, and causing the deaths of 751 people.

05-26.title: Zviad Gamsakhurdia
05-26.subtitle: Zviad Gamsakhurdia becomes the first elected President of the Republic of Georgia in the post-Soviet era.
05-26.text: Zviad Gamsakhurdia becomes the first elected President of the Republic of Georgia in the post-Soviet era.

05-26.title: Manchester United F.C.
05-26.subtitle: Manchester United won the UEFA Champions League final to become the first English football club to win three major championships in the same season.
05-26.text: Manchester United won the UEFA Champions League final to become the first English football club to win three major championships in the same season.

05-26.title: Yeji Was Born
05-26.subtitle: K-pop's fierce leader takes her first breath
05-26.text: In Seoul, South Korea, Hwang Ye-ji was born -- the dancer and singer who would become the charismatic leader of the girl group Itzy. Formed by JYP Entertainment in 2019, Itzy burst onto the K-pop scene with an empowering message of self-confidence that resonated with a generation. Yeji's fierce performance style, combining razor-sharp choreography with magnetic stage presence, helped establish Itzy as one of the defining girl groups of fourth-generation K-pop.

05-26.title: I-40 bridge disaster
05-26.subtitle: Barges being towed destroyed part of a bridge (aftermath pictured) near Webbers Falls, Oklahoma, causing vehicles to fall into the Robert S.
05-26.text: Barges being towed destroyed part of a bridge (aftermath pictured) near Webbers Falls, Oklahoma, causing vehicles to fall into the Robert S. Kerr Reservoir on the Arkansas River.

05-27.title: Emperor Dezong Was Born
05-27.subtitle: A reformer tested by rebellion and ruin
05-27.text: In the Tang dynasty heartland, Li Kuo was born -- the prince who would become Emperor Dezong and reign for twenty-six years, the third longest in Tang history. He began as a reformer, introducing new tax laws and attempting to crush powerful regional warlords. But his military campaigns backfired spectacularly, triggering rebellions that nearly toppled the dynasty. The chastened emperor spent his later years governing cautiously, having learned the hard way that ambition must be tempered by wisdom.

05-27.title: Belarusian Poet Born
05-27.subtitle: A voice that would not be silenced
05-27.text: In Belarus, Uladzimir Zhylka was born -- a poet whose lyrical voice would help define Belarusian literary culture in its most fragile years. Writing in a language that was constantly under pressure from Russian dominance, Zhylka crafted verses that celebrated Belarusian identity, landscape, and spirit. His death in 1933 at just thirty-three robbed Belarusian literature of one of its most promising voices during an era when cultural expression was increasingly dangerous.

05-27.title: World War II
05-27.subtitle: World War II: In the Le Paradis massacre, 99 soldiers from a Royal Norfolk Regiment unit are shot after surrendering to German troops; two survive.
05-27.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

05-27.title: Landfill
05-27.subtitle: A fire at a landfill in Centralia, Pennsylvania, spread to an abandoned coal mine, where it continues burning today.
05-27.text: A landfill is a site for the disposal of waste materials, including municipal solid waste. It is the oldest and most common form of waste disposal, although the systematic burial of waste with daily, intermediate, and final covers only began in the 1940s. In the past, waste was simply left in piles or thrown into pits.
05-27.location: Site for the disposal of waste materials

05-27.title: Vietnam War
05-27.subtitle: Vietnam War: American warships begin the first bombardment of National Liberation Front targets within South Vietnam.
05-27.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
05-27.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

05-27.title: Bus
05-27.subtitle: The deadliest road accident in England took place when the brakes on a coach failed and it crashed in North Yorkshire, killing 32 people.
05-27.text: The deadliest road accident in England took place when the brakes on a coach failed and it crashed in North Yorkshire, killing 32 people.

05-27.title: Somaliland War of Independence
05-27.subtitle: Somaliland War of Independence: The Somali National Movement launches a major offensive against Somali government forces...
05-27.text: The Somaliland War of Independence was a rebellion waged by the Somali National Movement (SNM) against the ruling military junta in Somalia led by General Siad Barre lasting from its founding on 6 April 1981 and ended on 18 May 1991 when the SNM declared what was then northern Somalia independent as the Republic of Somaliland. The conflict served as the main theater of the larger Somali Rebellion that started in 1978.
05-27.location: 1981–1991 conflict part of Somali Civil War

05-27.title: Christopher Reeve
05-27.subtitle: American actor Christopher Reeve was thrown from his horse, leaving him quadriplegic; he later became an activist on beh...
05-27.text: American actor Christopher Reeve was thrown from his horse, leaving him quadriplegic; he later became an activist on behalf of people with spinal cord injuries.

05-27.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
05-27.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-96, the first shuttle mission to dock with the International Space Station.
05-27.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-96, the first shuttle mission to dock with the International Space Station.

05-27.title: Abner Vinicius Was Born
05-27.subtitle: Brazil's left-back pipeline keeps producing
05-27.text: In Brazil, Abner Vinicius da Silva Santos was born into the world's most prolific factory of football talent. He would develop into a dynamic left-back with the attacking instincts and technical ability that Brazilian defenders are famous for. His journey from Brazilian domestic football to France's Ligue 1 with Lyon and selection for the national team continues the long tradition of Brazilian full-backs who combine defensive responsibility with creative flair.

05-27.title: 2006 Yogyakarta earthquake
05-27.subtitle: An earthquake registering 6.
05-27.text: An earthquake registering 6.4 Mw struck near the city of Yogyakarta on the southern side of the Indonesian island of Java, killing more than 5,700 people.

05-27.title: Barack Obama
05-27.subtitle: Barack Obama is the first president of the United States to visit Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park and meet Hibakusha.
05-27.text: Barack Hussein Obama II is an American politician who served as the 44th president of the United States from 2009 to 2017. A member of the Democratic Party, he was the first African American president. Obama previously served as a U.S. senator representing Illinois from 2005 to 2008 and as an Illinois state senator from 1997 to 2004.
05-27.location: President of the United States from 2009 to 2017

05-28.title: Eclipse Stops a Battle
05-28.subtitle: The sky goes dark and warriors drop their swords
05-28.text: According to Herodotus, a solar eclipse -- accurately predicted by the philosopher Thales of Miletus -- interrupted the Battle of Halys between the Lydians and the Medes. As day turned to night on the battlefield, the terrified warriors took it as a divine sign and immediately ceased fighting. The eclipse, one of the most famous astronomical events in ancient history, led to a peace treaty between the two kingdoms and stands as the earliest historical event dateable to a specific day.

05-28.title: Tommy Ladnier Was Born
05-28.subtitle: A trumpet like no other in New Orleans jazz
05-28.text: In Mandeville, Louisiana, Thomas James Ladnier was born into the cradle of jazz. He would become one of the most distinctive trumpet players of the early jazz era, his warm, expressive tone earning comparisons to the great Louis Armstrong himself. The influential French critic Hugues Panassie ranked Ladnier second only to Armstrong among all jazz trumpeters. His death at thirty-nine cut short a career that helped define the golden age of New Orleans jazz.

05-28.title: 28 May 1926 coup d'état
05-28.subtitle: The 28 May 1926 coup d'état: Ditadura Nacional is established in Portugal to suppress the unrest of the First Republic.
05-28.text: The 28 May 1926 coup d'état: Ditadura Nacional is established in Portugal to suppress the unrest of the First Republic.

05-28.title: Rise of Neville Chamberlain
05-28.subtitle: The rise of Neville Chamberlain culminated with his accession as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, being summoned to...
05-28.text: The early life, business career and political rise of Neville Chamberlain culminated on 28 May 1937, when he was summoned to Buckingham Palace to "kiss hands" and accept the office of Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. Chamberlain had long been regarded as Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin's political heir, and when Baldwin announced his retirement, Chamberlain was seen as the only possible successor.

05-28.title: Palestine Liberation Organization
05-28.subtitle: The Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) is founded, with Yasser Arafat elected as its first leader.
05-28.text: The Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) is founded, with Yasser Arafat elected as its first leader.

05-28.title: Falklands War
05-28.subtitle: Falklands War: The British War Cabinet, which never considered using nuclear weapons in the conflict, ordered that the n...
05-28.text: The Falklands War was a ten-week undeclared war between Argentina and the United Kingdom in 1982 over two British Overseas Territories in the South Atlantic: the Falkland Islands and its territorial dependency, South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands. The conflict began on 2 April 1982, when Argentina invaded and occupied the Falkland Islands, followed by the invasion of South Georgia the next day.

05-28.title: Mathias Rust
05-28.subtitle: Mathias Rust, a West German aviator, flew his Cessna 172 from Helsinki, Finland, through Soviet air defences, landing il...
05-28.text: Mathias Rust, a West German aviator, flew his Cessna 172 from Helsinki, Finland, through Soviet air defences, landing illegally near Red Square in Moscow.

05-28.title: Addis Ababa
05-28.subtitle: The capital city of Addis Ababa falls to the Ethiopian People's Revolutionary Democratic Front, ending both the Derg reg...
05-28.text: The capital city of Addis Ababa falls to the Ethiopian People's Revolutionary Democratic Front, ending both the Derg regime in Ethiopia and the Ethiopian Civil War.

05-28.title: Phil Foden Was Born
05-28.subtitle: Manchester's own prodigy enters the story
05-28.text: In Stockport, Greater Manchester, Philip Walter Foden was born just miles from the Etihad Stadium where he would one day dazzle the world. Rising through Manchester City's academy, he became the club's youngest-ever goalscorer in the League Cup and evolved into one of the Premier League's most gifted midfielders. His close control, creative vision, and eye for goal have drawn comparisons to the game's greatest playmakers, making him the heartbeat of Pep Guardiola's City dynasty.

05-28.title: The Football Association
05-28.subtitle: An independent commission appointed by the Football Association voted two-to-one to allow Wimbledon F.
05-28.text: An independent commission appointed by the Football Association voted two-to-one to allow Wimbledon F.C. to relocate from London to Milton Keynes.

05-28.title: Nepal
05-28.subtitle: The first meeting of the Constituent Assembly of Nepal formally declares Nepal a republic, ending the 240-year reign of the Shah dynasty.
05-28.text: The first meeting of the Constituent Assembly of Nepal formally declares Nepal a republic, ending the 240-year reign of the Shah dynasty.

05-28.title: Harambe
05-28.subtitle: Harambe, a gorilla, is shot to death after grabbing a three-year-old boy in his enclosure at the Cincinnati Zoo and Bota...
05-28.text: Harambe, a gorilla, is shot to death after grabbing a three-year-old boy in his enclosure at the Cincinnati Zoo and Botanical Garden, resulting in widespread criticism and sparking various internet memes.

05-29.title: Julian Wins at Ctesiphon
05-29.subtitle: A Roman emperor triumphs but cannot conquer
05-29.text: Under the walls of Ctesiphon, the mighty capital of the Sasanian Empire, Roman Emperor Julian defeated the Persian army in a pitched battle that showcased the last great offensive of pagan Rome. Julian's legions stormed the city's defenses and routed its garrison, but the capital itself proved impregnable. Unable to take the city and deep in enemy territory, Julian was forced to retreat -- a withdrawal during which he would be mortally wounded, ending the last serious challenge to Christianity's dominance.

05-29.title: Fort-Lamy Founded
05-29.subtitle: A French outpost becomes a future capital
05-29.text: French commander Emile Gentil established Fort-Lamy at the confluence of the Chari and Logone rivers in central Africa, planting the seed of what would become N'Djamena, the capital of Chad. The military outpost, named after a French officer killed in the Battle of Kouseri, grew from a colonial garrison into a thriving city. Renamed N'Djamena after independence, it stands today as the political and economic heart of one of Africa's largest nations.

05-29.title: The Rite of Spring
05-29.subtitle: During the premiere of the ballet Le Sacre du printemps by Igor Stravinsky  at the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées in Paris, ...
05-29.text: During the premiere of the ballet Le Sacre du printemps by Igor Stravinsky  at the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées in Paris, the avant-garde nature of the music and choreography caused a near-riot in the audience (report pictured).

05-29.title: Albert Einstein
05-29.subtitle: Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity is tested (later confirmed) by Arthur Eddington and Andrew Claude de la Cherois Crommelin.
05-29.text: Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity is tested (later confirmed) by Arthur Eddington and Andrew Claude de la Cherois Crommelin.

05-29.title: Edmund Hillary
05-29.subtitle: Edmund Hillary and Sherpa Tenzing Norgay become the first people to reach the summit of Mount Everest, on Tenzing Norgay's (adopted) 39th birthday.
05-29.text: Edmund Hillary and Sherpa Tenzing Norgay become the first people to reach the summit of Mount Everest, on Tenzing Norgay's (adopted) 39th birthday.

05-29.title: President of the United States
05-29.subtitle: U.
05-29.text: U.S. President Ronald Reagan begins his first visit to the Soviet Union when he arrives in Moscow for a superpower summit with the Soviet leader Mikhail Gorbachev.

05-29.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
05-29.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery completes the first docking with the International Space Station.
05-29.text: Space Shuttle Discovery completes the first docking with the International Space Station.

05-29.title: Gennaro Nigro Was Born
05-29.subtitle: An American soccer talent takes shape
05-29.text: In the United States, Gennaro Michael Nigro was born into a country where soccer was rapidly growing from a niche sport into a mainstream passion. His development as a midfielder through American soccer's expanding youth pipeline reflects the increasing depth of talent in the U.S. men's game, as a new generation of American players pushes toward the highest levels of the sport.

05-29.title: 2008 Iceland earthquake
05-29.subtitle: A doublet earthquake, of combined magnitude 6.
05-29.text: A doublet earthquake, of combined magnitude 6.1, strikes Iceland near the town of Selfoss, injuring 30 people.

05-29.title: Portland, Oregon
05-29.subtitle: Residents of Portland, Oregon, held a rally called Hands Across Hawthorne in response to an attack against a gay couple ...
05-29.text: Residents of Portland, Oregon, held a rally called Hands Across Hawthorne in response to an attack against a gay couple holding hands while crossing the Hawthorne Bridge.

05-30.title: Jerusalem's Walls Breached
05-30.subtitle: Roman legions close in on the Holy City
05-30.text: The armies of Titus breached the Second Wall of Jerusalem, driving the Jewish defenders back to their last line of defense. Roman engineers had methodically dismantled the city's fortifications while legionaries cut down every tree within fifteen kilometers to build a circumvallation -- a wall around the wall. The siege, which would culminate in the Temple's destruction, marked the end of Jewish political sovereignty for nearly two millennia and became one of history's most consequential military campaigns.

05-30.title: Cornelia Otis Skinner Born
05-30.subtitle: A daughter of the stage claims her spotlight
05-30.text: In Chicago, Cornelia Otis Skinner was born -- the daughter of famed actor Otis Skinner, she would carve her own distinguished career as both actress and author. Her one-woman shows became legendary for their wit and sophistication, while her books, including the bestselling 'Our Hearts Were Young and Gay,' charmed millions of readers. She proved that artistic talent could be inherited but had to be earned anew by each generation.

05-30.title: Auckland Harbour Bridge
05-30.subtitle: The Auckland Harbour Bridge, spanning Waitematā Harbour between the Saint Marys Bay and Northcote suburbs of Auckland, New Zealand, officially opened.
05-30.text: The Auckland Harbour Bridge, spanning Waitematā Harbour between the Saint Marys Bay and Northcote suburbs of Auckland, New Zealand, officially opened.

05-30.title: Buddhist crisis
05-30.subtitle: Buddhist crisis: A protest against pro-Catholic discrimination was held outside the National Assembly of South Vietnam i...
05-30.text: Buddhist crisis: A protest against pro-Catholic discrimination was held outside the National Assembly of South Vietnam in Saigon, the first open demonstration against President Ngô Đình Diệm.

05-30.title: Nigeria
05-30.subtitle: The Nigerian Eastern Region declares independence as the Republic of Biafra, sparking a civil war.
05-30.text: The Nigerian Eastern Region declares independence as the Republic of Biafra, sparking a civil war.

05-30.title: Curaçao
05-30.subtitle: A demonstration by workers and activists on the Caribbean island of Curaçao escalated into a violent uprising  which destroyed much of Willemstad.
05-30.text: A demonstration by workers and activists on the Caribbean island of Curaçao escalated into a violent uprising  which destroyed much of Willemstad.

05-30.title: Mariner program
05-30.subtitle: Mariner program: Mariner 9 is launched to map 70% of the surface, and to study temporal changes in the atmosphere and surface, of Mars.
05-30.text: Mariner program: Mariner 9 is launched to map 70% of the surface, and to study temporal changes in the atmosphere and surface, of Mars.

05-30.title: Japanese Red Army
05-30.subtitle: Members of the Japanese Red Army carried out the Lod Airport massacre in Tel Aviv, Israel, on behalf of PFLP External Op...
05-30.text: Members of the Japanese Red Army carried out the Lod Airport massacre in Tel Aviv, Israel, on behalf of PFLP External Operations, killing over 20 people and injuring almost 80 others.

05-30.title: Nuclear Testing
05-30.subtitle: Nuclear Testing: Pakistan conducts an underground test in the Kharan Desert.
05-30.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
05-30.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

05-30.title: Jared S. Gilmore Was Born
05-30.subtitle: A young actor finds his fairy tale role
05-30.text: In San Diego, California, Jared Scott Gilmore was born -- the child actor who would spend seven years playing Henry Mills in the ABC fantasy series 'Once Upon a Time.' Cast at age ten, he grew up on screen alongside a cast of fairy tale characters, becoming the heart and moral compass of the show. His long tenure in the role gave a generation of viewers a companion who grew from childhood through adolescence alongside them.

05-30.title: Convention on Cluster Munitions
05-30.subtitle: The Convention on Cluster Munitions, prohibiting the use, transfer, and stockpiling of cluster bombs, was adopted.
05-30.text: The Convention on Cluster Munitions, prohibiting the use, transfer, and stockpiling of cluster bombs, was adopted.

05-30.title: Crew Dragon Demo-2
05-30.subtitle: The Crew Dragon Demo-2 launches from the Kennedy Space Center, becoming the first crewed orbital spacecraft to launch fr...
05-30.text: The Crew Dragon Demo-2 launches from the Kennedy Space Center, becoming the first crewed orbital spacecraft to launch from the United States since 2011 and the first commercial flight to the International Space Station.

05-30.title: Donald Trump
05-30.subtitle: Donald Trump is convicted of falsifying business records in his New York trial, the first time a former President of the...
05-30.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
05-30.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

05-31.title: Emperor Stoned to Death
05-31.subtitle: A mob destroys Rome's fleeing ruler
05-31.text: As Vandal warships appeared on the horizon, Emperor Petronius Maximus attempted to flee Rome -- but a furious mob caught him and stoned him to death in the streets. His reign had lasted only two and a half months, during which he had alienated the Roman people by forcing the widow of his predecessor to marry him. Days later, the Vandals sacked Rome for two weeks, stripping the city of its remaining treasures in one of history's most infamous acts of plunder.

05-31.title: American Civil War
05-31.subtitle: American Civil War: Confederate troops under Joseph E.
05-31.text: American Civil War: Confederate troops under Joseph E. Johnston and G. W. Smith engaged Union forces under George B. McClellan at the Battle of Seven Pines outside Richmond, Virginia.

05-31.title: Lucile Godbold Was Born
05-31.subtitle: A pioneer of women's athletics takes flight
05-31.text: In South Carolina, Lucile Ellerbe Godbold was born -- a woman who would become one of the earliest American heroines of women's track and field. At the 1922 Women's World Games in Paris, she won a gold medal in shot put and a bronze in javelin, claiming more medals than any other competitor. In an era when women's athletics was barely tolerated, Godbold proved that female athletes could compete with power, grace, and determination at the highest international level.

05-31.title: South African Constitution of 1961
05-31.subtitle: The South African Constitution of 1961 becomes effective, thus creating the Republic of South Africa, which remains outs...
05-31.text: The South African Constitution of 1961 becomes effective, thus creating the Republic of South Africa, which remains outside the Commonwealth of Nations until 1 June 1994, when South Africa is returned to Commonwealth membership.

05-31.title: Uniform Monday Holiday Act
05-31.subtitle: In accordance with the Uniform Monday Holiday Act passed by the U.
05-31.text: In accordance with the Uniform Monday Holiday Act passed by the U.S. Congress in 1968, observation of Memorial Day occurs on the last Monday in May for the first time, rather than on the traditional Memorial Day of May 30.

05-31.title: Sinhalese people
05-31.subtitle: An organized mob of police and government-sponsored Sinhalese paramilitary forces began three days of attacks that led t...
05-31.text: An organized mob of police and government-sponsored Sinhalese paramilitary forces began three days of attacks that led to the burning of the Jaffna Library in Sri Lanka.

05-31.title: Gable Steveson Was Born
05-31.subtitle: Named for greatness, built for gold
05-31.text: In Apple Valley, Minnesota, Gable Dan Steveson was born -- named after the legendary wrestler Dan Gable and destined to live up to that namesake. At the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, he won gold in freestyle wrestling with a dramatic last-second reversal that became one of the Games' most iconic moments. A three-time age-group world champion and two-time NCAA champion at Minnesota, Steveson embodied the combination of explosive power and steely nerves that defines wrestling greatness.

05-31.title: Vanity Fair
05-31.subtitle: A Vanity Fair article revealed that the secret informant known as "Deep Throat", who had provided information about the ...
05-31.text: A Vanity Fair article revealed that the secret informant known as "Deep Throat", who had provided information about the Watergate scandal, was former FBI associate director Mark Felt (pictured).

05-31.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
05-31.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-124 carrying the second portion of the Japanese Kibō module to the International Space Station.
05-31.text: Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-124 carrying the second portion of the Japanese Kibō module to the International Space Station.

05-31.title: George Tiller
05-31.subtitle: American physician George Tiller, one of the few doctors in the country who performed late-term abortions, was shot and ...
05-31.text: American physician George Tiller, one of the few doctors in the country who performed late-term abortions, was shot and killed by Scott Roeder, an anti-abortion activist.

05-31.title: Shayetet 13
05-31.subtitle: Israeli Shayetet 13 commandos boarded the Gaza Freedom Flotilla while still in international waters trying to break the ...
05-31.text: Israeli Shayetet 13 commandos boarded the Gaza Freedom Flotilla while still in international waters trying to break the ongoing blockade of the Gaza Strip; nine Turkish citizens on the flotilla were killed in the ensuing violent affray.

05-31.title: 2013 El Reno tornado
05-31.subtitle: An extremely large, powerful, and erratic tornado struck Central Oklahoma, killing eight people and injuring more than 150 others.
05-31.text: The 2013 El Reno tornado was an extremely large, powerful, and erratic tornado that occurred and hit a rural part of Central Oklahoma during the early evening of Friday, May 31, 2013. This rain-wrapped, multiple-vortex tornado was the widest tornado ever recorded, peaking at 2.6 miles across. It was part of a larger weather system that produced dozens of tornadoes over the preceding days. The tornado initially formed at 6:03 p.m. CDT (23:03 UTC) about 8.3 miles (13.

06-01.title: Alfonso X Proclaimed King
06-01.subtitle: Castile crowns its wisest ruler
06-01.text: In the great cathedral of Seville, Alfonso X was proclaimed King of Castile, Leon, and Galicia, beginning a reign that would earn him the epithet 'the Wise.' A brilliant polymath, he commissioned the translation of Arabic scientific texts into Latin, reformed the Castilian legal code, and produced a monumental history of the world. His court became a beacon of learning where Christians, Muslims, and Jews collaborated in one of the medieval world's greatest intellectual enterprises.

06-01.title: Hap Day Was Born
06-01.subtitle: A Maple Leaf for life
06-01.text: In Owen Sound, Ontario, Clarence Henry Day was born -- the hockey man who would spend twenty-eight years serving the Toronto Maple Leafs in virtually every capacity. As a player, he captained the team; as a coach, he won five Stanley Cups; as an executive, he shaped the franchise's identity. Known as 'Hap' for his cheerful disposition, Day was inducted into the Hockey Hall of Fame in 1961, recognized as one of the most influential figures in Canadian hockey history.

06-01.title: Declaration of Conscience
06-01.subtitle: The Declaration of Conscience speech, by U.
06-01.text: The Declaration of Conscience speech, by U.S. Senator from Maine, Margaret Chase Smith, is delivered in response to Joseph R. McCarthy's speech at Wheeling, West Virginia.

06-01.title: Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty
06-01.subtitle: The Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty comes into effect.
06-01.text: The Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty was an arms control treaty between the United States and the Soviet Union. US President Ronald Reagan and Soviet General Secretary Mikhail Gorbachev signed the treaty on 8 December 1987. The US Senate approved the treaty on 27 May 1988, and Reagan and Gorbachev ratified it on 1 June 1988.

06-01.title: Cold War
06-01.subtitle: Cold War: George H.
06-01.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
06-01.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

06-01.title: Clinton National Airport
06-01.subtitle: On landing at Little Rock National Airport in the U.
06-01.text: Bill and Hillary Clinton National Airport, also known as Adams Field, is a joint civil-military airport on the east side of Little Rock, Arkansas, United States. It is operated by the Little Rock Municipal Airport Commission.
06-01.location: Airport in Little Rock, Arkansas, United States

06-01.title: Nepal's Royal Massacre
06-01.subtitle: A crown prince destroys his own dynasty
06-01.text: Inside the royal palace of Kathmandu, Crown Prince Dipendra of Nepal opened fire on his own family during a dinner gathering, killing his father King Birendra, his mother Queen Aishwarya, and seven other members of the royal family before turning the gun on himself. The massacre, reportedly triggered by a dispute over Dipendra's choice of bride, plunged Nepal into constitutional crisis and effectively ended the Shah dynasty's two-century reign over the Himalayan kingdom.

06-01.title: Cyclone Gonu
06-01.subtitle: Cyclone Gonu develops from an area of convection in the Arabian Sea, later becoming the worst recorded natural disaster in Oman.
06-01.text: Cyclone Gonu develops from an area of convection in the Arabian Sea, later becoming the worst recorded natural disaster in Oman.

06-01.title: General Motors
06-01.subtitle: General Motors files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
06-01.text: General Motors files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. It is the fourth largest United States bankruptcy in history.

06-01.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
06-01.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour makes its final landing after 25 flights.
06-01.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

06-01.title: Congress of Deputies
06-01.subtitle: For the first time since its transition to democracy, the Congress of Deputies in Spain passed a motion of no confidence against the government.
06-01.text: For the first time since its transition to democracy, the Congress of Deputies in Spain passed a motion of no confidence against the government.

06-02.title: Emperor Cao Mao Killed
06-02.subtitle: A puppet emperor's last desperate gamble
06-02.text: In the Wei capital of Luoyang, the young Emperor Cao Mao made a desperate attempt to overthrow his regent Sima Zhao, who had reduced the emperor to a mere figurehead. Leading a ragtag force of palace guards and servants, Cao Mao charged out of the palace gates -- only to be cut down by Sima Zhao's soldiers. The emperor's death, far from weakening the Sima clan, actually strengthened their grip on power and hastened the fall of the Wei dynasty during the Three Kingdoms period.

06-02.title: Johnny Weissmuller Was Born
06-02.subtitle: The swimmer who became Tarzan
06-02.text: In Timisoara, Hungary (now Romania), Janos Peter Weissmuller was born -- the swimmer who would dominate Olympic competition and then conquer Hollywood. Winning five gold medals across the 1924 and 1928 Olympics, he was the fastest swimmer alive before trading the pool for the jungle as cinema's most iconic Tarzan. His famous yell echoed through twelve films and became one of the most recognizable sounds in movie history, proving that greatness can take many forms.

06-02.title: 1962 FIFA World Cup
06-02.subtitle: During the FIFA World Cup, police had to intervene multiple times in fights between Chilean and Italian players in one o...
06-02.text: During the FIFA World Cup, police had to intervene multiple times in fights between Chilean and Italian players in one of the most violent games in football history.

06-02.title: Surveyor program
06-02.subtitle: Surveyor program: Surveyor 1 lands in Oceanus Procellarum on the Moon, becoming the first U.
06-02.text: The Surveyor program was a NASA program that, from June 1966 through January 1968, sent seven robotic spacecraft to the surface of the Moon. Its primary goal was to demonstrate the feasibility of soft landings on the Moon. The Surveyor craft were the first American spacecraft to achieve soft landing on an extraterrestrial body.
06-02.location: 1960s NASA program to soft-land robotic probes on the Moon

06-02.title: Emergency landing
06-02.subtitle: After an emergency landing due to an in-flight fire, 23 passengers aboard Air Canada Flight 797 were killed when a flash...
06-02.text: An emergency landing is a premature landing made by an aircraft in response to an emergency involving an imminent or ongoing threat to the safety and operation of the aircraft, or involving a sudden need for a passenger or crew on board to terminate the flight. It typically involves a forced diversion to the nearest or most suitable airport or airbase, or an off airport landing or ditching if the flight cannot reach an airfield.
06-02.location: Aircraft landing made in response to a crisis

06-02.title: Bosnian War
06-02.subtitle: Bosnian War: U.
06-02.text: The Bosnian War was an international armed conflict that took place in Bosnia and Herzegovina between 1992 and 1995. Following several earlier violent incidents, the war is commonly seen as having started on 6 April 1992 when the newly independent Republic of Bosnia and Herzegovina was internationally recognized. It ended on 21 November 1995 when the Dayton Accords were initialed.
06-02.location: 1992–1995 armed conflict in Bosnia and Herzegovina

06-02.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
06-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-91, the final mission of the Shuttle-Mir program.
06-02.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-91, the final mission of the Shuttle-Mir program.

06-02.title: Jay Idzes Was Born
06-02.subtitle: Two nations claim one defender's heart
06-02.text: In the Netherlands, Jay Noah Idzes was born to a family with Indonesian roots. Though raised and trained in Dutch football's famed youth system, he chose to represent Indonesia on the international stage, bringing European tactical discipline to the Indonesian national team. His career in Italian football's Serie A demonstrates how modern football's global reach creates players who bridge continents and cultures, enriching the sport wherever they play.

06-02.title: Mars
06-02.subtitle: Europe launches its first voyage to another planet, Mars.
06-02.text: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun. It is also known as the "Red Planet", for its orange-red appearance. Mars is a desert-like rocky planet with a tenuous atmosphere that is primarily carbon dioxide. At the average surface level the atmospheric pressure is a few thousandths of Earth's, atmospheric temperature ranges from −153 to 20 °C, and cosmic radiation is high.

06-02.title: Cumbria shootings
06-02.subtitle: A gunman carried out a shooting spree in Cumbria, England, killing 12 people and injuring 11 others before committing suicide.
06-02.text: A gunman carried out a shooting spree in Cumbria, England, killing 12 people and injuring 11 others before committing suicide.

06-02.title: Telangana
06-02.subtitle: The Indian state of Telangana was officially formed, with K.
06-02.text: Telangana is a state in India situated in the south-central part of the Indian subcontinent on the high Deccan Plateau. It is the eleventh largest state by area and the twelfth most populated state in India, according to the 2011 census. On 2 June 2014, Telangana was separated from the northwestern part of United Andhra Pradesh as a newly formed state, with Hyderabad as its capital.

06-02.title: Ankara
06-02.subtitle: Following a request from Ankara, the United Nations officially changed the name of the Republic of Turkey in the organiz...
06-02.text: Following a request from Ankara, the United Nations officially changed the name of the Republic of Turkey in the organization from what was previously known as "Turkey" to "Türkiye".

06-02.title: 2023 Odisha train collision
06-02.subtitle: A collision between two passenger trains and a parked freight train near the city of Balasore, Odisha, in eastern India ...
06-02.text: A collision between two passenger trains and a parked freight train near the city of Balasore, Odisha, in eastern India resulted in 296 deaths and more than 1,200 people injured.

06-03.title: Nepotianus Seizes Rome
06-03.subtitle: A pretender leads gladiators to the throne
06-03.text: In a scene that seemed to belong to an earlier, wilder age of Roman history, Nepotianus -- a minor member of the Constantinian dynasty -- proclaimed himself emperor and entered Rome at the head of a band of gladiators. His audacious coup seized the Eternal City but could not hold it; within a month, forces loyal to the usurper Magnentius defeated and killed him. Nepotianus's brief reign was a reminder that in the dying days of the Roman Empire, anyone with enough swords could reach for the purple.

06-03.title: Leo Picard Was Born
06-03.subtitle: A geologist who unlocked the desert's secrets
06-03.text: In Germany, Leo Picard was born -- the geologist who would emigrate to Palestine and become Israel's foremost expert on hydrogeology, the science of underground water. In a region where water is the most precious resource, Picard's work mapping aquifers and understanding the geological structure of the land was of incalculable practical importance. His research helped transform arid desert into productive farmland and shaped Israel's water management strategies for generations.

06-03.title: Maurice Herzog
06-03.subtitle: Herzog and Lachenal of the French Annapurna expedition become the first climbers to reach the summit of an 8,000-metre peak.
06-03.text: Herzog and Lachenal of the French Annapurna expedition become the first climbers to reach the summit of an 8,000-metre peak.

06-03.title: Buddhist crisis
06-03.subtitle: Buddhist crisis: South Vietnamese soldiers attacked Buddhist protesters  in Huế with liquid chemicals from tear gas gren...
06-03.text: Buddhist crisis: South Vietnamese soldiers attacked Buddhist protesters  in Huế with liquid chemicals from tear gas grenades, causing 67 people to be hospitalised.

06-03.title: Gemini 4
06-03.subtitle: The launch of Gemini 4, the first multi-day space mission by a NASA crew.
06-03.text: The launch of Gemini 4, the first multi-day space mission by a NASA crew. Ed White, a crew member, performs the first American spacewalk.

06-03.title: Southeast Asia Treaty Organization
06-03.subtitle: During a SEATO exercise in the South China Sea, a collision between HMAS Melbourne and USS Frank E.
06-03.text: During a SEATO exercise in the South China Sea, a collision between HMAS Melbourne and USS Frank E. Evans resulted in the latter vessel being cut in two and the deaths of 74 personnel.

06-03.title: Soviet Union
06-03.subtitle: A Soviet supersonic Tupolev Tu-144 crashes near Goussainville, France, killing 14, the first crash of a supersonic passenger aircraft.
06-03.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

06-03.title: Idi Amin
06-03.subtitle: Having invaded Uganda and deposed President Idi Amin, Tanzanian forces secured Uganda's western border, ending a seven-month war.
06-03.text: Having invaded Uganda and deposed President Idi Amin, Tanzanian forces secured Uganda's western border, ending a seven-month war.

06-03.title: Operation Blue Star
06-03.subtitle: Operation Blue Star, a military offensive, is launched by the Indian government at Harmandir Sahib, also known as the Go...
06-03.text: Operation Blue Star, a military offensive, is launched by the Indian government at Harmandir Sahib, also known as the Golden Temple, the holiest shrine for Sikhs, in Amritsar. The operation continues until June 6, with casualties, most of them civilians, in excess of 5,000.

06-03.title: Beabadoobee Was Born
06-03.subtitle: Bedroom recordings bloom into global stardom
06-03.text: In Iloilo City, Philippines, Beatrice Kristi Laus was born -- the girl who would become Beabadoobee and prove that a teenager with a guitar and a bedroom recording setup could conquer the music world. Moving to London as a child, she posted songs online that caught the attention of Dirty Hit Records. Her debut album 'Fake It Flowers' earned critical acclaim, and her dreamy, guitar-driven sound helped lead a revival of indie rock for a new generation.

06-03.title: Khartoum massacre
06-03.subtitle: Khartoum massacre: In Sudan, over 100 people are killed when security forces accompanied by Janjaweed militiamen storm a...
06-03.text: Khartoum massacre: In Sudan, over 100 people are killed when security forces accompanied by Janjaweed militiamen storm and open fire on a sit-in protest.

06-04.title: Emperor Harsha Was Born
06-04.subtitle: India's last great Buddhist ruler arrives
06-04.text: In northern India, Harshavardhana was born -- the prince who would become one of the subcontinent's most powerful and enlightened rulers. After his brother's assassination, Harsha forged the scattered kingdoms of northern India into a vast empire governed from the city of Kannauj. A patron of art, literature, and Buddhism, his forty-one-year reign represented the last flowering of Buddhist imperial power in India, a golden age described in vivid detail by the Chinese pilgrim Xuanzang.

06-04.title: Bhagat Puran Singh Was Born
06-04.subtitle: The saint who served the forgotten
06-04.text: In Rajewal, Punjab, Bhagat Puran Singh was born -- a man who would dedicate his entire life to serving the poorest and most vulnerable members of Indian society. In 1947, he founded Pingalwara, a home for the sick, disabled, and destitute in Amritsar that grew to serve thousands. An environmental campaigner ahead of his time, he raised awareness of pollution and soil erosion while writing books on ecology. His selfless devotion made him one of India's most beloved humanitarian figures.

06-04.title: The first Pulitzer Prizes are awarded
06-04.subtitle: The first Pulitzer Prizes are awarded: Laura E.
06-04.text: The first Pulitzer Prizes are awarded: Laura E. Richards, Maude H. Elliott, and Florence Hall receive the first Pulitzer for biography (for Julia Ward Howe). Jean Jules Jusserand receives the first Pulitzer for history for his work With Americans of Past and Present Days. Herbert B. Swope receives the first Pulitzer for journalism for his work for the New York World.

06-04.title: World War II
06-04.subtitle: World War II: The Dunkirk evacuation ends: the British Armed Forces completes evacuation of 338,000 troops from Dunkirk in France.
06-04.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

06-04.title: Cold War
06-04.subtitle: Cold War: In the Vienna summit, the Soviet premier Nikita Khrushchev sparks the Berlin Crisis by threatening to sign a s...
06-04.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
06-04.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

06-04.title: Major League Baseball
06-04.subtitle: Major League Baseball's Cleveland Indians hosted Ten Cent Beer Night, but had to forfeit the game to the Texas Rangers due to rioting by drunken fans.
06-04.text: Major League Baseball's Cleveland Indians hosted Ten Cent Beer Night, but had to forfeit the game to the Texas Rangers due to rioting by drunken fans.

06-04.title: Jerry Brown
06-04.subtitle: Governor Jerry Brown signed the California Agricultural Labor Relations Act, one of the first laws in the United States ...
06-04.text: Governor Jerry Brown signed the California Agricultural Labor Relations Act, one of the first laws in the United States guaranteeing collective bargaining rights to farmworkers.

06-04.title: Solidarity
