09-13.title: Yitzhak Rabin
09-13.subtitle: Israeli Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin shakes hands with Palestine Liberation Organization chairman Yasser Arafat at the W...
09-13.text: Yitzhak Rabin was an Israeli statesman and general who was the prime minister of Israel, having served two terms in office, 1974–1977, and from 1992 until his assassination in 1995. He was the first prime minister to have been born in Palestine.

09-13.title: Skies Reopen After 9/11
09-13.subtitle: America's planes fly again, changed forever
09-13.text: Two days after the September 11 attacks, civilian aircraft traffic resumed in United States airspace for the first time since the unprecedented grounding. The return of commercial flights came with dramatically heightened security measures that would permanently transform air travel. Armed marshals, reinforced cockpit doors, and new screening procedures became the new reality.

09-13.title: Corrupted Blood incident
09-13.subtitle: A software bug caused a simulated pandemic in the online video game World of Warcraft, serving as a model for epidemiolo...
09-13.text: A software bug caused a simulated pandemic in the online video game World of Warcraft, serving as a model for epidemiologists to understand how human interaction influences disease outbreaks.

09-14.title: Domitian Takes the Throne
09-14.subtitle: The last Flavian emperor ascends in Rome
09-14.text: The Roman Senate confirmed Domitian as emperor to succeed his brother Titus, completing the Flavian dynasty's hold on power. The last of the Flavians, Domitian would prove an efficient but autocratic ruler whose fifteen-year reign ended with his assassination. Ancient historians portrayed him as a tyrant, though modern scholarship has partially rehabilitated his reputation.

09-14.title: Roosevelt Becomes President
09-14.subtitle: America's youngest leader takes the oath
09-14.text: Theodore Roosevelt was sworn in as the twenty-sixth president at age forty-two, the youngest ever to hold the office, after President McKinley succumbed to an assassin's bullet. The energetic, progressive Roosevelt would transform the presidency into a dynamic force for reform, trust-busting, conservation, and American global engagement.

09-14.title: Totskoye nuclear exercise
09-14.subtitle: In a top secret nuclear test, a Soviet Tu-4 bomber drops a 40 kiloton atomic weapon just north of Totskoye village.
09-14.text: In a top secret nuclear test, a Soviet Tu-4 bomber drops a 40 kiloton atomic weapon just north of Totskoye village.

09-14.title: Congo Crisis
09-14.subtitle: Congo Crisis: Mobutu Sese Seko seizes power in a military coup, suspending parliament and the constitution.
09-14.text: Congo Crisis: Mobutu Sese Seko seizes power in a military coup, suspending parliament and the constitution.

09-14.title: Typhoon Sarah
09-14.subtitle: Typhoon Sarah dissipated after causing extensive damage along an erratic path across the Western Pacific, killing 71 in ...
09-14.text: Typhoon Sarah dissipated after causing extensive damage along an erratic path across the Western Pacific, killing 71 in Taiwan, the Philippines, and the Gotō Islands.

09-14.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
09-14.subtitle: The Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina declares the breakaway Croatian Republic of Herzeg-Bosnia to be illegal.
09-14.text: The Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina declares the breakaway Croatian Republic of Herzeg-Bosnia to be illegal.

09-14.title: Kiribati
09-14.subtitle: Kiribati, Nauru and Tonga join the United Nations.
09-14.text: Kiribati, Nauru and Tonga join the United Nations.

09-14.title: Windows Me Released
09-14.subtitle: Microsoft launches its most maligned system
09-14.text: Microsoft released Windows Millennium Edition to the consumer market. Intended as the successor to Windows 98 with enhanced multimedia features, it would become notorious for instability and frequent crashes, earning a reputation as one of the worst Windows versions ever released and hastening the transition to Windows XP.

09-14.title: Late-2000s financial crisis
09-14.subtitle: Late-2000s financial crisis: The Northern Rock bank received a liquidity support facility from the Bank of England, spar...
09-14.text: Late-2000s financial crisis: The Northern Rock bank received a liquidity support facility from the Bank of England, sparking a bank run—the United Kingdom's first in 150 years.

09-14.title: LIGO
09-14.subtitle: Physicists of the LIGO and Virgo projects first observed gravitational waves, the existence of which was predicted by Henri Poincaré in 1905.
09-14.text: Physicists of the LIGO and Virgo projects first observed gravitational waves, the existence of which was predicted by Henri Poincaré in 1905.

09-14.title: Abqaiq–Khurais attack
09-14.subtitle: Drone attacks on major processing facilities at Abqaiq and Khurais forced Saudi Arabia to cut more than half of its oil production.
09-14.text: Drone attacks on major processing facilities at Abqaiq and Khurais forced Saudi Arabia to cut more than half of its oil production.

09-14.title: Death of Queen Elizabeth II
09-14.subtitle: Death of Queen Elizabeth II: The Queen's coffin is taken from Buckingham Palace, placed on a gun carriage of The King's ...
09-14.text: Death of Queen Elizabeth II: The Queen's coffin is taken from Buckingham Palace, placed on a gun carriage of The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery and moved in a procession to Westminster Hall for her lying in state over the next four days with the queue of mourners stretching for miles along the River Thames.

09-15.title: Saicho, Buddhism's Pioneer Born
09-15.subtitle: The monk who would found a Japanese school
09-15.text: In the province of Omi, Japan, the Buddhist monk Saicho was born. He would found the Tendai school of Buddhism on Mount Hiei near Kyoto, one of the most influential religious institutions in Japanese history. His monastery became a center of learning that shaped Japanese Buddhism for centuries, producing the founders of nearly every major school that followed.

09-15.title: Donald Bailey Was Born
09-15.subtitle: The engineer whose bridge won a world war
09-15.text: In Rotherham, Yorkshire, Donald Bailey was born with an engineering mind that would help win World War II. He invented the Bailey bridge, a portable, pre-fabricated truss bridge that could be rapidly assembled under battlefield conditions. Field Marshal Montgomery declared that without it, 'we should not have won the war.' The design remains in use worldwide.

09-15.title: The Seven Year Itch
09-15.subtitle: The scene in The Seven Year Itch of Marilyn Monroe standing in a white dress over a subway grate was filmed by Billy Wilder.
09-15.text: The scene in The Seven Year Itch of Marilyn Monroe standing in a white dress over a subway grate was filmed by Billy Wilder.

09-15.title: Ku Klux Klan
09-15.subtitle: The Ku Klux Klan bombed the 16th Street Baptist Church, an African-American church in Birmingham, Alabama, killing four ...
09-15.text: The Ku Klux Klan bombed the 16th Street Baptist Church, an African-American church in Birmingham, Alabama, killing four girls and injuring at least 14 other people (memorial march pictured).

09-15.title: Soviet Union
09-15.subtitle: The Soviet Zond 5 spaceship is launched, becoming the first spacecraft to fly around the Moon and re-enter the Earth's atmosphere.
09-15.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

09-15.title: Greenpeace
09-15.subtitle: The first Greenpeace ship departs from Vancouver to protest against the upcoming Cannikin nuclear weapon test in Alaska.
09-15.text: Greenpeace is an independent global campaigning network, founded in Canada in 1971 by a group of environmental activists. Greenpeace states its goal is to "ensure the ability of the Earth to nurture life in all its diversity", and focuses its campaigning on worldwide issues such as climate change, deforestation, overfishing, commercial whaling, genetic engineering, anti-war, and anti-nuclear issues. It uses direct action, advocacy, research, and ecotage to achieve its goals.

09-15.title: Croatian National Resistance
09-15.subtitle: Three armed members of the Croatian National Resistance hijacked Scandinavian Airlines System Flight 130 in an attempt t...
09-15.text: Three armed members of the Croatian National Resistance hijacked Scandinavian Airlines System Flight 130 in an attempt to force the release of those arrested for the assassination of the Yugoslav ambassador the previous year.

09-15.title: Caesars Superdome
09-15.subtitle: At the Superdome in New Orleans, Muhammad Ali outpoints Leon Spinks in a rematch to become the first boxer to win the wo...
09-15.text: At the Superdome in New Orleans, Muhammad Ali outpoints Leon Spinks in a rematch to become the first boxer to win the world heavyweight title three times.

09-15.title: United States Senate Committee on the
09-15.subtitle: The Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously approves Sandra Day O'Connor to become the first female justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.
09-15.text: The Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously approves Sandra Day O'Connor to become the first female justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.

09-15.title: Felix Was Born
09-15.subtitle: Australia's gift to K-pop's Stray Kids
09-15.text: Felix Yongbok Lee was born in Sydney, Australia, to Korean parents. After training with JYP Entertainment in South Korea, he debuted as a member of Stray Kids in 2017. His remarkably deep voice, dancing ability, and bilingual charisma helped the group become one of K-pop's most successful acts, while his Australian background gave him unique global appeal.

09-15.title: Lehman Brothers
09-15.subtitle: Lehman Brothers files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, the largest bankruptcy filing in U.
09-15.text: Lehman Brothers files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, the largest bankruptcy filing in U.S. history.

09-15.title: Parsons Green train bombing
09-15.subtitle: A homemade bomb partially exploded on an eastbound District line train at Parsons Green tube station in West London, injuring 30 passengers.
09-15.text: A homemade bomb partially exploded on an eastbound District line train at Parsons Green tube station in West London, injuring 30 passengers.

09-16.title: Julia Drusilla Was Born
09-16.subtitle: A princess born to Rome's most famous family
09-16.text: Julia Drusilla, the daughter of the popular general Germanicus, was born into the most celebrated family in the Roman Empire. She would become the favorite sister of Emperor Caligula. Her death in 38 AD drove Caligula to grant her posthumous divine honors, the first Roman woman to receive such distinction.

09-16.title: Josef Schachter Was Born
09-16.subtitle: A rabbi in the Vienna Circle's orbit
09-16.text: Josef Schachter was born in Austria, where he became a rabbi who was also a member of the Vienna Circle, the legendary group of philosophers who transformed modern thought. From 1925 to 1936, he bridged the seemingly incompatible worlds of religious scholarship and analytical philosophy, demonstrating that faith and reason could coexist in a single brilliant mind.

09-16.title: Revolución Libertadora
09-16.subtitle: The military coup to unseat President Juan Perón of Argentina is launched at midnight.
09-16.text: The military coup to unseat President Juan Perón of Argentina is launched at midnight.

09-16.title: TCN
09-16.subtitle: TCN-9 Sydney is the first Australian television station to commence regular broadcasts.
09-16.text: TCN-9 Sydney is the first Australian television station to commence regular broadcasts.

09-16.title: Xerox 914
09-16.subtitle: The first successful photocopier, the Xerox 914, is introduced in a demonstration on live television from New York City.
09-16.text: The first successful photocopier, the Xerox 914, is introduced in a demonstration on live television from New York City.

09-16.title: National Hurricane Research Project
09-16.subtitle: The U.
09-16.text: The U.S. National Hurricane Research Project sought to weaken Hurricane Esther by seeding it with silver iodide, leading to the establishment of Project Stormfury.

09-16.title: Malaysia
09-16.subtitle: Malaysia was formed as an independent nation from the Federation of Malaya, the Colony of Singapore, the Crown Colony of...
09-16.text: Malaysia is a country in Southeast Asia. A federal constitutional monarchy, it consists of 13 states and three federal territories, separated by the South China Sea into two regions: Peninsular Malaysia on the Indochinese Peninsula and East Malaysia on the island of Borneo. Peninsular Malaysia shares land and maritime borders with Thailand, as well as maritime borders with Singapore, Vietnam, and Indonesia; East Malaysia shares land borders with Brunei and Indonesia, and maritime borders with the Philippines and Vietnam.

09-16.title: Elie Hobeika
09-16.subtitle: A Lebanese militia under the direct command of Elie Hobeika carried out a massacre in the Palestinian refugee camp of Sa...
09-16.text: A Lebanese militia under the direct command of Elie Hobeika carried out a massacre in the Palestinian refugee camp of Sabra and Shatila, killing at least 460 civilians.

09-16.title: Northern Xinjiang railway
09-16.subtitle: Construction of the Northern Xinjiang railway (terminus pictured) was completed between Ürümqi South and Alashankou, lin...
09-16.text: Construction of the Northern Xinjiang railway (terminus pictured) was completed between Ürümqi South and Alashankou, linking the railway lines of China and Kazakhstan and adding a sizeable portion to the Eurasian Land Bridge.

09-16.title: Pound sterling
09-16.subtitle: The British pound (coin pictured) was forced out of the European Exchange Rate Mechanism on Black Wednesday and suffered a major devaluation.
09-16.text: The British pound (coin pictured) was forced out of the European Exchange Rate Mechanism on Black Wednesday and suffered a major devaluation.

09-16.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
09-16.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-79 to dock with the Russian space station Mir.
09-16.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

09-16.title: Sam Howell Was Born
09-16.subtitle: A record-breaking quarterback from North Carolina
09-16.text: Sam Howell was born in Monroe, North Carolina, developing the arm strength and football IQ that made him one of the most prolific quarterbacks in UNC history. Setting school records for career passing yards and touchdown passes, he was drafted into the NFL and continues developing his craft with the Philadelphia Eagles.

09-16.title: Inspiration4
09-16.subtitle: Inspiration4, the first private orbital crewed spaceflight, is launched from the Kennedy Space Center.
09-16.text: Inspiration4, the first private orbital crewed spaceflight, is launched from the Kennedy Space Center.

09-17.title: Charles the Simple Born
09-17.subtitle: A Carolingian king with a misleading name
09-17.text: Charles the Simple, whose epithet meant 'straightforward' rather than foolish, was born into the declining Carolingian dynasty. As king of West Francia, his most significant act was granting Normandy to the Viking chieftain Rollo, a pragmatic decision that transformed raiders into settlers and gave the world the Normans.

09-17.title: Battle of Mabitac
09-17.subtitle: Filipino guerrillas score another victory
09-17.text: During the Philippine-American War, Filipino forces under General Juan Cailles defeated an American column at Mabitac. The battle was another guerrilla victory demonstrating the difficulty of pacifying a determined resistance. The Philippine insurgency foreshadowed the asymmetric conflicts that would define twentieth-century warfare.

09-17.title: People's Liberation Army Air Force Airborne
09-17.subtitle: The People's Liberation Army Air Force Airborne Corps (Then known as the PLAAF 1st Ground Forces Brigade) is founded.
09-17.text: The People's Liberation Army Air Force Airborne Corps (Then known as the PLAAF 1st Ground Forces Brigade) is founded.

09-17.title: Tintin in Tibet
09-17.subtitle: Tintin in Tibet, the twentieth volume of The Adventures of Tintin by the Belgian cartoonist Hergé and which he regarded ...
09-17.text: Tintin in Tibet is the twentieth volume of The Adventures of Tintin, the comics series by Belgian cartoonist Hergé. It was serialised weekly from September 1958 to November 1959 in Tintin magazine and published as a book in 1960. Hergé considered it his favourite Tintin adventure and an emotional effort, as he created it while suffering from traumatic nightmares and a personal conflict while deciding to leave his wife of three decades for a younger woman.
09-17.location: Comic album by Belgian cartoonist Hergé

09-17.title: Amman
09-17.subtitle: The Jordanian army entered Amman as part of operations to oust Palestinian fedayeen from the country in events later kno...
09-17.text: The Jordanian army entered Amman as part of operations to oust Palestinian fedayeen from the country in events later known as Black September (smoke over city pictured).

09-17.title: Space Shuttle Enterprise
09-17.subtitle: The Space Shuttle Enterprise is unveiled by NASA.
09-17.text: The Space Shuttle Enterprise is unveiled by NASA.

09-17.title: Solidarity
09-17.subtitle: Solidarity, a Polish trade union, was founded as the first independent labor union in an Eastern Bloc country.
09-17.text: Solidarity, a Polish trade union, was founded as the first independent labor union in an Eastern Bloc country.

09-17.title: Linux
09-17.subtitle: The first version of the Linux kernel (0.
09-17.text: The first version of the Linux kernel (0.01) is released to the Internet.

09-17.title: Bush Visits Islamic Center
09-17.subtitle: A president calls for unity after terror
09-17.text: Six days after the September 11 attacks, President George W. Bush visited the Islamic Center of Washington to condemn the rising wave of Islamophobia. Standing beside Muslim leaders, he declared that Islam was a religion of peace and that intimidation of Muslim Americans was contrary to American values. The speech was pivotal in the fraught post-9/11 atmosphere.

09-17.title: Fourpeaked Mountain
09-17.subtitle: Fourpeaked Mountain in Alaska erupts, marking the first eruption for the volcano in at least 10,000 years.
09-17.text: Fourpeaked Mountain in Alaska erupts, marking the first eruption for the volcano in at least 10,000 years.

09-17.title: Adbusters
09-17.subtitle: Adbusters, a Canadian anti-consumerist publication, organized a protest against corporate influence on democracy at Zucc...
09-17.text: The Adbusters Media Foundation is a Canadian-based not-for-profit, pro-environment organization founded in 1989 by Kalle Lasn and Bill Schmalz in Vancouver, British Columbia. Adbusters describes itself as "a global network of artists, activists, writers, pranksters, students, educators and entrepreneurs who want to advance the new social activist movement of the information age."

09-18.title: Emperor Trajan Was Born
09-18.subtitle: Rome's greatest warrior-emperor enters the world
09-18.text: In Italica, a Roman colony in Hispania, Marcus Ulpius Traianus was born. He would become Emperor Trajan, under whose rule the Roman Empire reached its maximum territorial extent. The first emperor born outside Italy, Trajan was declared optimus princeps, the best of rulers, a title no successor would ever match.

09-18.title: Father of Mauritian Independence
09-18.subtitle: A physician who became a nation's founding father
09-18.text: Seewoosagur Ramgoolam was born in Mauritius. He would become the architect of Mauritian independence, serving as the island's only chief minister, first prime minister, and fifth governor-general. Affectionately known as 'Chacha,' he guided the small Indian Ocean nation from colonial status to sovereignty, earning his place as the father of his country.

09-18.title: Korean War
09-18.subtitle: Korean War: The Korean People's Army retreated from the Battle of the Pusan Perimeter after six weeks of fighting, marki...
09-18.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

09-18.title: Dag Hammarskjöld
09-18.subtitle: United Nations Secretary-General Dag Hammarskjöld dies in an air crash while attempting to negotiate peace in the Katang...
09-18.text: United Nations Secretary-General Dag Hammarskjöld dies in an air crash while attempting to negotiate peace in the Katanga region of the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

09-18.title: Aeroflot Flight 213
09-18.subtitle: Aeroflot Flight 213 crashed into a mountain near Chersky Airport, killing 32 people.
09-18.text: Aeroflot Flight 213 crashed into a mountain near Chersky Airport, killing 32 people.

09-18.title: The Addams Family
09-18.subtitle: The first television adaptation of Charles Addams's "The Addams Family" premieres on ABC Television.
09-18.text: The first television adaptation of Charles Addams's "The Addams Family" premieres on ABC Television.

09-18.title: Voyager 1
09-18.subtitle: Voyager I takes the first distant photograph of the Earth and the Moon together.
09-18.text: Voyager I takes the first distant photograph of the Earth and the Moon together.

09-18.title: Anthrax Letters Mailed
09-18.subtitle: Bioterror arrives in America's mailboxes
09-18.text: One week after September 11, five letters laced with deadly anthrax spores were mailed to media outlets across the United States, initiating the worst biological attack in American history. The letters killed five people and paralyzed the postal system. The attacks deepened America's post-9/11 terror and launched one of the FBI's most complex investigations.

09-18.title: The CW
09-18.subtitle: The CW Television Network debuts in the US, following the merger of UPN and The WB.
09-18.text: The CW Television Network debuts in the US, following the merger of UPN and The WB.

09-18.title: CBS
09-18.subtitle: After 72 years on radio and television, CBS Television broadcasts the final episode of Guiding Light, the longest-runnin...
09-18.text: After 72 years on radio and television, CBS Television broadcasts the final episode of Guiding Light, the longest-running soap opera in American history.

09-19.title: Antoninus Pius Was Born
09-19.subtitle: The emperor who ruled through peace alone
09-19.text: Titus Aurelius Fulvus Boionius Arrius Antoninus was born near Lanuvium, Italy. As Emperor Antoninus Pius, he presided over the most peaceful and prosperous era of the Roman Empire, ruling for twenty-three years without fighting a single major war. His reign represents the apex of the Pax Romana, a golden age so complete that historians struggle to find dramatic events to record.

09-19.title: Ricardo Cortez Was Born
09-19.subtitle: Hollywood's original Latin lover was Austrian
09-19.text: Jacob Krantz was born in Vienna, Austria, but found fame in Hollywood under the stage name Ricardo Cortez, created to capitalize on the vogue for Latin lovers. The studio system remade the Austrian Jewish immigrant into a suave romantic lead, proving that in Hollywood, identity was infinitely malleable.

09-19.title: Jawaharlal Nehru
09-19.subtitle: Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Ayub Khan sign the Indus Waters Treaty for the control an...
09-19.text: Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Pakistani President Ayub Khan sign the Indus Waters Treaty for the control and management of the Indus, Chenab, Jhelum, Ravi, Sutlej and Beas rivers.

09-19.title: Kostas Georgakis
09-19.subtitle: Greek student Kostas Georgakis set himself on fire in Genoa, Italy, as a protest against the military junta of Georgios Papadopoulos.
09-19.text: Greek student Kostas Georgakis set himself on fire in Genoa, Italy, as a protest against the military junta of Georgios Papadopoulos.

09-19.title: Ötzi
09-19.subtitle: Ötzi, a well-preserved natural mummy of a man dating from about 3300 BC, was discovered by two German tourists in the Alps.
09-19.text: Ötzi, a well-preserved natural mummy of a man dating from about 3300 BC, was discovered by two German tourists in the Alps.

09-19.title: Industrial Society and Its Future
09-19.subtitle: Industrial Society and Its Future, the manifesto of American domestic terrorist Ted Kaczynski, was published in The Wash...
09-19.text: Industrial Society and Its Future, the manifesto of American domestic terrorist Ted Kaczynski, was published in The Washington Post almost three months after it was submitted.

09-19.title: Thai Military Coup
09-19.subtitle: Tanks roll through Bangkok's streets
09-19.text: The Royal Thai Army staged a coup d'etat against Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra, revoking the constitution and declaring martial law. The bloodless takeover, Thailand's first non-constitutional change in fifteen years, came after months of political crisis. Tanks encircled government buildings while Thaksin was at the UN General Assembly in New York, unable to prevent his overthrow.

09-19.title: Mariano Rivera
09-19.subtitle: Mariano Rivera surpassed Trevor Hoffman to become Major League Baseball's all-time career leader in saves.
09-19.text: Mariano Rivera surpassed Trevor Hoffman to become Major League Baseball's all-time career leader in saves.

09-19.title: Cumbre Vieja
09-19.subtitle: The Cumbre Vieja volcano, on the island of La Palma in the Canary Islands, erupts.
09-19.text: The Cumbre Vieja volcano, on the island of La Palma in the Canary Islands, erupts. The eruption lasts for almost three months, ending on December 13.

09-19.title: 2022 Michoacán earthquake
09-19.subtitle: A strong earthquake kills 2 and injures over 30 in Mexico's state of Michoacán.
09-19.text: A strong earthquake kills 2 and injures over 30 in Mexico's state of Michoacán.

09-19.title: Azerbaijan
09-19.subtitle: Azerbaijan launches a military offensive against the Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region; this leads to t...
09-19.text: Azerbaijan launches a military offensive against the Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region; this leads to the flight of the Armenian population.

09-20.title: Imperial Border Negotiations
09-20.subtitle: An empress and a king settle their frontier
09-20.text: Agnes of Poitou, regent of the Holy Roman Empire during her son Henry IV's minority, met with Andrew I of Hungary to negotiate the disputed border territory of Burgenland. The diplomatic encounter reflected the complex web of alliances and territorial claims that defined politics in the eleventh-century Holy Roman Empire.

09-20.title: Dorothy Vaughan Was Born
09-20.subtitle: The hidden figure who computed America's future
09-20.text: Dorothy Vaughan was born in Kansas City, Missouri. She became one of the most important yet long-overlooked figures in American space history. As the first African-American woman to supervise a group at NASA's Langley Research Center, she led calculations that helped send Americans into space. Her story, told in Hidden Figures, inspired millions.

09-20.title: Irish War of Independence
09-20.subtitle: Irish War of Independence: British police known as "Black and Tans" burn the town of Balbriggan and kill two local men i...
09-20.text: Irish War of Independence: British police known as "Black and Tans" burn the town of Balbriggan and kill two local men in revenge for an IRA assassination.

09-20.title: Hurricane Irene–Olivia
09-20.subtitle: Having weakened after making landfall in Nicaragua the previous day, Hurricane Irene regains enough strength to be renam...
09-20.text: Having weakened after making landfall in Nicaragua the previous day, Hurricane Irene regains enough strength to be renamed Hurricane Olivia, making it the first known hurricane to cross from the Atlantic Ocean into the Pacific.

09-20.title: Billie Jean King
09-20.subtitle: Billie Jean King defeated Bobby Riggs in straight sets at the Astrodome in Houston, Texas, in an internationally televis...
09-20.text: Billie Jean King defeated Bobby Riggs in straight sets at the Astrodome in Houston, Texas, in an internationally televised tennis match dubbed the "Battle of the Sexes".

09-20.title: The Cosby Show
09-20.subtitle: The Cosby Show, which became one of three U.
09-20.text: The Cosby Show, which became one of three U.S. television shows to have the highest ratings five years in a row, premiered with its pilot episode.

09-20.title: Margaret Thatcher
09-20.subtitle: British prime minister Margaret Thatcher (pictured) gave a landmark speech at the Belfry of Bruges, Belgium, against fed...
09-20.text: Margaret Hilda Thatcher, Baroness Thatcher, was a British stateswoman who served as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1979 to 1990 and Leader of the Conservative Party from 1975 to 1990. She was the longest-serving British prime minister of the 20th century and the first woman to hold the position. As prime minister, she implemented policies that came to be known as Thatcherism.
09-20.location: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1979 to 1990

09-20.title: South Ossetia
09-20.subtitle: South Ossetia declares its independence from Georgia.
09-20.text: South Ossetia, formally known as the State of Alania since 2017, or originally the Republic of South Ossetia is a partially recognised state in the South Caucasus. It has an officially stated population of just over 56,500 people (2022), who live in an area of 3,900 square kilometres (1,500 sq mi), with 33,000 living in the capital city, Tskhinvali. It borders only Russia and Georgia.
09-20.location: Partially recognized state in the South Caucasus

09-20.title: Rocket Attack on MI6
09-20.subtitle: Dissidents target British intelligence headquarters
09-20.text: The Real Irish Republican Army launched a rocket attack on MI6 headquarters at its distinctive riverside building in Vauxhall, London. The attack caused no casualties and minimal damage, but the audacity of striking Britain's spy agency sent a stark message that dissident republican groups remained capable of projecting violence into the heart of the British capital.

09-20.title: Islamabad Marriott Hotel bombing
09-20.subtitle: An explosive-laden truck detonated in front of the Marriott hotel in Islamabad, Pakistan, killing 54 people and injuring 266 others.
09-20.text: An explosive-laden truck detonated in front of the Marriott hotel in Islamabad, Pakistan, killing 54 people and injuring 266 others.

09-20.title: Don't ask, don't tell
09-20.subtitle: The United States military ends its "don't ask, don't tell" policy, allowing gay men and women to serve openly for the first time.
09-20.text: The United States military ends its "don't ask, don't tell" policy, allowing gay men and women to serve openly for the first time.

09-21.title: Emperor Avitus Enters Italy
09-21.subtitle: A Gallic senator claims Rome's crumbling throne
09-21.text: Emperor Avitus entered Italy with a Gallic army to consolidate power over the Western Roman Empire. A senator elevated through Visigothic support, his reign would last barely a year before he was deposed, a victim of the relentless power struggles that characterized the Western Empire's final decades.

09-21.title: Allen Lane Was Born
09-21.subtitle: The man who put great books in every pocket
09-21.text: Allen Lane was born in Bristol, England, and would revolutionize publishing by founding Penguin Books in 1935. His radical idea was simple: make high-quality literature available in affordable paperback editions. Penguin's distinctive covers became cultural icons, and Lane's democratic vision of reading transformed book publishing and literacy worldwide.

09-21.title: The Gambia
09-21.subtitle: The Gambia, Maldives and Singapore are admitted as members of the United Nations.
09-21.text: The Gambia, Maldives and Singapore are admitted as members of the United Nations.

09-21.title: Bahrain
09-21.subtitle: Bahrain, Bhutan and Qatar join the United Nations.
09-21.text: Bahrain, Bhutan and Qatar join the United Nations.

09-21.title: Boris Yeltsin
09-21.subtitle: Russian president Boris Yeltsin triggers a constitutional crisis when he suspends parliament and scraps the constitution.
09-21.text: Russian president Boris Yeltsin triggers a constitutional crisis when he suspends parliament and scraps the constitution.

09-21.title: Defense of Marriage Act
09-21.subtitle: The Defense of Marriage Act was passed by the United States Congress, prohibiting federal recognition of same-sex marria...
09-21.text: The Defense of Marriage Act was passed by the United States Congress, prohibiting federal recognition of same-sex marriage, while allowing states to adopt any marital definition of their choosing.

09-21.title: Murder of Ross Parker
09-21.subtitle: A hate crime sparks uncomfortable questions
09-21.text: In Peterborough, England, seventeen-year-old Ross Parker was murdered in a racially motivated attack. The case ignited debate over media coverage of racially motivated crimes and raised broader questions about racial tensions in multicultural Britain in the tense weeks following the September 11 attacks.

09-21.title: Galileo
09-21.subtitle: The Galileo spacecraft is terminated by sending it into Jupiter's atmosphere.
09-21.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
09-21.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

09-21.title: Westgate shopping mall attack
09-21.subtitle: Unidentified gunmen began a three-day attack on the upmarket Westgate shopping mall in Nairobi, Kenya, resulting in the ...
09-21.text: Unidentified gunmen began a three-day attack on the upmarket Westgate shopping mall in Nairobi, Kenya, resulting in the deaths of 67 people with at least another 175 wounded.

09-21.title: 2019 Albania earthquake
09-21.subtitle: A 5.
09-21.text: On 26 November 2019 at 03:54 CET (UTC+1), northwestern Albania was struck by a magnitude 6.4 earthquake with an epicentre 16 kilometres (9.9 mi) west-southwest of Mamurras. The earthquake lasted at least 50 seconds and was felt in Albania's capital Tirana, and in places as far away as Bari, Taranto and Belgrade, 370 kilometres (230 mi) northeast of the epicentre. The maximum felt intensity was VIII (Severe) on the Modified Mercalli intensity scale.

09-22.title: Tang Emperor Assassinated
09-22.subtitle: A warlord murders his puppet sovereign
09-22.text: The ruthless warlord Zhu Quanzhong killed Emperor Zhaozong of the Tang dynasty after seizing the imperial government. The assassination of the penultimate Tang emperor was the near-final act in the dissolution of one of China's greatest dynasties. Zhu would establish the Later Liang dynasty, plunging China into the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.

09-22.title: Paul Hugh Emmett Was Born
09-22.subtitle: A chemist who helped build the atomic age
09-22.text: Paul Hugh Emmett was born in Portland, Oregon. His pioneering work in catalysis advanced chemical reactions, while his contributions to the Manhattan Project helped separate uranium isotopes essential for the atomic bomb. His development of BET Theory, explaining gas adsorption on surfaces, remains fundamental to materials science.

09-22.title: Four Level Interchange
09-22.subtitle: The Four Level Interchange, first stack interchange in the world opened in Los Angeles.
09-22.text: The Four Level Interchange, first stack interchange in the world opened in Los Angeles.

09-22.title: François Duvalier
09-22.subtitle: François Duvalier (pictured), nicknamed Papa Doc, was elected President of Haiti as a populist before consolidating powe...
09-22.text: François Duvalier (pictured), nicknamed Papa Doc, was elected President of Haiti as a populist before consolidating power and ruling as a dictator for the rest of his life.

09-22.title: Mali
09-22.subtitle: The Sudanese Republic is renamed Mali after the withdrawal of Senegal from the Mali Federation.
09-22.text: The Sudanese Republic is renamed Mali after the withdrawal of Senegal from the Mali Federation.

09-22.title: India–Pakistan war of 1965
09-22.subtitle: The Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 between India and Pakistan over Kashmir ends after the United Nations calls for a ceasefire.
09-22.text: The Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 between India and Pakistan over Kashmir ends after the United Nations calls for a ceasefire.

09-22.title: Scarlet GN
09-22.subtitle: Red Dye No.
09-22.text: Red Dye No. 4 is banned by the US Food and Drug Administration after it is discovered that it causes tumors in the bladders of dogs.

09-22.title: Vela
09-22.subtitle: An American Vela satellite detected an unidentified flash of light near the Prince Edward Islands in the Indian Ocean, t...
09-22.text: An American Vela satellite detected an unidentified flash of light near the Prince Edward Islands in the Indian Ocean, thought to be a nuclear weapons test.

09-22.title: Military exercise
09-22.subtitle: During a military exercise, a Turkish Air Force Northrop F-5 crashes in Babaeski as a result of pilot error, killing one...
09-22.text: During a military exercise, a Turkish Air Force Northrop F-5 crashes in Babaeski as a result of pilot error, killing one crew member and also 65 soldiers on the ground.

09-22.title: Tugboat
09-22.subtitle: A tugboat towing a barge collided with a rail bridge in Mobile, Alabama, U.
09-22.text: A tugboat towing a barge collided with a rail bridge in Mobile, Alabama, U.S., deforming the tracks and causing the derailment of a passenger train eight minutes later, which killed 47 people and injured an additional 103.

09-22.title: Nordhordland Bridge
09-22.subtitle: The Nordhordland Bridge, crossing Salhusfjorden between Klauvaneset and Flatøy in Vestland, and Norway's second-longest bridge, officially opened.
09-22.text: The Nordhordland Bridge, crossing Salhusfjorden between Klauvaneset and Flatøy in Vestland, and Norway's second-longest bridge, officially opened.

09-22.title: Stephen Crichton Was Born
09-22.subtitle: Samoa's rugby league powerhouse arrives
09-22.text: Stephen Crichton was born in Sydney, Australia, to Samoan heritage. Rising through Australian rugby league, he became captain of the Canterbury Bulldogs and a key player for the Samoan national team. His physicality and skill earned him selection for New South Wales in the State of Origin series, the pinnacle of rugby league competition.

09-22.title: Insurgency in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
09-22.subtitle: Insurgency in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: All Saints Church in Peshawar, Pakistan, was attacked by two suicide bombers who killed 127 people.
09-22.text: Insurgency in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: All Saints Church in Peshawar, Pakistan, was attacked by two suicide bombers who killed 127 people.

09-23.title: Augustus Caesar Was Born
09-23.subtitle: The boy who would build an empire
09-23.text: In Rome, Gaius Octavius was born into an equestrian family of modest ambition. Adopted by Julius Caesar, the boy who would become Augustus transformed the Roman Republic into the Roman Empire, initiating the Pax Romana. His political genius, patience, and ruthless pragmatism made him arguably the most consequential leader in Western history.

09-23.title: Bill Stone Was Born
09-23.subtitle: One of the last links to the Great War
09-23.text: William Frederick Stone was born in Devon, England, and lived long enough to become one of the last surviving World War I veterans in the UK's armed forces. Though still in training when the Great War ended, he saw action in World War II. His extraordinary longevity, reaching 108 years, made him a living bridge between the century's defining conflicts.

09-23.title: Korean War
09-23.subtitle: Korean War: The Battle of Hill 282 is the first US friendly-fire incident on British military personnel since World War II.
09-23.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

09-23.title: Richard Nixon
09-23.subtitle: After being accused of financial improprieties, Senator Richard Nixon delivers his "Checkers speech" nationwide on telev...
09-23.text: Richard Milhous Nixon was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974. A member of the Republican Party, he represented California in both houses of the United States Congress before serving as the 36th vice president under President Dwight D. Eisenhower from 1953 to 1961. His presidency saw the reduction of U.S.
09-23.location: President of the United States from 1969 to 1974

09-23.title: Little Rock schools integration crisis
09-23.subtitle: Little Rock schools integration crisis: President Dwight D.
09-23.text: Little Rock schools integration crisis: President Dwight D. Eisenhower sends the 101st Airborne Division to Little Rock, Arkansas, and federalizes the Arkansas National Guard, ordering both to support the integration of Little Rock Central High School.

09-23.title: John F. Kennedy
09-23.subtitle: U.
09-23.text: John Fitzgerald Kennedy, also known as JFK, was the 35th president of the United States, serving from 1961 until his assassination in 1963. He was the youngest person elected president at 43 years, as well as the first Catholic president. Kennedy served at the height of the Cold War, and the majority of his foreign policy concerned relations with the Soviet Union and Cuba.
09-23.location: President of the United States from 1961 to 1963

09-23.title: Lai Kuan-lin Was Born
09-23.subtitle: From Taipei to K-pop stardom and beyond
09-23.text: Lai Kuan-lin was born in Taipei, Taiwan, and crossed to South Korea to pursue entertainment. After Produce 101, he joined the massively popular Wanna One before launching a solo career. He later transitioned into acting and directing in China, demonstrating the fluid, cross-border nature of modern East Asian pop culture.

09-23.title: Kauhajoki school shooting
09-23.subtitle: A gunman shot and killed ten students at the Seinäjoki University of Applied Sciences in Kauhajoki, Finland, before committing suicide.
09-23.text: A gunman shot and killed ten students at the Seinäjoki University of Applied Sciences in Kauhajoki, Finland, before committing suicide.

09-23.title: Teresa Lewis
09-23.subtitle: Teresa Lewis became the first woman to be executed by the U.
09-23.text: Teresa Lewis became the first woman to be executed by the U.S. state of Virginia since 1912, and the first woman in the state to be executed via lethal injection.

09-23.title: Rape in Germany
09-23.subtitle: Following a number of high-profile sexual assaults, major reforms were enacted to strengthen laws related to rape in Germany.
09-23.text: Following a number of high-profile sexual assaults, major reforms were enacted to strengthen laws related to rape in Germany.

09-23.title: 2019 Papua protests
09-23.subtitle: Twenty people die on the first of two days of rioting in Papua and West Papua over an alleged racist incident.
09-23.text: The 2019 Papua protests were a series of protests by Papuans in Indonesia that began on 19 August 2019 and mainly took place across Indonesian Papua region in response to the arrests of 43 Papuan students in Surabaya, East Java for alleged disrespect of the Indonesian flag. Many of the protests involved thousands of participants, and some grew from local protests in Surabaya to demanding an independence referendum for the whole region.
09-23.location: Series of protests by Papuans in Indonesian Papua

09-24.title: Emperor Vitellius Was Born
09-24.subtitle: The doomed ruler of the Year of Four Emperors
09-24.text: Aulus Vitellius was born into a prominent Roman family, destined for a throne he would hold only briefly. During the Year of the Four Emperors, his legions proclaimed him emperor in Germania, and he marched on Rome. But his reign lasted just eight months before Vespasian's forces captured the capital. Vitellius was killed and his body thrown into the Tiber.

09-24.title: Ham Fisher Was Born
09-24.subtitle: The cartoonist behind America's boxing hero
09-24.text: Hammond Edward Fisher was born in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania. His creation Joe Palooka, a kind-hearted heavyweight boxing champion, became one of the top five newspaper comic strips after its 1930 launch. The beloved character endured for decades, reflecting America's love for the underdog who fights fair.

09-24.title: USS Enterprise
09-24.subtitle: USS Enterprise, the world's first nuclear-powered aircraft carrier, is launched.
09-24.text: USS Enterprise, the world's first nuclear-powered aircraft carrier, is launched.

09-24.title: Warren Commission
09-24.subtitle: The Warren Commission released its report to the U.
09-24.text: The Warren Commission released its report to the U.S. president, concluding that Lee Harvey Oswald acted alone in the assassination of John F. Kennedy. The report was made public three days later.

09-24.title: Dougal Haston
09-24.subtitle: Dougal Haston and Doug Scott of the Southwest Face expedition became the first people to reach the summit of Mount Evere...
09-24.text: Dougal Haston and Doug Scott of the Southwest Face expedition became the first people to reach the summit of Mount Everest by ascending one of its faces.

09-24.title: Oba Chandler
09-24.subtitle: After his neighbor identified handwriting samples placed on local billboards by police, Oba Chandler was arrested three ...
09-24.text: After his neighbor identified handwriting samples placed on local billboards by police, Oba Chandler was arrested three years after he committed a triple murder in the Tampa Bay area in Florida.

09-24.title: Norodom Sihanouk
09-24.subtitle: Norodom Sihanouk (pictured) became King of Cambodia with the restoration of the monarchy after a 23-year interregnum.
09-24.text: Norodom Sihanouk (pictured) became King of Cambodia with the restoration of the monarchy after a 23-year interregnum.

09-24.title: Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty
09-24.subtitle: Representatives of 71 nations sign the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty at the United Nations.
09-24.text: The Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty (CTBT) is a multilateral treaty to ban nuclear weapons test explosions and any other nuclear explosions, for both civilian and military purposes, in all environments. It was adopted by the United Nations General Assembly on 10 September 1996, but has not entered into force, as it has not been ratified by all the required states. The treaty will establish the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty Organization to oversee monitoring, which is currently done by a Preparatory Commission.
09-24.location: 1996 treaty banning all nuclear weapons testing

09-24.title: Gaeul of IVE Was Born
09-24.subtitle: A future K-pop star enters the world
09-24.text: Kim Ga-eul was born in South Korea and would train under Starship Entertainment before debuting as Gaeul, a member of the massively successful girl group IVE. The group's rapid rise confirmed the Korean music industry's unparalleled ability to identify, train, and launch global pop stars who captivate audiences across every continent.

09-24.title: Saffron Revolution
09-24.subtitle: Between 30,000 and 100,000 people take part in anti-government protests in Yangon, Burma, the largest in 20 years.
09-24.text: Between 30,000 and 100,000 people take part in anti-government protests in Yangon, Burma, the largest in 20 years.

09-24.title: Mars Orbiter Mission
09-24.subtitle: The Mars Orbiter Mission makes India the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit, and the first nation in the world to do so in its first attempt.
09-24.text: The Mars Orbiter Mission makes India the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit, and the first nation in the world to do so in its first attempt.

09-24.title: Supreme Court of the United Kingdom
09-24.subtitle: The Supreme Court of the United Kingdom unanimously ruled that advice given by Prime Minister Boris Johnson to Queen Eli...
09-24.text: The Supreme Court of the United Kingdom unanimously ruled that advice given by Prime Minister Boris Johnson to Queen Elizabeth II that Parliament should be prorogued was unlawful.

09-24.title: NASA
09-24.subtitle: NASA's OSIRIS-REx capsule containing samples from the asteroid 101955 Bennu successfully lands back on Earth.
09-24.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

09-25.title: Senate Chooses Tacitus
09-25.subtitle: An assassinated emperor's unlikely successor
09-25.text: After the assassination of Emperor Aurelian, who had reunited the shattered Roman Empire, the Senate selected the elderly senator Tacitus as his successor. For the first time in decades, the Senate rather than the military dictated the imperial succession. Tacitus's brief reign represented a fleeting moment of senatorial authority in an empire increasingly dominated by soldiers.

09-25.title: Artur Sirk Was Born
09-25.subtitle: Estonia's controversial right-wing leader
09-25.text: Artur Sirk was born in Estonia, where he became a veteran of the independence struggle and later a leading figure in the right-wing Vaps Movement. A lawyer and military officer, Sirk became an outspoken opponent of the Estonian government, channeling veterans' frustrations into a political force that challenged the young democracy's fragile institutions.

09-25.title: TAT-1
09-25.subtitle: TAT-1, the first submarine transatlantic telephone cable system, is inaugurated.
09-25.text: TAT-1, the first submarine transatlantic telephone cable system, is inaugurated.

09-25.title: United States Army
09-25.subtitle: United States Army troops escorted nine students as Central High School in Little Rock, Arkansas was integrated.
09-25.text: The United States Army is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces. It is designated as the army of the United States in the United States Constitution. As a part of the United States Department of Defense, it is one of the six armed forces of the United States and one of the eight uniformed services of the United States. The Army is the most senior branch in order of precedence amongst the armed services.
09-25.location: Land service branch of the U.S. military

09-25.title: North Yemen civil war
09-25.subtitle: The North Yemen Civil War began when Abdullah al-Sallal dethroned the newly crowned Imam al-Badr and declared Yemen to b...
09-25.text: The North Yemen Civil War began when Abdullah al-Sallal dethroned the newly crowned Imam al-Badr and declared Yemen to be a republic under his presidency.

09-25.title: Sandra Day O'Connor
09-25.subtitle: Sandra Day O'Connor (pictured) became the first female justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.
09-25.text: Sandra Day O'Connor (pictured) became the first female justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.

09-25.title: Penaia Ganilau
09-25.subtitle: Fijian Governor-General Penaia Ganilau is overthrown in a coup d'état led by Lieutenant colonel Sitiveni Rabuka.
09-25.text: Fijian Governor-General Penaia Ganilau is overthrown in a coup d'état led by Lieutenant colonel Sitiveni Rabuka.

09-25.title: Ram Rath Yatra
09-25.subtitle: The Ram Rath Yatra, a political–religious rally organised to erect a temple to the Hindu deity Rama on the site of the B...
09-25.text: The Ram Rath Yatra, a political–religious rally organised to erect a temple to the Hindu deity Rama on the site of the Babri Masjid in Ayodhya, began in the Indian state of Gujarat.

09-25.title: NASA
09-25.subtitle: NASA launches the Mars Observer.
09-25.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

09-25.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
09-25.subtitle: NASA launches Space Shuttle Atlantis on STS-86 to the Mir space station.
09-25.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

09-25.title: PauknAir Flight 4101
09-25.subtitle: PauknAir Flight 4101, a British Aerospace 146, crashes near Melilla Airport in Melilla, Spain, killing 38 people.
09-25.text: PauknAir Flight 4101, a British Aerospace 146, crashes near Melilla Airport in Melilla, Spain, killing 38 people.

09-25.title: Lilas Ikuta Was Born
09-25.subtitle: The voice behind Yoasobi's global sensation
09-25.text: Lilas Ikuta was born in Japan and would become one of the most distinctive voices in contemporary Japanese music. As the vocalist Ikura of Yoasobi, the duo whose novel-inspired songs crossed linguistic barriers to achieve global viral success, her clear, emotive soprano became the defining sound of a new generation of Japanese pop music.

09-26.title: Caesar Honors Venus
09-26.subtitle: A dictator dedicates a temple to his divine ancestor
09-26.text: Julius Caesar dedicated the Temple of Venus Genetrix in Rome, honoring the goddess whom he claimed as his family's mythical ancestor. The temple was as much a political statement as a religious one, linking the most powerful man in Rome to divine lineage. It stood for centuries as a monument to the ambition that both built and destroyed the Republic.

09-26.title: Suzanne Belperron Was Born
09-26.subtitle: The jeweler who never signed her work
09-26.text: Suzanne Belperron was born in Saint-Claude, France, in the Jura mountains. She became one of the twentieth century's most influential jewelry designers, whose bold, sculptural creations adorned royalty and socialites on both sides of the Atlantic. She never signed her pieces, declaring that her style was her signature.

09-26.title: Albert Einstein
09-26.subtitle: Albert Einstein publishes the third of his Annus Mirabilis papers, introducing the special theory of relativity.
09-26.text: Albert Einstein publishes the third of his Annus Mirabilis papers, introducing the special theory of relativity.

09-26.title: Cambodian–Vietnamese War
09-26.subtitle: The Cambodian–Vietnamese War formally ended with the withdrawal of Vietnamese forces from the People's Republic of Kampu...
09-26.text: The Cambodian–Vietnamese War formally ended with the withdrawal of Vietnamese forces from the People's Republic of Kampuchea, a client state it had implemented to overthrow Democratic Kampuchea and the Khmer Rouge.

09-26.title: The Beatles
09-26.subtitle: The Beatles completed the recording of John Lennon's song "Happiness Is a Warm Gun", regarded by all the band members as...
09-26.text: The Beatles were an English rock band formed in Liverpool in 1960. The core lineup of the band comprised John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr. They are widely regarded as the most influential band in popular music and were integral to the development of 1960s counterculture and the recognition of popular music as an art form.

09-26.title: Stanislav Petrov
09-26.subtitle: Soviet Air Force officer Stanislav Petrov identifies a report of an incoming nuclear missile as a computer error and not...
09-26.text: Soviet Air Force officer Stanislav Petrov identifies a report of an incoming nuclear missile as a computer error and not an American first strike, thus preventing nuclear war.

09-26.title: 1997 Umbria and Marche earthquakes
09-26.subtitle: An earthquake strikes the Italian regions of Umbria and the Marche, causing part of the Basilica of St.
09-26.text: An earthquake strikes the Italian regions of Umbria and the Marche, causing part of the Basilica of St. Francis at Assisi to collapse.

09-26.title: Prague Anti-Globalization Riots
09-26.subtitle: Twenty thousand protesters take on the world banks
09-26.text: Some twenty thousand protesters descended on Prague during the IMF and World Bank summits, and demonstrations turned violent. The protests were part of the growing global anti-capitalist movement, as citizens challenged the institutions they blamed for widening inequality and environmental destruction worldwide.

09-26.title: Yves Rossy
09-26.subtitle: Swiss pilot and inventor Yves Rossy (pictured) flew a wingpack powered by jet engines across the English Channel.
09-26.text: Swiss pilot and inventor Yves Rossy (pictured) flew a wingpack powered by jet engines across the English Channel.

09-26.title: Death of Linda Norgrove
09-26.subtitle: Scottish aid worker Linda Norgrove and three Afghan colleagues were kidnapped by members of the Taliban in Kunar Province, Afghanistan.
09-26.text: Scottish aid worker Linda Norgrove and three Afghan colleagues were kidnapped by members of the Taliban in Kunar Province, Afghanistan.

09-26.title: Hurricane Helene
09-26.subtitle: Hurricane Helene makes landfall in Perry, Florida as a Category 4 hurricane, killing over 250 people, causing US$78.
09-26.text: Hurricane Helene was a powerful and devastating tropical cyclone that caused widespread catastrophic damage and numerous fatalities across the Southeastern United States in late September 2024. It was the strongest hurricane on record to strike the Big Bend region of Florida, the deadliest Atlantic hurricane since Hurricane Maria in 2017, and the deadliest to strike the mainland U.S. since Hurricane Katrina in 2005.

09-27.title: Emperor Ninmyo Born
09-27.subtitle: A Japanese sovereign of the Heian golden age
09-27.text: The future Emperor Ninmyo was born during the flowering of the Heian period. He would reign from 833 to 850, presiding over a court renowned for poetry, calligraphy, and refined aesthetics. His rule fell within the golden age of Japanese imperial culture, when the court at Kyoto set cultural standards that would define Japanese civilization for centuries.

09-27.title: Wreck of the Old 97
09-27.subtitle: A train disaster that became a country song legend
09-27.text: The Southern Railway's Fast Mail, known as the Old 97, derailed at the Stillhouse Trestle near Danville, Virginia, while traveling at excessive speed. The crash killed eleven people and inspired one of America's most famous folk ballads, which became seminal in country music and sparked a landmark copyright lawsuit.

09-27.title: World War II
09-27.subtitle: World War II: Nazi Germany, Fascist Italy and the Empire of Japan signed the Tripartite Pact in Berlin, officially formi...
09-27.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

09-27.title: ETA political-military
09-27.subtitle: Two members of ETA political-military and three members of the Revolutionary Antifascist Patriotic Front, sentenced to d...
09-27.text: Two members of ETA political-military and three members of the Revolutionary Antifascist Patriotic Front, sentenced to death for murder, became the last people to be executed in Spain.

09-27.title: Richard Stallman
09-27.subtitle: American software developer Richard Stallman announced plans for the Unix-like operating system GNU, the first free soft...
09-27.text: American software developer Richard Stallman announced plans for the Unix-like operating system GNU, the first free software developed by the GNU Project.

09-27.title: National League for Democracy
09-27.subtitle: The National League for Democracy is formed by Aung San Suu Kyi and others to fight dictatorship in Myanmar.
09-27.text: The National League for Democracy is formed by Aung San Suu Kyi and others to fight dictatorship in Myanmar.

09-27.title: Battle of Kabul
09-27.subtitle: The Battle of Kabul ends in a Taliban victory; an Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan is established.
09-27.text: The Battle of Kabul ends in a Taliban victory; an Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan is established.

09-27.title: Google
09-27.subtitle: The Google internet search engine retroactively claims this date as its birthday.
09-27.text: Google LLC is an American multinational technology corporation focused on information technology, online advertising, search engine technology, email, cloud computing, software, quantum computing, e-commerce, consumer electronics, and artificial intelligence (AI). It has been referred to as "the most powerful company in the world" by the BBC, and is one of the world's most valuable brands. Google's parent company Alphabet Inc. has been described as a Big Tech company.

09-27.title: Zug Parliament Massacre
09-27.subtitle: A gunman attacks Swiss democracy itself
09-27.text: Friedrich Leibacher entered the parliament of the Canton of Zug disguised as a police officer and opened fire, killing fourteen people before taking his own life. It was the first assassination of Swiss politicians since the nineteenth century and shattered Switzerland's self-image as a haven of peace and democratic stability.

09-27.title: Zhai Zhigang
09-27.subtitle: CNSA astronaut Zhai Zhigang becomes the first Chinese person to perform a spacewalk.
09-27.text: CNSA astronaut Zhai Zhigang becomes the first Chinese person to perform a spacewalk.

09-27.title: Mount Ontake
09-27.subtitle: Mount Ontake in central Japan unexpectedly erupted, killing 63 people in the nation's deadliest eruption in more than 100 years.
09-27.text: Mount Ontake in central Japan unexpectedly erupted, killing 63 people in the nation's deadliest eruption in more than 100 years.

09-27.title: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War
09-27.subtitle: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan launches an offensive against the self-proclaimed Republic of Artsakh in the Nag...
09-27.text: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan launches an offensive against the self-proclaimed Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region, inhabited predominantly by ethnic Armenians.

09-28.title: Confucius Was Born
09-28.subtitle: A teacher whose words shaped half the world
09-28.text: In the state of Lu during the turbulent Spring and Autumn period, Kong Qiu was born, the man the world would know as Confucius. His teachings on morality, governance, and harmony became the philosophical foundation of Chinese civilization and influenced all of East Asia. Twenty-five centuries later, over a billion people still live in societies shaped by his wisdom.
09-28.location: State of Lu (modern Qufu, Shandong, China)

09-28.title: Isabel Pell Was Born
09-28.subtitle: A socialite who fought the Nazis underground
09-28.text: Isabel Townsend Pell was born into American high society but found her calling in the shadows of occupied France. During World War II, she joined the French Resistance, risking her life to fight Nazi occupation. Her courage earned her the Legion of Honour, transforming a debutante into a decorated hero of the underground war against fascism.

09-28.title: Color television
09-28.subtitle: CBS makes the first color televisions available for sale to the general public, but the product is discontinued less than a month later.
09-28.text: CBS makes the first color televisions available for sale to the general public, but the product is discontinued less than a month later.

09-28.title: 1961 Syrian coup d'état
09-28.subtitle: A military coup in Damascus effectively ends the United Arab Republic, the union between Egypt and Syria.
09-28.text: A military coup in Damascus effectively ends the United Arab Republic, the union between Egypt and Syria.

09-28.title: Whaam!
09-28.subtitle: Whaam!, now considered one of Roy Lichtenstein's most important works, debuted at an exhibition held at the Leo Castelli Gallery, New York City.
09-28.text: Whaam!, now considered one of Roy Lichtenstein's most important works, debuted at an exhibition held at the Leo Castelli Gallery, New York City.

09-28.title: Cold War
09-28.subtitle: Against the backdrop of the Cold War, the Canadian ice hockey team defeated the Soviet team in the Summit Series.
09-28.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
09-28.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

09-28.title: Pope John Paul I
09-28.subtitle: Pope John Paul I died only 33 days after his papal election due to an apparent myocardial infarction, resulting in the f...
09-28.text: Pope John Paul I died only 33 days after his papal election due to an apparent myocardial infarction, resulting in the first year of three popes since 1605.

09-28.title: Sharon Visits the Temple Mount
09-28.subtitle: A politician's visit ignites years of violence
09-28.text: Israeli opposition leader Ariel Sharon visited the Temple Mount in Jerusalem, a site sacred to both Jews and Muslims. Palestinians viewed the visit as a deliberate provocation. The following day, widespread protests erupted, rapidly escalating into the Second Intifada, a five-year uprising that would claim thousands of lives on both sides.

09-28.title: Typhoon Xangsane
09-28.subtitle: Typhoon Xangsane passed Manila on its way to causing more than 300 deaths, mostly in the Phillippines and Vietnam.
09-28.text: Typhoon Xangsane passed Manila on its way to causing more than 300 deaths, mostly in the Phillippines and Vietnam.

09-28.title: Falcon 1
09-28.subtitle: Falcon 1 becomes the first privately developed liquid-fuel ground-launched vehicle to put a payload into orbit by the RatSat mission.
09-28.text: Falcon 1 becomes the first privately developed liquid-fuel ground-launched vehicle to put a payload into orbit by the RatSat mission.

09-28.title: War in Somalia
09-28.subtitle: War in Somalia: Somali National Army forces and their AMISOM and Raskamboni allies launched an offensive against Al-Shab...
09-28.text: War in Somalia: Somali National Army forces and their AMISOM and Raskamboni allies launched an offensive against Al-Shabaab in the latter's last major stronghold of Kismayo.

09-28.title: 2018 Sulawesi earthquake and tsunami
09-28.subtitle: The 7.
09-28.text: The 7.5 Mw 2018 Sulawesi earthquake, which triggered a large tsunami, leaves 4,340 dead and 10,679 injured.

09-29.title: Pompey the Great Was Born
09-29.subtitle: Caesar's rival enters the world
09-29.text: Gnaeus Pompeius Magnus was born into a prominent Roman family and would rise to become one of the Republic's greatest military commanders. Known as Pompey the Great, he conquered vast territories and formed the First Triumvirate with Caesar and Crassus. His tragic end, murdered on an Egyptian beach after losing to Caesar, marked the death of the Republic itself.

09-29.title: Lanza del Vasto Was Born
09-29.subtitle: Italy's apostle of nonviolent resistance
09-29.text: In San Vito dei Normanni, southern Italy, Lanza del Vasto was born into a noble family. He would become a poet, philosopher, and one of Europe's most dedicated practitioners of nonviolent activism, inspired by time spent with Mahatma Gandhi in India. His founding of the Community of the Ark brought Gandhian principles to European soil.

09-29.title: Willie Mays
09-29.subtitle: Willie Mays (pictured) of the New York Giants made The Catch, one of the most famous defensive plays in the history of Major League Baseball.
09-29.text: Willie Mays (pictured) of the New York Giants made The Catch, one of the most famous defensive plays in the history of Major League Baseball.

09-29.title: Kyshtym disaster
09-29.subtitle: The Kyshtym disaster is the third-worst nuclear accident ever recorded.
09-29.text: The Kyshtym disaster is the third-worst nuclear accident ever recorded.

09-29.title: STS-26
09-29.subtitle: NASA launches STS-26, the first Space Shuttle mission since the Challenger disaster.
09-29.text: NASA launches STS-26, the first Space Shuttle mission since the Challenger disaster.

09-29.title: Walt Disney Animation Studios
09-29.subtitle: The award-winning Disney animated film Beauty and the Beast premiered while unfinished at the New York Film Festival.
09-29.text: The award-winning Disney animated film Beauty and the Beast premiered while unfinished at the New York Film Festival.

09-29.title: Jaden McDaniels Was Born
09-29.subtitle: A five-star recruit bound for the Timberwolves
09-29.text: Jaden McDaniels was born in Federal Way, Washington, into a basketball family producing two NBA players. A five-star recruit and McDonald's All-American, he played college basketball at Washington before being drafted by the Minnesota Timberwolves. His length, defensive versatility, and emerging offense made him a key piece of the Timberwolves' championship ambitions.

09-29.title: Burt Rutan
09-29.subtitle: Burt Rutan's Ansari SpaceShipOne performs a successful spaceflight, the first of two required to win the Ansari X Prize.
09-29.text: Burt Rutan's Ansari SpaceShipOne performs a successful spaceflight, the first of two required to win the Ansari X Prize.

09-29.title: John Roberts
09-29.subtitle: John Roberts became the 17th Chief Justice of the United States; he would be the first Chief Justice to serve for twenty...
09-29.text: John Roberts became the 17th Chief Justice of the United States; he would be the first Chief Justice to serve for twenty years since Melville Fuller in 1908.

09-29.title: Gol Transportes Aéreos Flight 1907
09-29.subtitle: Gol Transportes Aéreos Flight 1907 collided in mid-air with an Embraer Legacy business jet near Peixoto de Azevedo, Braz...
09-29.text: Gol Transportes Aéreos Flight 1907 collided in mid-air with an Embraer Legacy business jet near Peixoto de Azevedo, Brazil, killing 154 people and triggering a national aviation crisis.

09-29.title: Calder Hall nuclear power station
09-29.subtitle: Calder Hall, the world's first commercial nuclear power station, is demolished in a controlled explosion.
09-29.text: Calder Hall, the world's first commercial nuclear power station, is demolished in a controlled explosion.

09-29.title: Stock market crash
09-29.subtitle: The stock market crashes with the Dow Jones dropping a then record 778 points after the United States House of Represent...
09-29.text: The stock market crashes with the Dow Jones dropping a then record 778 points after the United States House of Representatives vote on the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act fails during the beginning stages of the Great Recession.

09-30.title: Theoderic Triumphs Again
09-30.subtitle: The Ostrogoth king defeats Odoacer once more
09-30.text: The Ostrogoths under Theoderic the Great defeated the forces of Odoacer for the second time, advancing their campaign to conquer Italy. Theoderic, sent by the Byzantine emperor to reclaim the peninsula, was proving an unstoppable force. Within years, he would establish the Ostrogothic Kingdom, ruling Italy with Roman administrative skill and Gothic military might.

09-30.title: Waldo Williams Was Born
09-30.subtitle: Wales's greatest poet of peace and nation
09-30.text: In Haverfordwest, Pembrokeshire, Waldo Williams was born into a Welsh-speaking family that nurtured his love of language. He became one of the leading Welsh-language poets of the twentieth century, celebrated for verse combining mystical spirituality, passionate pacifism, and fierce Welsh nationalism. His poetry speaks of belonging, community, and bonds between people and land.

09-30.title: Nestor Makhno
09-30.subtitle: Nestor Makhno and Fedir Shchus led insurgents to successfully ambush the Central Powers that occupied southern Ukraine during World War I.
09-30.text: Nestor Makhno and Fedir Shchus led insurgents to successfully ambush the Central Powers that occupied southern Ukraine during World War I.

09-30.title: USS Nautilus
09-30.subtitle: The U.
09-30.text: The U.S. Navy submarine USS Nautilus is commissioned as the world's first nuclear-powered vessel.

09-30.title: James Dean
09-30.subtitle: American film actor James Dean suffered fatal injuries in a head-on car accident near Cholame, California.
09-30.text: American film actor James Dean suffered fatal injuries in a head-on car accident near Cholame, California.

09-30.title: Botswana
09-30.subtitle: Bechuanaland declares its independence, and becomes the Republic of Botswana.
09-30.text: Bechuanaland declares its independence, and becomes the Republic of Botswana.

09-30.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
09-30.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on STS-68.
09-30.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

09-30.title: ICANN
09-30.subtitle: The Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN), a nonprofit organization that manages the assignment of...
09-30.text: The Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN), a nonprofit organization that manages the assignment of domain names and IP addresses in the Internet, was incorporated.

09-30.title: Tokaimura nuclear accidents
09-30.subtitle: The Tokaimura nuclear accident causes the deaths of two technicians in Japan's second-worst nuclear accident.
09-30.text: The Tokaimura nuclear accident causes the deaths of two technicians in Japan's second-worst nuclear accident.

09-30.title: Death of Muhammad al-Durrah
09-30.subtitle: A child's death captured on camera shocks the world
09-30.text: On the second day of the Second Intifada, twelve-year-old Muhammad al-Durrah was shot dead at the Netzarim Junction in the Gaza Strip while crouching with his father during crossfire. The footage, broadcast worldwide, became one of the most iconic and contested images of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, galvanizing international outrage.

09-30.title: Jyllands-Posten
09-30.subtitle: The Danish newspaper Jyllands-Posten published controversial editorial cartoons depicting Muhammad, sparking protests ac...
09-30.text: The Danish newspaper Jyllands-Posten published controversial editorial cartoons depicting Muhammad, sparking protests across the Islamic world by many who viewed them as Islamophobic and blasphemous.

09-30.title: 2009 Sumatra earthquakes
09-30.subtitle: The 7.
09-30.text: The 7.6 Mw  Sumatra earthquake leaves 1,115 people dead.

10-01.title: Alexander Conquers Persia
10-01.subtitle: The world bowed to a 25-year-old king
10-01.text: On the dusty plains of Gaugamela in modern-day Iraq, a young Macedonian king led his outnumbered forces against the vast Persian army of Darius III. Through brilliant cavalry tactics and sheer audacity, Alexander the Great shattered the Achaemenid Empire in a single afternoon. The victory opened the gates of Babylon and made Alexander master of the known world.

10-01.title: Ashfaqulla Khan Born
10-01.subtitle: A flame lit for India's freedom
10-01.text: In the waning light of British India, Ashfaqulla Khan was born into a world demanding liberation. He would grow to become one of India's most beloved freedom fighters, co-founding the Hindustan Republican Association alongside Ram Prasad Bismil. His fearless resistance and ultimate martyrdom at age 27 made him an enduring symbol of India's independence struggle.

10-01.title: Defense Intelligence Agency
10-01.subtitle: The United States Defense Intelligence Agency is formed, becoming the country's first centralized military intelligence organization.
10-01.text: The United States Defense Intelligence Agency is formed, becoming the country's first centralized military intelligence organization.

10-01.title: Indonesian Army
10-01.subtitle: At dawn, seven Indonesian Army officers and a police inspector were assassinated by the September 30 Movement at Lubang Buaya, East Jakarta.
10-01.text: At dawn, seven Indonesian Army officers and a police inspector were assassinated by the September 30 Movement at Lubang Buaya, East Jakarta. In addition, two more officers were killed in Yogyakarta. The Army pinned the murders to the Communist Party of Indonesia, resulting in mass killings of suspected leftists.

10-01.title: Israel-Palestinian conflict
10-01.subtitle: Israel-Palestinian conflict: Israel attacks the Palestine Liberation Organization's Tunisia headquarters during Operation Wooden Leg.
10-01.text: Israel-Palestinian conflict: Israel attacks the Palestine Liberation Organization's Tunisia headquarters during Operation Wooden Leg.

10-01.title: Croatian War of Independence
10-01.subtitle: Croatian War of Independence: Yugoslav People's Army forces invaded the area surrounding Dubrovnik, Croatia, beginning a...
10-01.text: Croatian War of Independence: Yugoslav People's Army forces invaded the area surrounding Dubrovnik, Croatia, beginning a seven-month siege of the city.

10-01.title: Constitution of the Republic of Singapore
