10-20.text: Two police officers and a Brink's armored car guard are killed during an armed robbery carried out by members of the Black Liberation Army and Weather Underground in Nanuet, New York.

10-20.title: Aeroflot Flight 6502
10-20.subtitle: Aeroflot Flight 6502 crashed on approach to Kurumoch Airport in Samara, Russia (then Kuybyshev in the Soviet Union), killing 70 people on board.
10-20.text: Aeroflot Flight 6502 crashed on approach to Kurumoch Airport in Samara, Russia (then Kuybyshev in the Soviet Union), killing 70 people on board.

10-20.title: Kenneth Walker III Born
10-20.subtitle: A future Super Bowl MVP enters the world
10-20.text: In Arlington, Texas, Kenneth Walker III was born with the speed and vision that would make him one of football's most explosive runners. After winning the Walter Camp and Doak Walker Awards at Michigan State, he was drafted by the Seattle Seahawks, where he would be named MVP of Super Bowl LX, cementing his place among the game's elite.

10-20.title: Sloan Great Wall
10-20.subtitle: The Sloan Great Wall, once the largest cosmic structures known to humanity, is discovered by students at Princeton University.
10-20.text: The Sloan Great Wall, once the largest cosmic structures known to humanity, is discovered by students at Princeton University.

10-20.title: Libyan Crisis
10-20.subtitle: Libyan Crisis: Rebel forces capture Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi and his son Mutassim in his hometown of Sirte and ki...
10-20.text: Libyan Crisis: Rebel forces capture Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi and his son Mutassim in his hometown of Sirte and kill them shortly thereafter, ending the first Libyan civil war.

10-20.title: Prabowo Subianto
10-20.subtitle: Prabowo Subianto and Gibran Rakabuming Raka were sworn in as president and vice president of Indonesia, becoming the old...
10-20.text: Prabowo Subianto and Gibran Rakabuming Raka were sworn in as president and vice president of Indonesia, becoming the oldest and the youngest person to assume respective offices.

10-21.title: Pope Eusebius Dies in Exile
10-21.subtitle: Sixty-five days of banishment end in silence
10-21.text: On the island of Sicily, Pope Eusebius breathed his last, just sixty-five days after being exiled by the tyrannical Emperor Maxentius. His brief and turbulent pontificate had been consumed by a fierce dispute over the readmission of Christians who had lapsed during persecution. His death in exile underscored the perilous intersection of papal authority and imperial power.

10-21.title: Nikos Engonopoulos Born
10-21.subtitle: Greece's surrealist painter-poet awakens
10-21.text: In Athens, Nikos Engonopoulos was born into the artistic ferment that would produce Greece's Generation of the '30s. He would become one of the most important figures in Greek surrealism, wielding both paintbrush and pen with equal mastery. His vivid canvases and dreamlike poetry opened windows into the Greek subconscious that had never before been explored.

10-21.title: Dwight D. Eisenhower
10-21.subtitle: President Dwight D.
10-21.text: President Dwight D. Eisenhower approves the transfer of all US Army space-related activities to NASA, including most of the Army Ballistic Missile Agency.

10-21.title: Spoil tip
10-21.subtitle: A coal tip fell on the village of Aberfan, Wales, killing 144 people, mostly schoolchildren.
10-21.text: A coal tip fell on the village of Aberfan, Wales, killing 144 people, mostly schoolchildren.

10-21.title: 1968–1969 Japanese university protests
10-21.subtitle: At the height of the Japanese university protests, protesters occupied Shinjuku Station in Tokyo and clashed violently with police.
10-21.text: At the height of the Japanese university protests, protesters occupied Shinjuku Station in Tokyo and clashed violently with police.

10-21.title: Niki Lauda
10-21.subtitle: Niki Lauda claims his third and final Formula One Drivers' Championship Title by half a point ahead of McLaren team-mate...
10-21.text: Niki Lauda claims his third and final Formula One Drivers' Championship Title by half a point ahead of McLaren team-mate Alain Prost at the Portuguese Grand Prix.

10-21.title: Jaffna hospital massacre
10-21.subtitle: The Jaffna hospital massacre is carried out by Indian peacekeeping forces in Sri Lanka, killing 70 Tamil patients, doctors and nurses.
10-21.text: The Jaffna hospital massacre is carried out by Indian peacekeeping forces in Sri Lanka, killing 70 Tamil patients, doctors and nurses.

10-21.title: Honduras
10-21.subtitle: In Honduras, 131 people are killed when a Boeing 727 crashes on approach to Toncontín International Airport near the nation's capital Tegucigalpa.
10-21.text: Honduras, officially the Republic of Honduras, is a country in Central America. It is bordered to the west by Guatemala, to the southwest by El Salvador, to the southeast by Nicaragua, to the south by the Pacific Ocean at the Gulf of Fonseca, and to the north by the Gulf of Honduras, a large inlet of the Caribbean Sea. Its capital and largest city is Tegucigalpa.

10-21.title: Agreed Framework
10-21.subtitle: North Korea and the United States signed the Agreed Framework to limit the former's nuclear weapons program and to norma...
10-21.text: North Korea and the United States signed the Agreed Framework to limit the former's nuclear weapons program and to normalize relations between the two countries.

10-21.title: Dwarf Planet Eris Photographed
10-21.subtitle: An unseen world reveals itself to the lens
10-21.text: At the Palomar Observatory in California, astronomers captured images of a distant object that would eventually be identified as Eris, the most massive dwarf planet in our solar system. The discovery of this icy world beyond Neptune's orbit would challenge our very definition of what constitutes a planet, ultimately contributing to Pluto's famous demotion.

10-21.title: Canada
10-21.subtitle: In Canada, the 2019 Canadian federal election ends, resulting in incumbent Prime Minister Justin Trudeau remaining in of...
10-21.text: In Canada, the 2019 Canadian federal election ends, resulting in incumbent Prime Minister Justin Trudeau remaining in office, albeit with the Liberal Party in a minority government.

10-22.title: Council of Chalcedon Decides
10-22.subtitle: Christ's nature defined for all time
10-22.text: At the Council of Chalcedon, gathered bishops adopted the Chalcedonian Definition, declaring Christ to be fully God and fully human in two natures 'without confusion, without change, without division, without separation.' This theological formula, hammered out in heated debate, became the bedrock of orthodox Christian doctrine for both Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches.

10-22.title: Curly Howard Born
10-22.subtitle: The funniest Stooge takes his first pratfall
10-22.text: In Brooklyn, New York, Jerome Howard was born into a family that would reshape American comedy. As Curly, the youngest of the Three Stooges, he would become the most beloved member of the legendary comedy trio. His signature blend of slapstick, sound effects, and childlike exuberance made him an icon of physical comedy for generations.

10-22.title: Cuban Missile Crisis
10-22.subtitle: Cuban Missile Crisis: President Kennedy, after internal counsel from Dwight D.
10-22.text: Cuban Missile Crisis: President Kennedy, after internal counsel from Dwight D. Eisenhower, announces that American reconnaissance planes have discovered Soviet nuclear weapons in Cuba, and that he has ordered a naval "quarantine" of the Communist nation.

10-22.title: Jean-Paul Sartre
10-22.subtitle: Jean-Paul Sartre is awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature, though he does not accept the prize.
10-22.text: Jean-Paul Sartre is awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature, though he does not accept the prize.

10-22.title: The Supremes A' Go-Go
10-22.subtitle: With their album The Supremes A' Go-Go, the Supremes became the first all-female group to reach number one on the Billboard 200 chart.
10-22.text: With their album The Supremes A' Go-Go, the Supremes became the first all-female group to reach number one on the Billboard 200 chart.

10-22.title: TAN-SAHSA Flight 414
10-22.subtitle: TAN-SAHSA Flight 414 crashed in approach to Toncontín International Airport killing 131 out of 146 people onboard.
10-22.text: TAN-SAHSA Flight 414 crashed in approach to Toncontín International Airport killing 131 out of 146 people onboard.

10-22.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
10-22.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-52 to deploy the LAGEOS-2 satellite and microgravity experiments.
10-22.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

10-22.title: Baby Keem Born
10-22.subtitle: Hip-hop royalty runs in the family
10-22.text: In Las Vegas, Hykeem Carter Jr. was born into a family already touched by musical greatness, as cousin to Kendrick Lamar. As Baby Keem, he would emerge as one of hip-hop's most inventive young voices, contributing to major projects before breaking through with his own acclaimed work, bridging avant-garde experimentation with mainstream appeal.

10-22.title: Exploration of the Moon
10-22.subtitle: India launches its first uncrewed lunar probe mission Chandrayaan-1.
10-22.text: India launches its first uncrewed lunar probe mission Chandrayaan-1.

10-22.title: Australian Capital Territory
10-22.subtitle: The Australian Capital Territory becomes the first Australian jurisdiction to legalize same-sex marriage with the Marria...
10-22.text: The Australian Capital Territory becomes the first Australian jurisdiction to legalize same-sex marriage with the Marriage Equality (Same Sex) Act 2013.

10-22.title: Ottawa
10-22.subtitle: In Ottawa, Canada, the downtown core was placed on lockdown after a series of shootings at Parliament Hill.
10-22.text: In Ottawa, Canada, the downtown core was placed on lockdown after a series of shootings at Parliament Hill.

10-22.title: Trollhättan school stabbing
10-22.subtitle: A sword-wielding man attacked students and teachers at a high school in Trollhättan, killing three people in Sweden's deadliest school attack.
10-22.text: A sword-wielding man attacked students and teachers at a high school in Trollhättan, killing three people in Sweden's deadliest school attack.

10-23.title: Ussher's Creation Date
10-23.subtitle: A bishop calculates the birthday of everything
10-23.text: According to the meticulous biblical chronology of Archbishop James Ussher, this was the very day God created the world, at approximately six o'clock in the evening. Ussher's calculation, published in his Annals of the World, became one of the most famous dates in theological history, shaping how generations of Christians understood the age of the universe.

10-23.title: Douglas Jardine Born
10-23.subtitle: The captain who unleashed Bodyline
10-23.text: In Bombay, British India, Douglas Jardine was born with the steely temperament that would make him cricket's most controversial captain. Leading England's 1932-33 Ashes tour, he deployed the infamous Bodyline tactics against Australia's Donald Bradman, ordering bowlers to pitch at batsmen's bodies. The strategy won the series but nearly shattered Anglo-Australian relations.

10-23.title: Belgium
10-23.subtitle: Belgian artist Peyo's comic characters, the Smurfs, make their debut in Spirou magazine.
10-23.text: Belgian artist Peyo's comic characters, the Smurfs, make their debut in Spirou magazine.

10-23.title: Vietnam War
10-23.subtitle: Vietnam War: The 1st Cavalry Division (Airmobile), in conjunction with the Army of the Republic of Vietnam, launches an ...
10-23.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
10-23.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

10-23.title: Vietnam War
10-23.subtitle: Vietnam War: Operation Linebacker, a U.
10-23.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
10-23.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

10-23.title: Alexander McQueen
10-23.subtitle: The runway show for Alexander McQueen's collection The Hunger was staged at London's Natural History Museum.
10-23.text: The runway show for Alexander McQueen's collection The Hunger was staged at London's Natural History Museum.

10-23.title: Grand Theft Auto III Released
10-23.subtitle: Gaming's open world was born on these streets
10-23.text: Rockstar Games released Grand Theft Auto III, a title that would fundamentally transform the video game industry. Set in the fictional Liberty City, the game pioneered open-world gameplay with unprecedented freedom, allowing players to explore a living, breathing urban environment. Its commercial and cultural impact helped establish video games as a dominant entertainment medium.

10-23.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
10-23.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-120, with Pamela Melroy becoming the second female space shuttle commander.
10-23.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-120, with Pamela Melroy becoming the second female space shuttle commander.

10-23.title: Hurricane Patricia
10-23.subtitle: Hurricane Patricia, the most intense tropical cyclone on record in the Western Hemisphere, peaked with maximum sustained...
10-23.text: Hurricane Patricia, the most intense tropical cyclone on record in the Western Hemisphere, peaked with maximum sustained winds of 215 mph (345 km/h) south of Mexico.

10-23.title: 2018 Rome escalator accident
10-23.subtitle: An escalator accident in Rome left 24 people injured, mostly fans of the CSKA Moscow football team who were in town for ...
10-23.text: An escalator accident in Rome left 24 people injured, mostly fans of the CSKA Moscow football team who were in town for a UEFA Champions League match against Roma.

10-23.title: Myanmar Air Force
10-23.subtitle: Myanmar Air Force airstrikes a concert in Hpakant Township, Kachin state killing at least 80 people, including senior Ka...
10-23.text: The Myanmar Air Force is the aerial branch of the Tatmadaw, the armed forces of Myanmar. The primary mission of the Myanmar Air Force (MAF) since its inception has been to provide air bases force protection, anti-aircraft warfare, close air support (CAS), logistical, and transport to the Myanmar Army in counterinsurgency operations. It is mainly used in internal conflicts in Myanmar, and, on a smaller scale, in relief missions, especially after the deadly Cyclone Nargis of May 2008.
10-23.location: Aerial branch of Myanmar's armed forces

10-24.title: Emperor Domitian Born
10-24.subtitle: Rome's last Flavian enters a troubled world
10-24.text: In Rome, Titus Flavius Domitianus was born, the younger son of the future Emperor Vespasian. He would become the last ruler of the Flavian dynasty, reigning as emperor for fifteen years. His rule combined administrative competence with increasing autocracy and paranoia, culminating in his assassination in 96 AD and the Senate's condemnation of his memory.

10-24.title: US Eyes Danish West Indies
10-24.subtitle: America shops for Caribbean real estate
10-24.text: The United States government announced plans to purchase the Danish West Indies for seven million dollars, eyeing the strategic Caribbean islands of Saint Thomas, Saint John, and Saint Croix. The deal, driven by American desire for a naval base in the Caribbean, would not be finalized until 1917, when the islands became the US Virgin Islands.

10-24.title: Annexation of Tibet by the People's
10-24.subtitle: Annexation of Tibet by the People's Republic of China: The People's Liberation Army ceases all military operations in Ti...
10-24.text: Annexation of Tibet by the People's Republic of China: The People's Liberation Army ceases all military operations in Tibet, ending the Battle of Chamdo.

10-24.title: Nedelin catastrophe
10-24.subtitle: Nedelin catastrophe: An R-16 ballistic missile explodes on the launch pad at the Soviet Union's Baikonur Cosmodrome spac...
10-24.text: Nedelin catastrophe: An R-16 ballistic missile explodes on the launch pad at the Soviet Union's Baikonur Cosmodrome space facility, killing over 100 people, including Field Marshal Mitrofan Nedelin.

10-24.title: Dương Văn Đức
10-24.subtitle: Charges in a military court against generals Dương Văn Đức and Lâm Văn Phát of leading a coup attempt against South Viet...
10-24.text: Charges in a military court against generals Dương Văn Đức and Lâm Văn Phát of leading a coup attempt against South Vietnamese leader Nguyễn Khánh, were dropped..

10-24.title: Falsework
10-24.subtitle: Three people were killed when falsework collapsed during the construction of the Loddon Bridge in Berkshire, leading to ...
10-24.text: Three people were killed when falsework collapsed during the construction of the Loddon Bridge in Berkshire, leading to reform of construction practices in the UK.

10-24.title: Deep Space 1
10-24.subtitle: Deep Space 1 is launched to explore the asteroid belt and test new spacecraft technologies.
10-24.text: Deep Space 1 is launched to explore the asteroid belt and test new spacecraft technologies.

10-24.title: Afro-Asian Games Open
10-24.subtitle: Two continents unite through sport in Hyderabad
10-24.text: In the historic Indian city of Hyderabad, the inaugural Afro-Asian Games opened with 2,040 athletes from ninety-six nations marching into the stadium. The event represented an ambitious attempt to forge athletic bonds between Africa and Asia, two continents home to the majority of humanity. Though the games were never repeated, they left a legacy of cross-continental fellowship.

10-24.title: Manchester United F.C.
10-24.subtitle: English football club Manchester United defeated rivals Arsenal 2–0 in the Battle of the Buffet, ending the latter's record-breaking unbeaten run.
10-24.text: English football club Manchester United defeated rivals Arsenal 2–0 in the Battle of the Buffet, ending the latter's record-breaking unbeaten run.

10-24.title: Chang'e 1
10-24.subtitle: Chang'e 1, the first satellite in the Chinese Lunar Exploration Program, is launched from Xichang Satellite Launch Center.
10-24.text: Chang'e 1, the first satellite in the Chinese Lunar Exploration Program, is launched from Xichang Satellite Launch Center.

10-24.title: China National Space Administration
10-24.subtitle: The China National Space Administration launches an experimental lunar mission, Chang'e 5-T1, which will loop behind the Moon and return to Earth.
10-24.text: The China National Space Administration launches an experimental lunar mission, Chang'e 5-T1, which will loop behind the Moon and return to Earth.

10-24.title: Lam Wing-kee
10-24.subtitle: Lam Wing-kee, the owner of Causeway Bay Books in Hong Kong, known for publishing political books banned in mainland Chin...
10-24.text: Lam Wing-kee, the owner of Causeway Bay Books in Hong Kong, known for publishing political books banned in mainland China, was abducted by Chinese authorities.

10-25.title: Leo II Named Caesar
10-25.subtitle: A grandfather crowns the empire's youngest hope
10-25.text: In Constantinople, Emperor Leo I acclaimed his young grandson Leo II as Caesar of the Eastern Roman Empire, establishing the child as his designated successor. The act secured the dynasty's continuity but also set the stage for the brief and tragic reign of a boy emperor who would hold sole power for less than a year before his untimely death.

10-25.title: Funmilayo Ransome-Kuti Born
10-25.subtitle: Nigeria's lioness of liberation awakens
10-25.text: In Abeokuta, Nigeria, Funmilayo Ransome-Kuti was born into a prominent family. She would become one of Nigeria's most formidable women, championing education, women's suffrage, and anti-colonial resistance. As a political campaigner and educator, she mobilized thousands of Nigerian women in the fight for justice, earning reverence as the mother of Nigeria's women's movement.

10-25.title: 1911 Revolution
10-25.subtitle: The Xinhai Revolution spreads to Guangzhou, where the Qing general Fengshan is assassinated by the Chinese Assassination Corps.
10-25.text: The Xinhai Revolution spreads to Guangzhou, where the Qing general Fengshan is assassinated by the Chinese Assassination Corps.

10-25.title: USS Tang
10-25.subtitle: USS Tang, the U.
10-25.text: USS Tang, the U.S. Navy submarine credited with sinking more ships than any other American submarine, sank when it was struck by its own torpedo.

10-25.title: People's Volunteer Army
10-25.subtitle: The People's Volunteer Army ambushed the South Korean II Corps at the Battle of Onjong, and elsewhere engaged the 1st In...
10-25.text: The People's Volunteer Army ambushed the South Korean II Corps at the Battle of Onjong, and elsewhere engaged the 1st Infantry Division at the Battle of Unsan, marking China's entry into the Korean War.
10-25.image_credit: 1280px-中国人民志愿军胸章.svg.png - Wikimedia Commons

10-25.title: China
10-25.subtitle: The People's Republic of China replaces the Republic of China at the United Nations.
10-25.text: The People's Republic of China replaces the Republic of China at the United Nations.

10-25.title: Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic
10-25.subtitle: The Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic declares its sovereignty from the Soviet Union.
10-25.text: The Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic declares its sovereignty from the Soviet Union.

10-25.title: Dominik Szoboszlai Born
10-25.subtitle: Hungary's midfield maestro takes shape
10-25.text: In Szekesfehervar, Hungary, Dominik Szoboszlai was born with the creative spark that would make him one of European football's most exciting midfielders. Rising through Hungarian youth ranks to captain his national team, he would sign with Liverpool and establish himself as a versatile force in the Premier League, electrifying fans with his vision and technique.

10-25.title: Microsoft
10-25.subtitle: Microsoft releases Windows XP, which becomes one of Microsoft's most successful operating systems.
10-25.text: Microsoft releases Windows XP, which becomes one of Microsoft's most successful operating systems.

10-25.title: 2010 Mentawai earthquake and tsunami
10-25.subtitle: A magnitude 7.
10-25.text: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake strikes off Indonesia's Mentawai Islands, triggering a tsunami that kills at least 400 people.

10-25.title: Liz Truss
10-25.subtitle: At 49 days, Liz Truss concluded the shortest tenure as prime minister of the United Kingdom.
10-25.text: At 49 days, Liz Truss concluded the shortest tenure as prime minister of the United Kingdom.

10-26.title: Bulgaria Rises Again
10-26.subtitle: A saint's feast day becomes liberation day
10-26.text: On the feast of St. Demetrius of Thessaloniki, the brothers Asen and Peter rallied Bulgarians and Vlachs in a fierce uprising against Byzantine rule. The revolt, sparked by crushing new taxes, erupted in Moesia and swept across the Balkans. Its triumph created the Second Bulgarian Empire, restoring Bulgarian sovereignty after more than a century of Byzantine domination.

10-26.title: Ibrahim Abboud Born
10-26.subtitle: Sudan's first military ruler enters history
10-26.text: In a small village in eastern Sudan, Ibrahim Abboud was born. A career soldier who served in World War II, he would rise to become Sudan's first military head of state after a 1958 coup. His six-year rule ended with a popular uprising in 1964, making his presidency both a harbinger of military governance in Africa and a cautionary tale about its limits.

10-26.title: Austrian State Treaty
10-26.subtitle: After the last Allied troops have left the country, and following the provisions of the Austrian Independence Treaty, Au...
10-26.text: After the last Allied troops have left the country, and following the provisions of the Austrian Independence Treaty, Austria declares that it will never join a military alliance.

10-26.title: Hungarian Revolution
10-26.subtitle: Hungarian Revolution: In the towns of Mosonmagyaróvár and Esztergom, Hungarian secret police forces massacre civilians.
10-26.text: Hungarian Revolution: In the towns of Mosonmagyaróvár and Esztergom, Hungarian secret police forces massacre civilians. As rebel strongholds in Budapest hold, fighting spreads throughout the country.

10-26.title: Space Race
10-26.subtitle: Space Race: The Soyuz 3 mission achieves the first Soviet space rendezvous.
10-26.text: The Space Race was a 20th-century competition between the Cold War rivals, the United States and the Soviet Union, to achieve superior spaceflight capability. It had its origins in the ballistic missile-based nuclear arms race between the two nations following World War II and the onset of the Cold War.

10-26.title: Israel–Jordan peace treaty
10-26.subtitle: Israel and Jordan signed a peace treaty, settling relations between the two countries and pledging that neither would al...
10-26.text: Israel and Jordan signed a peace treaty, settling relations between the two countries and pledging that neither would allow its territory to become a staging ground for military strikes by a third country.

10-26.title: Ivory Coast Changes Hands
10-26.subtitle: Protests topple a military strongman
10-26.text: Following massive street protests against military leader Robert Guei, who had attempted to steal the presidential election, Laurent Gbagbo was swept into power as president of Ivory Coast. The dramatic transfer of power through popular uprising demonstrated the fragility of military rule in West Africa, though the nation's troubles were far from over.

10-26.title: George W. Bush
10-26.subtitle: President George W.
10-26.text: George Walker Bush is an American politician, businessman, and former U.S. Air Force officer who served as the 43rd president of the United States from 2001 to 2009. A member of the Republican Party and the eldest son of the 41st president, George H. W. Bush, he served as the 46th governor of Texas from 1995 to 2000.
10-26.location: President of the United States from 2001 to 2009

10-26.title: Rockstar Games released Grand Theft Auto
10-26.subtitle: Rockstar Games released Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas, considered one of the best video games ever made, in North America.
10-26.text: Rockstar Games, Inc. is an American video game publisher based in New York City. The company was established in December 1998 as a subsidiary of Take-Two Interactive, using the assets Take-Two had previously acquired from BMG Interactive. Founding members of the company were Terry Donovan, Gary Foreman, Dan and Sam Houser, and Jamie King, who worked for Take-Two at the time, and of which the Houser brothers were previously executives at BMG Interactive. Sam Houser heads the studio as president.

10-26.title: Microsoft
10-26.subtitle: Microsoft made a public release of Windows 8 and made it available on new PCs.
10-26.text: Microsoft made a public release of Windows 8 and made it available on new PCs.

10-26.title: October 2015 Hindu Kush earthquake
10-26.subtitle: A 7.
10-26.text: A 7.5 magnitude earthquake strikes in the Hindu Kush mountain range in South Asia, killing 399 people and leaving 2,536 people injured.

10-27.title: Constantine's Vision of the Cross
10-27.subtitle: In this sign, an empire would be transformed
10-27.text: On the eve of a battle that would decide the fate of Rome, Constantine the Great reportedly looked to the sky and saw a cross of light above the sun, accompanied by the words 'In this sign, conquer.' Whether divine revelation or political mythology, the vision would justify Constantine's embrace of Christianity and set the stage for the faith's triumph across the Roman world.

10-27.title: New York Subway Opens
10-27.subtitle: An underground city begins to breathe
10-27.text: Beneath the bustling streets of Manhattan, the first underground segment of the New York City Subway opened, connecting City Hall with Harlem. The Interborough Rapid Transit line transformed urban transportation, carrying passengers through tunnels that would become the arteries of the world's greatest city. Within decades, the subway would move millions daily.

10-27.title: Benjamin O. Davis Jr.
10-27.subtitle: Benjamin O.
10-27.text: Benjamin O. Davis, Jr. becomes the first African-American general in the United States Air Force.

10-27.title: Iskander Mirza
10-27.subtitle: Iskander Mirza, the first President of Pakistan, is deposed by General Ayub Khan, who had been appointed the enforcer of...
10-27.text: Iskander Ali Mirza was a Pakistani politician and military general who served as the fourth and last governor-general of Pakistan from 1955 to 1956, and then as the first president of Pakistan from the promulgation of the first constitution in 1956 until his overthrow in a coup d'état in 1958, following his declaration of martial law and unilateral abrogation of the constitution.

10-27.title: Saturn I SA-1
10-27.subtitle: NASA tests the first Saturn I rocket in Mission Saturn-Apollo 1.
10-27.text: NASA tests the first Saturn I rocket in Mission Saturn-Apollo 1.

10-27.title: Ronald Reagan
10-27.subtitle: Ronald Reagan delivers a speech on behalf of the Republican candidate for president, Barry Goldwater.
10-27.text: Ronald Reagan delivers a speech on behalf of the Republican candidate for president, Barry Goldwater. The speech launches his political career and comes to be known as "A Time for Choosing".

10-27.title: Philip Berrigan
10-27.subtitle: American Catholic priest Philip Berrigan led a protest against the Vietnam War by pouring blood over Selective Service records in Baltimore, Maryland.
10-27.text: American Catholic priest Philip Berrigan led a protest against the Vietnam War by pouring blood over Selective Service records in Baltimore, Maryland.

10-27.title: Widerøe Flight 744
10-27.subtitle: Widerøe Flight 744 suffered a controlled flight into terrain while on approach to Namsos Airport, Norway, killing two cr...
10-27.text: Widerøe Flight 744 suffered a controlled flight into terrain while on approach to Namsos Airport, Norway, killing two crew members and four passengers.

10-27.title: Nairi Hunanyan
10-27.subtitle: Armed men led by Nairi Hunanyan carried out a mass shooting at the Armenian parliament, killing Prime Minister Vazgen Sa...
10-27.text: Armed men led by Nairi Hunanyan carried out a mass shooting at the Armenian parliament, killing Prime Minister Vazgen Sargsyan (pictured), National Assembly speaker Karen Demirchyan, and six others.

10-27.title: Red Sox Break the Curse
10-27.subtitle: Eighty-six years of heartbreak end in a sweep
10-27.text: Under the October moon, the Boston Red Sox completed a stunning four-game sweep of the St. Louis Cardinals to win the 2004 World Series, ending the legendary Curse of the Bambino. For eighty-six years, since Babe Ruth was sold to the Yankees, the championship had eluded Boston. The celebration that erupted across New England was a lifetime in the making.

10-27.title: Leicester City F.C.
10-27.subtitle: Leicester City F.
10-27.text: Leicester City F.C. owner Vichai Srivaddhanaprabha dies in a helicopter crash along with four others after a Premier League match against West Ham United at the King Power Stadium in Leicester, England.

10-28.title: Nerva Adopts Trajan
10-28.subtitle: A Praetorian ultimatum reshapes imperial succession
10-28.text: Under intense pressure from the Praetorian Guard, the aging Emperor Nerva was compelled to adopt the celebrated general Marcus Ulpius Trajanus as his heir. The forced adoption proved a masterstroke of succession, as Trajan would become one of Rome's greatest emperors, expanding the empire to its maximum extent and earning the title Optimus Princeps, the best ruler.

10-28.title: Ambrogio Gianotti Born
10-28.subtitle: A priest who chose resistance over silence
10-28.text: In Italy, Ambrogio Gianotti was born into a world he would one day risk his life to defend. As a Catholic priest during World War II, he joined the Italian resistance movement, becoming a partigiano who fought against fascism and Nazi occupation. His dual calling as man of God and man of action embodied the moral courage of Italy's wartime resistance.

10-28.title: St. Louis
10-28.subtitle: In St.
10-28.text: In St. Louis, Missouri, U.S., the 630-foot (190 m)-tall steel catenary Gateway Arch was completed.

10-28.title: Prospero
10-28.subtitle: Prospero becomes the only British satellite to be launched by a British rocket.
10-28.text: Prospero becomes the only British satellite to be launched by a British rocket.

10-28.title: Hans-Adam II, Prince of Liechtenstein
10-28.subtitle: Hans-Adam II threatened to dismiss the Landtag of Liechtenstein over disagreements on the date of a referendum for the country's accession to the EEA.
10-28.text: Hans-Adam II threatened to dismiss the Landtag of Liechtenstein over disagreements on the date of a referendum for the country's accession to the EEA.

10-28.title: Sonay Kartal Born
10-28.subtitle: Britain's rising star of the tennis court
10-28.text: In London, Sonay Kartal was born, destined to become one of British tennis's brightest prospects. Rising through the ranks with determination and skill, she would achieve a career-high world ranking of No. 44 in singles, win her first WTA Tour title, and establish herself as Britain's No. 2 singles player, a testament to grit and perseverance.

10-28.title: 2007 Argentine general election
10-28.subtitle: In the Argentine general election, Cristina Fernández de Kirchner became the first woman to be elected president of Argentina.
10-28.text: Argentina held national presidential and legislative elections on Sunday, 28 October 2007, and elections for provincial governors took place on staggered dates throughout the year. For the national elections, each of the 23 provinces and the Autonomous City of Buenos Aires are considered electoral districts. Voter turnout was 76.2%. Buenos Aires Province Senator and First Lady Cristina Fernández de Kirchner of the Front for Victory won the election by 45.

10-28.title: NASA
10-28.subtitle: NASA successfully launches the Ares I-X mission, the only rocket launch for its short-lived Constellation program.
10-28.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

10-28.title: Turkistan Islamic Party
10-28.subtitle: The first terrorist attack in Beijing's recent history took place when members of the Turkistan Islamic Party drove a ve...
10-28.text: The first terrorist attack in Beijing's recent history took place when members of the Turkistan Islamic Party drove a vehicle into a crowd, killing five people and injuring thirty-eight others.

10-28.title: Cygnus Orb-3
10-28.subtitle: A rocket carrying NASA's Cygnus CRS Orb-3 resupply mission to the International Space Station explodes seconds after tak...
10-28.text: A rocket carrying NASA's Cygnus CRS Orb-3 resupply mission to the International Space Station explodes seconds after taking off from the Mid-Atlantic Regional Spaceport in Wallops Island, Virginia.

10-28.title: Jair Bolsonaro
10-28.subtitle: Jair Bolsonaro is elected president of Brazil with 57 million votes, with Workers' Party candidate Fernando Haddad as the runner-up.
10-28.text: Jair Bolsonaro is elected president of Brazil with 57 million votes, with Workers' Party candidate Fernando Haddad as the runner-up. It is the first time in 16 years that a Workers' Party candidate is not elected president.

10-28.title: 2023 Rugby World Cup final
10-28.subtitle: The 2023 Rugby World Cup final is held at the Stade de France in Saint-Denis, France.
10-28.text: The 2023 Rugby World Cup final is held at the Stade de France in Saint-Denis, France. It saw South Africa defeat New Zealand 12 to 11, claiming their fourth Webb Ellis Cup, becoming the first nation to do so.

10-29.title: Constantine Enters Rome
10-29.subtitle: A triumphant march over a tyrant's corpse
10-29.text: Two days after his legendary vision, Constantine the Great entered Rome in a grand adventus following his decisive victory at the Battle of the Milvian Bridge. The crowds erupted in jubilation as the body of his rival Maxentius was fished from the Tiber and beheaded. The new master of Rome would transform the empire, legalizing Christianity and founding Constantinople.

10-29.title: Emilienne Morin Born
10-29.subtitle: Love and anarchy in a revolutionary's heart
10-29.text: In France, Emilienne Morin was born into an anarcho-syndicalist family that shaped her destiny as a lifelong revolutionary. As journalist, activist, and companion to the legendary Spanish anarchist Buenaventura Durruti, she lived a life of clandestine resistance across Europe. Her writings and legal defense work sustained the anarchist movement through its darkest hours.

10-29.title: Curtiss C-46 Commando
10-29.subtitle: A C-46 airliner carrying the Cal Poly Mustangs football team crashed during takeoff from Toledo Express Airport in Ohio, U.
10-29.text: A C-46 airliner carrying the Cal Poly Mustangs football team crashed during takeoff from Toledo Express Airport in Ohio, U.S., resulting in 22 deaths.

10-29.title: Computer
10-29.subtitle: The first-ever computer-to-computer link is established on ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet.
10-29.text: The first-ever computer-to-computer link is established on ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet.

10-29.title: Galileo
10-29.subtitle: The American Galileo spacecraft makes its closest approach to 951 Gaspra, becoming the first probe to visit an asteroid.
10-29.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
10-29.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

10-29.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
10-29.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery blasts off on STS-95 with 77-year-old John Glenn on board, making him the oldest person to go into space at that time.
10-29.text: Space Shuttle Discovery blasts off on STS-95 with 77-year-old John Glenn on board, making him the oldest person to go into space at that time.

10-29.title: 1999 Odisha cyclone
10-29.subtitle: About 10,000 people died when the most intense tropical cyclone ever recorded in the North Indian Ocean made landfall in...
10-29.text: About 10,000 people died when the most intense tropical cyclone ever recorded in the North Indian Ocean made landfall in the Indian state of Odisha near Bhubaneswar.

10-29.title: Bin Laden Claims 9/11
10-29.subtitle: A terrorist's confession on global television
10-29.text: Al Jazeera broadcast an excerpt of a video in which Osama bin Laden publicly admitted for the first time his direct responsibility for the September 11, 2001 attacks. Released just days before the American presidential election, the tape injected the specter of terrorism directly into the democratic process, reminding the world that the War on Terror's shadow loomed large.

10-29.title: Hurricane Sandy
10-29.subtitle: Hurricane Sandy, the largest Atlantic hurricane on record, made landfall in New Jersey and caused nearly $75 billion in ...
10-29.text: Hurricane Sandy, the largest Atlantic hurricane on record, made landfall in New Jersey and caused nearly $75 billion in damages, becoming the second-most destructive storm in U.S. history.

10-29.title: Marmaray
10-29.subtitle: The first phase of the Marmaray project opened with an undersea rail tunnel (train pictured) across the Bosphorus strait.
10-29.text: The first phase of the Marmaray project opened with an undersea rail tunnel (train pictured) across the Bosphorus strait.

10-29.title: Boeing 737 MAX
10-29.subtitle: A Boeing 737 MAX plane crashes after taking off from Jakarta, Indonesia killing 189 people on board.
10-29.text: A Boeing 737 MAX plane crashes after taking off from Jakarta, Indonesia killing 189 people on board.  This is the first of two crashes that will lead to the plane being grounded worldwide.

10-30.title: Julia the Elder Born
10-30.subtitle: Augustus' only child, Rome's most tragic daughter
10-30.text: In Rome, Julia the Elder was born to Octavian, the future Emperor Augustus, and his second wife Scribonia. She would become the most politically significant woman of the early Roman Empire, mother and grandmother to future emperors. Yet her life ended in exile and disgrace, banished by her own father for adultery, a punishment that reflected the ruthless politics of imperial Rome.

10-30.title: Ragnar Granit Born
10-30.subtitle: Eyes that would unlock the science of sight
10-30.text: In Helsinki, Finland, Ragnar Granit was born with the intellectual curiosity that would illuminate how the human eye perceives light. His pioneering research into the neurophysiology of vision earned him the 1967 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, shared with Haldan Hartline and George Wald, for discoveries about the primary visual processes in the eye.

10-30.title: Hungarian Revolution
10-30.subtitle: Hungarian Revolution: The government of Imre Nagy recognizes newly established revolutionary workers' councils.
10-30.text: Hungarian Revolution: The government of Imre Nagy recognizes newly established revolutionary workers' councils. Army officer Béla Király leads anti-Soviet militias in an attack on the headquarters of the Hungarian Working People's Party.

10-30.title: Jean Shrimpton
10-30.subtitle: English model Jean Shrimpton wore a controversially short minidress to Derby Day at Flemington Racecourse in Melbourne, ...
10-30.text: English model Jean Shrimpton wore a controversially short minidress to Derby Day at Flemington Racecourse in Melbourne, Australia – a pivotal moment of the introduction of the miniskirt to women's fashion.

10-30.title: Korean People's Army
10-30.subtitle: A squad of 120 North Korean Army commandos landed in boats along a 25-mile long section of the eastern coast of South Ko...
10-30.text: A squad of 120 North Korean Army commandos landed in boats along a 25-mile long section of the eastern coast of South Korea in a failed attempt to overthrow the dictatorship of Park Chung Hee and reunify Korea.

10-30.title: Inex-Adria Aviopromet Flight 450
10-30.subtitle: Forty-five people are killed when Inex-Adria Aviopromet Flight 450 crashes into Suchdol, Prague, while on approach to Pr...
10-30.text: Forty-five people are killed when Inex-Adria Aviopromet Flight 450 crashes into Suchdol, Prague, while on approach to Prague Ruzyně Airport (now Václav Havel Airport Prague) in Czechoslovakia (present-day Czech Republic).

10-30.title: National Reorganization Process
10-30.subtitle: As the military dictatorship came to an end, Argentina's first democratic election in a decade resulted in Raúl Alfonsín being elected president.
10-30.text: As the military dictatorship came to an end, Argentina's first democratic election in a decade resulted in Raúl Alfonsín being elected president.

10-30.title: Space Shuttle Challenger
10-30.subtitle: Space Shuttle Challenger lifts off for mission STS-61-A, its final successful mission.
10-30.text: Space Shuttle Challenger lifts off for mission STS-61-A, its final successful mission.

10-30.title: Giselle Is Born
10-30.subtitle: A future voice of K-pop's virtual frontier
10-30.text: In Tokyo, Aeri Uchinaga was born, destined to become Giselle, a member of the groundbreaking South Korean girl group Aespa. Formed by SM Entertainment, the group would pioneer the concept of virtual avatars in K-pop, blending real and digital performance. Giselle's multilingual talents and distinctive rap style made her a standout in the global pop landscape.

10-30.title: Warren Zevon
10-30.subtitle: After his terminal-cancer diagnosis, Warren Zevon made his last public appearance on the Late Show with David Letterman,...
10-30.text: After his terminal-cancer diagnosis, Warren Zevon made his last public appearance on the Late Show with David Letterman, giving the advice to "enjoy every sandwich".

10-30.title: Jon Stewart
10-30.subtitle: American comedians Jon Stewart and Stephen Colbert hosted the Rally to Restore Sanity and/or Fear in Washington, D.
10-30.text: American comedians Jon Stewart and Stephen Colbert hosted the Rally to Restore Sanity and/or Fear in Washington, D.C.

10-30.title: European Union
10-30.subtitle: Sweden becomes the first European Union member state to officially recognize Palestine as an independent and sovereign state.
10-30.text: Sweden becomes the first European Union member state to officially recognize Palestine as an independent and sovereign state.

10-30.title: 2020 Aegean Sea earthquake
10-30.subtitle: A magnitude 7.
10-30.text: A magnitude 7.0 earthquake strikes the Aegean Sea between Greece and Turkey, triggering a tsunami. At least 119 people die mainly due to collapsed buildings.

10-31.title: The Last Western Emperor
10-31.subtitle: A boy-king inherits a dying empire
10-31.text: On the last day of October, the young Romulus Augustulus was placed on the throne of the Western Roman Empire by his father Orestes. He was little more than a figurehead, a child emperor presiding over the final chapter of a civilization that had endured for over a millennium. Within a year, he would be deposed, and the Western Empire would exist no more.

10-31.title: Asbjorg Borgfelt Born
10-31.subtitle: Norway's sculptor of enduring forms
10-31.text: In Norway, Asbjorg Borgfelt was born with the artistic sensibility that would guide her career as a sculptor. Working in a country known for its dramatic landscapes, she crafted works that reflected the Nordic tradition of blending natural forms with artistic vision. Her contributions to Norwegian sculpture enriched the nation's cultural heritage throughout the mid-twentieth century.

10-31.title: Bust
10-31.subtitle: Approximately 400 workers completed the 60-foot (18 m) busts of U.
10-31.text: Approximately 400 workers completed the 60-foot (18 m) busts of U.S. presidents George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Theodore Roosevelt, and Abraham Lincoln at Mount Rushmore in South Dakota.

10-31.title: Hungarian Revolution of 1956
10-31.subtitle: Hungarian Revolution of 1956: A Revolutionary Headquarters is established in Hungary.
10-31.text: Hungarian Revolution of 1956: A Revolutionary Headquarters is established in Hungary. Following Imre Nagy's announcement of October 30, banned non-Communist political parties are reformed, and the MDP is replaced by the MSZMP.  József Mindszenty is released from prison. The Soviet Politburo makes the decision to crush the Revolution.

10-31.title: Vietnam War October surprise
10-31.subtitle: Vietnam War October surprise: Citing progress with the Paris peace talks, US President Lyndon B.
10-31.text: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. North Vietnam was supported by the Soviet Union and China, while South Vietnam was supported by the United States and other anti-communist nations. The conflict was the second of the Indochina wars and a proxy war of the Cold War between the Soviet Union and US.
10-31.location: War in Southeast Asia from 1955 to 1975

10-31.title: Provisional Irish Republican Army
10-31.subtitle: Three Provisional Irish Republican Army members escaped from Mountjoy Prison in Dublin aboard a hijacked helicopter that...
10-31.text: Three Provisional Irish Republican Army members escaped from Mountjoy Prison in Dublin aboard a hijacked helicopter that landed in the prison's exercise yard.

10-31.title: Indira Gandhi
10-31.subtitle: Indian prime minister Indira Gandhi was assassinated by two of her own Sikh bodyguards, sparking riots that killed thousands of Sikhs.
10-31.text: Indira Gandhi was an Indian stateswoman who served as the prime minister of India from 1966 to 1977 and again from 1980 until her assassination in 1984. She was India's first and only female prime minister, and a central figure in Indian politics as the leader of the Indian National Congress (INC). She was the daughter of Jawaharlal Nehru, the first prime minister of India. She was the mother of Rajiv Gandhi, who succeeded her as prime minister.

10-31.title: Singapore Airlines Crash
10-31.subtitle: Wrong runway, wrong weather, eighty-three dead
10-31.text: During a typhoon at Taipei's Chiang Kai-shek International Airport, Singapore Airlines Flight 006 attempted takeoff from the wrong runway and plowed into construction equipment at high speed. The Boeing 747 erupted in flames, killing eighty-three of the 179 people aboard. The disaster underscored the lethal consequences of pilot error in severe weather conditions.

10-31.title: Houston
10-31.subtitle: A federal grand jury in Houston, Texas indicts former Enron chief financial officer Andrew Fastow on 78 counts of wire f...
10-31.text: A federal grand jury in Houston, Texas indicts former Enron chief financial officer Andrew Fastow on 78 counts of wire fraud, money laundering, conspiracy and obstruction of justice related to the collapse of his ex-employer.

10-31.title: Moons of Pluto
10-31.subtitle: The discovery of the Plutonian moons Nix and Hydra, based on photographs taken by the Hubble Space Telescope nearly five months prior, was announced.
10-31.text: The discovery of the Plutonian moons Nix and Hydra, based on photographs taken by the Hubble Space Telescope nearly five months prior, was announced.

10-31.title: VSS Enterprise
10-31.subtitle: During a test flight, the VSS Enterprise, a Virgin Galactic experimental spaceflight test vehicle, suffers a catastrophi...
10-31.text: During a test flight, the VSS Enterprise, a Virgin Galactic experimental spaceflight test vehicle, suffers a catastrophic in-flight breakup and crashes in the Mojave Desert, California.

10-31.title: 2017 New York City truck attack
10-31.subtitle: An Uzbek immigrant drove a rented truck into cyclists and runners in Lower Manhattan, New York City, killing eight people.
10-31.text: An Uzbek immigrant drove a rented truck into cyclists and runners in Lower Manhattan, New York City, killing eight people.

11-01.title: Alemanni Cross the Rhine
11-01.subtitle: Barbarians breach Rome's ancient frontier
11-01.text: Emperor Valentinian I received alarming news: the Alemanni had crossed the Rhine and invaded Gaul, breaching a frontier that had defined the boundary between Roman civilization and Germanic wilderness for centuries. The incursion demanded an immediate military response and foreshadowed the relentless pressure that would eventually overwhelm the Western Empire's defenses.

11-01.title: Nordahl Grieg Born
11-01.subtitle: A poet who flew into the darkness of war
11-01.text: In Bergen, Norway, Nordahl Grieg was born with a voice that would speak for a generation. Poet, novelist, journalist, and political activist, he became one of Norway's most celebrated writers. During World War II, he served as a war correspondent, accompanying bombing missions over Europe. On one such mission to Berlin in 1943, his aircraft was shot down, silencing a brilliant voice forever.

11-01.title: Nuclear weapons testing
11-01.subtitle: Nuclear weapons testing: The United States successfully detonates Ivy Mike, the first thermonuclear device, at the Eniwetok atoll.
11-01.text: Nuclear weapons tests are experiments carried out to determine the performance of nuclear weapons and the effects of their explosion. Over 2,000 nuclear weapons tests have been carried out since 1945. Nuclear testing is a sensitive political issue. Governments have often performed tests to signal strength. Because of their destruction and fallout, testing has seen opposition by civilians as well as governments, with international bans having been agreed on.
11-01.location: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

11-01.title: Kerala
11-01.subtitle: The Indian states Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Mysore are formally created under the States Reorganisation Act; Kanyakuma...
11-01.text: The Indian states Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Mysore are formally created under the States Reorganisation Act; Kanyakumari district is joined to Tamil Nadu from Kerala. Delhi was established as a union territory.

11-01.title: Hockey puck
11-01.subtitle: After being struck in the face with a hockey puck, Jacques Plante played the rest of the game wearing a face mask, now m...
11-01.text: After being struck in the face with a hockey puck, Jacques Plante played the rest of the game wearing a face mask, now mandatory equipment for goaltenders in ice hockey.

11-01.title: Arecibo Observatory
11-01.subtitle: The Arecibo Observatory in Arecibo, Puerto Rico, with the largest radio telescope ever constructed, officially opens.
11-01.text: The Arecibo Observatory in Arecibo, Puerto Rico, with the largest radio telescope ever constructed, officially opens.

11-01.title: Elvis on Tour
11-01.subtitle: Elvis on Tour, a concert film that documented Elvis Presley's tour throughout the United States, opened.
11-01.text: Elvis on Tour, a concert film that documented Elvis Presley's tour throughout the United States, opened.

11-01.title: Alberto Natusch
11-01.subtitle: In Bolivia, Colonel Alberto Natusch executes a bloody coup d'état against the constitutional government of Wálter Guevara.
11-01.text: In Bolivia, Colonel Alberto Natusch executes a bloody coup d'état against the constitutional government of Wálter Guevara.

11-01.title: Head of the Chechen Republic
11-01.subtitle: President of the Chechen Republic Dzhokhar Dudayev declares sovereignty of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria from the Russian Federation.
11-01.text: President of the Chechen Republic Dzhokhar Dudayev declares sovereignty of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria from the Russian Federation.

11-01.title: Chhattisgarh Becomes a State
11-01.subtitle: A new star joins India's federal constellation
11-01.text: Carved from sixteen districts of eastern Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh officially became the twenty-sixth state of India. The creation of this new state, with Raipur as its capital, recognized the distinct cultural identity and developmental needs of central India's tribal heartland. Rich in minerals and forests, Chhattisgarh began its journey as a sovereign Indian state.

11-02.title: Tang Dynasty Assassination Plot
11-02.subtitle: Imperial intrigue on the Silk Road's edge
11-02.text: Emperor Gaozu of Tang sanctioned the assassination of a Western Turkic khagan by his Eastern Turkic rivals, a calculated act of imperial realpolitik. This early episode in the Tang campaigns against the Western Turks demonstrated the dynasty's willingness to exploit nomadic rivalries to secure China's vast frontiers, a strategy that would define Tang foreign policy for generations.

11-02.title: James Dunn Born
11-02.subtitle: From Wall Street to Hollywood's golden spotlight
11-02.text: In New York City, James Dunn was born to a stockbroker's family but chose the footlights over finance. After catching the eye of studio executives in the Broadway musical Sweet Adeline, he signed with Fox Film and became a Hollywood leading man. His career, spanning vaudeville to film, exemplified the dazzling possibilities of American entertainment in its golden age.

11-02.title: M1 motorway
11-02.subtitle: The first section of the M1 motorway, the first inter-urban motorway in the United Kingdom, is opened between the presen...
11-02.text: The first section of the M1 motorway, the first inter-urban motorway in the United Kingdom, is opened between the present junctions 5 and 18, along with the M10 motorway and M45 motorway.

11-02.title: Ngo Dinh Diem
11-02.subtitle: President Ngô Đình Diệm of South Vietnam was assassinated, marking the culmination of a coup d'état led by Dương Văn Minh.
11-02.text: President Ngô Đình Diệm of South Vietnam was assassinated, marking the culmination of a coup d'état led by Dương Văn Minh.

11-02.title: Velma Barfield
11-02.subtitle: The serial killer Velma Barfield became the first woman to be executed in the United States since 1962.
11-02.text: The serial killer Velma Barfield became the first woman to be executed in the United States since 1962.

11-02.title: British Satellite Broadcasting
11-02.subtitle: British Satellite Broadcasting and Sky Television plc merge to form BSkyB as a result of massive losses.
11-02.text: British Satellite Broadcasting and Sky Television plc merge to form BSkyB as a result of massive losses.

11-02.title: ISS Gets Its First Crew
11-02.subtitle: Humanity begins its permanent stay in space
11-02.text: American astronaut William Shepherd and Russian cosmonauts Sergei Krikalev and Yuri Gidzenko floated aboard the International Space Station as its first resident crew, beginning Expedition 1. Their 136-day stay launched an unbroken human presence in orbit that continues to this day, marking one of humanity's most remarkable achievements in cooperative exploration.
11-02.location: International Space Station, Low Earth Orbit

11-02.title: Lewis Hamilton
11-02.subtitle: Lewis Hamilton (pictured) overtook Timo Glock in the final corners of the Brazilian Grand Prix to win the Formula One championship by one point.
11-02.text: Lewis Hamilton (pictured) overtook Timo Glock in the final corners of the Brazilian Grand Prix to win the Formula One championship by one point.

11-02.title: Ethiopia–Tigray peace agreement
11-02.subtitle: A peace agreement is signed between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front, ending the Tigray War.
11-02.text: A peace agreement is signed between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front, ending the Tigray War.

11-03.title: The Poet Lucan Born
11-03.subtitle: Rome's fiery bard of civil war
11-03.text: In Cordoba, Roman Spain, Marcus Annaeus Lucanus was born into a family of literary distinction, the nephew of the philosopher Seneca. He would become one of Rome's most gifted poets, composing Pharsalia, an epic poem about the civil war between Caesar and Pompey. His brilliance and his entanglement in a conspiracy against Nero led to his forced suicide at just twenty-five.

11-03.title: Adi Dassler Born
11-03.subtitle: A cobbler who would shoe the world
11-03.text: In Herzogenaurach, Bavaria, Adolf 'Adi' Dassler was born with a cobbler's instinct for craft. Working from his mother's laundry room, he would create athletic shoes that Olympic champions wore to victory. His founding of Adidas in 1949, after a bitter split with his brother Rudolf, who founded Puma, launched one of the most iconic brands in sporting history.

11-03.title: Godzilla
11-03.subtitle: The first film featuring the giant monster known as Godzilla was released (poster pictured) nationwide in Japan.
11-03.text: The first film featuring the giant monster known as Godzilla was released (poster pictured) nationwide in Japan.

11-03.title: Suez Crisis
11-03.subtitle: Suez Crisis: During an invasion of the Gaza Strip, Israeli soldiers shot and killed hundreds of Palestinian refugees and...
11-03.text: Suez Crisis: During an invasion of the Gaza Strip, Israeli soldiers shot and killed hundreds of Palestinian refugees and local inhabitants in Khan Yunis.

11-03.title: Sputnik program
11-03.subtitle: Sputnik program: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 2.
11-03.text: Sputnik program: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 2. On board is the first animal to enter orbit, a dog named Laika.

11-03.title: Richard Nixon
11-03.subtitle: U.
11-03.text: Richard Milhous Nixon was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974. A member of the Republican Party, he represented California in both houses of the United States Congress before serving as the 36th vice president under President Dwight D. Eisenhower from 1953 to 1961. His presidency saw the reduction of U.S.
11-03.location: President of the United States from 1969 to 1974

11-03.title: Mariner program
11-03.subtitle: Mariner program: NASA launches the Mariner 10 toward Mercury.
11-03.text: Mariner program: NASA launches the Mariner 10 toward Mercury. On March 29, 1974, it becomes the first space probe to reach that planet.

11-03.title: Compact of Free Association
11-03.subtitle: The Compact of Free Association becomes law, granting the Federated States of Micronesia and the Marshall Islands independence from the United States.
11-03.text: The Compacts of Free Association (COFA) are international agreements establishing and governing the relationships of free association between the United States and the three Pacific Island sovereign states of the Federated States of Micronesia (FSM), the Republic of the Marshall Islands (RMI), and the Republic of Palau. As a result, these countries are sometimes known as the Freely Associated States (FASs). All three agreements next expire in 2043.

11-03.title: Space Shuttle program
11-03.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Atlantis launches on STS-66.
11-03.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

11-03.title: Abdullah Çatlı
11-03.subtitle: Abdullah Çatlı, a leader of the ultra-nationalist Grey Wolves, was killed in a car crash near Susurluk, Turkey, sparking...
11-03.text: Abdullah Çatlı, a leader of the ultra-nationalist Grey Wolves, was killed in a car crash near Susurluk, Turkey, sparking a scandal that exposed the depth of the state's complicity in organized crime.

11-03.title: Hailey Baptiste Born
11-03.subtitle: An American tennis talent begins her story
11-03.text: In Washington, D.C., Hailey Baptiste was born, destined to become one of American tennis's rising stars. With powerful groundstrokes and competitive fire, she would climb to a career-high world ranking of No. 39 in singles, winning titles on both the WTA Tour and Challenger circuits. Her journey represents the new generation of American tennis excellence.

11-03.title: 2020 United States presidential election
11-03.subtitle: The 2020 United States presidential election takes place between Democratic Joe Biden and Republican incumbent President Donald Trump.
11-03.text: The 2020 United States presidential election takes place between Democratic Joe Biden and Republican incumbent President Donald Trump. On November 7, Biden was declared the winner.

11-04.title: Constantinople Riots
11-04.subtitle: A city erupts over theological tyranny
11-04.text: The streets of Constantinople exploded in violence as citizens rioted against Emperor Anastasius I's attempts to impose Monophysite changes to Christian liturgy and his depositions of Chalcedonian patriarchs. A furious mob surged through the capital, attempting to proclaim the general Areobindus as a rival emperor. The riots exposed the explosive power of religious controversy in Byzantine politics.

11-04.title: Lucretiu Patrascanu Born
11-04.subtitle: A communist who dared to disagree
11-04.text: In Bacau, Romania, Lucretiu Patrascanu was born into the intellectual ferment of early twentieth-century Eastern Europe. He would become a leading Romanian communist politician, lawyer, and sociologist, but his independent thinking brought him into fatal conflict with the Stalinist establishment. Arrested, imprisoned, and ultimately executed in 1954, he became a martyr of conscience.

11-04.title: Robert A. Lovett
11-04.subtitle: Robert A.
11-04.text: Robert A. Lovett, United States Secretary of Defense, issued a memorandum establishing the National Security Agency, with responsibility for all communications intelligence for the government.

11-04.title: Soviet Union
11-04.subtitle: Soviet troops enter Hungary to end the Hungarian revolution against the Soviet Union that started on October 23.
11-04.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

11-04.title: Operation Fishbowl
11-04.subtitle: The United States concludes Operation Fishbowl, its final above-ground nuclear weapons testing series, in anticipation o...
11-04.text: The United States concludes Operation Fishbowl, its final above-ground nuclear weapons testing series, in anticipation of the 1963 Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty.

11-04.title: Arno
11-04.subtitle: The Arno River floods Florence, Italy, to a maximum depth of 6.
11-04.text: The Arno River floods Florence, Italy, to a maximum depth of 6.7 m (22 ft), leaving thousands homeless and destroying millions of masterpieces of art and rare books. Venice is also submerged on the same day at its record all-time acqua alta of 194 cm (76 in).

11-04.title: Salvador Allende
11-04.subtitle: Salvador Allende takes office as President of Chile, the first Marxist to become president of a Latin American country through open elections.
11-04.text: Salvador Guillermo Allende Gossens was a Chilean socialist politician who served as the 29th president of Chile from 1970 until his death in 1973. As a socialist committed to democracy, he has been described as the first Marxist to be elected president in a liberal democracy in Latin America.

11-04.title: Yitzhak Rabin
11-04.subtitle: Israeli prime minister Yitzhak Rabin (pictured) was assassinated by Yigal Amir, a right-wing extremist, at a peace rally...
11-04.text: Yitzhak Rabin was an Israeli statesman and general who was the prime minister of Israel, having served two terms in office, 1974–1977, and from 1992 until his assassination in 1995. He was the first prime minister to have been born in Palestine.

11-04.title: Tyrese Maxey Born
11-04.subtitle: Philadelphia's future franchise cornerstone
11-04.text: In Dallas, Texas, Tyrese Maxey was born with the basketball instincts that would make him one of the NBA's most exciting young guards. After playing for the Kentucky Wildcats, he was drafted by the Philadelphia 76ers, where his explosive scoring ability and relentless competitive drive quickly established him as a cornerstone of the franchise.

11-04.title: Barack Obama
11-04.subtitle: Barack Obama becomes the first person of biracial or African-American descent to be elected as President of the United States.
11-04.text: Barack Obama becomes the first person of biracial or African-American descent to be elected as President of the United States.

11-04.title: Aviation accidents and incidents
11-04.subtitle: In the first aviation incident involving an Airbus A380, Qantas Flight 32 suffered an uncontained engine failure and mad...
11-04.text: In the first aviation incident involving an Airbus A380, Qantas Flight 32 suffered an uncontained engine failure and made an emergency landing at Changi Airport in Singapore with no casualties.

11-05.title: Battle of Qantish
11-05.subtitle: Berber swords shatter a caliphate's grip
11-05.text: Berber forces led by Sulayman ibn al-Hakam clashed with the Umayyad caliph Muhammad II of Cordoba at the Battle of Qantish, dealing a devastating blow to Cordoban authority. The victory marked another chapter in the fragmentation of Al-Andalus, as rival factions tore apart the once-mighty caliphate that had made Iberia a center of Islamic civilization.

11-05.title: Natalie Schafer Born
11-05.subtitle: Television's most glamorous castaway
11-05.text: In Red Bank, New Jersey, Natalie Schafer was born with the elegance and comedic timing that would define a television icon. Though she worked steadily in theater and film for decades, she achieved immortality as the gloriously pampered Lovey Howell on Gilligan's Island, a role that made her one of the most recognized faces of 1960s American television.

11-05.title: Korean War
11-05.subtitle: Korean War: The 27th British Commonwealth Brigade succeeded in preventing a Chinese breakthrough at the Battle of Pakchon.
11-05.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

11-05.title: USS Rentz
11-05.subtitle: USS Rentz, USS Reeves and USS Oldendorf visit Qingdao, China; the first US naval visit to China since 1949.
11-05.text: USS Rentz, USS Reeves and USS Oldendorf visit Qingdao, China; the first US naval visit to China since 1949.

11-05.title: Ultranationalism
11-05.subtitle: Israeli ultra-nationalist rabbi Meir Kahane (pictured) was assassinated in a New York City hotel by an Arab gunman.
11-05.text: Israeli ultra-nationalist rabbi Meir Kahane (pictured) was assassinated in a New York City hotel by an Arab gunman.

11-05.title: Aline Chrétien
11-05.subtitle: Aline Chrétien (pictured) thwarted André Dallaire's attempt to assassinate her husband, Canadian prime minister Jean Chr...
11-05.text: Aline Chrétien (pictured) thwarted André Dallaire's attempt to assassinate her husband, Canadian prime minister Jean Chrétien, by locking the bedroom door in 24 Sussex Drive, their official residence in Ottawa.

11-05.title: Matty Beniers Born
11-05.subtitle: Seattle hockey's foundational draft pick
11-05.text: In Hingham, Massachusetts, Matty Beniers was born into a hockey family. He would become the Seattle Kraken's first-ever draft selection, chosen second overall in 2021. After starring at the University of Michigan, he won the Calder Memorial Trophy as NHL Rookie of the Year in 2023, establishing himself as the face of Seattle's expansion franchise.

11-05.title: Saddam Hussein
11-05.subtitle: Saddam Hussein, the former president of Iraq, and his co-defendants Barzan Ibrahim al-Tikriti and Awad Hamed al-Bandar, ...
11-05.text: Saddam Hussein, the former president of Iraq, and his co-defendants Barzan Ibrahim al-Tikriti and Awad Hamed al-Bandar, are sentenced to death in the al-Dujail trial for their roles in the 1982 massacre of 148 Shia Muslims.

11-05.title: Chang'e 1
11-05.subtitle: China's first lunar satellite, Chang'e 1, goes into orbit around the Moon.
11-05.text: China's first lunar satellite, Chang'e 1, goes into orbit around the Moon.

11-05.title: United States Army
11-05.subtitle: U.
11-05.text: The United States Army is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces. It is designated as the army of the United States in the United States Constitution. As a part of the United States Department of Defense, it is one of the six armed forces of the United States and one of the eight uniformed services of the United States. The Army is the most senior branch in order of precedence amongst the armed services.
11-05.location: Land service branch of the U.S. military

11-05.title: Mars Orbiter Mission
11-05.subtitle: India launches the Mars Orbiter Mission, its first interplanetary probe.
11-05.text: India launches the Mars Orbiter Mission, its first interplanetary probe.

11-05.title: Donald Trump
11-05.subtitle: Donald Trump becomes the first president of the United States to be elected to a non-consecutive second term in 132 year...
11-05.text: Donald John Trump is an American politician, media personality, and businessman who is the 47th president of the United States. A member of the Republican Party, he served as the 45th president from 2017 to 2021.
11-05.location: President of the United States (2017–2021; since 2025)

11-06.title: Constantinople's Walls Crumble
11-06.subtitle: Even the mightiest fortress bows to nature
11-06.text: A powerful earthquake shook Constantinople to its foundations, toppling fifty-seven towers of the legendary Theodosian Walls that had protected the city for decades. The damage to these massive fortifications, considered the most elaborate defensive system ever built, left the Byzantine capital temporarily vulnerable. A frantic rebuilding effort would restore the walls in record time.

11-06.title: McKinley Re-Elected
11-06.subtitle: America chooses continuity with a fateful twist
11-06.text: President William McKinley won re-election alongside his vice-presidential running mate, New York Governor Theodore Roosevelt. The victory seemed to guarantee four more years of McKinley's steady hand, but fate had other plans. Within a year, McKinley would fall to an assassin's bullet, and the energetic Roosevelt would transform the American presidency forever.

11-06.title: Meet the Press
11-06.subtitle: Meet the Press, the longest running television program in history, makes its debut on NBC Television.
11-06.text: Meet the Press, the longest running television program in history, makes its debut on NBC Television.

11-06.title: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ
11-06.subtitle: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ is appointed to head the South Vietnamese government by General Dương Văn Minh's junta, five days after ...
11-06.text: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ is appointed to head the South Vietnamese government by General Dương Văn Minh's junta, five days after the latter deposed and assassinated President Ngô Đình Diệm.

11-06.title: United States Atomic Energy Commission
11-06.subtitle: The United States Atomic Energy Commission tests the largest U.
11-06.text: The United States Atomic Energy Commission tests the largest U.S. underground hydrogen bomb, code-named Cannikin, on Amchitka Island in the Aleutians.

11-06.title: Kelly Barnes Dam
11-06.subtitle: The Kelly Barnes Dam in Stephens County, Georgia, collapsed; the resulting flood killed 39 people and caused US$2.
11-06.text: The Kelly Barnes Dam in Stephens County, Georgia, collapsed; the resulting flood killed 39 people and caused US$2.8 million in damages.

11-06.title: Sumburgh disaster
11-06.subtitle: Sumburgh disaster: A British International Helicopters Boeing 234LR Chinook crashes 2.
11-06.text: Sumburgh disaster: A British International Helicopters Boeing 234LR Chinook crashes 2.5 miles (4.0 km) east of Sumburgh Airport killing 45 people. It is the deadliest civilian helicopter crash on record.

11-06.title: Day'Ron Sharpe Born
11-06.subtitle: Brooklyn's big man begins his journey
11-06.text: In Greenville, North Carolina, Day'Ron Sharpe was born with the size and athleticism that would take him to the NBA. After a standout freshman season at the University of North Carolina, he was drafted by the Brooklyn Nets, where his rebounding and interior presence made him a valuable contributor to one of basketball's most storied franchises.

11-06.title: Ufton Nervet
11-06.subtitle: A man committing suicide parked his car on the railway tracks in Ufton Nervet, Berkshire, England, causing a derailment ...
11-06.text: A man committing suicide parked his car on the railway tracks in Ufton Nervet, Berkshire, England, causing a derailment that also killed six people on the train.

11-06.title: Tammy Baldwin
11-06.subtitle: Tammy Baldwin becomes the first openly gay politician to be elected to the United States Senate.
11-06.text: Tammy Baldwin becomes the first openly gay politician to be elected to the United States Senate.

11-06.title: Syrian civil war
11-06.subtitle: Syrian civil war: The Syrian Democratic Forces launched a successful military campaign to isolate and eventually capture...
11-06.text: The Syrian civil war was an armed conflict that began with the Syrian revolution in March 2011, when popular discontent with the Ba'athist regime ruled by Bashar al-Assad triggered large-scale protests and pro-democracy rallies across Syria, as part of the wider Arab Spring. The Assad regime responded to the protests with lethal force, which led to a series of defections, the emergence of armed opposition groups, and the civilian uprising descending into a civil war.

11-07.title: Athanasius Banished
11-07.subtitle: A patriarch exiled over a grain fleet
11-07.text: Athanasius, the twentieth Pope of Alexandria and champion of Trinitarian theology, was banished to the distant city of Trier on charges of blocking a grain fleet bound for Constantinople. The exile was the first of five he would endure during his turbulent forty-five-year episcopacy, a testament to both imperial hostility and his own unyielding defense of orthodoxy.

11-07.title: Battle of Leliefontein
11-07.subtitle: Three Victoria Crosses in a single afternoon
11-07.text: During the Second Boer War, the Royal Canadian Dragoons fought a fierce rearguard action at Leliefontein in South Africa. In the swirling chaos of cavalry combat, three Canadian soldiers displayed such extraordinary valor that each was awarded the Victoria Cross, Britain's highest military honor. The battle became one of Canada's proudest moments in the conflict.

11-07.title: Sidney Lanier Bridge
11-07.subtitle: A ship collision with the Sidney Lanier Bridge in the U.
11-07.text: A ship collision with the Sidney Lanier Bridge in the U.S. state of Georgia resulted in a bridge collapse (pictured), which killed ten people.

11-07.title: Bangladesh
11-07.subtitle: In Bangladesh, a joint force of people and soldiers takes part in an uprising led by Colonel Abu Taher that ousts and ki...
11-07.text: Bangladesh, officially the People's Republic of Bangladesh, is a country in South Asia. It is the eighth-most populous country in the world and among the most densely populated with a population of over 174 million within an area of 148,460 square kilometres (57,320 sq mi). Bangladesh shares land borders with India to the north, west, and east, and Myanmar to the southeast.

11-07.title: Cold War
11-07.subtitle: Cold War: The command post exercise Able Archer 83 begins, eventually leading to the Soviet Union to place air units in ...
