Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

11-24.title: Lee Harvey Oswald
11-24.subtitle: Lee Harvey Oswald, the assassin of President John F.
11-24.text: Lee Harvey Oswald, the assassin of President John F. Kennedy, is killed by Jack Ruby on live television. Robert H. Jackson takes a photograph of the shooting that will win the 1964 Pulitzer Prize in Photography.

11-24.title: D. B. Cooper
11-24.subtitle: After collecting a ransom payout of $200,000, D.
11-24.text: Dan Cooper, best known as D. B. Cooper, was the alias of an unidentified man who hijacked Northwest Orient Airlines Flight 305, a Boeing 727 aircraft flying from Portland, Oregon, to Seattle, Washington, on November 24, 1971. Cooper told the flight crew he had a bomb and demanded $200,000 in ransom and four parachutes upon landing in Seattle.

11-24.title: Donald Johanson
11-24.subtitle: Donald Johanson and Tom Gray discover the 40% complete Australopithecus afarensis skeleton, nicknamed "Lucy" (after The ...
11-24.text: Donald Johanson and Tom Gray discover the 40% complete Australopithecus afarensis skeleton, nicknamed "Lucy" (after The Beatles song "Lucy in the Sky with Diamonds"), in the Awash Valley of Ethiopia's Afar Depression.

11-24.title: Space Shuttle program
11-24.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Atlantis launches on STS-44.
11-24.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

11-24.title: Crossair Flight 3597 Crashes
11-24.subtitle: A singer's voice silenced near Zurich
11-24.text: Near Zurich Airport, Crossair Flight 3597 crashed into wooded hills during its approach from Berlin, killing twenty-four of the thirty-three people aboard. Among the dead were singer Melanie Thornton and two members of the German band Passion Fruit. The accident was attributed to pilot error during a non-precision approach in poor visibility conditions.

11-24.title: Avdhela Project
11-24.subtitle: The Avdhela Project, an Aromanian digital library and cultural initiative, was launched in Bucharest, Romania.
11-24.text: The Avdhela Project, an Aromanian digital library and cultural initiative, was launched in Bucharest, Romania.

11-24.title: Sukhoi Su-24
11-24.subtitle: A Russian Sukhoi Su-24 attack aircraft was shot down by a Turkish fighter jet after the former allegedly strayed into Tu...
11-24.text: A Russian Sukhoi Su-24 attack aircraft was shot down by a Turkish fighter jet after the former allegedly strayed into Turkish airspace and ignored warnings to change course.

11-24.title: Colombia
11-24.subtitle: The government of Colombia and the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia–People's Army sign a revised peace deal, bring...
11-24.text: The government of Colombia and the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia–People's Army sign a revised peace deal, bringing an end to the country's more than 50-year-long civil war.

11-24.title: Hibiscus Rising
11-24.subtitle: Hibiscus Rising (pictured), a sculpture commemorating the life of David Oluwale, was unveiled in Leeds.
11-24.text: Hibiscus Rising (pictured), a sculpture commemorating the life of David Oluwale, was unveiled in Leeds.

11-25.title: Servius Tullius Triumphs
11-25.subtitle: A legendary king celebrates victory over Etruria
11-25.text: Servius Tullius, the legendary sixth king of Rome, celebrated the first of his three triumphs for his victory over the Etruscans. Though much of his story blends history with myth, his reign was credited with establishing the Roman census, reforming the army, and expanding the city's boundaries. His triumph marked Rome's growing power over its Etruscan neighbors.

11-25.title: Tsunami
11-25.subtitle: A tsunami, caused by an earthquake in the Tyrrhenian Sea, devastates Naples and the Maritime Republic of Amalfi, among other places.
11-25.text: A tsunami, caused by an earthquake in the Tyrrhenian Sea, devastates Naples and the Maritime Republic of Amalfi, among other places.

11-25.title: Rudolf Hoss Born
11-25.subtitle: The commandant of industrialized death
11-25.text: In Baden-Baden, Germany, Rudolf Hoss was born into a devoutly Catholic family. He would become the commandant of Auschwitz, overseeing the murder of over one million people in the most horrific death camp of the Holocaust. Captured after the war while hiding under a false name, he was tried, convicted, and executed at Auschwitz itself, returning to the scene of his crimes.

11-25.title: World War II
11-25.subtitle: World War II: Statehood of Bosnia and Herzegovina is re-established at the State Anti-fascist Council for the National L...
11-25.text: World War II: Statehood of Bosnia and Herzegovina is re-established at the State Anti-fascist Council for the National Liberation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

11-25.title: Agatha Christie
11-25.subtitle: Agatha Christie's murder-mystery play The Mousetrap opens at the Ambassadors Theatre in London's West End after a premiere in Nottingham, UK.
11-25.text: Agatha Christie's murder-mystery play The Mousetrap opens at the Ambassadors Theatre in London's West End after a premiere in Nottingham, UK.  It will become the longest continuously running play in history.

11-25.title: Mishima Incident
11-25.subtitle: After failing to instigate a military coup to restore the powers of the Emperor of Japan, author Yukio Mishima and a mem...
11-25.text: After failing to instigate a military coup to restore the powers of the Emperor of Japan, author Yukio Mishima and a member of his militia committed ritual suicide.

11-25.title: Gang of 25
11-25.subtitle: A group of Conservative members of Parliament wrote a letter outlining their opposition to the economic policy of Margar...
11-25.text: A group of Conservative members of Parliament wrote a letter outlining their opposition to the economic policy of Margaret Thatcher, leading to speculation over a split from the party.

11-25.title: Notting Hill
11-25.subtitle: Thirty-six top musicians gather in a Notting Hill studio and record Band Aid's "Do They Know It's Christmas?" in order t...
11-25.text: Thirty-six top musicians gather in a Notting Hill studio and record Band Aid's "Do They Know It's Christmas?" in order to raise money for famine relief in Ethiopia.

11-25.title: Baku Earthquake Strikes
11-25.subtitle: The Caspian Sea floor shakes a nation
11-25.text: A magnitude 6.8 earthquake struck offshore Baku, Azerbaijan, followed a minute later by a 6.5 aftershock. The strongest earthquake to hit the region in 158 years, it killed twenty-six people, injured six hundred, and caused widespread damage to the Azerbaijani capital. The twin shocks reminded a developing nation of the seismic risks beneath its oil-rich foundations.

11-25.title: One Direction
11-25.subtitle: English-Irish boy band One Direction released their third studio album Midnight Memories which debuted at number one on ...
11-25.text: English-Irish boy band One Direction released their third studio album Midnight Memories which debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 and became the best-selling album of 2013.

11-26.title: Queen Adosinda Imprisoned
11-26.subtitle: A monastery becomes a political prison
11-26.text: The Asturian queen Adosinda was confined to a monastery by King Mauregatus, who feared that her nephew might use her influence to reclaim the throne. The forced monasticism of a royal woman was a common political tool in medieval Iberia, where the line between religious devotion and political imprisonment was perilously thin. The Kingdom of Asturias teetered on dynastic crisis.

11-26.title: Anna Maurizio Born
11-26.subtitle: The scientist who listened to the bees
11-26.text: In Switzerland, Anna Maurizio was born with a curiosity about the natural world that would lead her to spend over three decades studying bees. Working at the Federal Dairy Industry and Bacteriological Institute in Liebefeld, she developed new methods for analyzing pollen content in honey, advancing both apiculture and botanical science with meticulous devotion.

11-26.title: National Hockey League
11-26.subtitle: The National Hockey League is formed, with the Montreal Canadiens, Montreal Wanderers, Ottawa Senators, Quebec Bulldogs,...
11-26.text: The National Hockey League is formed, with the Montreal Canadiens, Montreal Wanderers, Ottawa Senators, Quebec Bulldogs, and Toronto Arenas as its first teams.

11-26.title: Mongolian People's Republic
11-26.subtitle: The Mongolian People's Republic is officially established after a new constitution, passed by the first State Great Khural, abolishes the monarchy.
11-26.text: The Mongolian People's Republic is officially established after a new constitution, passed by the first State Great Khural, abolishes the monarchy.

11-26.title: World War II
11-26.subtitle: World War II: Yugoslav Partisans convene the first meeting of the Anti-Fascist Council for the National Liberation of Yu...
11-26.text: World War II: Yugoslav Partisans convene the first meeting of the Anti-Fascist Council for the National Liberation of Yugoslavia at Bihać in northwestern Bosnia.

11-26.title: Southern Television broadcast interruption
11-26.subtitle: A speaker claiming to represent the "Intergalactic Association" interrupted a broadcast of Southern Television in South East England.
11-26.text: A speaker claiming to represent the "Intergalactic Association" interrupted a broadcast of Southern Television in South East England.

11-26.title: Brink's
11-26.subtitle: Six robbers broke into a Brink's-Mat warehouse at Heathrow Airport in London and stole £26 million in gold, diamonds and cash.
11-26.text: Six robbers broke into a Brink's-Mat warehouse at Heathrow Airport in London and stole £26 million in gold, diamonds and cash.

11-26.title: Tony Blair
11-26.subtitle: Tony Blair becomes the first Prime Minister of the United Kingdom to address the Oireachtas, the parliament of the Republic of Ireland.
11-26.text: Tony Blair becomes the first Prime Minister of the United Kingdom to address the Oireachtas, the parliament of the Republic of Ireland.

11-26.title: Bush Certified as Florida Winner
11-26.subtitle: A contested election reaches its bitter climax
11-26.text: Florida Secretary of State Katherine Harris certified George W. Bush as the winner of Florida's electoral votes, a decision that effectively handed him the presidency despite losing the national popular vote to Al Gore. The certification, amid fierce legal battles over ballot recounts, made the 2000 election the most contested in American history since 1876.

11-26.title: Friendly fire
11-26.subtitle: In a friendly-fire incident, a skirmish occurred between U.
11-26.text: In a friendly-fire incident, a skirmish occurred between U.S.-led NATO forces and Pakistani security forces at two military checkposts along the Afghanistan–Pakistan border.

11-26.title: Uncrewed spacecraft
11-26.subtitle: The robotic probe Insight lands on Elysium Planitia, Mars.
11-26.text: The robotic probe Insight lands on Elysium Planitia, Mars.

11-27.title: Luoyang Becomes Capital
11-27.subtitle: A dynasty reborn finds its new heart
11-27.text: Emperor Guangwu of Han declared Luoyang the capital of the restored Eastern Han dynasty, establishing a seat of power that would endure for nearly two centuries. The city, strategically located at the crossroads of China's central plains, became a center of learning, trade, and Buddhist influence. Its selection marked the definitive rebirth of Han civilization after years of civil war.

11-27.title: Jovette Bernier Born
11-27.subtitle: Quebec's voice on the airwaves and the page
11-27.text: In Saint-Fabien, Quebec, Jovette Bernier was born into French Canadian culture that she would help amplify through journalism, literature, and radio. Known simply as Jovette to her many listeners and readers, she became one of Quebec's most prominent media personalities, bridging the worlds of print and broadcast with warmth, wit, and an unmistakable Quebecois voice.

11-27.title: Korean War
11-27.subtitle: Korean War: The Chinese People's Volunteer Army launched multiple attacks against United Nations forces, beginning the Battle of Chosin Reservoir.
11-27.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

11-27.title: Lyndon B. Johnson
11-27.subtitle: President Lyndon B.
11-27.text: Lyndon Baines Johnson, also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States, serving from 1963 to 1969. He became president after the assassination of John F. Kennedy, under whom he had served as the 37th vice president from 1961 to 1963. A Southern Democrat, Johnson previously represented Texas in Congress for over 23 years, first as a U.S. representative from 1937 to 1949, and then as a U.S. senator from 1949 to 1961.
11-27.location: President of the United States from 1963 to 1969

11-27.title: First Exoplanet Atmosphere Detected
11-27.subtitle: Sodium glows in an alien sky
11-27.text: Astronomers announced the first-ever detection of an atmosphere on a planet outside our solar system, finding sodium in the skies of the gas giant HD 209458 b. This breakthrough observation, made using the Hubble Space Telescope, opened an entirely new field of exoplanet science. For the first time, humanity could chemically analyze the air of a distant world.
11-27.location: Space Telescope Science Institute, Baltimore, USA

11-27.title: Lady Gaga
11-27.subtitle: Lady Gaga performed the first concert of The Monster Ball Tour, which became the highest-grossing tour in history for a debut headlining artist.
11-27.text: Lady Gaga performed the first concert of The Monster Ball Tour, which became the highest-grossing tour in history for a debut headlining artist.

11-27.title: Mohsen Fakhrizadeh
11-27.subtitle: Nuclear physicist Mohsen Fakhrizadeh, regarded as the chief of Iran's nuclear program, was assassinated, allegedly by Mossad.
11-27.text: Nuclear physicist Mohsen Fakhrizadeh, regarded as the chief of Iran's nuclear program, was assassinated, allegedly by Mossad.

11-28.title: Treaty of Andelot Signed
11-28.subtitle: A Frankish king names his nephew as heir
11-28.text: At Andelot-Blancheville, King Guntram of Burgundy and Queen Brunhilda of Austrasia signed a treaty recognizing Brunhilda's son Childebert II as Guntram's heir. The agreement, brokered amid the constant fratricidal warfare of the Merovingian kingdoms, represented a rare moment of dynastic cooperation in an era when Frankish politics was dominated by assassination and betrayal.

11-28.title: Mary Bothwell Born
11-28.subtitle: A voice that soared from contralto to soprano
11-28.text: In Ontario, Canada, Mary Bothwell was born with a vocal range that defied easy classification. Beginning her career as a contralto, she eventually transitioned to soprano roles, performing in both opera and concert repertoire. Her parallel talent as a painter made her a true Renaissance woman of Canadian arts, bridging the worlds of music and visual expression.

11-28.title: All-Albanian Congress
11-28.subtitle: At the All-Albanian Congress, the Assembly of Vlorë was constituted, which declared the independence of the Albanian Vilayet from the Ottoman Empire.
11-28.text: At the All-Albanian Congress, the Assembly of Vlorë was constituted, which declared the independence of the Albanian Vilayet from the Ottoman Empire.

11-28.title: Mariner program
11-28.subtitle: Mariner program: NASA launches the Mariner 4 probe toward Mars.
11-28.text: Mariner program: NASA launches the Mariner 4 probe toward Mars.

11-28.title: Michel Micombero
11-28.subtitle: In a military coup, Michel Micombero abolished the Burundian monarchy and declared the country a republic with himself as president.
11-28.text: In a military coup, Michel Micombero abolished the Burundian monarchy and declared the country a republic with himself as president.

11-28.title: Pulsar
11-28.subtitle: The first pulsar (PSR B1919+21, in the constellation of Vulpecula) is discovered by two astronomers Jocelyn Bell Burnell and Antony Hewish.
11-28.text: A pulsar is a highly magnetized rotating neutron star that emits beams of electromagnetic radiation out of its magnetic poles. This radiation can be observed only when a beam of emission is pointing toward Earth, and is responsible for the pulsed appearance of emission. Neutron stars are very dense and have short, regular rotational periods. This produces a very precise interval between pulses that ranges from milliseconds to seconds for an individual pulsar.
11-28.location: Type of neutron star with beams of radiation

11-28.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
11-28.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-9, the first mission to carry the European Space Agency's Spacelab module.
11-28.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

11-28.title: South African Airways Flight 295
11-28.subtitle: South African Airways Flight 295 suffered a catastrophic in-flight fire and crashed into the Indian Ocean east of Maurit...
11-28.text: South African Airways Flight 295 was a scheduled international passenger flight from Chiang Kai-shek International Airport, Taipei, Taiwan, to Jan Smuts International Airport, Johannesburg, South Africa, with a stopover in Plaisance Airport, Plaine Magnien, Mauritius. On 28 November 1987, the aircraft serving the flight, a Boeing 747-200 Combi named Helderberg, experienced a catastrophic in-flight fire in the cargo area, broke up in mid-air, and crashed into the Indian Ocean east of Mauritius, killing all 159 people on board.
11-28.location: 1987 aviation accident in the Indian Ocean

11-28.title: Cold War
11-28.subtitle: Cold War: Velvet Revolution: In the face of protests, the Communist Party of Czechoslovakia announces it will give up its monopoly on political power.
11-28.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
11-28.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

11-28.title: Jackson Yee Born
11-28.subtitle: China's boy-band star becomes a cinema icon
11-28.text: In Huaihua, Hunan, Yi Yangqianxi was born, destined to become Jackson Yee, one of China's most celebrated entertainers. Rising to fame as a member of the boy group TFBoys, he reinvented himself as a serious actor, winning the Golden Rooster Award for Best Actor. His journey from teen idol to cinematic artist mirrored China's own cultural evolution.

11-28.title: LaMia Flight 2933
11-28.subtitle: LaMia Flight 2933 crashed near Medellín, Colombia, killing 71 people, many of whom were players from Chapecoense Football Club.
11-28.text: LaMia Flight 2933 crashed near Medellín, Colombia, killing 71 people, many of whom were players from Chapecoense Football Club.

11-29.title: Frankish Kingdom Divided
11-29.subtitle: Four sons carve up their father's realm
11-29.text: Following the death of King Chlothar I at Compiegne, his four sons divided the vast Frankish Kingdom among themselves. Charibert I, Guntram, Sigebert I, and Chilperic I each received a portion of the realm their father had spent a lifetime unifying. The partition set the stage for decades of fratricidal warfare that would define the Merovingian dynasty.

11-29.title: Mildred Gillars Born
11-29.subtitle: An American voice that betrayed its own country
11-29.text: In Portland, Maine, Mildred Gillars was born into an America she would one day betray. During World War II, she broadcast Nazi propaganda from Berlin under the nickname 'Axis Sally,' using her American accent to demoralize Allied troops. After the war, she became the first woman convicted of treason against the United States, serving twelve years in prison.

11-29.title: Enos
11-29.subtitle: Enos, a chimpanzee, is launched into space.
11-29.text: Enos, a chimpanzee, is launched into space. The spacecraft orbits the Earth twice and splashes down off the coast of Puerto Rico.

11-29.title: Lyndon B. Johnson
11-29.subtitle: U.
11-29.text: Lyndon Baines Johnson, also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States, serving from 1963 to 1969. He became president after the assassination of John F. Kennedy, under whom he had served as the 37th vice president from 1961 to 1963. A Southern Democrat, Johnson previously represented Texas in Congress for over 23 years, first as a U.S. representative from 1937 to 1949, and then as a U.S. senator from 1949 to 1961.
11-29.location: President of the United States from 1963 to 1969

11-29.title: Atari, Inc.
11-29.subtitle: Atari releases Pong, the first commercially successful video game.
11-29.text: Atari releases Pong, the first commercially successful video game.

11-29.title: Tellico Dam
11-29.subtitle: The controversial Tellico Dam project by the Tennessee Valley Authority is completed despite being ordered to shut down ...
11-29.text: The controversial Tellico Dam project by the Tennessee Valley Authority is completed despite being ordered to shut down from the Supreme Court of the United States' decision in Tennessee Valley Authority v. Hill.

11-29.title: Time bomb
11-29.subtitle: A time bomb planted by North Korean agents on Korean Air Flight 858 detonated over the Andaman Sea, killing all 115 people on board.
11-29.text: A time bomb planted by North Korean agents on Korean Air Flight 858 detonated over the Andaman Sea, killing all 115 people on board.

11-29.title: Yunus Musah Born
11-29.subtitle: A midfielder with three nations in his heart
11-29.text: In New York City, Yunus Musah was born to Ghanaian parents, growing up across the United States, Italy, and England before choosing to represent the American national team. His dynamic midfield play at Valencia, AC Milan, and Atalanta showcased the global nature of modern football talent, bridging cultures and continents with every precise pass.

11-29.title: Armed Forces of the Philippines
11-29.subtitle: The Armed Forces of the Philippines lay siege to the Peninsula Manila after soldiers led by Senator Antonio Trillanes stage a mutiny.
11-29.text: The Armed Forces of the Philippines lay siege to the Peninsula Manila after soldiers led by Senator Antonio Trillanes stage a mutiny.

11-29.title: United Nations General Assembly resolution 67/19
11-29.subtitle: In resolution 67/19, the United Nations General Assembly voted to accord the status of a non-member observer state to Palestine.
11-29.text: In resolution 67/19, the United Nations General Assembly voted to accord the status of a non-member observer state to Palestine.

11-30.title: Gregory of Tours Born
11-30.subtitle: The father of French history draws breath
11-30.text: In Clermont-Ferrand, Gaul, Gregory was born into a prominent Gallo-Roman family. As Bishop of Tours, he would compose the History of the Franks, the indispensable chronicle of Merovingian life that earned him the title Father of French History. Without his vivid accounts of kings, saints, and miracles, much of early medieval France would remain shrouded in darkness.

11-30.title: Jacques Barzun Born
11-30.subtitle: A French mind that illuminated American culture
11-30.text: In Creteil, France, Jacques Barzun was born, destined to become one of America's most influential public intellectuals. Emigrating to the United States, he spent decades at Columbia University, writing with equal authority about education, baseball, detective fiction, and classical music. His magnum opus, From Dawn to Decadence, became a landmark of cultural history.

11-30.title: World War II
11-30.subtitle: World War II: Signing of the Sino-Japanese Treaty of 1940 between the Empire of Japan and the newly formed Wang Jingwei-...
11-30.text: World War II: Signing of the Sino-Japanese Treaty of 1940 between the Empire of Japan and the newly formed Wang Jingwei-led Reorganized National Government of the Republic of China. This treaty was considered so unfair to China that it was compared to the Twenty-One Demands.

11-30.title: Mutesa II of Buganda
11-30.subtitle: Mutesa II, Kabaka of Buganda, was temporarily deposed and exiled to London by Andrew Cohen, the British governor of Uganda.
11-30.text: Mutesa II, Kabaka of Buganda, was temporarily deposed and exiled to London by Andrew Cohen, the British governor of Uganda.

11-30.title: Sylacauga
11-30.subtitle: A meteorite crashed through a roof in Sylacauga, Alabama, and hit a sleeping woman in the first verified case of a human...
11-30.text: A meteorite crashed through a roof in Sylacauga, Alabama, and hit a sleeping woman in the first verified case of a human being injured by an extraterrestrial object.

11-30.title: Pakistan People's Party
11-30.subtitle: The Pakistan Peoples Party is founded by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto, who becomes its first chairman.
11-30.text: The Pakistan Peoples Party is founded by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto, who becomes its first chairman.

11-30.title: The Wall
11-30.subtitle: The Wall, a rock opera and concept album by the English band Pink Floyd, was first released.
11-30.text: The Wall is the eleventh studio album by the English rock band Pink Floyd, released on 30 November 1979 by Harvest/EMI and Columbia/CBS Records. It is the last album to include all four post-Barrett-era band members. The album is a rock opera which follows the story of "Pink", a jaded rock star, as he constructs a psychological "wall" of social isolation. The Wall topped the US charts for 15 weeks and reached number three in the UK.

11-30.title: Cold War
11-30.subtitle: Cold War: In Geneva, representatives from the United States and the Soviet Union begin to negotiate intermediate-range n...
11-30.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
11-30.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

11-30.title: 1999 Seattle WTO protests
11-30.subtitle: A series of protests by anti-globalization activists against the World Trade Organization Ministerial Conference of 1999...
11-30.text: A series of protests by anti-globalization activists against the World Trade Organization Ministerial Conference of 1999 in Seattle forced the cancellation of the opening ceremonies.

11-30.title: 101st Shuttle Mission Launches
11-30.subtitle: The last human spaceflight of the millennium
11-30.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour roared into orbit on STS-97, the 101st Space Shuttle mission and the last human spaceflight of the twentieth century. The crew installed the first set of solar arrays on the International Space Station and prepared a docking port for the Destiny Laboratory Module, literally powering up humanity's permanent home in orbit.

11-30.title: John Sentamu
11-30.subtitle: John Sentamu was enthroned as Archbishop of York, becoming the first black archbishop in the Church of England.
11-30.text: John Sentamu was enthroned as Archbishop of York, becoming the first black archbishop in the Church of England.

12-01.title: Hasan ibn Ali Born
12-01.subtitle: The Prophet's grandson enters a divided world
12-01.text: In Medina, Hasan ibn Ali was born, the eldest grandson of the Prophet Muhammad and son of Ali and Fatima. He would briefly serve as caliph before ceding power to preserve Muslim unity, earning the title 'the peacemaker.' As the second Imam of Shia Islam, his legacy shaped the fundamental theological divide between Sunni and Shia that endures to this day.

12-01.title: Karna Birmingham Born
12-01.subtitle: Australia's illustrator of childhood wonder
12-01.text: In Australia, Karna Maria Birmingham was born with the artistic vision that would fill countless children's books with beauty. As an illustrator and printmaker, she brought stories to life through her delicate artwork, becoming one of Australia's most beloved visual storytellers. Her illustrations captured the innocence and wonder of childhood across generations of young readers.

12-01.title: Cold War
12-01.subtitle: Cold War: Opening date for signature of the Antarctic Treaty, which sets aside Antarctica as a scientific preserve and b...
12-01.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
12-01.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

12-01.title: Vietnam War
12-01.subtitle: Vietnam War: The first draft lottery in the United States is held since World War II.
12-01.text: Vietnam War: The first draft lottery in the United States is held since World War II.

12-01.title: Croatian Spring
12-01.subtitle: A period of political and economic reforms in the Socialist Republic of Croatia came to an end as the League of Communis...
12-01.text: A period of political and economic reforms in the Socialist Republic of Croatia came to an end as the League of Communists of Yugoslavia decided to purge the state's reformist leadership.

12-01.title: Boeing 727
12-01.subtitle: Two Boeing 727s, TWA Flight 514 and Northwest Orient Airlines Flight 6231, crashed in the eastern United States in unrel...
12-01.text: Two Boeing 727s, TWA Flight 514 and Northwest Orient Airlines Flight 6231, crashed in the eastern United States in unrelated circumstances, killing 95 people on board both aircraft.

12-01.title: 1988 Ordzhonikidze bus hijacking
12-01.subtitle: Five armed men hijacked a bus carrying schoolchildren and a teacher in Ordzhonikidze (now Vladikavkaz, Russia), and were...
12-01.text: Five armed men hijacked a bus carrying schoolchildren and a teacher in Ordzhonikidze (now Vladikavkaz, Russia), and were later given an Ilyushin Il-76 aircraft and ransom for the release of the hostages.

12-01.title: Cold War
12-01.subtitle: Cold War: East Germany's parliament abolishes the constitutional provision granting the Communist Party the leading role in the state.
12-01.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
12-01.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

12-01.title: Cold War
12-01.subtitle: Cold War: Ukrainian voters overwhelmingly approve a referendum for independence from the Soviet Union.
12-01.text: Ukraine is a country in Eastern Europe. It is the second-largest country in Europe after Russia, which borders it to the east and northeast. Ukraine also borders Belarus to the north; Poland and Slovakia to the west; Hungary, Romania and Moldova to the southwest; and the Black Sea and the Sea of Azov to the south and southeast. Kyiv is the nation's capital and largest city, followed by Kharkiv, Odesa, and Dnipro. The official language of Ukraine is Ukrainian.

12-01.title: Mexico's Democratic Revolution
12-01.subtitle: Seventy-one years of one-party rule end peacefully
12-01.text: Vicente Fox Quesada was inaugurated as president of Mexico, marking the first peaceful transfer of power to an opposition party in Mexican history. His victory over the Institutional Revolutionary Party, which had ruled continuously since 1929, was a watershed moment for Mexican democracy. The nation proved that democratic change could come through ballots rather than bullets.

12-01.title: Vivianne Miedema
12-01.subtitle: Vivianne Miedema scored six goals and had four assists for Arsenal W.
12-01.text: Vivianne Miedema scored six goals and had four assists for Arsenal W.F.C. in their 11–1 victory over Bristol City W.F.C., which broke the record for the most goals scored in a FA Women's Super League match.

12-02.title: Emperor Jianwen of Liang Born
12-02.subtitle: A prince destined for a puppet emperor's throne
12-02.text: Xiao Gang was born into the Chinese Liang dynasty's royal family, initially far from the line of succession. Fate and tragedy would elevate him to crown prince after his elder brother's death, and eventually to emperor. But his reign unfolded under the shadow of the rebel general Hou Jing, who held him captive, making Jianwen an emperor in chains.

12-02.title: Elisa Godinez Born
12-02.subtitle: Cuba's future first lady arrives
12-02.text: In Cuba, Elisa Godinez Gomez was born, destined to become the First Lady of Cuba as the wife of President Fulgencio Batista during his first presidential term from 1940 to 1944. Her life at the center of Cuban politics during one of the island nation's most turbulent periods placed her at the intersection of power, privilege, and revolutionary upheaval.

12-02.title: World War I
12-02.subtitle: World War I: Russia and the Central Powers sign an armistice at Brest-Litovsk, and peace talks leading to the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk begin.
12-02.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

12-02.title: Korean War
12-02.subtitle: Korean War: UN forces began a retreat from North Korea following defeat at the Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River.
12-02.text: The Korean War was an armed conflict on the Korean peninsula between North Korea and South Korea and their allies. North Korea was supported by China and the Soviet Union, while South Korea was supported by the United Nations Command (UNC) led by the United States. The war, which ended in stalemate, was the first great clash of arms of the Cold War and was one of, if not its deadliest proxy war.

12-02.title: Cuban Revolution
12-02.subtitle: Cuban Revolution: The yacht Granma, carrying Fidel Castro, Che Guevara and 80 other members of the 26th of July Movement, reached the shores of Cuba.
12-02.text: Cuban Revolution: The yacht Granma, carrying Fidel Castro, Che Guevara and 80 other members of the 26th of July Movement, reached the shores of Cuba.

12-02.title: Space Shuttle Atlantis
12-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on STS-27, a classified mission for the United States Department of Defense.
12-02.text: Space Shuttle Atlantis is a retired Space Shuttle orbiter vehicle which belongs to NASA, the spaceflight and space exploration agency of the United States. Atlantis was manufactured by the Rockwell International company in Southern California and was delivered to the Kennedy Space Center in Eastern Florida in April 1985. Atlantis is the fourth operational and the second-to-last Space Shuttle built. Its maiden flight was STS-51-J made from October 3 to 7, 1985.

12-02.title: Malayan Communist Party
12-02.subtitle: The Malayan Communist Party and the Malaysian government signed a peace accord to end a 21-year communist insurgency.
12-02.text: The Malayan Communist Party and the Malaysian government signed a peace accord to end a 21-year communist insurgency.

12-02.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
12-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-35, carrying the ASTRO-1 spacelab observatory.
12-02.text: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Named after the first American ship to circumnavigate the globe, and the female personification of the United States, Columbia was the first of five Space Shuttle orbiters to fly in space, debuting the Space Shuttle launch vehicle on its maiden flight on April 12, 1981 and becoming the first spacecraft to be re-used after its first flight when it launched on STS-2 on

12-02.title: Space Shuttle Discovery
12-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-53 for the United States Department of Defense.
12-02.text: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-53 for the United States Department of Defense.

12-02.title: Space Shuttle program
12-02.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: STS-61: NASA launches the Space Shuttle Endeavour on a mission to repair the Hubble Space Telescope.
12-02.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

12-02.title: Enron Files for Bankruptcy
12-02.subtitle: Eleven billion dollars vanish into thin air
12-02.text: Less than two months after revealing massive accounting fraud, the Texas-based energy giant Enron filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, evaporating nearly eleven billion dollars in shareholder wealth overnight. The collapse, then the largest bankruptcy in American history, destroyed the accounting firm Arthur Andersen and became the defining corporate scandal of the early twenty-first century.

12-02.title: Ghost Ship warehouse fire
12-02.subtitle: Thirty-six people died when a fire broke out at an illegally converted warehouse in Oakland, California, the deadliest U.
12-02.text: On December 2, 2016, at about 11:20 p.m. PST, a fire started in a former warehouse that had been unlawfully converted into an artist collective with living spaces in Oakland, California, which was hosting a concert with 80–100 attendees. The building, located in the Fruitvale neighborhood, was zoned for only industrial purposes; residential and entertainment uses were prohibited. The blaze killed 36 people, making it the deadliest fire in the history of Oakland.

12-03.title: Berengar Crowned Emperor
12-03.subtitle: A pope crowns Italy's embattled king
12-03.text: Pope John X placed the imperial crown on the head of Berengar I of Italy, making him Holy Roman Emperor. The coronation came at a time of extraordinary turmoil, with Saracen raiders threatening Rome itself. John X, a politically astute pontiff, sought to unify Italy under Berengar's banner, though the new emperor's hold on power would prove tragically fragile.

12-03.title: Bert Hawke Born
12-03.subtitle: Western Australia's future Labor premier
12-03.text: In Kapunda, South Australia, Albert Hawke was born into a working-class family that shaped his lifelong commitment to the Labor movement. He would rise to become the eighteenth Premier of Western Australia, serving from 1953 to 1959. His government championed workers' rights and social welfare, leaving a lasting imprint on the state's political landscape.

12-03.title: Cardiothoracic surgery
12-03.subtitle: Cardiac surgeon Christiaan Barnard (pictured) performed the first successful human heart transplant on Louis Washkansky ...
12-03.text: Cardiac surgeon Christiaan Barnard (pictured) performed the first successful human heart transplant on Louis Washkansky at Groote Schuur Hospital in Cape Town, South Africa.

12-03.title: Pakistan Air Force
12-03.subtitle: With the Pakistani Air Force launching the unsuccessful pre-emptive airstrikes on several forward Indian Air Force airba...
12-03.text: With the Pakistani Air Force launching the unsuccessful pre-emptive airstrikes on several forward Indian Air Force airbases and radar installations, the 1971 India-Pakistan War begins and India also joins the Bangladesh Liberation War, siding with Bangladesh.

12-03.title: The Who concert disaster
12-03.subtitle: Eleven people were crushed in a human stampede at a concert by British rock band the Who in Cincinnati, Ohio, U.
12-03.text: Eleven people were crushed in a human stampede at a concert by British rock band the Who in Cincinnati, Ohio, U.S.

12-03.title: NASA
12-03.subtitle: NASA lost contact with the Mars Polar Lander moments before it reached the atmosphere of Mars and disappeared.
12-03.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

12-03.title: Rocket Mail Delivery
12-03.subtitle: A crewed rocket delivers the US Mail
12-03.text: At the Mojave Air and Space Port in California, XCOR Aerospace made history with the first crewed rocket aircraft delivery of United States Mail. The daring flight demonstrated the potential of private spaceflight companies to perform practical missions, blending the romance of rocketry with the mundane necessity of postal service in one extraordinary demonstration.

12-03.title: 2009 Hotel Shamo bombing
12-03.subtitle: A suicide bombing in Mogadishu, Somalia, killed 25 people, including three ministers of the Transitional Federal Government.
12-03.text: A suicide bombing in Mogadishu, Somalia, killed 25 people, including three ministers of the Transitional Federal Government.

12-04.title: The Poet Persius Born
12-04.subtitle: Roman satire gains a sharp new voice
12-04.text: In Volterra, Etruria, Aulus Persius Flaccus was born into a wealthy equestrian family. Despite a tragically short life of just twenty-seven years, he would produce a small but influential body of satirical poetry that dissected Roman society with surgical precision. His six surviving satires, dense with moral philosophy, influenced writers from Juvenal to the Renaissance humanists.

12-04.title: Albert Norden Born
12-04.subtitle: A journalist who served two Germanys
12-04.text: In Berlin, Albert Norden was born into a politically engaged family. As a communist politician and journalist, he would navigate the turbulent waters of twentieth-century German history, from the Weimar Republic through Nazi persecution, exile, and eventually prominence in the German Democratic Republic. His career embodied the ideological fault lines that divided Germany for decades.

12-04.title: Million Dollar Quartet
12-04.subtitle: The Million Dollar Quartet (Elvis Presley, Jerry Lee Lewis, Carl Perkins, and Johnny Cash) get together at Sun Studio for the first and last time.
12-04.text: The Million Dollar Quartet (Elvis Presley, Jerry Lee Lewis, Carl Perkins, and Johnny Cash) get together at Sun Studio for the first and last time.

12-04.title: Gemini 7
12-04.subtitle: Launch of Gemini 7 with crew members Frank Borman and Jim Lovell.
12-04.text: Launch of Gemini 7 with crew members Frank Borman and Jim Lovell. The Gemini 7 spacecraft was the passive target for the first crewed space rendezvous performed by the crew of Gemini 6A.

12-04.title: Fred Hampton
12-04.subtitle: Fred Hampton, the leader of the Illinois chapter of the Black Panther Party, and Mark Clark were killed in a raid by Chi...
12-04.text: Fred Hampton, the leader of the Illinois chapter of the Black Panther Party, and Mark Clark were killed in a raid by Chicago police officers in what many scholars consider an illegal assassination.

12-04.title: Indo-Pakistani War
12-04.subtitle: Indo-Pakistani War: The Indian Navy launched a successful attack against the Pakistan Navy at Karachi, sinking three ships with no Indian casualties.
12-04.text: Indo-Pakistani War: The Indian Navy launched a successful attack against the Pakistan Navy at Karachi, sinking three ships with no Indian casualties.

12-04.title: Assassinations of George Moscone and Harvey
12-04.subtitle: Following the murder of Mayor George Moscone, Dianne Feinstein becomes San Francisco's first female mayor.
12-04.text: Following the murder of Mayor George Moscone, Dianne Feinstein becomes San Francisco's first female mayor.

12-04.title: United States Navy
12-04.subtitle: US Navy aircraft from USS John F.
12-04.text: US Navy aircraft from USS John F. Kennedy and USS Independence attack Syrian missile sites in Lebanon in response to an F-14 being fired on by an SA-7. One A-6 Intruder and A-7 Corsair are shot down. One American pilot is killed, one is rescued, and one is captured.

12-04.title: Jackson Holliday Born
12-04.subtitle: Baseball royalty's next chapter begins
12-04.text: In Stillwater, Oklahoma, Jackson Holliday was born to former MLB All-Star Matt Holliday. The younger Holliday would follow his father's path to the major leagues, being selected first overall by the Baltimore Orioles in the 2022 draft. His debut in 2024 continued a family legacy that has become one of baseball's most compelling dynasties.

12-04.title: December 2005 protest for democracy in
12-04.subtitle: Tens of thousands of people in Hong Kong protest for democracy and call on the government to allow universal and equal suffrage.
12-04.text: Tens of thousands of people in Hong Kong protest for democracy and call on the government to allow universal and equal suffrage.

12-04.title: Il Canto degli Italiani
12-04.subtitle: "Il Canto degli Italiani" officially became the national anthem of Italy, 66 years after it was provisionally chosen fol...
12-04.text: "Il Canto degli Italiani" officially became the national anthem of Italy, 66 years after it was provisionally chosen following the birth of the Italian Republic.

12-05.title: Cicero's Final Oration
12-05.subtitle: Rome's greatest orator speaks his last warning
12-05.text: In the Roman Senate, Marcus Tullius Cicero delivered the fourth and final of his Catiline Orations, urging the senators to act decisively against the conspiracy of Catiline. The speech, a masterpiece of rhetorical persuasion, sealed the fate of the conspirators. Cicero's defense of the republic in its darkest hour remains one of history's most celebrated acts of political oratory.

12-05.title: Walt Disney Born
12-05.subtitle: The man who taught the world to dream
12-05.text: In Chicago, Illinois, Walter Elias Disney was born into a modest family. He would become the most influential figure in the history of animation, creating Mickey Mouse, Snow White, and an entertainment empire that bears his name. With twenty-two Academy Awards, more than any individual in history, Disney transformed American culture and gave the world a vocabulary of shared wonder.

12-05.title: Soviet Union
12-05.subtitle: The Soviet Union adopts a new constitution and the Kirghiz Soviet Socialist Republic is established as a full Union Republic of the USSR.
12-05.text: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991. It was the world's third-most populous country, largest by area, and bordered twelve countries. A diverse multinational state, it was organized as a federal union of national republics, the largest and most populous being the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic. In practice, its government and economy were highly centralized.

12-05.title: Great Smog of London
12-05.subtitle: The "Great Smog of London" (pictured) began and lasted for five days, causing 12,000 deaths and leading to the Clean Air Act 1956.
12-05.text: The Great Smog was a severe air pollution event that affected London, England, in December 1952. A period of unusually cold weather, combined with an anticyclone and windless conditions, collected airborne pollutants—mostly arising from the use of coal—to form a thick layer of smog over the city. It lasted from Friday, 5 December to Tuesday, 9 December 1952, then dispersed quickly when the weather changed.

12-05.title: Controlled-access highway
12-05.subtitle: Britain's first motorway, the Preston Bypass, opened to the public.
12-05.text: Britain's first motorway, the Preston Bypass, opened to the public.

12-05.title: Gough Whitlam
12-05.subtitle: Gough Whitlam took office as the 21st Prime Minister of Australia and formed a duumvirate with his deputy Lance Barnard,...
12-05.text: Gough Whitlam took office as the 21st Prime Minister of Australia and formed a duumvirate with his deputy Lance Barnard, ending 23 years of Liberal–Country Party government.

12-05.title: Egypt
12-05.subtitle: Egypt breaks diplomatic relations with Syria, Libya, Algeria, Iraq and South Yemen in retaliation to preventing Presiden...
12-05.text: Egypt breaks diplomatic relations with Syria, Libya, Algeria, Iraq and South Yemen in retaliation to preventing President Anwar el-Sadat from pursuing negotiations with Israel at the Tripoli confer.

12-05.title: Soobin Born
12-05.subtitle: Tomorrow's leader finds his voice today
12-05.text: In Sangnok-gu, Ansan, South Korea, Choi Soo-bin was born, destined to lead one of K-pop's most successful new groups. As leader of Tomorrow X Together, formed by Big Hit Entertainment in 2019, Soobin would captivate global audiences with his vocals, songwriting, and charismatic stage presence, representing the next generation of Korean pop music excellence.

12-05.title: Space Shuttle Endeavour
12-05.subtitle: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches on STS-108, carrying the Expedition 4 crew to the International Space Station.
12-05.text: Space Shuttle Endeavour is a retired orbiter from NASA's Space Shuttle program and the fifth and final operational Shuttle built. It embarked on its first mission, STS-49, in May 1992 and its 25th and final mission, STS-134, in May 2011. STS-134 was expected to be the final mission of the Space Shuttle program, but with the authorization of STS-135 by the United States Congress, Atlantis became the last shuttle to fly.

12-05.title: Westroads Mall shooting
12-05.subtitle: A gunman killed nine people at a shopping mall in Omaha, Nebraska.
12-05.text: A gunman killed nine people at a shopping mall in Omaha, Nebraska.

12-05.title: Exploration Flight Test-1
12-05.subtitle: Exploration Flight Test-1, the first flight test of Orion, is launched.
12-05.text: Exploration Flight Test-1, the first flight test of Orion, is launched.

12-06.title: Hasan al-Askari Born
12-06.subtitle: The eleventh Imam arrives under watchful eyes
12-06.text: In Medina, Hasan al-Askari was born into the line of the Prophet Muhammad. As the eleventh of the Twelve Imams in Shia Islam, he lived most of his life under the strict surveillance of Abbasid caliphs in the garrison town of Samarra. Despite his confinement, his spiritual authority among the Shia faithful remained unshakeable until his death at just twenty-eight.

12-06.title: Agnes Moorehead Born
12-06.subtitle: Five decades of fearless performance
12-06.text: In Clinton, Massachusetts, Agnes Moorehead was born with a talent that would span radio, stage, film, and television across five decades. From her haunting performance in Citizen Kane to her iconic role as Endora on Bewitched, she earned four Academy Award nominations, a Primetime Emmy, and two Golden Globes, becoming one of America's most versatile and respected actresses.

12-06.title: Irish National Liberation Army
12-06.subtitle: The Irish National Liberation Army exploded a time bomb in Ballykelly, Northern Ireland, killing eleven British Army soldiers and six civilians.
12-06.text: The Irish National Liberation Army exploded a time bomb in Ballykelly, Northern Ireland, killing eleven British Army soldiers and six civilians.

12-06.title: Marc Lépine
12-06.subtitle: 25-year-old Marc Lépine, claiming to be "fighting feminism", killed fourteen women before committing suicide at the Écol...
12-06.text: 25-year-old Marc Lépine, claiming to be "fighting feminism", killed fourteen women before committing suicide at the École Polytechnique in Montreal, Canada.

12-06.title: Italian Air Force
12-06.subtitle: An Italian Air Force military jet, abandoned by its pilot after an on-board fire, crashed into a high school near Bologn...
12-06.text: An Italian Air Force military jet, abandoned by its pilot after an on-board fire, crashed into a high school near Bologna, killing 12 students and injuring 88 other people.

12-06.title: Babri Masjid
12-06.subtitle: The Babri Masjid in Ayodhya, India, was demolished by Hindu Kar Sevaks, who believed that it was built on the birthplace of Rama.
12-06.text: The Babri Masjid in Ayodhya, India, was demolished by Hindu Kar Sevaks, who believed that it was built on the birthplace of Rama.

12-06.title: Food and Drug Administration
12-06.subtitle: The United States Food and Drug Administration approves Saquinavir, the first protease inhibitor to treat HIV/AIDS.
12-06.text: The United States Food and Drug Administration approves Saquinavir, the first protease inhibitor to treat HIV/AIDS. Within 2 years of its approval, annual deaths from AIDS in the United States fall from over 50,000 to approximately 18,000.

12-06.title: A&M Records, Inc. v. Napster, Inc.
12-06.subtitle: A&M Records, Inc.
12-06.text: A&M Records, Inc. v. Napster, Inc.: The Recording Industry Association of America sues the peer-to-peer file-sharing service Napster, alleging copyright infringement.

12-06.title: Tehran Military Crash
12-06.subtitle: A transport plane falls into an apartment block
12-06.text: An Iranian Air Force C-130 military transport aircraft crashed into a ten-story apartment building in a residential area of Tehran, killing all ninety-four people aboard and twelve more on the ground. The disaster, caused by engine failure shortly after takeoff, devastated an entire neighborhood and remains one of Iran's worst aviation tragedies.

12-06.title: NASA
12-06.subtitle: NASA reveals photographs taken by Mars Global Surveyor suggesting the presence of liquid water on Mars.
12-06.text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space exploration. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., NASA operates ten field centers across the US and is organized into mission directorates for Science, Space Operations, Exploration Systems Development, Space Technology, Aeronautics Research, and Mission Support.

12-07.title: Cicero Is Assassinated
12-07.subtitle: The republic's voice is silenced forever
12-07.text: On a road near his seaside villa at Formiae, Marcus Tullius Cicero was overtaken by soldiers sent to execute him. Proscribed as an enemy of the state by Mark Antony, Rome's greatest orator extended his neck from his litter and met death with stoic dignity. His head and hands were displayed in the Forum, a gruesome warning that the Roman Republic was truly dead.

12-07.title: Kateryna Bilokur Born
12-07.subtitle: She saw the souls of flowers
12-07.text: In a village in the Poltava region of Ukraine, Kateryna Bilokur was born into peasant life. Despite receiving no formal training, she became one of Ukraine's most celebrated folk artists, painting flowers in oil on canvas with a transcendent beauty that moved critics to declare she could see the souls of flowers. Her art elevated Ukrainian folk tradition to fine art.

12-07.title: World War II
12-07.subtitle: World War II: Attack on Pearl Harbor: The Imperial Japanese Navy carries out a surprise attack on the United States Paci...
12-07.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

12-07.title: Apollo 17
12-07.subtitle: Apollo 17, the last Apollo Moon mission, is launched.
12-07.text: Apollo 17, the last Apollo Moon mission, is launched. The crew takes the photograph known as The Blue Marble as they leave the Earth.

12-07.title: Texas
12-07.subtitle: In Texas, Charles Brooks Jr.
12-07.text: Texas is the most populous state in the South of the United States. It borders Louisiana to the east, Arkansas to the northeast, Oklahoma to the north, New Mexico to the west, and an international border with the Mexican states of Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo León, and Tamaulipas to the south and southwest, that forms a natural boundary delineated by the Rio Grande. Texas has a coastline on the Gulf of Mexico to the southeast.

12-07.title: Galileo
12-07.subtitle: The Galileo spacecraft arrives at Jupiter, a little more than six years after it was launched by Space Shuttle Atlantis during Mission STS-34.
12-07.text: Galileo was an American robotic space probe that studied the planet Jupiter and its moons, as well as the asteroids Gaspra and Ida. Named after the Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei, it consisted of an orbiter and an entry probe. It was delivered into Earth orbit on October 18, 1989, by Space Shuttle Atlantis, during STS-34.
12-07.location: First NASA mission to orbit Jupiter (1989–2003)

12-07.title: Dane Belton Born
12-07.subtitle: A future NFL safety starts his journey
12-07.text: In Tampa, Florida, Dane Belton was born with the football instincts that would carry him to the NFL. After a standout career at the University of Iowa, he was drafted by the New York Giants, where his combination of range, intelligence, and tackling ability made him a reliable presence in the defensive backfield of one of football's most storied franchises.

12-07.title: Al Anbar Governorate
12-07.subtitle: The United States transferred its last base in the Al Anbar Governorate to the Iraqi government, ending the Anbar campaign.
12-07.text: The United States transferred its last base in the Al Anbar Governorate to the Iraqi government, ending the Anbar campaign.

12-07.title: Furry convention
12-07.subtitle: The annual furry convention Midwest FurFest was targeted in an unsolved chlorine gas attack.
12-07.text: A furry convention is a formal gathering of members of the furry fandom – people who are interested in the concept of fictional non-human animal characters with human characteristics. These conventions provide a place for fans to meet, exchange ideas, transact business and engage in entertainment and recreation centered on this concept. Originating in California during the mid-1980s, as of 2016 there are over 50 furry conventions worldwide each year.
12-07.location: Formal gathering of members of the furry fandom

12-07.title: JAXA
12-07.subtitle: The JAXA probe Akatsuki successfully enters orbit around Venus five years after the first attempt.
12-07.text: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA)  is the Japanese national air and space agency. Through the merger of three previously independent organizations, JAXA was formed on 1 October 2003. JAXA is responsible for research, technology development and launch of satellites into orbit, and is involved in many more advanced missions such as asteroid exploration and possible human exploration of the Moon. Its motto is One JAXA and its corporate slogan is Explore to Realize.
12-07.location: Japan's national air and space agency

12-08.title: Horace Is Born
12-08.subtitle: Rome's finest lyric poet takes his first breath
12-08.text: In Venusia, a small town in southern Italy, Quintus Horatius Flaccus was born to a freed slave father who invested everything in his son's education. Horace would become the leading lyric poet of the Augustan age, his Odes, Satires, and Epistles setting the standard for Latin verse. The rhetorician Quintilian judged his lyrics the only Latin poetry worth reading.

12-08.title: Sun Li-jen Born
12-08.subtitle: The Rommel of the East draws first breath
12-08.text: In Anhui Province, China, Sun Li-jen was born into a nation on the brink of transformation. He would become one of China's most brilliant military commanders, earning the nickname 'Rommel of the East' for his stunning victories against Japanese forces in Burma during World War II. His New 1st Army was hailed as the finest Chinese military unit of the war.

12-08.title: World War II
12-08.subtitle: World War II: Japanese forces simultaneously invade Shanghai International Settlement, Malaya, Thailand, Hong Kong, the ...
12-08.text: World War II: Japanese forces simultaneously invade Shanghai International Settlement, Malaya, Thailand, Hong Kong, the Philippines, and the Dutch East Indies. (See December 7 for the concurrent attack on Pearl Harbor in the Western Hemisphere.)

12-08.title: Dwight D. Eisenhower
12-08.subtitle: U.
12-08.text: U.S. President Dwight D. Eisenhower delivers his "Atoms for Peace" speech, which leads to an American program to supply equipment and information on nuclear power to schools, hospitals, and research institutions around the world.

12-08.title: John Lennon
12-08.subtitle: English musician John Lennon  was murdered at the entrance of the Dakota, where he resided in New York City.
12-08.text: English musician John Lennon  was murdered at the entrance of the Dakota, where he resided in New York City.

12-08.title: Cold War
12-08.subtitle: Cold War: The Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty is signed by U.
12-08.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
12-08.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

12-08.title: Belovezha Accords
12-08.subtitle: Belarusian, Russian and Ukrainian leaders signed the Belovezh Accords, agreeing to dissolve the Soviet Union and establi...
12-08.text: Belarusian, Russian and Ukrainian leaders signed the Belovezh Accords, agreeing to dissolve the Soviet Union and establish the Commonwealth of Independent States.

12-08.title: Cricket Australia
12-08.subtitle: The Australian Cricket Board's cover-up of Shane Warne and Mark Waugh's involvement with bookmakers was revealed.
12-08.text: The Australian Cricket Board's cover-up of Shane Warne and Mark Waugh's involvement with bookmakers was revealed.

12-08.title: DeMario Douglas Born
12-08.subtitle: Speed and agility personified on the gridiron
12-08.text: In Broward County, Florida, DeMario Douglas was born with the quickness that would make him one of football's most elusive receivers. After starring at Liberty University, he was drafted by the New England Patriots, where his explosive play-making ability and precise route-running continued the franchise's tradition of developing talented wide receivers.

12-08.title: SpaceX COTS Demo Flight 1
12-08.subtitle: With the second launch of the Falcon 9, and the first launch of the Dragon, SpaceX becomes the first private company to ...
12-08.text: With the second launch of the Falcon 9, and the first launch of the Dragon, SpaceX becomes the first private company to successfully launch, orbit and recover a spacecraft.

12-08.title: Syrian civil war
12-08.subtitle: The Syrian civil war ended when Bashar al-Assad's party, the Syrian Ba'ath Party, surrendered to the Syrian opposition.
12-08.text: The Syrian civil war was an armed conflict that began with the Syrian revolution in March 2011, when popular discontent with the Ba'athist regime ruled by Bashar al-Assad triggered large-scale protests and pro-democracy rallies across Syria, as part of the wider Arab Spring. The Assad regime responded to the protests with lethal force, which led to a series of defections, the emergence of armed opposition groups, and the civilian uprising descending into a civil war.

12-09.title: Belisarius Enters Rome
12-09.subtitle: The eternal city opens its gates without a fight
12-09.text: The Byzantine general Belisarius marched into Rome unopposed as the Gothic garrison fled the capital. The bloodless entry marked a stunning early success in Emperor Justinian's ambitious campaign to reclaim the lost provinces of the Western Roman Empire. For the first time in sixty years, Rome was under imperial control, though a long and devastating war with the Goths lay ahead.

12-09.title: Margaret Brundage Born
12-09.subtitle: The pastel queen of Weird Tales
12-09.text: In Chicago, Margaret Brundage was born with an artistic talent that would define the golden age of pulp fiction illustration. Working in pastels on illustration board, she created most of the covers for Weird Tales magazine between 1933 and 1938, her sensuous and sometimes controversial artwork becoming inseparable from the era's greatest horror and fantasy fiction.

12-09.title: World War I
12-09.subtitle: World War I: Field Marshal Allenby captures Jerusalem from the Ottoman Empire.
12-09.text: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers. Major areas of conflict included Europe and the Middle East, as well as parts of Africa and the Asia-Pacific. The war saw important developments in weaponry including tanks, aircraft, artillery, machine guns, and chemical weapons.

12-09.title: Cold War
12-09.subtitle: Cold War: Harry Gold is sentenced to 30 years in jail for helping Klaus Fuchs pass information about the Manhattan Project to the Soviet Union.
12-09.text: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc, which began in the aftermath of the Second World War and ended with the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.
12-09.location: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

12-09.title: Coronation Street
12-09.subtitle: The first episode of Coronation Street, the world's longest-running television soap opera, is broadcast in the United Kingdom.
12-09.text: Coronation Street is a British television soap opera created by Granada Television and shown on ITV since 9 December 1960. The programme centres on a cobbled, terraced street in the fictional town of Weatherfield in Greater Manchester. The location was itself based on Salford, the hometown of the show's first screenwriter and creator, Tony Warren.

12-09.title: Douglas Engelbart
12-09.subtitle: Douglas Engelbart gave what became known as "The Mother of All Demos", publicly debuting the computer mouse, hypertext, ...
12-09.text: Douglas Engelbart gave what became known as "The Mother of All Demos", publicly debuting the computer mouse, hypertext, and the bit-mapped graphical user interface using the oN-Line System (NLS).

12-09.title: William P. Rogers
12-09.subtitle: U.
12-09.text: U.S. secretary of state William P. Rogers proposed a plan, later called the Rogers Plan, for a ceasefire in the War of Attrition; Egypt's and Jordan's acceptance of the plan over Palestine Liberation Organization objections led to civil war in Jordan in September 1970.

12-09.title: World Health Organization
12-09.subtitle: A World Health Organization commission of scientists certified the global eradication of smallpox (virus pictured), maki...
12-09.text: A World Health Organization commission of scientists certified the global eradication of smallpox (virus pictured), making it the only human infectious disease to date to have been completely eradicated.

12-09.title: Diana Nikitina Born
12-09.subtitle: Latvia's ice princess takes her first glide
12-09.text: In Riga, Latvia, Diana Nikitina was born, destined to become her nation's most celebrated figure skater. A Golden Bear of Zagreb champion and Latvian national champion, she represented Latvia at the 2018 Winter Olympics in PyeongChang. Her career brought international attention to Baltic figure skating and inspired a new generation of Latvian athletes.

12-09.title: Space Shuttle program
12-09.subtitle: Space Shuttle program: Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on STS-116 carrying the P5 truss segment of the International Space Station.
12-09.text: The Space Shuttle program was the fourth human spaceflight program carried out by the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), which accomplished routine transportation for Earth-to-orbit crew and cargo from 1981 to 2011. Its official program name was Space Transportation System (STS), taken from a 1969 plan for a system of reusable spacecraft where it was the only item funded for development, as a proposed nuclear shuttle in the plan was cancelled in 1972.

12-09.title: Rod Blagojevich
12-09.subtitle: Rod Blagojevich, the governor of Illinois, was arrested on corruption charges, including for attempting to sell the U.
12-09.text: Rod Blagojevich, the governor of Illinois, was arrested on corruption charges, including for attempting to sell the U.S. Senate seat being vacated by president-elect Barack Obama.

12-09.title: Park Geun-hye
12-09.subtitle: Park Geun-hye, the president of South Korea, was impeached, marking the culmination of the country's political scandal.
12-09.text: Park Geun-hye, the president of South Korea, was impeached, marking the culmination of the country's political scandal.

12-10.title: Last Emperor of Chen Born
12-10.subtitle: A poet-prince destined for dynasty's end
12-10.text: In the refined courts of the Chinese Chen dynasty, the future Emperor Houzhu was born. The last ruler of his line, he would be remembered more for his poetry and love of luxury than for statecraft. When the Sui dynasty conquered the Chen in 589, Houzhu was found hiding in a well with his concubines, a pitiful end to a once-proud southern dynasty.

12-10.title: First Nobel Prizes Awarded
12-10.subtitle: A dynamite fortune fuels humanity's highest honor
12-10.text: On the fifth anniversary of Alfred Nobel's death, the first Nobel Prizes were awarded in Stockholm, fulfilling the Swedish chemist's extraordinary bequest. The prizes in Physics, Chemistry, Medicine, Literature, and Peace transformed Nobel's dynamite fortune into the world's most prestigious recognition of human achievement, a legacy of inspiration that endures over a century later.

12-10.title: Theodore Roosevelt
12-10.subtitle: U.
12-10.text: U.S. President Theodore Roosevelt is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for his role in the mediation of the Russo-Japanese War, becoming the first American to win a Nobel Prize in any field.

12-10.title: Second World War
12-10.subtitle: Second World War: The Royal Navy capital ships HMS Prince of Wales and HMS Repulse were sunk by Imperial Japanese Navy torpedo bombers east of Malaya.
12-10.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

12-10.title: Zanzibar
12-10.subtitle: Zanzibar gains independence from the United Kingdom as a constitutional monarchy, under Sultan Jamshid bin Abdullah.
12-10.text: Zanzibar is a Tanzanian archipelago off the coast of East Africa. It is located in the Indian Ocean, and consists of many small islands and two large ones: Unguja and Pemba Island. The capital is Zanzibar City, located on the island of Unguja. Its historic centre, Stone Town, is a World Heritage Site.

12-10.title: Otis Redding
12-10.subtitle: Soul and blues singer, Otis Redding, along with members of his back-up group, The Bar-Kays, are killed when their helico...
12-10.text: Soul and blues singer, Otis Redding, along with members of his back-up group, The Bar-Kays, are killed when their helicopter crashes in Lake Monona in Madison, Wisconsin.

12-10.title: Mae Salong
12-10.subtitle: Around the northern Thai village of Mae Salong, remnants of Chinese anti-communist forces now fighting on behalf of the ...
12-10.text: Around the northern Thai village of Mae Salong, remnants of Chinese anti-communist forces now fighting on behalf of the Thai government launched a five-year campaign against local communist insurgents.

12-10.title: Arab–Israeli conflict
12-10.subtitle: Arab–Israeli conflict: Prime Minister of Israel Menachem Begin and President of Egypt Anwar Sadat are jointly awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.
12-10.text: Arab–Israeli conflict: Prime Minister of Israel Menachem Begin and President of Egypt Anwar Sadat are jointly awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

12-10.title: Raúl Alfonsín
12-10.subtitle: Raúl Alfonsín (pictured) became the first democratically elected president of Argentina to take office after more than s...
12-10.text: Raúl Alfonsín (pictured) became the first democratically elected president of Argentina to take office after more than seven years of military dictatorship.

12-10.title: Mongolian Revolution
12-10.subtitle: Mongolian Revolution: At the country's first open pro-democracy public demonstration, Tsakhiagiin Elbegdorj announces th...
12-10.text: Mongolian Revolution: At the country's first open pro-democracy public demonstration, Tsakhiagiin Elbegdorj announces the establishment of the Mongolian Democratic Union.

12-10.title: Jeremie Frimpong Born
12-10.subtitle: Speed that blurs the sideline
12-10.text: In Amsterdam, Jeremie Frimpong was born with the explosive pace that would make him one of European football's most exciting full-backs. After beginning at Manchester City's academy and shining at Celtic, he became a key player at Bayer Leverkusen before joining Liverpool. His ability to combine defensive duties with attacking flair has redefined the modern full-back role.

12-10.title: Tornado outbreak of December 10–11, 2021
12-10.subtitle: A widespread, deadly, and violent tornado outbreak slams the Central, Midwestern, and Southern regions of the United States.
12-10.text: A widespread, deadly, and violent tornado outbreak slams the Central, Midwestern, and Southern regions of the United States. Eighty-nine people are killed by the tornadoes, with most of the fatalities occurring in Kentucky, where a single tornado kills 57 people, and injures hundreds of others. Another tornado in Edwardsville, Illinois strikes an Amazon warehouse, killing six workers.

12-11.title: Han Dynasty Ends
12-11.subtitle: Four centuries of glory yield to a new age
12-11.text: Emperor Xian of Han, the fourteenth and last emperor of the Eastern Han dynasty, was forced to abdicate by Cao Pi, son of the legendary warlord Cao Cao. The abdication ended over four centuries of Han rule, one of China's greatest imperial dynasties, and ushered in the tumultuous Three Kingdoms period that would inspire literature and legend for millennia.

12-11.title: Hermina Tyrlova Born
12-11.subtitle: The mother of Czech animation awakens
12-11.text: In the Moravian countryside, Hermina Tyrlova was born with the imagination that would make her the mother of Czech animation. Over a career spanning decades, she produced more than sixty animated children's short films using puppets and stop-motion techniques. Her work enchanted generations of Czech children and established a tradition of animation excellence that endures in Zlin to this day.

12-11.title: World War II
12-11.subtitle: World War II: Germany and Italy declare war on the United States, following the Americans' declaration of war on the Emp...
12-11.text: World War II or the Second World War was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. Nearly all of the world's countries participated. Tanks and aircraft played major roles, the latter enabling the strategic bombing of cities and delivery of the only nuclear weapons used in war. World War II was the deadliest conflict in history, causing the death of 60 to 75 million people.

12-11.title: French Upper Volta
12-11.subtitle: French Upper Volta and French Dahomey gain self-government from France, becoming the Republic of Upper Volta (now Burkin...
12-11.text: French Upper Volta and French Dahomey gain self-government from France, becoming the Republic of Upper Volta (now Burkina Faso) and the Republic of Dahomey (now Benin), respectively, and joining the French Community.

12-11.title: Apollo 17
12-11.subtitle: Apollo 17 becomes the sixth and final Apollo mission to land on the Moon.
12-11.text: Apollo 17 becomes the sixth and final Apollo mission to land on the Moon.

12-11.title: Onyeka Okongwu Born
12-11.subtitle: Atlanta's high-flying big man takes shape
12-11.text: In Los Angeles, Onyeka Okongwu was born with the athleticism and basketball instincts that would make him a first-round NBA draft pick. After a dominant season at USC, he was selected by the Atlanta Hawks, where his shot-blocking, rebounding, and energy off the bench quickly made him one of the league's most impactful young frontcourt players.

12-11.title: Cronulla
12-11.subtitle: Demonstrations in Cronulla, a suburb of Sydney, against recent violence towards locals turned into a series of race riots.
12-11.text: Demonstrations in Cronulla, a suburb of Sydney, against recent violence towards locals turned into a series of race riots.

12-11.title: Mexican drug war
12-11.subtitle: The first action in the Mexican drug war began as President Felipe Calderón ordered Mexican soldiers and Federal Police ...
12-11.text: The first action in the Mexican drug war began as President Felipe Calderón ordered Mexican soldiers and Federal Police units into the state of Michoacán.

12-11.title: Insurgency in the Maghreb
12-11.subtitle: Insurgency in the Maghreb: Two car bombs explode in Algiers, Algeria, one near the Supreme Constitutional Court and the ...
12-11.text: Insurgency in the Maghreb: Two car bombs explode in Algiers, Algeria, one near the Supreme Constitutional Court and the other near the offices of the United Nations.

12-11.title: Bernie Madoff
12-11.subtitle: American stockbroker Bernie Madoff was arrested and charged with securities fraud in a $64.
